If God didn't allow sin to come into the world, [who did]{.class_3759}? If God didn't allow the Cherub to become Satan, [who did]{.class_3759}? If there is one supreme, ultimate "Sovereign" (with absolute control), how can circumstances be laid at the feet of anyone else but HIM, when all is said and done?

This is the argument that the Atheist has with God.

To solve the problem, John Calvin (1509--1564) set up a system where God acted arbitrarily in "electing" and "choosing" some, while reprobating others---on no other grounds than His "good pleasure." But the truth again lies between the positions. God DID have a reason for chastening Job, and although He confesses no ["cause"]{.class_3760} to Satan (1:8)---in the sense of Acts 18:14---He gets [three ]{.class_3759}positive results from Job's trials:

1\. He gets a true book written recording the real sufferings of a real man, and, in this book, suffering is tied to the SUPERNATURAL. That, in itself, is worth \$500,000,000,000,000 to the human race.

2\. He succeeded in showing Job his real "self" which lay at the root of Job's religious and moral self, and thereby, he humbled Job to the place where he accepted his chastening (42:1--6).

3\. He gave Himself "cause" to double Job's possessions and then reward and bless him for "fearing God and eschewing evil."

Therefore, when John Calvin gives us his HPD of Ephesians 1:4 about the "arbitrary good pleasure" of God being behind His acts, Calvin is merely confessing that he refused to have a God whom he could not comprehend intellectually. It is true that Calvin occasionally gets it right: "Let us not attribute to Him either cruelty or ignorance, as if He did things in spite and unadvisedly, but let us acknowledge that He proceeds in everything and through everything with admirable justice, with goodness and equity in all that He does." But if you tried to sew that statement into Calvin's "Limited Atonement" and "Irresistible Grace" (see any Systematic Theology by any Reformed theologian), the whole "cotton pickin" theological garment would split at the seams.

We have commented on verses 25--26 under Job 7:7.

9:27 If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:

28 I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.

29 If I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?

30 If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;

31 Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.

32 For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, and we should come together in judgment.

33 Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, that might lay his hand upon us both.

34 Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:

35 Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.

Job grumbles on, talking now to God and then to himself and then to his friends, and before he finishes this discourse, his friends are so angry they call him a liar to his face (11:3).

If Job says to himself, "All right I will forget the whole mess. I'll go out and get stoned and comfort myself" (vs. 27), it still won't work because he will still have to give account of himself to God (vs. 28). "If I am pre-destinated to be damned, like some of those babies Calvin and Augustine talked about, what in the world have I been doing praying, sacrificing, and 'eschewing' evil? I'dda done better to get drunk and stay drunk! And what if I did clean up (vs. 30)? I could scrub up till I was as lilywhite as Mr. Clean, but if God had a mind to, He could shove me into a sewage ditch (vs. 31), and I'd be so stinking 'nasty' when I climbed out. My clothes would be afraid of contracting leprosy (Lev. 13:51--52) from [me]{.class_3759}" (vs. 31). Job raves on: "God isn't a sinner like me (vs. 32). I haven't got a chance to present my case in court (vs. 32), and if I got there, who in the world would represent me (vs. 33)? Who ever heard of a man who could represent a SINNER and GOD at the same time? Let Him quit beating me to death! I already fear Him. What is the point in terrorizing me when I already fear Him? If He'd let up on me a minute, I would answer, but I haven't drawn a breath of air without mucous or pain, or both, for seven days."

Job is in the "crucible." The refining fires of the Master Refiner (Mal. 3:2--3) are scorching his soul as well as his body, and the torrid heat waves of God's "ore furnace" are suffocating him. Oh, unhappy man!

"[If I wash myself with snow water"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30)---which was considered to be more pure than ordinary water---["and make my hands never so clean" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 30)---that is, cleaner than ever before---["Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch"]{.class_3760} (vs. 31). Job doesn't have much confidence left in God, does he? Or perhaps he has the ultimate confidence in God: He has faith to believe that God will get him filthy in a minute, if he tries to clean himself up (Rom. 4:5, 5:4).

Somewhere in the Arctic wastelands of ice and snow, the polar star shines down on a bronze plaque which marks the grave of an Arctic explorer. This man died in the nineteenth century, and his grave is still marked with this inscription: "Now wash me, and I shall be whiter than SNOW." When water from melted snow is rubbed into wool, it whitens it like no bleach can do the job, and after all, our probationer (Job 7:18) is one of God's "sheep." Sheep cannot clean themselves even by rubbing against a fence. [The Shepherd ]{.class_3759}has to clean them (1 John 1:7). We-e-e-l-l-l GLORY!!

Job's fixed day to appear in court (vs. 33) cannot come until the ["daysman"]{.class_3760} shows up, and the ["daysman,"]{.class_3760} of course, is the great Advocate of 1 John 2:1---the Lord Jesus Christ. (Origen, writing the so-called [Septuagint]{.class_3759}, nearly one hundred years after the apostle John was dead, has written the word "MEDIATOR" into the text by transferring 1 Timothy 2:5 back into the Old Testament. This (as countless other proofs) was one more attempt to kid the faculty of Pensacola Christian College, Bryan, Concordia, Covenant, Eastern, Dordt, San Francisco, Biola, and Eastern Mennonite College into thinking that the apostles were stupid enough to use an Alexandrian Greek Old Testament.)

It is most remarkable to notice that no sooner has Job pleaded for a ["daysman,"]{.class_3760} than the very requirements for the perfect daysman show up (Job 10:4--5). The ["daysman"]{.class_3760} must "learn obedience" by the things which He suffers, and He must be made ["like unto his brethren"]{.class_3760} from Abraham's seed---not the ["nature of angels"]{.class_3760} (Heb. 2:16).

The [RV ]{.class_3759}(1881) reads "lye" for ["snow water."]{.class_3760} Taylor (Dead Bible) adds the word "lye" to "purest water" so that he will offend neither the Holy Spirit nor the apostate committee of 1885, but he was simply copying the footnote of the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901), which read in this manner. Unable to find the references in Leviticus 13 and Jude 23 (on infected clothing), the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759} paraphrases verse 13 clear out of the original intention of the author, while the [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}puts the "lye" back into verse 30. The word "lye" occurs in no Hebrew manuscripts on earth. It was added to suit the fancy of the "eminent textual authorities" who resented the words of the Holy Spirit.

According to the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, the phrases used by Job (vss. 5--8) are "poetical," and God is stepping around on the Atlantic and Pacific oceans in verse 8. We may properly evaluate the "qualified and unquestioned scholarship" of these "godly, dedicated men," who "devoted their lives to etc., etc.," by saying that Rev. Rawlinson (the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, p. 155) is talking through his hat while the Devil is stepping on his brains. Earthquakes [will ]{.class_3759}remove mountains (Zech. 14:1--6), and they [will ]{.class_3759}overturn them (Rev. 16:20); you have to be a Bible- rejecting infidel to believe otherwise. Even if it is one who believes in the "full, plenary, verbal inspiration of the originals!"

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw958.xhtml}

CHAPTER 10

10:1 My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.

2 I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.

3 Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?

The groaning and lamenting continues. Job is weary of his life (vs. 1). He is going to complain in the bitterness of his soul (vs. 1) no matter what God, man, or the Devil does about it. He will say to God: ["Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). Didn't God say: ["Come now, saith the Lord, let us reason together"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 1:18)? Then why all of the silence? (See The Silence of God, Sir Robert Anderson.) ["Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress\...despise\...and shine upon the\...wicked"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3)? Job has unwittingly taken Satan's place as the "adversary" and the "accuser" (Prov. 17:11 and comments). He is accusing God of being an oppressor and being in collusion with evil doers.

10:4 Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as a man seeth?

5 Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man's days,

6 That thou inquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?

7 Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.

8 Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.

9 Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?

10 Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?

11 Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.

12 Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy visitation has preserved my spirit.

13 And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.

"[Hast thou eyes of flesh?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). Not until Matthew chapter 1. ["Seest thou as a man seeth?" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 4). Not until Matthew chapter 1. ["Are thy days as the days of man?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). Not until Luke 4. ["Are thy years as man's days?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). Not until Luke chapter 23. Job's point is Satan's point up until Luke chapter 4 (see Heb. 5:8--9). Not even the third or the second member of the Trinity would rebuke Satan (Zech. 3:2) until Luke 4:8. Satan's accusation to God about man is (or was!) simply: "What do YOU know about it? You've never been hungry or thirsty, and you've never had to put up with fatigue, death, temptation, or sickness!" But at Luke 23 (see Col. 2:14--15), it is all over but the shouting, and the shouting is on the way (1 Thess. 4:13--18).

"[Thou knowest that I am not wicked"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). Well, he wasn't wicked in the sense that the word is used by the Pharisees in Luke 5:30 and John 9:24 (see also Paul's usage in Gal. 2:15). This Old Testament usage was to denominate a very wicked, immoral, ruthless [lawbreaker]{.class_3761}. In this sense, Job was not "wicked," but in the sense of Romans 3:12--23 and Isaiah 64:6 (and Ecc. 7:20), there wasn't a straw to choose between Job and Judas. ["And there is none that can deliver out of thine hand"]{.class_3762} (vs. 7): That is, "What are you working me over for as though you were trying to find something wrong? You know [where ]{.class_3761}and [how ]{.class_3761}and [when ]{.class_3761}I have sinned, and I couldn't get away from you if I tried---["there is none that can deliver out of thine hand!"]{.class_3762} There is no need to take Job apart like a busted watch; he is not going anywhere, because who could get away from God anyway (Psa. 139:1--11)? At this point, Job is dealing directly with God, and he might as well have been alone in the city dump as far as his "comforters" were concerned.

"[Thine hands have made me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8). True: See Job 13:12 and Psalm 139:5.

"[Thou has made me as the clay"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). True: see Genesis 3:19.

"[Thou has clothed me with skin and flesh"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). True.

"[Thou hast\...fenced me with bones and sinews"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). True.

"[Thou hast granted me life and favor"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). True: ["I know that this is with thee."]{.class_3762} \[And imagine someone saying that Job was merely "obeying the commandments" (6:10) when he said, ["I have not concealed the words of the Holy One!"]{.class_3762}\]

Job's question is a question that has been asked (or at least contemplated by every adult human being on this earth who believed in God at all. God has poured us out "like milk" and curdled us "like cheese" (vs. 10), and after going to all of the trouble described in verses 8--12, what is the point in destroying His own creation (vs. 8) or oppressing a sinner (vs. 3) for being a human being (vss. 5--6)? Haven't these thoughts ever occured to some of you?

Man will have to have his Maker do some "eye opening" for him before he can answer these questions. Mankind needs an "eyesalve" (Rev. 3:18). [Hagar ]{.class_3759}needed it to see the Angel of the Lord (Gen. 16); [Job ]{.class_3759}needed it to see his own righteousness; [Elisha ]{.class_3759}needed it to see his Divine protection (2 Kings 6:17); [Abraham ]{.class_3759}needed it to see the sacrifice (Gen. 22:13); the two on the road to Emmaus needed an "eye opener" to see the Resurrection (Luke 24:31); [John ]{.class_3759}needed his eyes opened to see the Holy Ghost (John 1:33); and [Elisha ]{.class_3759}needed God to open his eyes to see the Rapture (2 Kings 2)---and so will you.

Verse 10 indicates the conception and development of the human embryo where the soft juices are changed into a firm mass. Verse 11 shows that our bones are like stake posts; the cross bars are the sinews, and the whole "tabernacle" (Cf. Exod. 25:9 and comments) has a cloth tent put over it---["skin and flesh."]{.class_3760} (For a really edifying fifteen minutes, try Adam Clarke's exposition of verse 10: "Respicit ad fetus in matris utero primam formationem quum in embryonem\...lac quodommodo referent me formasti\...ac dein concrescere in utero ac coalescere jubet\...chyle\...chylopoetic organs\...lacteal vessels\....)

The "paraphrase" of the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759} is far enough off of the sense of the passage to omit altogether; so we shall omit it. Duhm and Budde object to ["iniquity"]{.class_3760} in verse 6 and want it to read "built." Peake (and others) think that verse 7 should read "there is no treachery." The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(written long after the New Testament was completed) says that verse 8 should read "afterward thou didst change thy mind." The [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}changes the punctuation at verse 13 so that verse 14 will read differently, and Bullinger thinks ["wicked"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7) should be "lawless." Charlie Brown thinks Snoopy should be the Red Baron.

10:14 If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.

15 If I be wicked, woe unto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift up my head. I am full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;

16 For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.

17 Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.

Notice how Job fluctuates on the matter of his own self-righteousness. ["If I be wicked"]{.class_3760} (9:29). ["If I be wicked"]{.class_3760} (10:15) is found on either side of ["Thou knowest I am not wicked"]{.class_3760}! Conscience will not let us off, and you can bet your last Green Stamp that when the Pharisee was thanking God that he was "not like other men" (Luke 18:11), something way down deep inside him was saying: "Yea, but you could be."

Sin marks a man (vs. 14), and although you can pull a nail out of a nail hole, the hole still remains to "mark" where the nail was. God will not acquit any man from his iniquity without a just basis for doing it (Rom. 3:26), for if God acquitted men for iniquity, God Himself would be unrighteous (Exod. 34:7).

The Liberal "god" of 1900--1980, who freely forgives and "pardons you" with "arms wide open," is a sort of an integrated, diluted, synthesized, composite Santa Claus. You will find him described many times in the writings of Mary Baker Eddy, Charles Allen, Emmet Fox, Emily Cady, Leslie Weatherhead, and Ramakrishna Maharajahambatra Flopdoodle. These people assume that no new birth divides the saved from the lost; so, they indiscriminately mix the verses aimed at the child of God (1 Cor. 13) with the ones aimed at Israel (Matt. 6--7) and the ones aimed at an Old Testament saint (Psa. 23). Never once do they do what the word of God itself does---draw a straight line in red blood between the Christ-rejecting "sacramentalist," or do-gooder, and the sinner who has been saved by grace through faith and has been [born again ]{.class_3759}by receiving a free gift (Col. 1:14). A god who would tolerate anything, and forgive anything on the grounds of "I'm sorry," is no more holy than the sinner who makes the confession. Pharaoh, as Balaam and Judas, said ["I have sinned,"]{.class_3760} yet God pardoned him for nothing and forgave them nothing---absolutely NOTHING (2 Pet. 2; Jude 11; Rom. 9).

"[I am full of confusion"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15), and since ["God is not the author of confusion"]{.class_3762} (1 Cor. 14:33), somewhere near Job there must be a Satanic hand. Job's affliction increases (vs. 16) the more he thinks about it. He begs God to look on his condition (vs. 15) and to "consider" it. Although the Lord Jesus Christ is a [lion ]{.class_3761}(Rev. 5:5) and although He will be [fierce ]{.class_3761}at His Advent (Jer. 25:30), the [fierce lion ]{.class_3761}who is hunting Job (1 Pet. 5:8) is not the Lord Jesus Christ (vs. 16). Job's boils, sores, scabs, and skeletal frame are a "witness" against him (vs. 17), and as far as he is concerned God has declared war on him (cf. 10:17 and 6:4).

10:18 Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!

19 I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.

20 Are not my days few? cease then, and let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,

21 Before I go whence I shall not return, even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;

22 A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.

On and on Job goes. He has stated that he will complain in the bitterness of his soul (10:1), and when Job says he will do something, he does it!

"[Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18). To fight with me (vs. 17)? To mark me for a target (vs. 14)? To destroy me (vs. 8)? To oppress me (vs. 3)?[ "Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!"]{.class_3762} (vs. 18, cf. 3:9--12 and 16). ["Cease then, and let me alone"]{.class_3762} (vs. 20). Job desires to go to his grave and off into the dreamless sleep of the land of departed spirits. This is called, ["a land of darkness\...and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness"]{.class_3762} (vs. 22).

Now, all of this would be true from the standpoint of revelation [before the time of Moses]{.class_3759}, and some of what Job says is repeated later by Solomon, [after the time of David]{.class_3759}. No one but Jonah ever came up from ["Sheol"]{.class_3759} to tell about it (Jonah 2:3--7), and when Samuel came up (1 Sam. 28:15), he gave no report on the conditions of "Abraham's bosom." Neither did the young men of 1 Kings 17:22 or 2 Kings 4:34, who were both revived from a state of physical death. (See the treatment under Acts 9:40 in that Commentary.)

The ["grave"]{.class_3760} of which Job speaks is the Hebrew word ["keber,"]{.class_3759} and it is not to be confounded with ["Sheol"]{.class_3759} (Gen. 49:33 and comments). What Job unwittingly describes here is the "outer darkness" of Matthew 22:13 and 2 Peter 2:17. Notice how the description matches the destination of the Antichrist (Job 15:22--23, 30, and 34), even though both he and the false prophet are in a "Lake of Fire" after Armageddon (Rev. 19:20). However, recently (in the last two years!) the foot-dragging scientists have found stars whose fire is so hot that the flames are BLACK. We appreciate this information, although, of course, we know it will not solve the scientists' problems; for they have so many other items like this to get caught up on before they catch up with the [AV ]{.class_3759}of 1611, that there isn't time to wait for them.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1006.xhtml}

CHAPTER 11

11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?

3 Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?

4 For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.

5 But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;

6 And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.

Now Zophar shows up. He is supposed to represent the "moralist." As we have said before, all attempts to bracket these three men are useless. Ewald is fairly typical of those who try to make "representative personalities" out of Job's three friends (see [The Biblical Illustrator]{.class_3759}, Baker Book House, p. xiii). Zophar the Naamathite repeats exactly what Bildad said (11:13 and 8:5) and exactly what Eliphaz just said (11:15--17 and 5:24--26). All three men condemn the wicked (see 11:20; 8:13, and 4:8).

Since a ["fool's voice is known by a multitude of words"]{.class_3760} (Ecc. 5:3), Zophar takes Job for a fool (vs. 2) and says: ["Should thy lies make men hold their peace?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). But what lies had Job told? He had ascribed greatness to God (9:1--10), he had confessed his own ignorance (9:15--21), he had confessed his proneness to make mistakes (9:9--31), he had confessed that he was a sinner (7:20), he had honored God as his Maker (10:8--13), and he had confessed that God was Holy (10:14--15) and that he could not justify himself (9:2). Now, that may not be batting 1,000, but it is way past anything that Willie Mays, Babe Ruth, Ty Cobb, Lou Gehrig, Joe Dimaggio, Andy Messerschmidt, or Brooks Robinson ever did in four decades. Really, the only lie that Job told was an "implication" (10:3), not an out-and-out lie; and in Job's condition who could have thrown the first stone? At any rate, Zophar picks up a fair sized boulder and chucks it: ["God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6).

Now, this is true. If we all got our "just desserts," we'd all be in Hell (see Ezra 9:13); so, Cain's complaint (Gen. 4:13) is really not valid; neither is Job's. But again, if Job was getting less than he had coming to him, I wonder how Zophar the Naamathite got out of an equal share? If a man who ["feared God and eschewed evil"]{.class_3760} (1:1) was as wicked as Zophar pictures Job to be (see 11:11, 14, and 20), I wonder where that puts Zophar?!

"[For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). [Now Zophar is lying]{.class_3761}. Job did NOT say either of those things. He implied them when he spoke Job 10:7 and 9:22, but he did not say what Zophar said he said anymore than he said what you find in the [New English Bible]{.class_3761}, the [New American Standard]{.class_3761}, the [New Scofield]{.class_3761}, or the ["Living"]{.class_3761} (oh, brother!) [Bible]{.class_3761}.

"[But oh that God would speak\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 5), and He does, (Job 38--42). ["And open his lips against thee"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). Well, God finally does open His lips against Job, but when He does, Job responds [correctly ]{.class_3761}(42:7), while Zophar does NOT (42:8). Job is just as anxious for God to ["speak"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5) as Zophar is. As a matter of fact, he is much more anxious (see 13:23--24).

That isn't all. God has spoken, and the words which He spoke are equated with His mouth (1 Kings 13:26) and His commandment (2 Sam. 12:9), for they are the oracles (Rom. 3:2) which came from His lips (11:5). We know [whose doctrine ]{.class_3759}to believe (John 7:17) by the inductive scientific method (Mal. 3:6), and we know that we receive less than our iniquity deserves. If we were paid off in the same coin we minted, we'd all be in the Lake of Fire (Eph. 2:1--8); and we are no more concerned about what Johann Flugel, Emilan Gutheil, Sandor Ferenczi, Karl Menninger, and William Menniger (psychiatrists) think about this conviction than if they had never graduated from high school.

11:7 Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?

8 It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?

9 The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

10 If he cut off, and shut up or gather together, then who can hinder him?

11 For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?

12 For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass's colt.

Verse seven is one of the hallmark questions of the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}(see Job 3:20). In Philosophy, the question comes under the heading of ["Epistemology"]{.class_3759} (see Col. 2:8), and a man is termed a Deist, Theist, Pantheist, Agnostic, Atheist, or Polytheist depending upon he how answers the question. The question is, "Can you get to [know ]{.class_3759}God by searching for Him, and can you comprehend the [true essence of God ]{.class_3759}if you find Him?"

The second half of the question lies at the root of the wrangling and hassling which went on at the Council of Nicaea in A.D. 325, while the first half of the question forms the basis for the great philosophical discourses that went on between 1600--1900 to usher in the Laodicean age of apostasy. Rene Descartes (1594--1650) and Christian Wolff (1679--1754)---what a name, man, what a name!---were two of the first Bible- rejecting "thinkers" to lead the way to Anarchy and International Communism. Descarte's philosophy terminated in God being a subjective idea of the mind, with self-existence being the only provable reality. Spinoza (1632--1677) and Leibnitz (1646--1716) then contributed their "bit," which briefly was this: "Man by 'searching' can find out the Almighty, but when He is 'found out,' He will be discovered to be, part-and-parcel, an energy force of the material universe." Spinoza was a pantheist exactly like the B.C. Greek philosophers. George Berkeley (1685--1753) and David Hume (1711--1776) returned to this original position (see any Gnostic work of 400--100 B.C.) but denied that matter had any reality; therefore Jesus Christ never actually materialized: [we only had an idea about Him materializing]{.class_3759}, etc. Immanuel Kant (1724--1804) finally polished off what sanity was left among the philosophers by saying that we can know nothing for certain about anything as it "is in itself" (see Zophar's question!). When Kant got through with his Creator, God [might ]{.class_3759}be there and then again He [might not, ]{.class_3759}but it would be "helpful to you to believe that He was there" so you would "behave yourself." In [Religion Within the Limits of Reason Alone]{.class_3759}, Kant set the pace for the next four hundred philosophers to follow; i.e., what I can rationalize and understand is "scripture," and what I cannot is myth and legend. Hegel (1770--1831), Heidegger (1889), Sartre (1905), Russell, James, Dewey, and Nibert Wiener (1894--1964) all follow this basic pattern, and in spite of their professed "individualism," they are seven peas in a pod when it comes to answering Zophar's question. The answer is (according to them): "You can't find out anything [for certain ]{.class_3759}about God in the sense of personal knowledge; you can only understand that he is an [operative energy ]{.class_3759}in the force field of the Universe." That is, the last seven major philosophers (plus Darwin, Haeckel, Huxley, Comte, Schlick, Wittgenstein, Stuart Chase, and S. I. Hayakawa) are agnostic on God- knowledge (Theology), and they are infidelic on 1 John 5:8--12, Romans 8:1--30, Ephesians 1--2, Colossians 1--3, Galatians 1, John 17, 2 Peter 2, 3, and Revelation 20--22.

The answer to Zophar's question was given in careful detail more than one thousand years before Wolff, Kant, or Descartes kicked the slats out of their cribs. A man CAN know God (John 17:3, 7--8, 23, 25, 8:19, 37, 44--45; 1 John 3:1--2, 5:19; Matt. 11:27; Heb. 10:30; Col. 1:6, etc., etc.), and if the last five thousand "qualified authorities" thought that John, Paul, and Matthew were as uninformed as they were, they will find out differently when they hit the White Throne Judgment. Although Man, by nature, does not "seek God" (Rom. 3:11), and the Bible is certainly NOT the "record of man's search for God" (how dumb can you get? Adam knew God [before the flood ]{.class_3759}better than Gideon knew Him [after the Exodus!]{.class_3759}), any sinner on this earth can find God in fifteen seconds if he desires (John 7:17). The trouble with Man is heart trouble, not head trouble; and the heady, highminded theories (2 Tim. 3:4) of reprobate minds (Rom. 1:28), who turned God into "man" (Rom. 1:23) and man into a jackass (Job 11:12!), are not to be classified with rational processes. When Paul says, ["I know whom I have believed"]{.class_3760} (2 Tim. 1:12), he has just finished calling that WHOM: ["God manifest in the flesh"]{.class_3760} (1 Tim. 3:16). And if Descartes, Gibons, Spinoza, Comte, Hume, Hobbes, Mach, Russell, Jorzybski, Kierkegaard, and Sartre don't agree with that, they can go on home to their eternal destination, and Paul can go on to his; that's the way it will come out whether you accept it or not (Rom. 3:3--5).

However, finding God out "unto perfection" is "somethin' else," as they say down South. His [peace ]{.class_3759}is past understanding (Phil. 4:7), His love is unquenchable (Song of Sol. 8:7), His [riches ]{.class_3759}are unsearchable (Eph. 3:8), His [gift ]{.class_3759}is unspeakable (2 Cor. 9:15), His [joy ]{.class_3759}is unspeakable (1 Peter 1:8), and His [words ]{.class_3759}are unutterable (2 Cor. 12:1--4). We-e-l-l GLOREE! But some of us do know Him even if YOU don't, and if you don't, then we invite you to ["taste and see that the Lord is good"]{.class_3760} (1 Pet. 2:3). He is more than ["good,"]{.class_3760} He is PRECIOUS (1 Pet. 2:4--6), and if Kant can't "ken" that, ole' Drunk Jerry can. ("Drunk Jerry" was the name the bartender gave to Mr. Jerry McCauley before Jerry became one of the greatest Rescue Mission workers in the world.) ["Not many wise\...are called" ]{.class_3760}(1 Cor. 1:26, 27), and if you are putting off salvation until you can comprehend an Infinite Holy Spirit, whose Eternal Presence cannot be described in terms of language (see Exod. 3:14 and comments), you are going to wait till Hell freezes over. Of course, the "knower" cannot know the [essence ]{.class_3759}of the "unknowable object" until he merges into the unknown object; even Buddha had that much sense. But we are ["complete in him"]{.class_3760} (Col. 2:9--10), and all the ["fulness of the godhead"]{.class_3760} dwelles bodily in Him. We know more than some of you think we know.

If you want to know God, spend some time communing with Him (1 Sam. 1:13). If you want to know God, listen to the people whom He recommends (Matt. 10:40). If you want to know God, junk your library, your TV set, your radio, your magazines, and your newspapers, and go out on the backside of a desert (Gal. 1:17) and spend some time with a Book that sells more copies in a year than the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759} does in ten years.

"[It is as high as heaven"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8). (See Psa. 139:8.) ["Deeper than hell"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). (See Psa. 139:8.) ["What canst thou know?"]{.class_3762} The "perfection of God" (vs. 7) is the subject of these questions. The measure of God's perfection is ["longer than the earth, and broader than the sea"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9). (See Eph. 3:18 and comments.)

"[If he cut off\...shut up\...gather together"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10). If He cuts off your children (like He did Job's), if He shuts you up in the sick room (like He did Job), if He gathers together an "array of terrors" (like He did to Job: see 6:4 and 10:17), then ["who can hinder him?"]{.class_3762} Nobody. If God turns you over to the "ungodly" (Psa. 22:16), or to Satan ["for the destruction of the flesh"]{.class_3762} (1 Cor. 5:5), you won't get loose till God Himself puts a stop to it. If the Communists or Catholics ever succeed in arresting you at night and shutting you up in one of the Psycho-Political Mental Wards, you won't get out sane unless God Himself overrules the Devil. God is in charge.

A Canadian missionary in China was arrested and tried by a "People's Court" (see Acts 21:28 for the old joke about "the people"). He was committed to open custody but was forced to take indoctrination courses. During these instructions on Communism, one instructor roughed him up pretty regularly. Finally, this instructor said to him (before a packed classroom of "learners"): "Where is your God? You believe in prayer; go on and ask your God to do something for you and see if He will do it. Let's SEE how your God works." (Cf. Matth. 27:42 and the Logical Positivists at Vienna.) The missionary could think of nothing, but he knelt to pray anyway. The Lord gave him the words. He prayed for God to [blind ]{.class_3759}the Chinese instructor so that he might know that God was real. When he got off his knees the instructor was as blind as a bat. Soon after, that Chinese Communist was converted, and then, his own people (see Acts 21:28 for the old "shell game" about "the people") killed him by driving chopsticks up through his nostrils.

"[For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass's colt"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). With one of the greatest devotional "nuggets" of either Testament staring them in the face, the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3761}, the [New English Bible]{.class_3761}, Clarke, Heavenor, Kline, Jamieson, Fausset, Maurer, Brown, Dummelow, Feinberg, Lange, Umbreit, and Ewald either botch it up or else walk by it like a lamp post. ["Vain men"]{.class_3762} (vs. 11) do seek wisdom (["for vain man would be wise"]{.class_3762}), and even separate themselves to find it (see Prov. 18:1 and comments), but it is useless; for without the ["fear of the Lord,"]{.class_3762} there is no "beginning" of wisdom, because only the new birth that turns the ["ass's colt"]{.class_3762} into a LAMB (Exod. 13:13) will do the job. It is a ["wild ass's colt"]{.class_3762} which carries the King Messiah of Israel into Jerusalem to be crucified. The wild ass cannot be tamed until it has been ridden; wild asses run around late in the woods (or the desert) at night; "some of their mothers is wild" (to quote an old Southern farmer); and you cannot tame a wild ass by whipping him (see Prov. 26:3), for he "can't understand no whippin till he gets broke in" (to cite the same authority); or---to use the vernacular Koine---"he ain't been broke in till he's done been RID." (See Matt. 21:1--6 and comments.) Do you know why Descartes, Spinoza, Hegel, Leibnitz, Santayana, and the rest of the Philosopher's Union couldn't "find out God" (11:7)? [Because they wouldn't let Him put the bit and bridle on them]{.class_3761}. They broke their fool necks (literally---Exod. 13:13) trying to be ["wise"]{.class_3762} (text) when it was for nothing (text). (Class is dismissed: Please don't flop your ears as you go out.)

11:13 If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;

14 If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.

15 For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:

16 Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:

17 And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.

18 And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.

19 Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.

20 But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.

Now Zophar reiterates the speech of Eliphaz in Job 5:24--26. He tells Job that if Job will clean up and "get right" and "go to praying" (vs. 13), he will forget his misery ["as waters that pass away"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16) provided, of course, that he repents (vs. 14) before he prays. Job will shine forth ["as the morning"]{.class_3760} (Second Advent reference), he will lift up his face ["without spot"]{.class_3760} (Second Advent reference), and he will ["rest in safety"]{.class_3760} (Second Advent reference) while ["the eyes of the wicked shall fail"]{.class_3760} (Second Advent reference). The word ["spot"]{.class_3760} is one of the most crucial words in the New Testament revelation concerning the Antichrist, so one will find the word spiritualized by Spiros Zodhiates in his misnamed work [The Behavior of Belief ]{.class_3759}(which is one of the most vivid books on "The Behavior of UNBELIEF" that you can obtain these days). The "spotted face" can have a spotted garment (Jude 23) that was spotted by a LEOPARD'S mark (Rev. 13:2; Lev. 13:24, 38), and the ETHIOPIAN (Jer. 13:23) can no more ["change\...his spots"]{.class_3760} than a Bible-rejecting man in the Tribulation can keep himself ["unspotted from the world"]{.class_3760} (James 1:27). Since Christ is a Lamb ["without blemish and without spot"]{.class_3760} (1 Pet. 1:19) and His bride is SPOTLESS (Eph. 5:27), it is fitting that the Antichrist be a spotted animal (Rev. 13:2) who uses an "X to mark the spot." And Zodhiates' exposition of the text (in James) is as shallow and as non-revealing as the exposition of Job 11:15 (here) given by Clarke, Dummelow, Henry, Lange, Watson, and Gaebelein (plus the majority of "eminent Bible-rejecting authorities"). (See [Mark of the Beast ]{.class_3759}for the "spot" passages.)

The ["waters that pass away"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16) is a reference hearkening back to the verbal testimony of Shem, Ham, and Japheth (["their fathers,"]{.class_3760} Job 8:9), and Job's age, which will ["shine forth\...as the morning"]{.class_3760} (vs. 17), so plainly matches Matthew 13:43 and Malachi 4:1--4 that only a saved Ph.D. with forty years of education in a fundamental university could possibly fail to see the prophecy in the passage.

"[Thou shalt dig about thee\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 18) is a reference to Ezekiel 12:5--7 when the Jew will dig his wells as Abraham and Isaac dug them (Gen. 21:25, 26:19), without any fights over "the ole' water hole." The ridiculous misparaphrasing of this clause into "you will take your time" (the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3761}) is just about as much a paraphrase of the Authorized Truth as Timothy O'Leary's work on [A Hope Addict]{.class_3761}. (The [ASV's]{.class_3761} perversion of the passage \[any edition\] also follows Taylor's line of thinking, and both of these corrupt corruptions match the [New English Bible ]{.class_3761}of 1971.) The word for ["dig"]{.class_3762} here (Hebrew ["chaphar"]{.class_3761}) has been translated as "DIG" in the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(any edition---same trash) in Genesis 21:30, 26:15, 18--19, 21; Exodus 7:24; and Ecclesiastes 10:8. Here the capricious whim of these "recognized" infidels (Evangelical Conservatives foremost), in translating the word in another fashion, is quite typical of some of the modern hypocrites on the faculties of Wheaton, Fuller, Dallas, Bob Jones, Columbia, etc., who are always complaining about the [King James Bible ]{.class_3761}translating one word several different ways. The kettle is always trying to call the pot "black," isn't it? And especially when the "pot" was made by God, preserved by God, used by God, blessed by God, and can stew any scholarly jackrabbit down to "pot licker" in fifteen seconds.

"[Many shall make suit unto thee"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19) is used as it is used in 1980 in reference to a "law suit." Many will come to Job seeking his wisdom and advice for their problems; so, these "suits" are called "prayer" and "petition" in a modern Lawsuit (1980). (The archaic readings of Taylor and Dodd at this point are not to be taken seriously.) The remainder of the passage is true of Job's own life and also true of the Tribulation remnant of Israel (see Prov. 30:17 and 11:7). Umbreit, Gesenius, Peake, and Budde suggest the usual alterations of the passage, but none of them are worth wasting time with. The dogmatism of Rev. Rawlinson (the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3761}, p. 194), concerning verse 17, is par for the Scholars Union---who think anyone is "dogmatic" who tells them they don't know what they are talking about. Rawlinson simply tells God that "the Hebrew cannot POSSIBLY bear this meaning" (i.e., ["thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning"]{.class_3762}). If it doesn't mean that, then you've lost two cross-references to the Second Advent of Christ, and if it doesn't mean [that]{.class_3761}, anyone's guess is just as good as anyone else's as to what it [does ]{.class_3761}mean. Anarchists all think alike.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1051.xhtml}

CHAPTER 12

12:1 And Job answered and said,

2 No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.

3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?

4 I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.

5 He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.

6 The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.

Job has begun to regain some of his sense of humor, although it is what we used to call "G.I. humor." Marching on his "dogs," and being reduced to the level of a "dog" by his "dog-tags," the World War I and World War II infantryman was called "Dog-face." And if you have ever wondered why they say "fight like dogs," you should either watch a gang battle of four curs or else watch "The Battalion in the Attack." Job has begun to smile wryly under the bombardment of 11:11--12, and now he says: ["No doubt but ye are the people"]{.class_3760} (See Acts 21:28 for the old heave-ho about "THE PEOPLE") ["and wisdom shall die with you"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). The English "original" is much stronger than the weak paraphrases by Dodd ([NEB]{.class_3759}) and Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3759}). An ironic statement like verse 2 should have some "iron" in it; so Job's speech in the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) is pure irony without any mixture of "sugar and spice" or anything "nice." Furthermore, the peculiar choice of words by Job (obliterated by Dodd and Taylor) is a veiled reference to the "gods" of Genesis 6:1--5, who were "men of renown." Since this reference (again) deals with the coming of "humanoids" from outer space, there is an immediate ecumenical conspiracy by the scholars to rid the text of the revelation at any cost: Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown change it to "a people," the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}says "Men," and Adam Clarke makes it "the wisest MEN." (p. 65.)

"[But I have understanding as well as you\...who knoweth not such things as these?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). Who doesn't know that a man can't ever grasp the totality of God's nature (11:7)? Who doesn't know that a man is born like a wild jackass (11:12), except Clarke, Dummelow, Henry, Lightfoot, Rice, Heavenor, Kline, Bullinger, Ellicott, and Zodhiates? Who doesn't know that, if you repent and get right, God will bless you (11:16--19)? ["I am as one mocked of his neighbour"]{.class_3762} (vs. 4---see 11:3 and 5:9). Job says that it is just as if he had knelt in his living room and prayed, "God will you please show me what's wrong?" and then some visitor on the couch said, "Sure Job, I'll be glad to," (["his neighbour\...answereth him"]{.class_3762}). The classic illustration is where someone is taking statistics in an asylum, and the "zoo" is standing in line. They ask the first man who he is, and he swears that he is "Napoleon." They ask him how he knows this, and he says, "Because God told me I was." Whereupon the fifth-in-line pokes his head out and says indignantly: "I DID NOT!" For a Biblical case, see Elijah answering for Baal (1 Kings 18:27) when the prophets of Baal are calling upon their "god" (Job 12:4); Elijah says: "He put you on 'hold' boys, so I'll answer on the other line!"

One of the thoughts of verse 5 is that a man who is sure of his footing will turn up his nose if you offer him a torch (English) or a flashlight (American). The ["lamp despised"]{.class_3760} is needed by no one except a man who ["is ready to slip with his feet."]{.class_3760} Being unable to make heads or tails out of the verse, Adam Clarke simply refuses to comment on it or even list it. But Adam Clarke is not the only man to have trouble with the Holy Bible while he is tearing it to pieces. The infamous [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 (promoted by John R. Rice and Bob Jones III, and--- make out your own list this time; I am tired of typing it) could not fathom the verse with the aid of lexicons, grammars, Hebrew and Chaldean roots, eighteen centuries of Alexandrian yogurt, and the combined faculties of Princeton, Harvard, Yale, Westminster, Heidelburg, Edinburgh, Colgate, Chicago, and Louisville. Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3759}) follows the blind into the ditch on the same verse and takes any reference to ["feet"]{.class_3760} or ["lamps"]{.class_3760} clear out of the verse. So now that everybody is on the train, off it puffs into perdition: Kline ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_3759}, p. 472) has no lamps or feet, the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}has no lamps or feet, the [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}(1971) has no lamps or feet, and you couldn't find any lamps or feet in the [New RSV ]{.class_3759}if you were a centipede with three flashlights on each foot. Fausset, however, calls our attention (1866---before the publishing of the [RV]{.class_3759}) to the fact that Proverbs 25:19 is the proper cross-reference and that Job is a useless lamp to people who have no need for his light. They are not going to follow Job's [advice ]{.class_3759}or [leadership ]{.class_3759}because his feet are ["ready to slip."]{.class_3760} And Job is telling the truth. The only real problem is why American Christians, who desire to know the word of God (and the word of God is ["light"]{.class_3760}---see Psa. 119:130) and follow God's leadership, would reverence such flickering matches as the [NEB, TEV, TLB, ASV, New ASV, RV, RSV]{.class_3759}, [New RSV]{.class_3759}, and [Good News for Modern Man]{.class_3759}. These pitiful flint sparks are still looked upon as "BIBLE" versions in the face of the glare of the noonday Sun ([AV]{.class_3759}, 1611), which illuminates (Eph. 5:13) everything on the face of the earth.

Before taking up the further critical review of the Bible rejecting Christians of 1880--1980, we shall finish the chapter.

12:7 But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:

8 Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.

9 Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?

10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.

11 Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?

The passage is a glory to the infallible authority of the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759}. We will save our comments on its critics until we have finished the chapter, for what follows is too magnificent and illuminating to cloud with the pitiful conjectures of educated sinners.

"[But ask now the beasts\...and the fowls\...the earth\...and the fishes\...shall declare unto thee"]{.class_3760} (vss. 7--8). Job's bitter and sarcastic comment preceding this (vs. 6) is one of the greatest proofs in the world that the man who quotes scripture indiscriminately (and without regard for its context) is not to be trusted in guiding anyone anywhere spiritually. No man is ["secure"]{.class_3762} who provokes God, and it is only the permissive will of God that allows the hand of the robbers (vs. 6) to receive ["abundantly"]{.class_3762} (Jer. 52:17--20). If the tabernacles of the robbers ["prosper"]{.class_3762} (vs. 6), it will only be [temporary ]{.class_3761}(Job 15:34, 18:6, 14), and Job knows this as well as he knows his own name. He is just mad and bitter, and his sense of humor, at this time, is just a little more acid than Archie Bunker's.

But oh my! What we can learn from "the beasts, the fowls, the earth, and the fishes" (vss. 7--8) if we will listen to them before correcting our Protestant Bible. And how needful it is, at this point, for us to shut the major commentators' works out completely; Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Ellicott, Brown, Kline, Heavenor, and Gesenius (combined) have not given enough information on the two verses to fill one quarter of a page. But, oh brother! what have we here!?

What do the ["beasts"]{.class_3760} teach us?

Well, they teach us that [ministers ]{.class_3759}are like OXEN if they are saved (1 Cor. 9:9--10), but without the "operation" they are "bulls" (Psa. 22:12) who would murder Jesus Christ. The beasts teach us that [false prophets ]{.class_3759}are dogs (2 Pet. 2:22), that their leaders are "wolves in sheep's clothing" (Matt. 7:15), that [Christians ]{.class_3759}are "sheep" (John 10:14, 28), and that Israel needs a shepherd (Ezek. 34:6, 11, 15). We know that self- righteous [sacramentalists ]{.class_3759}are like snakes (Matt. 23:33) and that they give birth to vipers (Matt. 23:33) who are ["twofold more the children of Hell"]{.class_3760} than themselves (Matt. 23:15). We learn from the beasts that the two dirtiest animals on earth (pigs and dogs: "SCHWEINHUND") are types of lost men and women (2 Pet. 2:22), that unsaved men are like wild asses (1:12 and comments), and that Jesus Christ and the Devil are both like LIONS, with the "King of the Jungle" being "[the Lion of the tribe of Juda"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 5:5).

"[Ask now the beasts\..."!]{.class_3760}

All right then, if you won't do that, then go ask the ["fowls."]{.class_3760} We learn that people are like birds (Matt. 6:26) and that [doves ]{.class_3759}(Matt. 3:16) are types of the Holy Spirit (John 1:32), for they represent Christ's eyes (Song of Sol. 5:12). Prayer warriors are like [eagles ]{.class_3759}(Isa. 40:31) in the good sense, while Pharaoh and Nebuchadnezzar (Ezek. 17:3; Hos. 8:1) are types of eagles in the bad sense. Fowls are types of spirits (Rev. 18:2); a [raven ]{.class_3759}(Gen. 8:7) is an unclean spirit (Matt. 13:19), and [vultures ]{.class_3759}are ravenous men (Lev. 11:14). The providence of God watches the [sparrow ]{.class_3759}(Matt. 10:29--31), and the ["birds of the air have nests"]{.class_3760} where God's Son had to sleep on the ground. Some preachers are like [cardinals ]{.class_3759}(red "hoods"!), some are like peacocks (see Acts 24:2--8); some are like [canaries]{.class_3759}, some are like [mockingbirds ]{.class_3759}(they can only repeat the material from denominational headquarters), some are like [hawks ]{.class_3759}who pounce on sin, some are like [bats ]{.class_3759}who play both ends against the middle, some are like [swans ]{.class_3759}and perform their services gracefully, some are like [sparrows ]{.class_3759}and can only get shot or batted around, some are like [seagulls ]{.class_3759}as they "fish for men" one at a time, and some are like high flying [eagles]{.class_3759}---alone, above the clouds, searching out the smallest detail in topography and screaming the warning before the storm.

"[Ask now\...the fowls of the air"!]{.class_3760}

Or perhaps you would prefer to be a "fisher" of men (Matt. 4:19)? The "fishes" of the sea are loners, or they swim in schools. Some of them are timid; some are fierce; some are camouflaged while others are gaily decorated; some of them are small; some are big; and some of them are so tiny you cannot see them with the naked eye. Fishes are vegetarian as well as carnivorous; they swim anywhere from 15,000 feet down up to the surface and on top of the surface. You must vary net, line, hook, rod, reel, gun, gig, arrow, trap, basket, and explosives to get them. They are sometimes beautiful, sometimes ugly, sometimes just plain homely, and at other times fascinating. They are found everywhere on this earth from Baffin Bay to the Equator, and they have feeding times (as men), spawning times (as men), survival struggles (as men), common enemies (as men), over-population problems (as men), sickness and death problems (as men), and regular habits of movement (as men).

"[Ask now\...the fishes of the sea"!]{.class_3760}

But if you still don't have any sermonic material---and you sure won't find any in Dummelow, Lange, Henry, Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, or Peake---try speaking ["to the earth, and it shall teach thee"]{.class_3760}!

Trees come in families (as men); they grow and die (as men); they are resurrected (as men); they fade and fall (as men); they are cut down (as men). Some are taller than others; some are hardier than others; some require extreme care in pruning and fertilizing, while some of them grow wild (as men!). Some trees are waterproof, and some are bugproof. Some bear fruit; some don't. And nearly the same thing can be said of [flowers ]{.class_3759}(see Gen. 2:7 and comments). The [fossil record ]{.class_3759}of the earth shows there have been two catastrophies since Genesis 1:1. [The rock strata ]{.class_3759}shows evidence of an upheaval that occured in a matter of hours. [The oil deposits ]{.class_3759}and "ossiferous fissures" show the mark of a volcanic tidal disturbance that was accompanied by earthquakes and hurricanes of stupendous proportions. The great "rift" in the earth (Galilee to Queen Victoria Falls) shows there has been a displacement of the poles. The two mountain ranges (The Pyrenees to the Himalayas and the Andes to the Sierra Nevadas) show "orogenesis" was not by rainfall, glaciers, etc., and "time would fail me to tell" of what a man can learn by "speaking to the earth."

"[Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). Well, Heidegger doesn't. Darwin didn't. Sartre don't. The United Nations dun't. The HEW disn't. Einsein dasn't. The faculty at Harvard and Columbia darsn't. Wiener and Kierkegaard dain't, and neither "daid" Socrates, Aristotle, Plato, Anaximander, Anaximenes, Stalin, Lenin, Pythagoras, or the Kennedy\
family.

If there is anything that will keep a man from learning by a real teacher---God's creation (see Psa. 19:1--6)---it is the Western system of higher education and the "findings of science." The Bible points to [Ornithology ]{.class_3759}(Matt. 6--7), [Icthyology ]{.class_3759}(Ecc. 9:12), [Biology ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 147), [Geology ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 8:3), [Anthropology ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 139), [Geography ]{.class_3759}(Eccl. 1), [Ethnology ]{.class_3759}(Gen. 10), and [Astronomy ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 19:1--5); and since it is the only accurate TEXTBOOK on science the world has ever seen [or ever will see]{.class_3759}, there is not too much sense in believing in the "findings" of any group of scientists (Christian or otherwise) where these deluded idiots think their investigations are superior to the written revelation recorded by Moses, Luke, Elihu, Solomon, Samuel, David, Matthew, Mark, John, Peter, James, Jude, Paul, and the rest of the inspired writers.

A GI friend of mine named "Austin" returned home from Korea and Manchuria, in 1952, as a hopeless drunk. He was witnessed to by a dear old soul in Memphis, Tennessee (a Mrs. Weaver), who mailed him tracts from time to time and prayed for him. He finally checked into a hospital, bleeding internally and thinking that he was dying with bleeding ulcers. After all kinds of tests (with no results), he sat down forlornly in a waiting room of one of the doctors. A Christian doctor just happened to come in, and going up to Austin, he placed one arm around his neck and said sympathetically: "What's the matter with you son? ARE YOU LOST?" [He was]{.class_3759}. Austin was led to Christ by that doctor in less than two minutes.

Do you know what the REAL trouble is with these materialistic-logical-positivistic-cybernetical-evolutionary-phenomenological-synthesized-computerized-international- Socialists? Well, it sure ain't "ulcers."

A "shrink" tried for six months to teach a German Shepherd how to dribble a volleyball. About the time he thought he had gotten his "programming" over, he put the dog in his office with the ball, went out, and locked the door. Five minutes later he tiptoed over to the locked door and peered through the keyhole to see how his instructions had turned out. When he stared through the hole, he found himself looking right smack into a large yellow-brown eye!

"[Ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee"!]{.class_3760} You never met a "dumb" animal in your life who disobeyed God's orders like a "rational" man disobeys them. And in spite of the fact that no animal can write or work with fractions (or equations) or invent any different tools to use in a period of 100,000,000 years (according to Darwin), the first spider on the North Pacific coast who lifted a shell 2,000 times its own weight (by the laws of contraction and expansion) did it on wet threads of silk through "INSTINCT." I wonder why human instinct can't do that? Let's see you lift 3,600 tons of concrete 1,800 feet in the air without studying math, physics, mechanics, or chemistry. Where is your "instinct," son?

If a canary had the bill of a toucan, he couldn't pick up seed. (Ain't "mother nature" wonderful?) How could a pelican keep a fish in his mouth if he had the beak of a chicken, or vice versa? How far could a duck swim under water with no webbed feet? Fishing birds have barbed hooks on the back of their claws to match the serrations on the front. Did you ever try to hang onto a live fish with a thumbless hand? Who equipped the fish to divide H[2]{.class_3770}O into three parts and then utilize the oxygen through the gills? Did you? Did any educated nut like you? If animals have no memory for anything abstract, how do they pass on their "acquired knowledge" through four thousand years? There are hundreds of species alive today who were here forty million years ago, if we are to believe the fanatical followers of Darwin, Huxley, and the News Media.

Who taught plunging birds to attack at an [angle ]{.class_3759}instead of a perpendicular plunge? "The arch of the cycloid" was a problem that only two men in Europe could solve in the eighteenth century; how did the plunging birds of 1,000 B.C. solve it? Did they know "by instinct" the "tilt of the earth on its axis and the consequent atmospheric rotation and pressure accompanying it"? (Ain't "nature" grand?) ["Ask now\...the fowls\...and they shall tell thee"]{.class_3760}!

There is a shore bird on the Pacific coast who has a beak which exactly fits the hole of the worm it feeds on. It also has a [hinged flap ]{.class_3759}about 3/8 inches from the end of the beak. This allows the beak to open [inside the hole ]{.class_3759}like a pair of long forcepts which doctors use. The bird then washes the sand off its beak at the water's edge before swallowing the worm. Why didn't all the shore birds develop this hinged beak to get worms? (Yes? As you were saying, Darwin?)

"Instinct" is nothing automatic, nor is it anything that has "evolved" from any species."Instinct is IMPARTED WISDOM," and you'd better believe it.

"[The hand of the LORD hath wrought this"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9), and if you don't know it, it is because you have been brainwashed by left-wing TV programs, newspapers, radio, and college-trained teachers for so long that you can't see anything beyond the materialistic nose on your face. Job knows the Lord hath ["wrought"]{.class_3762} these things; he also knew that birds, beasts, and fishes [prey ]{.class_3761}on one another (as in 12:6) with God's knowledge and permission. And God will allow this "preying" until Isaiah chapter 11 and Romans chapter 8, and nothing any group of scientists or educators on this earth (or from outer space) attempt to do will ever solve this problem. [Killing beasts ]{.class_3761}and [ravenous fowls ]{.class_3761}are part of the Great Tribulation which precedes the advent of the Lord Jesus Christ (Rev. 6:8 and 19:17).

And before you waste any more money on the commentaries about Job published by "reputable publishing companies" (to cite John R. Rice), you had better give prayerful and careful consideration to this fact: No major commentator since 1880 gave the passage in Job 12:7--8 the space that he gave to locating ["the land of Uz."]{.class_3760} All gnatstrainers swallow alike.

"[In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 10), showing that animals can be classified as ["souls"]{.class_3762} (Num. 31:28), that ["in him we live, and move, and have our being"]{.class_3762} (Acts 17:28), and that God is the final court of appeal for anything alive (Isa. 57:16). Men's ears are only courts for "trying words" (vs. 11); pleasant words are ["meat"]{.class_3762} (Heb. 5:14), for they are like honey (Psa. 119:103), bread (Luke 4:4), apples (Prov. 25:11), and milk (1 Peter 2:2). And woe be to the itching ear (2 Tim. 4:3) that is ["dull of hearing"]{.class_3762} (Acts 28:27) and refuses to "try" the words that are ["tried in a furnace"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 12:6), and purified by the Refiner till they are seven times purer (Psa. 12:6) than the purest words Gautama Buddha, Lao-tzu, Mao Tse-tung, Confucius, Mohammed Kagawa, or the Popes ever spoke---or thought. Any time you think that such words as Romans 3:1--3; John 5:24, 3:36, and Romans 8 are not true, try them out. And don't hand us any flim-flam about "existential-empirical-proveable-data" till you have tried that Book and its promises. What could be any more ridiculous than a nuclear-physicist talking about "humanism," and "agnosticism" when he hasn't even tried out the words spoken by the [Author ]{.class_3761}of his own life?

12:12 With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.

13 With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.

14 Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.

15 Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.

The ["ancient"]{.class_3760} of verse 12 is an indirect reference to Shem, and the ["length of days"]{.class_3760} is a reference to a time when six hundred years was a short lifetime (see Gen. 5 and comments). ["The Ancient of days"]{.class_3760} is the white-haired Messiah of Daniel 7:13 and Revelation 1:14, and with Him is ["wisdom and strength"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13). The Ancient of Days ["hath counsel and understanding"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13), for He has the Spirit of "counsel and understanding" according to Isaiah 11:2.

"[He breaketh down, and it cannot be built again"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). Even when Ezra and Nehemiah rebuilt the walls, they were torn down again in A.D. 70 (Titus), and even after they are rebuilt in the Tribulation (Rev. 11:1--2), they will be torn down before the Lord Jesus Christ rebuilds them (Acts 15:16) in the Millennium. ["He shutteth up a man"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14)---like he did Noah and his sons---["and there can be no opening"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14). After Noah and his sons were shut in, God sends out the ["waters and\...they overturn the earth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 15). Time and time again, these Deluge references appear in the [Book of Job]{.class_3761}, showing that the greatest illustrations of sin and judgment that Job and his friends can think of belong to Genesis 4--10---not Exodus chapter 4 and 1 Kings chapter 18. When God shuts up a man in prison (Isa. 61:1--2), he cannot get out till "deliverance is preached to the captives" (Luke 4:18; Eph. 4:8). When God shuts up a man in a hospital room, he will have to look UP.

The One who ["shutteth, and no man openeth"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 22:22) will open when He gets ready, and once He slams the door shut (Rev. 3:7), thirty-five "new translations" in the "language of the people" will not open one door for the truth on the face of this earth (see Rev. 2--3 and comments). God shut Jonah up in the belly of the whale (never mind Wuest's Greek and Zodhiates' Greek on Matthew 12:40; those guys don't know their Bible any better than Jonah). Jonah was "down in the mouth" till the Lord opened the mouth of that whale. "He withheld the waters" (vs. 15) in the days of Elijah, as He will withhold them the last 3 1/2 years of Daniel's Seventieth Week.

12:16 With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.

17 He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.

18 He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.

19 He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.

20 He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.

21 He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.

22 He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.

23 He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again.

24 He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.

25 They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.

Job is "under way" now. His jet burners are on, and no one can stop his discourse. He is talking about God's power to do what He wants to do when He wants to do it, and when Job is on this frequency, he is tuned in with Elihu and God Himself.

"[The deceiver and the deceived are his"]{.class_3760} (see 2 Tim. 3:13). He messed up Ahithophel and then messed up the people who listened to Hushai. He messed up Judas, and He then messed up those who bargained with Judas. The Antichrist is a ["deceiver"]{.class_3762} (1 John 4:1), and God will handle him as He sees fit. ["He leadeth counsellors away spoiled"]{.class_3762} (vs. 17) like He stripped Jerusalem of her great men and led them off naked and barefoot (Isa. 20:2--3). ["And maketh the judges fools"]{.class_3762} (vs. 17) like He did Samson. Imagine the great judge and deliverer of his people (Judg. 16:21) blind and naked, being made fun of by a howling mob of people whose god couldn't even sit up straight in front of a piece of [furniture ]{.class_3761}that represented Samson's God (1 Sam. 5:4)!

"[He looseth the bond of kings"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18) like He did when He let Manasseh out of "hock" and restored him to his kingdom. ["He\...girdeth their loins with a girdle"]{.class_3762} (vs. 18) like He did to Cyrus (Isa. 22:21--25). ["He leadeth princes away spoiled"]{.class_3762} (vs. 19) like He did Zedekiah and Jehoiakin (2 Chron. 36:6--7, 18), and ["He overthroweth the mighty"]{.class_3762} (vs. 19) like He overthrew Pharaoh (Exod. 15) and Nebuchadnezzar (Jer. 50:23).

He decreed that the corpse of a sinner be burned who, at one time, controlled all of Europe and nearly half of Russia. He hung the corpse of another sinner upside down in Rome---a man who had helped conquer Ethiopia and North Africa. He hung Rudolph Hoess by his neck after that sinner supervised the killing of nearly two million Jews. Napoleon and Charlemagne stumbled and fell under His Almighty hand, and they blended their dust with the dust of Alexander the Great, Xerxes, Hannibal, Scipio, Caesar, and Darius the Great. And "where are the snows of yesteryear?"

"[He removeth away the speech of the trusty"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20) like the time He gave Zecharias (Luke 1:20) a case of lock-jaw. ["He\...taketh away the understanding of the aged"]{.class_3762} (vs. 20). (See Prov. 8:15 and comments.) So, senility overthrows the last years of many a great soldier of the cross. ["He poureth contempt upon princes"]{.class_3762} (vs. 21) like He did to Zedekiah's crowd (Jer. 52) for rejecting the warnings of Jeremiah. ["He\...weakeneth the strength of the mighty"]{.class_3762} (vs. 21) by emotional shocks (Judg. 11:35) or by superior competition (1 Sam. 18:8). Jephthah and Saul knew what the verse meant if they ever read it. Samson lost his strength (Judg. 16:21) because he had failed to fill the requirements of Numbers 6:9. ["He discovereth deep things out of darkness"]{.class_3762} (vs. 22) like His revelation of the Universe (Job 26:5--14; Eph. 3:18) or His revelation of the future (Psa. 68). ["He\...bringeth out to light the shadow of death"]{.class_3762} vs. 22) in the sense of solving man's problem with death and the grave (1 Cor. 15:55) and in the sense of revealing the "death machine" of A.D. 1990--2000 (Amos 5:8; Jer. 13:16; Psa. 44:19, etc.).

"[He increaseth the nations"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23) as in Isaiah 9:3 and causes them to put out enough "leaves" (Matt. 24:32) to cover an ocean; and then ["He\...destroyeth them"]{.class_3762} (vs. 23) like He did Egypt, Babylon, Rome, Greece, Persia, and England because they are "as a drop of a bucket" (Isa. 40:15). ["He enlargeth the nations"]{.class_3762} (vs. 23) because ["Japheth"]{.class_3762} means "He will enlarge" (see Gen. 9:27 and comments), and then "straitens" them out again---squeezes 'em into a narrow place---so that they have to side with Arabia against Israel to keep their cars running on the Interstate. ["He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 24), in that they "lose heart for the battle" (see Deut. 20:8) and flee ["when no man pursueth"]{.class_3762} (2 Kings 7:6), running away naked (Amos 2:16). ["He\...causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way"]{.class_3762} (vs. 24)---to cite the African expression (1950--1970)---unless God shows the way. The Jews will wander here again during Daniel's Seventieth Week (Hos. 2:14--16), exactly as they did before (Psa. 78). ["They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man"]{.class_3762} (vs. 25). (See Job 5:14.) ["The nations"]{.class_3762} (see vs. 23) will be in this condition at the end of the Tribulation. On an earth that is also "staggering like a drunken man" (Isa. 24:18--20), the United Nations will be "bringing in the Kingdom" with a drugged population (Rev. 9:21) who are controlled and tormented by monsters (Rev. 9:1--11) and fed on blood (Rev. 16:5--6) while they are being pounded by hail (Rev. 16:21) in pitchblack darkness. The NCC and the HEW will be "pressing hopefully onward" to eliminate "man's inhumanity to man" so that "man will be worthy of the name of man" and the earth will be "a better place to live on" through the abolishment of prejudice, intolerance, racial minorities, etc. Same song, same key, same tune, same tempo, same insanity. [The most optimistic people in the world are in asylums.]{.class_3761}

Having reached the one third mark in Job's mighty outburst against Zophar the Naamathite, we must return for a moment to the Scholars Union to see how many times the [AV ]{.class_3759}text has lashed their proud hides into a bleeding welt.

1\. The pitiful [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 has retained the word "Jehovah" instead of ["LORD"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9) so that the Kingdom Halls will not get upset. By limiting the word "Jehovah" to the Old Testament and then calling Christ a "creature" in the New Testament ([ASV]{.class_3759}, 1901, John 9:38), Philip Schaff and his wrecking crew (many of the faculty members at Dallas, Northwestern, Pensacola Christian College, Midwestern, Piedmont, Bob Jones, etc.) have unwittingly created the most authentic Jehovah's Witness Bible in print, surpassed only by the [New World Translation]{.class_3759}.

2\. Taylor (The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---God have mercy on us---[Bible]{.class_3759}) has changed the third person plural of verse 6 to a second person singular; verse 9 has been removed three verses out of its place---while deceiving you into thinking the order was verses 7--9 (!)---and the "staggering" of the drunken man has been toned down so the respectable kiddies in the Christian day school won't get too upset. (You understanding, Taylor does this after paraphrasing Jonathan as a "son of a bitch" in 1 Samuel 20:30. There is nothing like "consistency" is there?)

3\. The [New Scofield Reference "Bible"]{.class_3759} has made five changes in the [King James ]{.class_3759}text while professing to be "the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759}," and three of these changes are simply the old, rotten, stinking [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 stuck back into the text, because God would not honor that depraved stench even with every major Christian school in America (plus John R. Rice) plugging it fifty times a year. Once a bummer, always a bummer.

4\. The [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}(1971) turns the doctrinal truth of verse 11 into a question; the reference to the antediluvian age is erased in verse 12, and Dodd has removed verse 22 and placed it after verse 25. At least he was more honest than Taylor (see above), for Dodd has also removed the number markings [in the margin]{.class_3759} so that you could tell the verse was out of place. (For more of this hanky-panky, see the [New American Standard "Bible" ]{.class_3759}used at Bob Jones and Pensacola Christian College: 1 John 5:6--8.)

5\. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3761}, in line with the blind leadership of the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901) committee, has substituted "Jehovah" at verse 9 and altered[ "princes"]{.class_3762} (vs. 19) to "priests."

6\. Adam Clarke lets the cat out of the bag concerning his real feelings about "date of authorship," for he tells us that the writer of Job "might have had his eyes on Isaiah 3:1--3." (Oh, remarkable deduction!) This would mean that the date of the writing of the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}was more than two hundred years [after ]{.class_3759}the time of Solomon.

7\. Kline wants verse 9 to read "holding their god in their hand" ([RV]{.class_3759}, 1881), and he thinks that ["princes" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 19) should read "priests" ([ASV]{.class_3759}, 1901).

8\. Heavenor thinks Moffatt is "probably right," and then he turns the doctrinal truth of verse 12 into a question. Elihu's exception (32:7--10) only proves the rule, and those of us who have wasted eyesight studying the "scientific method" of Husserl (1859--1938) and his "phenomenology" or Buddha's "presuppositionalessness" or Hume's analysis of "causation" or Berkeley's "discontinuity of dualism," etc., know how much worth ("Axiological Value") to place on the inductive scientific method as it seeks to replace the Bible as the supreme authority. ["Days should speak"]{.class_3760} (32:7)---that is the law---and ["multitude of years should teach wisdom"]{.class_3760} (32:7), but even if ["great men are not always wise"]{.class_3760} (32:9), that doesn't give some theological sophomore the right to convert Job 12:12 into a question. (See our remarks under Colossians 2:8 on the utility of the "inductive method" for overthrowing law and order and replacing it with disordered guesswork and falsehood.)

9\. The ["trusty"]{.class_3760} should be "those secured in their eloquence" (vs. 20) according to Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown; Umbreit is running around with "gods" in his hands (vs. 6) like Wolke, Ewald, Budde, Dummelow, and---but why go on?

Conservative and Fundamental Hebrew and Greek scholars (when push comes to shove) will believe ANYTHING before they can tolerate the power and authority of the Words of Truth; in this respect they are no different than any unsaved philosopher who ever lived. "No" unsaved philosopher, living or dead, could stand the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759}.

All the ruckus about the word ["princes"]{.class_3760} (Heb., ["kohen"]{.class_3759}) being "priests" in verse 19, is just the same old lying, hypocritical garbage that we have been exposed to since Genesis 3:1. The same word is translated four different ways in the sloppy [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901, while those old reprobates were complaining about "[uniform ]{.class_3759}translations". They have translated the word as "priest" here, but as\
"chief," "chief minister," and "minister" in 2 Samuel and 1 Chronicles. There is only one translation they will not allow; they will not allow the correct reading in the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611).

Anything but believe the Bible.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1133.xhtml}

CHAPTER 13

13:1 Lo, mine eye hath seen all this, mine ear hath heard and understood it.

2 What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.

3 Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.

4 But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.

Job read it "right." He had no trouble in understanding God's power (vs. 1). What his "comforters" knew, he knew (vs. 2). He wasn't inferior to them. But Job wasn't interested in talking with [them ]{.class_3759}anymore. (See 5:8 and 6:2.) ["Ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). And my, my, what material there is in that verse for sermons. The physicians of Science, Religion, Philosophy, and Education have never been able to heal themselves (Luke 4:23). They are like drugged Beatnicks talking about "leading others to the truth," when they can't lead a dog across the street. They are like Maharaji Gurus blabbing about "inner peace," while a legion of demons swarms over their depraved and defiled consciences. They are dopeheaded hippies talking about "straightening out the Establishment," when they can't even straighten up their own bedrooms.

Science, Religion, Philosophy, and Education. The Big Four! Those monumental and colossal flops! Those four sacred cows of self-righteous, God-hating (Rom. 1:30), backbiting (Rom. 1:30), heady, highminded (2 Tim. 3:4) International Socialists. The great Four of Darwinian monkeymen. When could they heal ANY sinner, dead or alive, anywhere on this earth? And Dr. Baptism, Dr. Reformation, Dr. Sacrament, and Dr. Morality are just as helpless as the "Big Four," when faced with Sin, Death, and Satan, as a shad minnow in a school of bluefish.

With rare exceptions, the great pioneers of science and education have died agnostic---without knowledge (4:21).
