With rare exceptions (1 Cor. 1:26), the great religious leaders of five continents have died agnostic. With rare exceptions, the critical philosophers and "liberated" intellects of Europe and America died, like William James and John Dewey, in absolute pitch-black darkness, with no light on God, eternity, or the destiny of their own souls. In their life course they paid \$50.00 to \$100.00 an hour to sit at the feet of the "forgers of lies" who couldn't cure a sick cat when it came to [spiritual ]{.class_3759}or [eternal ]{.class_3759}issues. ["Physicians of no value"]{.class_3760} could be the placard on the door of 95 percent of the world's popes, preachers, premiers, "chairmen," priests, educators, scholars, teachers, presidents, doctors, and lawyers. The Great Physician of John chapters 9 and 11, who set Himself up as the Absolute Truth (John 14:6) and His word as the infallible truth (John 17:17), never makes a house call on a man like Herod (Luke 23:9), who thought that God should submit to him as "datum" to be critically analyzed before an intelligent "value judgment" could be "hypothesized." You aren't examining Biblical revelations under a microscope, sonny; that Bible is examining YOU (Heb. 4:12--13). It is more alive than the enzymes, viruses, genetic codes, proteins, shells, macro-molecules, neutrons, protons, and nucleic acids you're messing around with; and long before you can deify man by creating life in a test tube, that Book will come through "on the money" with a mathematical "statistical probability" of 10 to the 157th power against it. Don't give us the shaft, doctor. You don't have the final answers to anything; they're in the Book. ["Ye are all physicians of no value"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). The only hope of any of you quack physicians is to keep on professing uncertainty. That is your "forte," because that will save you from the thing you fear the most---[exposure and ridicule ]{.class_3759}(see John 12:43). If there is anything that a modern philosopher, educator, or scientist cannot stand---including all of the born- again ones---it is [ridicule ]{.class_3759}or [exclusion]{.class_3759}. Those who ["loved the praise of men more than the praise of God"]{.class_3760} (John 12:43) included the orthodox Pharisees as well as the Bible-rejecting Sadducees. Don't forget [that]{.class_3759}.

A dying prostitute said to R. G. Lee (Bellevue Baptist Church, Memphis): "Oh, I've heard you preach lotsa times\...lotsa times\...lotsa times." No minister can cure the soul-sickness if the diseased patient doesn't take the medicine." Faraday (1791--1867) put a silver cup into some acid; it ate the silver "medicine" up. He then added a chemical to the acid, and all of the silver reformed; it could be taken up in a solid lump. Moral: if a saved sinner like Faraday (or you) could do that with chemicals which God created, what will stop God from disintegrating you in the grave (1 Cor. 15:36--37) and then reassembling you on the other side of it?

Trained scientists (1930) were shown cards that had a number of squares drawn on them. These cards were held up five to ten seconds, and then the scientists were asked to state the number of squares they had seen by the "empirical, objective, pure, phenomenal, sensory, etc.," method. Their answers ranged from 25 to 50 squares on the cards that had 40 squares, and ranged from 200 to 600 on cards that had 450 squares. Moral: Just because you "see it" under a microsope, doesn't mean that it is [there ]{.class_3759}or that you can see straight. Fingerprint experts in Chicago (1920--1930) went half crazy trying to locate a safecracker who opened the safe dials with his feet; bare toes don't match "finger prints." Moral: If you think science has ANY of the answers, you ought to read the literature of Professor Shadduck. (Two French poodles passed a German Shepherd on the street. "My name is FIFI," said one poodle, "F-I-F-I." "My name is MIMI," said the other poodle, "M-I-M-I." "My name is FIDO," said the German Shepherd, "P-H-Y-D-E-A-U-X"! Moral: Semantic-positivistic, existentialistic-phenomenology does not always spell "B-R-A-I-N-S." Sometimes it spells "BALONEY.")

He "possessed no follicle appendages on the cutaneous apex of his cranial structure, anterior to the sagittal suture and posterior to the lambdoidal suture, where said follicle appendages habitually germinate." That is, [He was bald on top of his noodle]{.class_3759}. "Neritic sediments in Globigermina strata cannot be batholiths, or plutons or lopoliths, for Pyroclastic Tuff and Obsidean (or Pumice) are closer to Garnetiferous schist and Aphanitis Phyolite; these are also subject to Diastrophism." That is, ["This junk on the bottom of the ocean cannot be rocks or granite like the other stones since they get torn up once in a while."]{.class_3759} Moral: Don't think because Dr. A. T. Robertson's Greek grammar is "over your head," that anyone who studied under him was tall enough to scratch Clarence Larkin's instep.

Education and Religion (per se) are great bluffers; they are buffoons, clowns, or jesters, who know that their real, absolute knowledge is a blank. And where these ridiculous imposters get "serious" and begin to talk about "Mathematical certainties," they automatically ignore the most remarkable phenomenon in the universe: the Bible. Here is a Book (see Gen. 38--50 and comments) which exhibits a mathematical marvel that no scientist can even attempt to explain; no, not even with the aid of apostate Conservatives, Liberals, and Neo-Orthodox theologians, and the entire libraries of Astruc, Graf, Ewald, Juenen, Hupfeld, and Wellhausen to "save his face" when he goofs. Here is a Book which proposes to tell five hundred (plus) details of the future of life on this earth where they are connected with [one man]{.class_3759}. Forty-eight of those predictions came to pass in less than four years, and if you denied 50 percent of them, you will still be left with mathematical odds greater than one out of 400,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000.

And now you Bible-rejecting "physicians" are going to teach [us ]{.class_3759}something about "values," are you? You are going to offer yourselves (Science, Religion, Philosophy, and Education) as "blind guides of the blind" so that we won't have to stumble around in the dark with our Bible? You are, are you? Do you think any of us, who have spent "all we have on physicians" (Luke 8:43) for twenty-seven to forty years, are going to place any more confidence in you than we would in a professional politician? You are going to "liberate us from Mid- Victorian morals," are you? You are going to free us from our "subjective prejudices" and enable us to progress up to where YOU are, are you? I mean, some of you meatheads actually think that we poor, dumb, stupid people who believe the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}(no matter what John R. Rice says!) do so because "we don't know any better," don't you? Well, you are right: [we don't know "any better." ]{.class_3759}And the day that you or your crowds show us something "better" is the day we will abandon the Old Liner Zion and board your vermin-infested, worm-eaten, water-logged raft. Before we take your "piper cub" to the North Pole (without radar or an instrument panel), would you please reduplicate for us the following "items" which we already have:

1\. Forgiveness of sins and a conscience clear of offense toward God and man ("pure phenomenology" if you ever got it, buster.).

2\. A God who can create and sustain spiritual life as well as physical life (John 3:1--7).

3\. A Saviour who can answer prayer at a second's notice, if necessary, and fulfill promises as wild as these: ["All things work together for good," "shall supply all your need," "able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye always having all sufficiency in all things,"]{.class_3760} and no more death, pain, sorrow or crying. Don't you go blabbing your big fat mouth off to us about "wishful thinking" and "hopeful subjectivity" until you can supply our need as well as the Lord God Almighty has already done it through (and with) His word.

Go stick your thermometer in your own mouth, Dr. Education. Go take your own pulse and blood pressure, Dr. Science. Go get your stethoscope and check your own "innards," Dr. Religion; and don't come around to us sinners, sitting on the ash heap, and give us this handout about "relative complexity and disinterested passion for understanding, which enables us to accept the total evidence of the methodological conclusions."

Go give yourself an enema, Dr. Philosophy; you need it.

13:5 O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.

6 Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.

7 Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?

8 Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?

9 Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye so mock him?

10 He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.

11 Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?

12 Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.

13 Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will.

14 Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?

15 Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.

16 He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.

Job tells his listeners to shut up (vs. 5), for even if a fool holds his peace (Prov. 17:28), he can be counted as a man of wisdom (vs. 5). Bildad, Zophar, and Eliphaz have done some "reasoning" (vs. 6) with Job; now he implores them to listen to some of his own reasonings.

"[Will ye speak wickedly for God?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). Will you accuse me of being a sinner, a fool, a liar, and a hypocrite---they just had---when that is not God's feelings about the matter? Will you say that God wouldn't treat me this way unless I was a first class reprobate? Would God say that? Then will you ["talk deceitfully for him?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 7). \[See comments under verse 10.\] Job continues: ["Will you accept his person?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8); that is, are you trying to get in good with Him (see James 2:1--4) by acting like you think He would if He "knew all the facts?" ["Will ye contend for God?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8); that is, are you trying to put on God's judicial robes and sit as a judge in His place because He hasn't yet spoken up? ["Is it good that he should search you out?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9). If God decided to search you out like He is searching me, would it be good, according to you? Wouldn't you holler bloody-murder about "What did I do to deserve this, etc."? Job asks them if they are mocking God like a man mocks a man (vs. 9). And they are doing this if they begin to get into that old ["gain is godliness"]{.class_3762} bit (1 Tim. 6:5) that is so prevalent today among the Christian celebrities and monument builders. John and Betty Stam (killed, 1934) never ran "1000 in Sunday School," and neither did John Birch or Haralan Popov. ["If ye do secretly accept persons"]{.class_3762} (vs. 10) like those Christians who have ["men's persons in admiration because of advantage"]{.class_3762} (Jude 16), you will only classify yourself as a publican and sinner (Matt. 5:47).

In his latter years, a reknown Christian journalist spent his time trying to get into big churches by giving them free publicity in his newspaper. Now, this evangelist did not ["secretly accept persons"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10); he [openly ]{.class_3759}downed persons and small churches with their pastors that did not agree with him. He publicly attacked some who pointed out the inconsistencies in the way "results" were obtained in some churches, slandered authors and tape ministries which succeeded in getting their works published without coming to him (or his crowd) for help, and then this "man from God" had the audacity to sink over \$50,000 into a building for his own newspaper while talking about "Scriptural [stewardship]{.class_3759}" of money. He wasn't a ["man sent from God"]{.class_3760}---he was a promotional opportunist. A real Christian does not limit his friends to the wealthy, healthy, and educated if he is really "godly" or anywhere near "godly." If all of your inner circle of friends are wealthy, healthy, and educated, it means you have picked your friends for their ability to help you: you are UNGODLY. James had something to say to you about that kind of person, and if you are too pious and "dedicated" to take it to heart, then read about one of your kinfolk (Laban---Gen. 24:30) who couldn't wait to welcome rich people into his "fellowship." Laban considered Jacob a good "prospect" for "visitation," and there is a lot of that kind of hogwash going on in America today among Fundamentalists.

"[Shall not his excellency make you afraid?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). It did Job (see 3:25 and 23:15). ["Your remembrances are like unto ashes\...clay"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12). Man is born into a fireplace (5:7), so he winds up as a pile of ashes (see 2:8 and comments). Augustus Severus said: "Just think, someday that pot will hold all that is left of a man to whom the world was not enough!" Job goes on: ["Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will"]{.class_3762} (vs. 13). This is the reckless talk of a genuine fanatic, but verse 15 shows that no matter how reckless and fanatical it is, it is genuine; Job is quite prepared (as Simon Peter) to seal his talk with his own blood---just like Paul (Acts 20:24). Job will take his life in his hands (vs. 14) and be prepared to throw it, or drop it, at a moment's notice if he has to (vs. 15). If God kills him on the spot for blasphemy (vs. 15), it will not change his mind about himself or about God. Job is the true, fanatical, bigoted dogmatist---the terror of every educated American on earth, for there is nothing that an educated ass fears more than the charges of bigotry, dogmatism, or fanaticism. If you want to stand a Ph.D.'s hair on end, just accuse him of one of those things and watch him deny everything but his degree to convince you that he is "broadminded," "open-minded," or "liberal."

"[Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth\...?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). Why do I cut off my nose to spite my face? The picture is not that of a wild beast carrying off someone else's flesh (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown), nor is it "why should I anxiously desire to save my life?" (Ewald). Rather it is: "I am ready to be responsible for my own death (["put my life in mine hand"]{.class_3762}) just as much as if I began eating my flesh right now."

"[Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). Again, if Job had stopped at the colon (see 1:22 and comments), the arguments might have ended, and God might have relieved Job of his pain and misery in the next chapter. But Job cannot resist the P.S. ["But I will maintain mine own ways before him"]{.class_3762} (vs. 15). Ah, Job, if you had only not said that. Man's goings ["are of the Lord"]{.class_3762} (see Prov. 16:9 and 20:24). Even if Job had a reference to his own moral standards, the ad lib is unnecessary. How good could any man be without the power, mercy, and grace of God to sustain him? But still, there is beautiful faith in Job's confession: ["Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him."]{.class_3762} You see, it is not Science, Philosophy, Religion, or Education; like David, Job could say: ["What wait I for? My hope is in thee"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 39:7)---not the United Nations, the humanoids from outer space, the International Bankers, the Illuminati, the National Education Association, UNESCO, or the Council on Foreign Relations. Those of us who have entrusted our souls to the Lord God (2 Tim. 1:12) know it is better to ["trust in the LORD"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 118:8) than to ["put confidence in man."]{.class_3762} We know Who to trust: God Almighty. And on eternal issues we wouldn't trust the Library of Science, or the combined works of all of the Nobel Prize winners, as far as we could throw the Empire State Building with our left hand.

The Lord Jesus Christ fulfills Job's confession (literally) in Matthew 27:43 and Hebrews 2:13. Paul says if God is going to kill him (Phil. 1:21), let the axe fall (Acts 25:11). Death is not going to affect our trust in the Trustworthy One a particle. A martyr, on the way to the block, passes his watch to the axeman and says: "Take this, I won't be needing it anymore where I am going." Another martyr is on the way to the block chuckling, "In five minutes I'll know the answer! In five minutes I'll know what it is all about!" Thousands of Chinese Christians were given a simple proposition between 1930 and 1970: "Deny your faith in the Lord Jesus or deny your [life]{.class_3759}." They fell headless, sightless, armless, legless, and earless into bloody pits because they refused to transfer their trust from God Almighty to the "People's Party." Our trust is in a Person---HIM (vs. 15); so don't try to integrate us with the Buddhists who teach a "presence" or the scientists who trust a "field of electrical forces" or the philosophers who worship the infinite "thinghood of the whatness of the thing-in-itself." Just write us off, okay? Just put us down as implacable ENEMIES of everything you and your crowd stand for, if you and your crowd are a bunch of Bible-rejecting humanists who quote scripture on "love" to eliminate your opposition. (You had to do it before 1990, so why not do it now and just dismiss all of [this truth ]{.class_3759}as "hate literature?")

The unsaved man says: "Till he slay me, I will sin against him." That is the real creed of the "People's Liberation Front."

George Whitefield says that salvation is like this: a man is hanging by a rope from the precipice of a huge cliff; a rainstorm is washing the dirt and gravel off the cliff in torrents. The man is swinging wildly over a five hundred foot abyss, clinging to a wet slippery rope about thirty feet long. As each lightning flash illuminates his terrible predicament, he slips one or two inches nearer the end of the rope, swinging now toward the cliff and then away from it in an effort to get a toe-hold on the slippery rocks. With ten inches left to the rope, the Angel of the Lord suddenly appears in the darkness, standing calmly in mid-air. He speaks to the doomed wretch:

"Dost thou believe that I am able to save thee?"

"Yes, yes. My God, Yes!" screams the terrified man.

"All right," says the Angel, ["let go!"]{.class_3761}

Well, you either trust Him or you don't. And since 95 percent of the educators, doctors, scientists, psychiatrists, lawyers, and politicans DON'T, they will go on swinging and slipping on their wet ropes of infidelity till they lose their grip and dash their souls on the rocks of God's judgments lying beneath them. You're a fool to trust yourself or any man or any institution or any system or any ideology when you could put your trust in the Creator of the universe who loved you enough to take your place as a sinner. (You're welcome. Anytime I can offer any assistance, count on me!)

13:17 Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.

18 Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.

19 Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.

20 Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.

21 Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.

22 Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.

Job's audience has begun to cough and clear their throats, and he has to pound the pulpit to get their attention (vs. 17). Job says: ["I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18), and he was. But not the way he expected to be. Job has "ordered his cause" in that he has drawn up his legal brief after much prayer, and he is now ready to present his case for his "day in court." ["Who is he that will plead with me?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19). Job is asking for the prosecuting attorney (wherever he is. See Job 1:6) to appear and file the charges (Isa. 50:8). ["I shall give up the ghost"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19). This is the common expression, still used internationally, for lying down and expiring. ["The ghost"]{.class_3760} was the breath of a person who took on a bodily shape (see 1 Cor. 3:17) after their decease. ["Only do not two things unto me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20). Job says that he is ready to appear in court and answer; he will not "hide among the stuff" (vs. 20) like Saul (1 Sam. 10:22) and Adam (Gen. 3:8). However, he requires two "breaks." One: stop tearing me to pieces with disease and pain. Two: speak up so I know with whom I am dealing (vs. 22).

13:23 How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.

24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?

25 Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?

26 For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.

27 Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly into all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.

28 And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten."

Job asks God five questions:

1\. ["How many are mine iniquities and sins?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23).

2\. ["Wherefore hidest thou thy face?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24).

3\. ["Wherefore\...holdest me for thine enemy?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24).

4\. ["Wilt thou break a leaf\...?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25).

5\. ["Wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25).

Observe that verse 24 is a direct prophetic reference to Israel in Deuteronomy 31:18 and Matthew 13:44. Naturally, Biederwolf and Payne missed it in their "complete" collections of prophetic Bible references.

Now, Job is within his rights. David prays the prayer of verse 23 in Psalm 19:12 and 13, and every believer in the New Testament should pray this petition before putting 1 John 1:9 into practice. (This prayer was answered for David in Psa. 40:12.) ["For thou writest bitter things against me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26), and if we could see the "handwriting on the wall" (Rev. 20:12) and the "punishment-charged scroll" (John 19:22), we wouldn't go around spouting off a lot of steam about "man's dignity, greatness, and ingenuity."[ "And]{.class_3760} [makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26). (See Jer. 17:1 and Psa. 25:11.)

If there were not some plan of redemption that would ["take away"]{.class_3760} these sins (Heb. 9:15), no man would pass the judgment of God in any condition. Forgiveness of sins (["remission"]{.class_3760}) is not enough (Heb. 9:22), and until these sins are "redeemed" (Heb. 9:15), the guilty cannot be "cleared" (Rom. 3:25). If you had to face judgment tonight for everything you thought and said (and did) between the ages of 18 and 38, wouldn't you be in a situation?

"[Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27) like a condemned criminal (Acts 16:24) or a slave. ["And lookest narrowly unto all my paths"]{.class_3762} (vs. 27) like he was a virus under observation. ["Thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet. And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten"]{.class_3762} (vss. 27--28).

On the last two verses, the entire body of Conservative and Liberal Scholarship (the [Interpreter's "Bible,"]{.class_3759} for example) defects to Infidelity and leaves the believer to shift for himself. [Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown ]{.class_3759}suggest the changes made by Umbreit and Gesenius for the verse which, roughly, would produce the following: "Thou drawest for thyself a line or trench round my soles beyond which I must not move." The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(with the Syriac and Arabic) makes the ["HE"]{.class_3760} refer to the first person---"I"---and so does [Adam Clarke. Taylor ]{.class_3759}([TLB]{.class_3759}) gets rid of the ["heels,"]{.class_3760} the ["print,"]{.class_3760} the ["feet,"]{.class_3760} and the ["stocks,"]{.class_3760} while The [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}drops ["the rotten thing,"]{.class_3760} the ["garment," ]{.class_3760}and the ["moth,"]{.class_3760} by simply striking out the whole verse. The [ASV ]{.class_3759}(with Budde, Peak, and "the rest of 'em") lands somewhere between the [LXX ]{.class_3759}and [RSV, ]{.class_3759}and the comments on the passage by Kline and Heavenor (Wycliffe and the [New Bible Commentary]{.class_3759}) are about as enlightening as a discourse on "Field stripping the M-1" by Cassius Clay.

Now, the[ "heels"]{.class_3760} of the feet are very interesting. This is where Jacob grabs his brother to supplant him (Hos. 12:3); this is the prophesied point of contact between the Lord's Christ and the Devil's Christ (Gen. 3:15); David speaks of the "iniquity of his heels" (Psa. 49:5) following him, and his enemies "raising up their heels" against him (Psa. 41:9). It is the snare that takes the "wicked" by his own heel in Job 18:9; therefore, any "Bible" that erases the word ([NEB, TLB ]{.class_3759}both erased it) is back to the old God-dishonouring task of obliterating cross-references to the truth. The print on "the heels" of Job's feet could be a reference to a chain or a "stock," but one must never forget that (literally) Job was neither in chains nor in stocks. His figurative references, therefore, apply to something beyond his contemporary situation. Furthermore, the rendering of verse 28: ["He, as a rotten thing"]{.class_3760} indicates something living is gnawing away at Job. I wonder how it is that ["their worm"]{.class_3760} that ["dieth not"]{.class_3760} (Mark 9:44) escaped the notice of the commentators when Job has worms on him at this time (see 7:5 and 17:14)? In Isaiah 51:8 we are told that the WORM eats up wool just like the ["moth eaten"]{.class_3760} garment of Job 13:28. Herod is ["eaten of worms"]{.class_3760} (Acts 12:23), and God has been known to prepare a ["worm" ]{.class_3760}on more than one occasion (Jonah 4:7).

Now, isn't it rather peculiar that with these obvious references sitting before them that the Jewish Publication Society and the [ASV ]{.class_3759}committee (plus Gesenius, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Henry, Peake, Ewald, Umbreit, and Maurer) could lead us nowhere except into an empty refrigerator?

Christ calls Himself a ["worm"]{.class_3760} at His death (Psa. 22:6), and the Antichrist has worms spread under him at his fall (Isa. 14:11). The manna (Exod. 16:20 and comments) ["bred worms, and stank."]{.class_3760} If that were not enough, the nation of Israel (called ["Jacob"]{.class_3760} in Isaiah 41:14) is called a ["worm"]{.class_3760} in reference to their troubles in the Tribulation. How does one explain 100 percent defection from the correct text in view of these references? In this reading, the [AV ]{.class_3759}of 1611 has corrected the accumulated errors of every major commentator and revisor in the field of Biblical exegesis in the last 1900 years. Job's situation is literally true in regard to being consumed by a ["rotten thing."]{.class_3760} This situation applies to the Lord Jesus Christ at His crucifixion, to a man in Hell, and to Israel in the Tribulation. So, you may safely reject the readings of 100 percent of the commentators where they differ with the [Authorized Text ]{.class_3759}of the Protestant Reformation.

In coming through the chapter, we should note that the [RSV]{.class_3759} has wrecked many a good sermon (Heavenor's opinion) by messing up Job 13:15 (see Preface). This is very typical of dead orthodox apostates, since the majority of them deeply resented the Biblical preaching that they heard during their times. (Note the same irrational fanaticism exhibited by Dr. A. T. Robertson when he went about getting rid of Acts 26:28.) The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}has altered ["your bodies"]{.class_3760} to "your mounds" in verse 12, and Rev. Rawlinson believes it is absolutely incredulous that Job could have said verse 15 as it stands. The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}has added eight words to the verse that mean nothing one way or another.

Verse 25 reminds us of David's complaint against Saul (1 Sam. 26:20). Why waste time chasing dry leaves and stubble? Is it worth your time?

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1210.xhtml}

CHAPTER 14

14:1 Man that is born of a woman is of few days, and full of trouble.

2 He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.

3 And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.

5 Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;

6 Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.

In the last third of Job's answer to Zophar, Job gets objective enough for awhile to discuss ["man"]{.class_3760} instead of "me." "I," "me," and "my" do not show up in regular succession until verse 13. He does say ["me"]{.class_3760} once in verse 3.

"[Man that is born of a woman\...full of trouble"]{.class_3760} (vs. 1). As a skeptic said: "I was born worn out and have had a good many relapses." One of the Hebrew words for "man" (Hebrew "nashim") means "sorrowful" or "incurable." (Remember those ["physicians of no value"]{.class_3762}?) Man that is ["born of a woman"]{.class_3762} is unclean (25:4), for a clean thing cannot come out of an unclean thing. This led the Bible-rejecting philosophers of Rome (the pope, College of Cardinals, archbishops, et al.) to invent the "Immaculate Conception" of Mary; the reasoning being that Jesus Christ would have to have a sinless mother in order to be born sinless.

Now the "logical reasoning" behind this is quite like the "logical reasoning" that Origen (A.D. 184--254) used to call a pastor a "priest." It is also like the reasoning he used to get rid of a literal interpretation of Genesis 2--4. To reason in this fashion, the first thing you must do is reject a clear passage of scripture. In Origen's case, you would reject 1 Peter 2:9 and John 17:11. The way the Catholic Pope handles Mary's conception is by ignoring the direct revelation of God not three verses from the announcement of Gabriel to Mary, and not ten verses from the "Ave Maria" (Hail Mary)! You see, carefully tucked away in Luke 1, right between the two miracles (vss. 34 and 4), are these words: ["For with God nothing shall be impossible"]{.class_3760} (vs. 37). You see, the statement in Job about ["who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean"]{.class_3760} (14:4) was a reference to what MEN cannot do. Notice exactly the same construction in Luke 18:27 with regard to salvation.

By giving Mary two divine attributes (which were Christ's alone)---a sinless birth and a bodily ascension ahead of either resurrection---the blasphemous popes and archbishops came as close as they could to installing Mary into the Trinity without doing it. Blasphemers of a feather blaspheme together.

"[He cometh forth like a flower\...he fleeth also as a shadow" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 2). (See 7:7 and comments.) ["And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one\...into judgment with thee?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). The problem is an old one; and when Descarte, Kant, Hume, Sartre, Plato, Fichte, Schelling, Hegel, Heidegger, and Russell couldn't solve it, they just cancelled God out and pretended He wasn't there. (See Acts 17:32 and comments.) Now, there is only one way that God could truly lower Himself enough to enter into a contest with His creation, and that would be by becoming (Himself) ["manifest in the flesh"]{.class_3762} and then living, suffering, and dying as His creation has to do it (Heb. 2:14). This God Almighty did (1 Tim. 3:16), if we accept the Authorized Text of the 1611 Holy Bible in 1 Timothy 3:16 instead of the disgusting and deplorable nonsense found in the [ASV ]{.class_3761}and [New ASV ]{.class_3761}on that verse. (Any edition of the [RSV]{.class_3761} or the [New English Bible]{.class_3761} has also attacked the verse since it is one of the strongest verses in the New Testament on the Deity of Jesus Christ. The [ASV ]{.class_3761}of 1901 is just as rotten and just as godless as the worst of the Liberal translations at this point.)

If the Lord Jesus Christ was God ["manifest in the flesh,"]{.class_3760} then the world's philosophers who tried to avoid a personal encounter with God are in for a terrible and tragic surprise. In their vast ignorance and lamentable blindness (Eph. 4:18), it never entered their minds that perhaps the Bible record was right and their education and "logic" was wrong (1 Cor. 1:18). And no matter how many times they "re-adjust" their theories and "datum" and no matter how many times they acknowledge their "agnosticism" and "need to re-think a problem," there is something they will not do: They will NOT accept the Biblical record of John and Paul as the true picture of Jesus Christ.

God has appointed days, months, and bounds that man cannot pass over (vs. 5), and those who major in "breaking out of bounds" (Hos. 5:10) to integrate and mongrelize the nations (Acts 17:26) will give account to God for the dead, wounded, and dying young people who were the victims of their Socialistic pipedreams. The man who "crosses the boundaries" (Isa. 5:8) is a TRESPASSER.

"[Turn from him"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6) in the sense of "quit dogging him, quit harassing him, get off his back." ["Till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day"]{.class_3762} (vs. 6 cf. 7:1). "Here lies the bones of Mary Jones, for her death had no terrors. She lived an old maid, she died an old maid: no hits, no runs, no errors." Born unclean into a world of affliction, man is of few days, full of trouble, and unable to communicate with his creator. If that weren't enough, must God then plague him and chasten him every day, from morning till night (Psa. 73:14), in addition to his natural problems?

14:7 For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.

8 Though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the ground;

9 Yet through the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughs like a plant.

10 But man dieth, and wasteth away: yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is he?

11 As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:

12 So man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens be no more, they shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep.

Trees "have it made" in comparison with man. You can cut down a Catawba tree every two years (Pensacola, Florida), and it will grow back every time whether you fertilize it or not. Many trees, if they are ["cut down,"]{.class_3760} sprout again (see Israel in Rom. 11:17, 24), and even if the trunk dies (["the stock"]{.class_3760} vs. 8), the roots will put out new shoots that will pop up anywhere from one inch to ten feet from the original stump (vss. 8--9). But ["man dieth and wasteth away; yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is he?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10). Well, he is "somewhere."

When asked about whether he would rather be killed in a train crash or a plane crash, a dusky one answered immediately: "A train crash, yassuh." "Why?" asked the inquirer. "Well," said the dusky one, "if you's in a train crash, why deah you is. But if you is in a plane crash---well, wheah is you?!" Well, you're somewhere. [Eleanor Roosevelt ]{.class_3759}didn't know ([Christian Victory]{.class_3759}, March 1953, p. 56). [Einstein ]{.class_3759}didn't know (see his remark to Queen Elizabeth: Jan. 9, 1939). [Homer ]{.class_3759}didn't know either ([Dionysius and Immortality]{.class_3759}, 1899, p. 21). [H. G. Wells]{.class_3759} didn't know ([Experiment in Autobiography]{.class_3759}, 1934). [Mary Baker Eddy ]{.class_3759}didn't know (Christian Science). [Joe Smith ]{.class_3759}didn't know (Mormons). [Russell ]{.class_3759}and [Rutherford ]{.class_3759}thought that "man" went to a hole in the ground (Jehovah's Witnesses). The popes and bishops invented places for "man" to go (Limbo and Purgatory), and you couldn't have gotten a definitive statement on "where is man after death" from Kant, Sartre, Heidegger, Fromm, Tillich, Neibuhr, Barth, or Brunner if you turned them over to the NKVD or the Gestapo. If you were to take the fifty top leaders in the Health, Education, and Welfare Department you would be fortunate, indeed, to get a clear answer from five of them in regards to Job's question: ["Man dieth\...and where is he?"]{.class_3760} (Job 14:10).

The Unknown God (Acts 17:23 and comments) is just as "unknown" today as He was when Plato and Aristotle were trying to substitute the depraved thinking of degenerate sinners for the authority of the Old Testament. If you really want an honest confessional of faith from the HEW (or the NEA or the United Nations), it would have to be the classic statement of Professor Vernon Kellog of Stanford University (Darwinism Today): "For my part it seems better to go back to the OLD and SAFE IGNORAMUS STANDPOINT." That is exactly what Paul noticed when he preached to the Greek Faculty at the University of Athens in A.D. 59 (Acts 17:23--29; Eph. 4:18: Pure, unadulterated ignorance.)

Well, ["where is he?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10). In the Old Testament, the saved man is in a dreamless sleep (1 Sam. 28:15) with his fathers (Gen. 50:3), and his soul is no more "in the grave" than his spirit (1 Kings 17:22). Job knows this for certain (Job 14:12).

And on the basis of this truth (Ecc. 3:19--20), Pastor Russell and Judge Rutherford ([Watchtower Society]{.class_3759}) constructed the great worldwide missionary movement which teaches that [unregenerate sinners ]{.class_3759}lapse into unconscious darkness at death. That is, Russell and Rutherford stole the Old Testament promises given to Old Testament [saints ]{.class_3759}and applied them to Christ-rejecting "do-gooders" in the New Testament, exactly as Rome pilfered Exodus 22--31 for itself. Unsaved men, however, do [not ]{.class_3759}go into a dreamless sleep in either Testament. They are spoken of as being in conscious torment (Luke 16:19--24; Num. 16:30) wherever a doctrinal statement (Matt. 24:41) is given in reference to the after-life (Rev. 20:10). The New Testament saint is "living with Christ" (1 Thess. 5:10), and he is no more "in the grave" (1 Thess. 5:10; Phil. 1:23) than he is in the casket. No Christian's soul ever laid in a coffin (2 Cor. 5:8) three seconds, unless he was buried alive (Catholic Inquisition, for example) before his soul departed (Gen. 49:33). No New Testament Christian is in "limbo" or "purgatory" or "hell" or the grave or anything like any of them, nor anything that anyone invented to replace them. The fact that you or your kinfolk (and possibly your employer---oh yeah man! there is more to Bible rejection than "convictions"!) or your church doesn't believe that is about as "significant" as a Campbellite thinking that Acts 2:38 is the "plan of salvation." Unsaved men are in Hell and they are burning (Luke 16:23), according to the Lord Jesus Christ. If someone doesn't believe Him, they can argue it out with Him and "prove conclusively from the scriptures" that He was misinformed while they, with their superior knowledge, were right. And don't think that they won't get a chance to do this (Rom. 3:6), because they will (Rom. 3:4). You'd better start your research now, because you might drop into Hell in a matter of seconds. Unsaved men in either Testament are in conscious torment (Rom. 2:16; Matt. 25:46), and if you don't like that (Heb. 2:2--3), keep on trusting your "religion" and see if the Bible is telling the truth about it (Rev. 20:15). There is nothing like "firsthand" experience to convince a skeptic, is there?

When Reinhold Niebuhr ([Discerning the Signs of the Times]{.class_3759}, 1946) writes about "Sin and Evil," he is very careful to omit any reference to the eternal results of sin and evil. When Niebuhr speaks of "original sin" ([Beyond Tragedy]{.class_3759}, 1937, pp. 11--30), he is very, very careful to forget to tell you that there is no destiny fixed for any man (saved or lost) who handles the "original sin" problem one way or another. Neibuhr could no more answer Job's question of 1800 B.C. than he could spit and swallow at the same time. When Paul Tillich speaks of "Sin and Evil" ([The Eternal Now]{.class_3759}, 1963, pp. 50--56), he never tells you the consequences of sin after death, nor is life after death even related to sin even where "Sin" has been defined as "turning toward ourselves" or "estrangement from ourselves." In the Revelation of God Almighty, Tillich's sins are against [God Almighty]{.class_3759}, and they were [paid for by God Almighty]{.class_3759}, and Tillich will give an account to God Almighty about his own acceptance or refusal of that payment (Rom. 2:16, Heb. 9:27).

When Erich Fromm ([The Art of Loving]{.class_3759}, 1956) speaks of "Freedom," he is very careful to confine his remarks about "man" to a "cradle-to-grave" bracket. Beyond the grave, Fromm has no more information than Will Herberg ([Judaism and Modern Man]{.class_3759}, 1951). Herberg's "last judgment" (pp. 120--235) is about as true to fact as Mao's [Red Book]{.class_3759}, or G. T. Armstrong's [World Tomorrow]{.class_3759}. Bishop Pike is tumbling around in a barren patch of Judean desert, trying to find his dead son; Harry Emerson Fosdick is saying that the body of Jesus rotted in a grave in Palestine; Adolph Harnack is teaching that the resurrection is an "idea" instead of a fact; Bishop Gore theorizes that the bodily resurrection is only spiritual; and Professor Guignebert thinks Christ's body was cast into a pit instead of laid in a tomb. And "time would fail me to tell" of the fanatical, superstitious, archaic dogmatism of these narrowminded, reactionary bigots whose bungling guesswork is so "insular" (twentieth century cliche) that they wouldn't know the works of Clarence Larkin from Bullinger's [Companion Bible]{.class_3759}.

Stephen, Paul, Peter, James, and John are in the third heaven (2 Cor. 12:1--3) beyond Alpha Draconis, and they are in the company of Moody, Torrey, Finney, Spurgeon, Wesley, Toplady, Booth, Paton, Carey, Goforth, et al. When they come back into this solar system (1 Thess. 4:14) to get their new bodies (1 John 3:1--3), they will be preceded by a blast (1 Thess. 4:11--13) that will lift the living saints off their feet (Rom. 8:11) and transform them in the ["twinkling of an eye"]{.class_3760} (1 Cor. 15:49--52). And there isn't a Bible-believing Christian on the face of this earth who has to be agnostic or ignorant or theorize about the question: "Where do the dead go?" Leave the speculation to the unsaved "Christians" and degenerate church leaders. We know where the saints [went ]{.class_3759}and where they [are ]{.class_3759}and where they [will be,]{.class_3759} and our dogmatism is rooted and grounded in an Authority that has never "slipped a hitch" in thirty-eight centuries, and that is more than you can say for the contents of the Library of Congress in Washington, D.C.

"[As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). As the waters were "assuaged" following Genesis 8:1--3 (another reference to Noah's flood) and the ["flood\...drieth up"]{.class_3762} (another reference to Noah's flood), and as the water deposits silt and mud at the estuaries until the land "creeps" out into the sea (Persian Gulf, Nile Delta, Mississippi Delta, etc.), ["so man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens be no more\..."]{.class_3762} (vs. 12). And whether Job realizes the doctrinal content of this statement or not, it fits marvelously into the passage in Revelation 20:11--15. When these passages are compared with such passages as Ezekiel 37 and Revelation 11:11--12, we arrive at two conclusions:

1\. The saved people from Adam to Moses will probably be resurrected at the White Throne Judgment and judged on the basis of Romans 2:12--16.

2\. The possible exception will be the saved from Abraham to Moses (Israel), who will be resurrected at the beginning of the Millennium (Ezek. 37). ["They shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) which is why Samuel is "disquieted" when the Lord gives him a pre-resurrection appearance and Lazarus (Luke 16:24) does not answer the rich man's requests. But the application of these passages to the Lost is "mooching" a little. Dathan and Abiram go down ALIVE into the pit (Num. 16:33).

14:13 O that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!

14 If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.

15 Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.

Job is pleading for a chance to state his case. God's wrath will be past (1 Thess. 5:9) in the Tribulation before Job is resurrected, even if he is resurrected before the White Throne Judgment. The ["set time"]{.class_3760} of verse 13 will either be Daniel 7:9--10 at the Judgment of nations (Matt. 25:31) or the White Throne (Rev. 20) where saved servants are also present (Rev. 11:18), in spite of the denial of this truth by 99 percent of the Premillennial expositors. Where Lindsay borrows from Pentecost, after Pentecost has drained what Kirban borrowed from Larkin, there isn't much point in looking for anything advanced.

"[If a man die, shall he live again?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). Yes, he will. Job answers his own question with ["all the days\...will I wait, till my change come"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14). And what a change that will be for the ["children of God"]{.class_3762} (Rom. 8:21) who change (Phil. 3:21) into thirty-three-year-old males (Judg. 13:3, 6, 13, 15) with sinless bodies (Phil. 3:21) that are incorruptible and glorious (1 Cor. 15:42--48). (See Phil. 3:21 and comments in that Commentary.)

"[Thou shalt call"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). You bet He will "call" (Psa. 50:4--6; Song of Sol. 2:6--12; Job 37:1--4), and He will "call" so loudly, you will literally "jump out of your skin" when your heart is ["moved out of his place,"]{.class_3762} and the ["day star arise in your hearts"]{.class_3762} (2 Pet. 1:19). (Sure, there is plenty in the infallible English that those Greek and Hebrew scholars didn't find, and aren't finding, and won't find out in the next thirty years.) ["And I will answer thee"]{.class_3762} (vs. 15). "Hey Job! Come up hither!" (See Rev. 11:12). "Coming, Father!" Whhaaaamm! There goes Job. Christ said He would call the sheep by their names (John 10:3) just like He called out Lazarus by name (see Job 37:1--4 and comments.)

14:16 For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?

17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag, and thou sewest up mine iniquity.

18 And surely the mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of his place.

19 The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.

20 Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.

21 His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.

22 But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.

And now the future hopes are temporarily put aside (vss. 11--15). Job is back in the "nasty now and now" (vs. 16), and ["now"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16) God is counting cadence (vs. 16) while Job walks; God is checking him out with electronic buggers (vs. 16). His transgression is sealed up and sewn shut in a bag which contains his iniquities (vs. 17). And if mountains and rocks (vs. 18) and stones (vs. 19) are subject to the great universal law of devolution---not "evolution" or "revolution"---how much more is a weak, frail, sinful human being who can't even outlive a redwood tree (vs. 19)? Where is Lenin? Where is M. L. King Jr.? Where is FDR? What happened to Hitler? Where is old Ho Chi Minh? Where is Marx, Trotsky, Jack Kennedy, and Mussolini? ["Man dieth\...and where is he?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10). Rocks can be removed from their place by landslides, earthquakes, and avalanches. Mountains can tumble over like ten pins in earthquakes, and the tops of mountains have been known to sail gracefully thirty-four miles into the air (Krakatoa, August 27, 1883) with an explosive force twenty-five times greater than the biggest H-bomb that man ever set off. If you want real "fireworks," watch God turn the solar system into a "cherry bomb" after the Millennium. (After all, a ten-megaton H-bomb isn't too much to waste time with.) An earthquake can not only topple mountains and roll rocks around; it can also kill 148,807 people and cause \$2,800,000,000 worth of damage (Japan, September 1, 1923). The rain\
water can ["wear the stones"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19), and too much rain will take the crop right on down the valley (vs. 19) as it ["washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth."]{.class_3760} There are no "big" men in hurricanes, floods, forest fires, tornadoes, earthquakes, tidal waves, and typhoons. There are brave sinners and cowardly sinners, but all of them are scared, unless they are either drugged or drunk. When God turns His elements loose---and they are HIS---He ["destroyest the hope of man"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19).

When the South Fork Dam at Lake Connemaugh broke (May 31, 1899), it turned loose 20,000,000 tons of water from a 450 acre lake. This mass of water tumbled seventy-two feet into a valley and then rushed down another 380 feet before it hit the town of Johnstown, Ohio. And let me tell you something! When that 20,000,000 tons of water went through Johnstown, Ohio, it went through in ten minutes, leaving behind 2,015 dead people, with one hundred of them burned beyond recognition and a total of 777 unidentified bodies. In the midst of that screaming, roaring, burning, drowning nightmare, there were Methodists crying: "We'll be saved by the Methodist church," and Catholics snarling back: "Only the Catholic church can save!" Well, there isn't an institution or organization (church or otherwise) on this earth that can save anyone from death, judgment, or damnation, and you are very unwise to think that there is. You will die when God gives Satan your life (Job 2:6; Heb. 2:14), and you will face judgment whether you are prepared or unprepared (Heb. 9:27). Friend, when that 20,000,000 tons of water (moving at forty mph) came down that valley, you could see eighty-ton locomotives rolling along with the tide like a wiped-out surfer. If you ever survive such a catastrophe---there were only four blind men in Johnstown, and all four of them survived---you had better give the glory to God instead of your "church."

"[Thou prevailest forever against him, and he passeth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20). God goes right on living, and we go right on dying. Man comes and goes: ["he passeth"]{.class_3762} (Ecc. 9:1--2), and there is no discharge (Ecc. 8:8) from dying unless the C.O. (Heb. 2:10) blows "reveille" before "taps" (1 Cor. 15:52).

"[Thou changest his countenance"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20). The wrinkled skin tissue deteriorates; the eyelids drop and the lips begin to tremble. When time, with iron-shod hoof stamps across a man's face, it leaves the "hoof prints" behind. ["And sendest him away"]{.class_3762} (vs. 20) to the funeral parlor (morgue) and the cemetery (graveyard).

"[His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not"]{.class_3760} (vs. 21); and if they don't graduate and get the degrees (or get elected or promoted), ["they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not"]{.class_3762} (vs. 21). That is, after you are dead, you will never know whether your boy became a president or a "pusher," a dentist or a drunkard, a doctor or a dopehead, a lawyer or a leech, a broker or a bum, a general or a junkie, a justice or a jail bird. ["But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul\...shall mourn"]{.class_3762} (vs. 22). Clarke's comment is excellent: "The sum of the life of man is this: pain of body and distress of soul; and he is seldom without the one or the other, and often\...both" (p. 74).

Life is not all "hop, skip, and jump." Much of it is "sag, bag, and drag." For those of us who have been very fortunate and have gotten off "easily" with less than our quota of pain and mourning, the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}is a warning: There have crossed this ground, where we walk, literally thousands of people who have hungered, mourned, and suffered for years, and then bled and died in poverty, pain, and misery. God has been more than merciful to many of us; therefore, we should be quick to repent and confess our sins of griping, murmuring, complaining, and ingratitude as soon as they crop up, on [any ]{.class_3759}occasion.

Poor, old Jacob has to get the news "through the grapevine" that his first born son is committing incest with his own wife (Jacob's); then he has to gaze at a bloody piece of a robe which he had made especially for his favorite boy, and he screams: "Oh my God! It's Joe! He's been run over!" (That's right. Don't kid us about this blather of "living Bibles" in the "up-to-date language" of the twentieth century man. You birds who use those translations have never [preached ]{.class_3759}or talked plain once in your life in public. [You'd lose your incomes if you did]{.class_3759}. Go kid the Communists; they'll believe a lie anytime before the truth. Don't try to snow us; we read you out the first time you inserted the news media vocabulary in your sermons instead of the street language.\]

And the next time Jacob turns around, one of his boys is in jail. Haven't some of you been along that road before? There goes a king, reduced to the level of a pauper, as he cries: ["Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! would God I had died for thee, O Absalom, my son, my son!"]{.class_3760} Here is Jephthah, home from the wars, riding a white charger, shouting the victory. And then minutes later it is: "Oh, my God! Not my girl! Not my [little girl!"]{.class_3759}

I find a Christian woman (1964) crying in a mental ward. She is a divorcee. Her oldest son has been released from the chain gang, after serving two years. She wants me to witness to him. I do. He talks about some kind of a "conversion experience" in the "sweat box" where he was confined for three days. I beg him to come out to a Saturday night Bible class. He is on the point of coming when suddenly his "gang" shows up: three cyclists on their "choppers"---black jackets, iron crosses, the works. He looks at them and looks at me and says, "Sorry, preacher, not tonight." In twenty-four hours he is in the County Jail, and in thirty days he has been sentenced to the "Pen" for ten years. He and his "buddies" went out and tried to rob a National Guard Armory. You think that boy's mother didn't shed some tears?

I ate dinner a dozen times (1963--1969) with an unsaved man who was married to a Christian woman. His two young boys were saved. After stepping out on his wife a number of times, he was threatened with divorce. He threatened to kill her. The two boys were caught up in the middle of the trouble. Divorce proceedings went through. He then repented and came forward on a gospel invitation to accept Christ. They tried reconciliation; it worked for about six months and then "back to wallowing in the mire" (2 Pet. 2:22). They broke up. He trailed her. She called the police. He threatened her again over the phone. Finally, he walked back into his house, pulled out a pistol, and shot her twice. He hit her both times, and one shot was in a vital place; but she survived and recovered in the hospital. In the meantime, her former husband faced charges on attempted murder. He was convicted and then sent to Chatahoochee and back to jail. His boys, today, are growing up without a father. Now when their father gets out of jail, he will not be able to get decent employment because of his record, and the years keep right on coming---don't they? Don't you know that family has wet many a pillow with their tears?

Here is Samson's mother and father crying in each other's arms: "The last time we saw our boy he was living in sin with a Negro woman, and we just heard today that he is going to be sacrificed at the feast of Dagon in Gaza!"

Here is Saul's mother weeping her heart out: "My boy and three of my grandsons are missing in action! Oh God, what shall I do?"

I was awakened one night around midnight by a man and his wife. (They were both Christians.) Between the sobs and swallows and moanings, they told me what they wanted me to do: I was to go with them to the wife's mother and tell her that her oldest boy (then in his thirties) had just been killed in a gunfight between him and someone who had been fooling with his estranged wife. That mother got up at midnight and received that news while drinking coffee in her kitchen. The last thing she said to me, with faltering lips and fluttering eyelids, was: "Brother Ruckman, I am so glad that some of your young men stopped by here last year and led him to Christ." But that didn't stop the funeral.

Here, on exhibit, is Judy Garland---five feet tall, one hundred pounds. Between 1966 and 1967 she was a chronic drinker and "pill-popper." She slept between 3:00 and 7:00 in the morning with the lights on and the radio blaring. When she flopped a second "come-back" (1968), she said to her young husband--- twelve years younger than she---"Make me LAUGH, Mickey, make me laugh! I can sing if you make me LAUGH!" Half a year after her marriage (170 days), she was buried in her wedding dress. Twenty-one thousand strangers attended the funeral, but her own children never showed up.

She didn't leave a dime behind; they buried her jewelry with her. The poor kid who married Judy had found her dead in the bathroom with blood running out of her nose and mouth. Do you remember little [Judy?]{.class_3759} The last time I saw Judy she was singing "Somewhere over the rainbow blue birds fly! If birds fly over the rainbow, why then, oh why can't I?" Judy's "wish upon a star" did [not ]{.class_3759}leave the world "far behind her"; neither did her "troubles melt like lemon drops away above the chimney tops." No, Judy died like thousands of celebrities die: alone, lost, depressed, defeated, miserable, sick, and with no more assurance of eternal life than the Catholic priest in London who performed the "sacrament" of her "holy matrimony."

What are [these ]{.class_3759}four hundred women doing, tearing their hair, screaming, praying, cursing, and rolling on the ground? Simple: Herod has just murdered their babies (Matt. 2:16).

Grace Metalious ([Peyton Place]{.class_3759}) died with \$125,000 worth of bad debts, after selling eight million copies of a dirty book at five dollars a copy. Grace married a school teacher when she was seventeen and divorced him to marry a disk jockey. She was drunk in court when her divorce came through. She then left the D.J. and returned to her first husband; then she stepped out on him with a journalist who had five children. When she died, there were twenty-five people at her funeral, and her name was not even mentioned once during the "last rites." Twenty-five people. Such are the paths of worldly honor and glory. Do you think that Grace Metalious didn't spend some wretched hours in her short and dismal life of failure?

Smith Reynolds (of Reynolds Tobacco Company) took his life when he was twenty-one years old, after three marriages and after spending over \$2,000,000---Reynolds had been a multimillionaire. The note he left on the table, before blowing his brains out, said: "I've seen everything, done everything, been everywhere. What the HELL is there that is worth living for?" Well, my poor sinner friend, there is nothing worth living for or dying for apart from the person and work of the Lord Jesus Christ. Reynolds only said (and did) what millions would say and do if they had the guts and the money. Life is not "a bowl of cherries" or a "Mardi Gras."

Policemen on the battery at New York (1870) saw a suicide drowning in the icy waters of December. They threw him a rope. The drowning man took the rope and threw it back across the water about eight feet and said, "To Hell with your rope! I'm done for!" And down he went---for good.

One of my young men was passing out tracts at the State Fair. He gave one to a woe-begone looking character, whose attire looked like he had fallen off the back end of a garbage truck. The pitiful washout looked at my young preacher boy and said: "What the hell do I need that for?" The young man said: "Well, this is to keep you from going to Hell." "I'm not [going ]{.class_3761}to hell," insisted the wretch, and before the young man could show him the truth of the scriptures, the man went on: ["I'm already there."]{.class_3761} (Now, it is retorts like this that led the great superstitious idolaters of American religion and education \[Tillich, James, Pike, Fosdick, Peale, Dewey, Blake, et al.\] to assume that "the only hell you have is hell on earth," or "hell is just in your mind," etc. But this is not the truth. The "hell" you have here is the "earnest of your inheritance" \[Rom. 2:9\], and it is a "foretaste of damnation divine" \[Rom. 2:3--5\], exactly as the indwelling Holy Spirit is the earnest of God's inheritance \[Eph. 1:14\] and a "foretaste of glory divine." Still some folks get more than their share of the "appetizer" before the Lake of Fire receives them to their eternal home.)

I know of an unsaved woman who wouldn't take any water for three weeks after her little girl died under open-heart surgery. Before that mother accompanied the rolling bed into the operating room, the last thing she heard her little girl (eight years old) say was: "Mamma, please give me a drink of water! Mamma, please give me a drink of water!"

That is what the Lord Jesus Christ said the rich man asked for [in hell ]{.class_3759}(Luke 16:24).

And oh, the suffering that God's longsuffering has seen on this earth? And oh the hospital bills and lawyer bills, the doctor bills and court costs, and oh the nights and days of agonized floor walking, and oh the cries and groans of the wounded, the dying, the tortured, and oh the pitiful wails of starving children and the desolate cries of those left without family, friends, funds, hope, or health in this world. And oh, the screams of tortured men and women falling beneath the pincers, the rack, the coals of fire, the whips, pulleys, ropes, knives, scourges, and pieces of broken glass. ["His flesh upon him shall have pain"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22).

And if you think that the combined efforts of the Roman Catholic Church, the Communist International, and the Federation of European States, working in conjunction with the United Nations and the humanoids from outer space---plus the entire body of research of every scientist and philosopher who ever lived---will solve these problems, you are a MADMAN. And you mark it down. Every organization and man listed above has had to use (or is using or will use) [physical and political violence ]{.class_3759}against his "fellow man" to obtain the "[solution ]{.class_3759}for his fellow man." When [any ]{.class_3759}political party (or church)---the Nazis were nothing out of the ordinary---has to use arrests, subversion, subpoenas, force, torture, imprisonment, surgery, brain washing techniques, "mental clinics," or confiscation of goods and property to obtain its objectives, it has already eliminated itself as a qualified authority for obtaining any worthwhile objective it professes to believe in.

Now, we need to come down for a moment from these terrible heights of realism and contemporary truth and confront, once more, the purveyors of "unrealism" and falsehood: The major Bible commentators and revision committees of 1880--1980.

1\. The corrupt English [ASV ]{.class_3759}(the [RV ]{.class_3759}of 1885) has altered verse 14 and 15 to the subjunctive ("would") in order to destroy the doctrinal truth of the prophetic statements "I will answer\...will I wait." The corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 (make out your own list; you know 'em by now!) follows the same course, in spite of the fact that ["tigarah"]{.class_3759} (Hebrew) is a second person, singular, masculine, [Kal ]{.class_3759}FUTURE of the verb ["garah."]{.class_3759} But, as we have said before, the trick at Bob Jones, Pensacola Christian, Maranatha, Pillsbury, etc., is to cover up the truth and overthrow the words of authority. When in doubt, always correct the [ASV ]{.class_3759}and the [New ASV]{.class_3759} with the [AV ]{.class_3759}of 1611.

(And if some of you should run across the cute little ninety minute cassette tapes on the [AV]{.class_3759}, produced by Bob Jones University, you would do well to study the three hours of tapes on the [AV ]{.class_3759}made ten years earlier by the author. As we have stated (a dozen times): Just before any Fundamentalist gets ready to replace the [AV ]{.class_3759}with Christian Education as the final authority, he always "blows kisses at it." The tapes put out by BJU on the [AV ]{.class_3759}are as fine an example of "Post Office" or "Spin the Bottle" (teenage kissing games in the 1930's) in the theological realm as you will ever find. And don't forget that Judas and Joab both kissed their victims before they [murdered ]{.class_3759}them.)

2\. Kenneth Taylor (the ["Living"]{.class_3759}---God rest us!---[Bible]{.class_3759}) has altered verse 14 so that Job's assurance (see [AV ]{.class_3759}on the same verse) becomes only a "hope." He also follows the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 on verse 15, and then removes both "flesh" and "soul" from verse 22.

3\. The [NEB ]{.class_3759}(C. H. Dodd) has altered verse 14 completely and then has eradicated "soul" so that only material things can be dealt with. "Soul" has been translated as "life blood dries up." Why Dodd should suddenly insert "blood" here, (where the word doesn't appear in a single Hebrew manuscript) and then erase it from the New Testament in Matthew 27:3--4; Romans 5:9; Colossians 1:14; and Acts 20:26 is a problem that Dodd will have to iron out with the Author of the Book.

4\. The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(Alexandrinus---written 360 years after the completion of the New Testament) has added ten words to the Hebrew text and then cancelled ["and where is he?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) to replace it with: "and is no more." Typical Alexandrian mush.

The only difficult part of the passage is verse 17, and this is not hard for a believer who knows that God keeps tears in [bottles ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 56:8) and works in [books ]{.class_3759}(Rev. 20:12). Why not ["transgressions"]{.class_3760} in [bags]{.class_3759}? The "bag" today would be a brief case or a document in a sealed envelope, to be opened by the "grand jury," etc. Job's sins are in a legal brief, and it is sewn shut so it cannot be tampered with until the day of reckoning.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1291.xhtml}

CHAPTER 15

15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,

2 Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?

3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?

4 Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.

5 For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.

Eliphaz is exasperated. He was the first man to talk to Job (Chap. 4), and at that time, he figured that he had at least tried to help Job out. But now after hearing Job answer Zophar and Bildad, Eliphaz has lost his "cool," without going through what Job went through, and he "climb's Job's frame" like Tertullian (A.D. 150--220) did when he took the pagans apart, or like Martin Luther (A.D. 1483--1546) when he used to read the riot act to the Popes.

"[Vain knowledge\...east wind\...unprofitable talk\...speeches\...no good\...casteth off fear\...restrainest prayer before God\...iniquity\...crafty"]{.class_3760} (vss. 2--5). Eliphaz has done just about everything short of consigning Job to Hell. The "hot air" of verse 2 is the famous "Sirroco" of the near East, which brings drought (Jonah 4:8) and locusts (Exod. 10:13). In verse 4, Eliphaz accuses Job of forsaking his "fear of God" (Job 1:1 and comments), and also he accuses Job of forsaking prayer meeting night and spending his time on his knees tuning the TV instead of petitioning the throne of grace. ["Thine own]{.class_3762} [mouth condemneth thee, and not I"]{.class_3762} (vs. 6). Well, granted Job had put his foot in his mouth, at least he knew he was going to do it, for he had prophesied beforehand (Job 9:20).

Eliphaz is rubbing it in pretty bad, for Job has been talking about a Perry Mason situation for a good while (see 14:17, 13:26, 13:18); so now, Eliphaz says (in substance): "You're guilty of perjury, Job. You've taken the stand and sworn to tell the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth, and instead you are your own worst witness. Your testimony is against yourself; you're the best witness the prosecution has." (See Jer. 18:17, Ezra 17:10, and 19:12.)

Now, we do not know who the first man was to alter verse 4 to mean that Job was "hurting other people's devotions" and preventing them from praying to God, but this ridiculous HPD flops through the texts of the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_3759}, and other corrupt revisions like a crippled duck. The first writer to have trouble with the text was the writer of the obscene [Septuagint]{.class_3759}, which reads: "accomplished such words" for ["restrainest prayer."]{.class_3760} From here on (150--1900 A.D.), the destructive critics of the Bible hang around the stinking exposition like buzzards on a carcass.

Meredith Kline (an evangelical Conservative) says that Job is undermining "religion," instead of restraining prayer. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Fundamentalists) believe that Job is destroying somebody's devotion to God by what he is saying. The defunct [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901), recommended by Pre millennialists, has written "hinderest devotion" for ["restrainest,"]{.class_3760} and the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}(Rawlinson) says that Job prevented other people from having pious meditations on the "divine goodness" of God (p. 216). Like who?

I said, ["LIKE WHO?"]{.class_3759}

Who has heard Job say anything wrong from the time he was born until his three friends showed up? [Whose devotions ]{.class_3759}was Job "hindering?" His friends weren't conducting "devotions"; they were mauling him. His wife wasn't conducting "devotions," and she hadn't heard Job utter any "vain words." No one else had come to see Job since he was smitten. Where then did these ridiculous expositors, revisors, commentators, theologians, and scholars get this crippled duck from? You figure it out.

This time Adam Clarke has it figured out and figured out right. Job's buddies are accusing Job of getting bitter and angry with God, and when this happens, a man's prayer life (or "devotions") stops. They accuse Job of trying to talk his way through his troubles instead of praying his way through, and if DeWette, Budde, Schlottmann, Dillman, Peake, Meyer, and Gesenius altered the text to avoid that inference, it only proves, for the umpteenth time, that a "hit dog always hollers."

15:7 Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before hills?

8 Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?

9 What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?

10 With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.

11 Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?

12 Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,

13 That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?

14 What is man, that he should be clean? and he which is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

15 Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.

16 How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?

Eliphaz now takes the place of the "accuser of the brethren" (Rev. 12:10), and in his role as "prosecuting attorney," he puts Job on the witness stand and slams thirteen questions at him before Job can answer "yea or nay."

Are you the first man that was born? I thought it was Adam! Where you around here before Genesis 1:1 (see 38:4)? Did God give you special revelation He didn't give us, and are you the only one who knows it? What do you know that we don't? Have you got some secret source of comfort we don't have? What have you got going on that makes you talk the way you do? Seen something? Who are you anyway to be talking about righteousness? You are a man, aren't you? Well, when was a "man" anything when it came to cleanliness or righteousness?

Whatever Eliphaz's problem is, he certainly has a better grasp of the nature of "man" than Jeremy Bentham (1748--1832), Henri Bergson (1859--1941), Edmund Husserl (1859--1938), David Hume (1711--1776), Rene Descartes (1596--1650), A. N. Whitehead (1861--1947), C. S. Pierce (1839--1910), Ernst Mach (1839--1916), William James (1842--1910), or Leo Tolstoy (1828--1910). That is, Eliphaz is at least thirty-three centuries ahead of the best brains of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, which isn't bad yardage.

"Man" is a wreck.

According to the Holy Spirit, "man" is EVIL (Luke 11:13), living in an EVIL world (Gal. 1:4), with an EVIL imagination (Gen. 6:5) and spiritual equipment (Rom. 3:9--18) that couldn't salvage a leaky canoe. And herein lies the major difference in the approach to history by the Bible believing Christian and the "Charismatic Love-In." All unsaved "Christians" approach "man" and history from a positive point of view (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_3759}). When Frank Edwards ([Stronger than Science]{.class_3759}), R. L. Dione ([God Drives a Flying Saucer]{.class_3759}), Eric Norman ([Gods and Devils from Outer Space]{.class_3759}), and Erick Von Daniken ([Chariots of the Gods]{.class_3759}) write on the future, they invariably adopt the anti-Biblical view of Satan in regard to man's capabilities and potentialities and the "help" to come from "humanoids." This view is the positive view. Briefly, it is the view that contact with "life from outer space" will "open up new vistas of research" and "avenues of escape and survival," which will enable men to "live peacefully together in love and\...etc." The Biblical view is that Satan will reign over the Roman Catholic United Nations---all Charismatics included---as "god-incarnate"; and at the end of a seven year period of international slaughter, the Lord Jesus Christ will return and untangle the mess that education, religion, science, and "lovism" have made of things.

Eliphaz knows something about "man" that Schopenhauer (1788--1860), Spencer (1820--1903), Wittgenstein (1889--1951), Montague (1873--1953), and Karl Marx (1818--1883) didn't know. He knew that "man" is filthy (vs. 16), abominable (vs. 16), and unrighteous (vs. 14). Hegel (1770--1831) and Leibnitz (1646--1716) didn't know THAT. And since no one can bring a clean thing out of an unclean one (see 14:4), why would any fool think that the religious, philosophical, scientific, and educational systems produced by these filthy, abominable, unrighteous rascals would be anything but filthy (Isa. 28:8), abominable (Luke 16:15), and unrighteous (Isa. 64:6)? (Of course, some of you don't "like" it. You will never meet a lost politician, scientist, educator, preacher, or priest on God's earth who likes the negative picture of man, since their incomes depend upon hanging up a POSITIVE picture before the public.)

Verse 10 shows that Job's age is well over fifty and probably over seventy. The ["very aged men,"]{.class_3760} much older than his father, would have to be around 170--190 years old. This would allow Job's father to be around 110--130 and Job himself to be around sixty to eighty years old.

"[Are the consolations of God small with thee?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). That is, "Do you despise the good things that God has done for you? Do you count God's comfort as a 'small thing'?" ["Is there any secret thing with thee?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 11; see vs. 8).

Job doesn't answer the harangue---just yet.

"[Why doth thine heart\...thy eyes"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). (See Prov. 6:13 and Psa. 35:19.) The eyes and the heart are always connected (Job 31:7, 27 and comments). A man who allows his heart to "carry him away" (vs. 12) will get into hot water (31:7--9), or on hot coals (Prov. 6:28), for ["he that trusteth in his own heart is a fool"]{.class_3762} (Prov. 28:26). ["And what do thy eyes wink at?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12), in the sense of Proverbs 6:13 and 10:10, which see. (Verse 14 should be compared with 14:2, 4 and 25:4.)

"[Behold he putteth no trust in his saints"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15); so, to help the Roman Catholic Church out (see Jer. 7:4 and Job 5:1), The ["Living"]{.class_3761}---Hail Mary!---[Bible ]{.class_3761}has erased ["saints,"]{.class_3762} the [New English Bible ]{.class_3761}has erased ["saints,"]{.class_3762} the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901) has erased ["saints,"]{.class_3762} the [RSV ]{.class_3761}(1952) has erased ["saints,"]{.class_3762} the [Common Bible ]{.class_3761}(1973) has erased ["saints,"]{.class_3762} and---forget it. The [RV, TEV]{.class_3761}, and [New ASV ]{.class_3761}have all erased the ["saints."]{.class_3762} Boy, don't some of you Bible-believing people wish you could get cooperation like that in backing up the doctrines of your church. Man, there is ecumenicity if you ever saw it. Premillennialists, Catholics, Liberals, Conservatives, Modernists, and Evangelicals all busting their necks to cover-up for Saint Jude, Saint Cyril, Saint Thomas, Saint Patrick, and Saint Augustine. (And don't give us that old bit about "the Hebrew word means 'holy ones'." No, not that old gag again! Not after translating the same word \[Hebrew ["qadashim"]{.class_3761}\] as "saints" in Daniel 7:22 and 7:25 ([ASV]{.class_3761}, 1901). No, don't give us all this jazz about "the Hebrew and "the Greek" when you are just lying like a dog on a throw rug, and you are no more sincere in your desire to produce a "consistent" translation than A. T. Robertson or the Lockman Foundation.)

God does not put any trust in His saints (vs. 15), not even when they are St. Peter, St. James, and St. John (Matt. 16:23 and John 2:25). Men who want you to trust [them ]{.class_3759}instead of the Bible or their [emotional experiences ]{.class_3759}instead of the Bible, can always be spotted (2 Cor. 10:12). They always appeal to some man's recommendation of falsehood or some [emotional fleshy experience ]{.class_3759}instead of dealing with the Biblical FACTS.
