He refused to believe in the Biblical record of salvation, and he put no confidence in Christ's Deity at all. Rosenberg rejected the plan of salvation and refused to listen to anyone praying. But Ribbentrop, Frick, and Keitel were "somethin' else!" In his cell, Ribbentrop accepted the Lord Jesus Christ for the redemption of his soul ([I Was the Nuremberg Jailor]{.class_3759}, Col. Andrus, p. 151), and when he was preparing to be hanged, he said, "I put my trust in Christ." On the gallows, the last thing he said was said to a Lutheran Chaplain (Gerecke): "I'll see you again!" And he will. The [truthless ]{.class_3759}Gauleiter of Poland (Hans Frick) made a confession of faith before he died, and it was as good, or better, than any Pope ever uttered. He insisted that he believed in Jesus Christ as his Saviour and that Christ's blood paid for all his sin. (You couldn't get that confession out of Johnny Carson or Dean Martin for \$1,000,000.)

General Wilhelm Keitel not only confessed Christ constantly, but he read his own Bible in his cell daily and conducted his own "devotions" weeks before he was hung. The prison Chaplain (Gerecke) said that General Keitel had full assurance of salvation "in the blood of Jesus," and not once did he ever doubt his salvation. When they put the noose around his neck, Keitel said: "I call on the Almighty God to have mercy on the German people!" And as the lever was tugged and Keitel's body dropped, he shouted, "DEUTSCHLAND UBER ALLES!" (Alongside Wilhelm Keitel, spiritually speaking, Churchill and Roosevelt wouldn't qualify for the bush league.)

2\. The wicked become old and mighty in power that [they may build for the righteous]{.class_3759}. Those of us who are saved are daily enjoyng the "benefits of science" and the "latest discoveries and inventions" and "improved communications and transportation," although these little progressive tidbits were produced largely by CAINITES (see Gen. 4:22 and comments).

3\. The wicked become mighty to demonstrate God's long suffering. What could prove the character of God better than seeing God put up with what we ourselves could never tolerate? When God tells Moses that He is ["long suffering"]{.class_3760} (see Exod. 34:6 and comments), He has said a "mouthful" that would take 1000 centuries to explain. How much did God "take" off of Heydrich, Mussolini, Himmler, Hess, Goebbels, Ribbentrop, Von Papen, Kurt Franz, and the SS before He racked them out? How much did God take from Tully, Wallenstein, Bishop Bonner, the Jesuits, and the Spanish Inquisitors (1400--1600) before he took them home to the Lake of Fire? How much did God put up with from Lenin, Marx, Trotsky, Stalin, Engels, Darwin, Freud, and Einstein before they hit the judgment as unprepared as the crew of the USS Nevada on the 7th of December, 1941?

If you had seen what God saw in the wake of the cavalry of Genghis Khan or Baibars or Attila. If you had seen what God saw the night the Titanic went down and the day the Lusitania was sunk. If you had seen what God saw when the Crusaders swarmed into Jerusalem and Acre. If you had seen what God looked down on as the Black Death hit Europe. If you had seen what God observed on the Death March from Bataan, or if you could see what God sees this minute, looking down on this sick, Christ-rejecting, Bible-denying, money-mad, sex-crazy, self-righteous, starving, bleeding, suffering, dying creation (Rom. 8:22), YOU would put an end to it today. Not so with the Lord (2 Pet. 3:9). God allows the wicked to become mighty in power (John 19:10) [to show His own long suffering ]{.class_3759}(Acts 13:18).

God has given me many Christian friends around the world, in all kinds of ministries. One of my friends who is a missionary in Fairbanks, Alaska, told me of this case with which he was personally acquainted. He said that in Fairbanks in the 1940's, there was a godless reprobate who spent his time going from saloon to saloon, picking up women, picking fights, and raising "hell" in general. His special talent was going into bar rooms on Wednesday night and conducting mock [prayer ]{.class_3759}and [testimony ]{.class_3759}meetings. He would stand up on a table in the middle of the juke-joint, brandishing a quart or a fifth, and then he would pretend to "lead the congregation in prayer." Gales of laughter would accompany the "prayer." Then he would call for some "testimonies." A dozen or so drunks would always stagger to their feet and "thank God" for their bottle, their lack of morals, their money, or their companion in sin; the "testimonies" were always interspersed with cursing and vile language. The "pastor" on the pool table would then give a brief exhortation before closing his "prayer meeting." It was always an hilarious hour for the night-lifers in Fairbanks, and it went on this way for several years.

Now, what do you suppose finally happened to that character? Do you think that God suddenly struck him down with lightning? Do you think he got arrested and "put away for good" in the penitentiary? Or do you think that he suddenly dropped dead of a heart attack in the middle of a "testimony" meeting?

No, nothing of the kind happened.

No, what happened was, God saved that dirty rascal and filled him with the Holy Spirit and sent him right back into those dives where he had made a mockery out of Christianity and the Bible; and for the next ten years his ministry was passing out tracts in those hellholes. Ah, the long suffering of God that makes ["monuments of mercy"]{.class_3759} out of wicked men.

When I was a lost sinner in the Philippines (1945--1947, 86th Inf. Div., Angeles, Luzon), I once drew a picture of the Last Supper, with all of the disciples drunk. I had the Lord with His eyes "crossed," and wine bottles dumped all over the table. The feet of Peter, James, and John were sticking out from under the table. I showed this picture to some of the GI's in my outfit, and I chuckled over it when I pulled it on them. They didn't chuckle. Some of them didn't think it was funny at all. Even though many of them were just as lost and as hell-bound as I was, they had had some religious training and some Bible background. One of them, trembling, said to me: "Lieutenant, there are some things that just aren't funny!" But I thought it was a riot; one of the best pictures I had ever done. That is, I thought that until I got saved on the 14th of March, 1949.

Three months after I found the Lord Jesus Christ as my own Saviour, I was kneeling in a dry baptistry in Dixon Mills, Alabama. I was there to paint a baptistry for Rev. Roy McCallum of the Central Baptist Church. I closed my eyes and held out my turps, brushes, rags, and paint tubes and asked the Lord to bless them and use my talent for His glory. Kneeling there in my overalls, with my eyes closed, I heard a voice say quietly, "Do you see any reason why I shouldn't take both of your hands off at the wrist, right now?" And with this question there arose before my mind an old picture that I had once done. I'll give you one guess what it was about. I said, "No, Lord. I oughta be in Hell right now, anyway." "All right," said the Lord, "Go ahead and paint." And I've been painting for Him ever since. Ah, the long suffering of God, as He erects monuments of Mercy that testify to His patience and lovingkindness!

4\. The wicked grow old to [make their overthrow more spectacular ]{.class_3759}when it comes. It is a long way from an absolute monarch (Dan. 4) to a yard dog (Dan. 4); it is a good distance from a Roman throne (Matt. 2:16) to the bottomless pit. It is a "far piece" from diamond and gold-studded uniforms (Goering) to the Nuremberg jail. When Hitler, Mussolini, Alexander, and the bigshots fall, they usually fall hard.

5\. They grow and become mighty in power to [show clearly that there must be a future day of adjustment]{.class_3759}. There is no scientific logic in the world that argues for the existence of a "final reckoning" with the Creator any stronger than the fact that all the wrongs do NOT all get "righted" here. If there is any God, there is a day of reckoning (Acts 17:31), and the only way you can avoid that conclusion is to become an Atheist. (They have a "Dial-a-prayer" service for Atheists; you dial a number and nobody answers.)

21:8 Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.

9 Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.

10 Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.

11 They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.

12 They take the timbral and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.

13 They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave.

14 Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.

15 What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?

16 Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

Now Job recites about some of the material blessings which wicked people enjoy, as well as godly people. Many of them have large families (vs. 11), and these children are protected with conveniences and luxuries which many families do not have (vs. 8). ["Their houses are safe from fear"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9), for they have patented burglar alarm systems, built in sprinkling systems, termite control, rat control, and large switch boxes with adequate amperage systems for heavy wiring. Their property is insured for twice its worth; or they live on tax exempt property, and they carry hospital insurance and life insurance on all the members of the family. ["Their seed is established"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8), because they can go to good private schools and stay out of the "asphalt jungles" and "ghettos." The police and the mayor will "go to bat" for their kids if they get in trouble.

"Kids" are from a FLOCK (vs. 11). Their livestock prospers without hoof and mouth disease, scurvy, scabs, etc. (vs. 10). They have enough money (vs. 13) to hire lawyers, pay doctor bills, take care of traffic tickets, and get their children through college so that they can get ["established"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8) in the "establishment." (There are Christians in America who have never worked for a living a day in their lives.) Many of these people live model lives. No one would ever suspect that they are "wicked," for many of them go to church, take the sacraments, pay their bills, and contribute to the Red Cross, Easter Seals, Heart Fund, United Drive, Peace Corps, and the Salvation Army. Their days and nights are filled with dancing, stringed bands, and salon orchestras; and they have music ringing in their ears in the shopping centers and "Malls," and then they go home to hi-fi, tweeter-woofer, eight-track African, acid, jungle, sex music. They rejoice at ["the sound of the organ"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12), since Jubal was an organ man (see Gen. 4:21 and comments), and then ["IN A MOMENT,"]{.class_3762} they go down to the grave (vs. 13).

But it is impossible to discover their wickedness unless one considers verses 14--15. You see, wicked people are not always characterized by immoral lives, lawless trespassing, boot- legging, income tax evasion, extortion, blackmail, drug peddling, or embezzlement. The root of wickedness lies a lot deeper down than that (1 Tim. 6:10). You see, the Bible always "digs a little deeper" than Freudian or Gestalt psychology. In Ezekiel 16:49, for example, we are told that the real trouble with the citizens of Sodom was not "sodomy"---at least not originally. The "original sin" (to quote the Catholic fable) was ["PRIDE,"]{.class_3760} and the next two sins had no direct connection with sodomy at all: they were ["FULLNESS OF BREAD"]{.class_3760} and ["ABUNDANCE OF IDLENESS."]{.class_3760}

Now, the sin of the pre-deluge population, which led eventually to fornication with "strange flesh," was [self-sufficiency:]{.class_3759} ["WHAT is the Almighty, that we should serve him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). Not "WHO" is the Almighty, but ["what"]{.class_3760}! That is, "Is the Almighty somebody important? Is he a king, president, dictator, or WHAT?" ["And what PROFIT should WE have, if we pray unto him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15 cf. Job 22:17). Why pray to an invisible "unknown God" when the "gods" are present (Psa. 82:6, 7) to answer prayers on the spot? These people will not ["always call upon God"]{.class_3760} (27:10); they will only call on Him when He can benefit them commercially or physically. Their religion smacks strangely of that flavor which we find in a whole shelf of "positive thinking" books bottled in the twentieth century style. You "repent" so that society will accept your "[nice ]{.class_3759}personality." You "believe" that God is in the flowers, the mountains, the rain, and the mosquitoes so that when the ecumenical Charismatics swallow you up, you will see God in [everything]{.class_3759}---even Vatican politics. Have "faith" because "faith is operative in the redeemed society," and this can be proved by vacuum cleaner sales made with the "positive approach." You pray because it "relieves tension" and enables some dead saint to raise funds for your hospital bills. But what this bunch actually say, in their HEARTS, is ["Depart from us; for we desire NOT the knowledge of thy ways"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). And nothing could prove this better than the attitude that this generation takes toward the [written or spoken ]{.class_3759}WORDS of God (see Job 23:12 and Acts 20:27). God's "ways" are revealed in His Book; so, those today, who hold His truth ["in unrighteousness"]{.class_3760} (Rom. 1:18) and pervert it to match their "own ways" (Rom. 1:21), could join the Anti-Noah Club without a credit reference.

Where God's ways call for [separation ]{.class_3759}(2 Cor. 6:14), [segregation ]{.class_3759}(Acts 17:26--27), and [isolation ]{.class_3759}(Isa. 5:8) from the Roman Catholic Whore of Revelation 17 and 18 (Rev. 18:4), the Full-Gospel Charismatics simply substitute a counterfeit "sign" for "the knowledge of God's ways." The trouble is always "heart trouble." The trouble is that the pre-deluge sinners didn't want God interfering with their card parties, their country club dinners, their United Drives, their building funds, their school curriculum, their shopping lists, their real estate deals, their home entertainment, and their ecumenical "worship services" which they designed to pad a falling attendance, making up the deficit by stuffing the church with unsaved pagans. From those sins comes the list given in Romans 1:29--32.

In this present world system (33 A.D. to A.D. 1999), ["the love of MONEY is the ROOT of all evil"]{.class_3760} (1 Tim. 6:10), and the alteration of that text to the reading found in the [NASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition) and the [RSV ]{.class_3759}(any edition) is the surest proof in the world that the roots of sin are always deeper than the basement. It is [financial income ]{.class_3759}that causes "revision committees" to try their hand at selling "bibles." It is [income ]{.class_3759}that causes non- denominational institutions to compromise Bible doctrines. It is [income ]{.class_3759}that prevents the truth from being preached over television (barring the bare skeleton framework of John 3:16 and Romans 12:12). It is income which produces the street people, the drug culture, the X-rated movies, the liquor traffic, SIECUS literature, and the political applications of the teachings of Karl Marx. (And don't give us this gas about these revision committees not making any money. They were elevated and established as "qualified authorities" by their own perversions of God's word, and that [did ]{.class_3759}affect their incomes. Don't kid us, girlies; some of us are pruning trees, mending nets, cleaning out septic tanks, digging ditches, hauling rocks, and tilling dirt while you're trimming your fingernails and getting your hair "done.")

The reaction of the modern Conservative or Evangelical scholar to the Bible, these days, is identical with the reaction of the populace of Jerusalem before God wiped them out:[ "As for the word that thou hast spoken unto us in the name of the Lord, we will not hearken unto thee"]{.class_3760} (Jer. 44:16). That is exactly how the population of Noah's day responded to his messages, preached off the bow of his ark.

"[Lo, their good is not in their hand"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16). You are not the author of your goodness; God is (Gal. 5:22), if you have any at all (Rom. 3:12). And further, the good things of verses 8--12 come from God (James 1:17), and Job knows this. His reasoning, then, is: "If God gives wicked men all these good things and they take credit for it themselves, should I follow this example? No, for ["the counsel of the wicked is far from me"]{.class_3762} (vs. 16). Job is not guilty of telling God to depart from him (Job 21:14) for the same reasons that the wicked tell God to depart from them. If Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar think this, Job is about to straighten them out in no uncertain terms, for he can read their thoughts (21:17).

21:17 How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and how oft cometh their destruction upon them! God distributeth sorrows in his anger.

18 They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away.

19 God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it.

20 His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

21 For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?

22 Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.

23 One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.

24 His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow.

25 And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.

26 They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.

Verses 17 through 21 refer again to the Antichrist (see 20:5--29 and comments). God is angry with the wicked (vs. 17) every day (John 3:36). The Antichrist and his crowd [will ]{.class_3759}be blown away like stubble and chaff (Dan. 2:35), and a storm (Job 21:18) [will ]{.class_3759}accompany that Advent (Rev. 16:21; Psa. 66:7--8). God [does ]{.class_3759}lay up iniquity for Satan's children (Rom. 2:5), and He [will ]{.class_3759}reward the Antichrist and Satan to their faces, and they will know it (Rev. 19:20). He shall ["drink of the wrath of the Almighty"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20) at the Second Advent (Rev. 6:17) and in Eternity (Rev. 20:10). The number of his months shall be ["cut off in the midst"]{.class_3760} (vs. 21), since these days are numbered (Rev. 12:12) with variables (Dan. 12:11--12) so that only God knows the exact number (Matt. 24:36). (Don't follow Ellen G. White halfway down the block on those numbers; she had no more idea what she was talking about than Pastor Russell or Judge Rutherford.)

And from here, Job passes on to the idea that good people die as well as bad people (vss. 23, 25), and young ones as well as old ones (vs. 24). Since God judges "those that are high" (vs. 22), He doesn't make any mistakes about death, even where a variety of deaths would lead one to think that there is no plan or purpose in life. Some of ["those that are high"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22) are between 300,000 to 20,000,000 miles higher than kings and presidents (Isa. 24:21), and they are called "principalities and powers" (Eph. 6:12) under ["the god of this world"]{.class_3760} (2 Cor. 4:4). The ones referred to here are the mighty of this earth.

When Job comes to verse 24, the Scholars Union comes apart at the seams again, and buttons, zippers, stitches, patches, and cloth go all over the room. But before watching another one of these pitiful disintegrations of common sense, logic, rational power, and literary power, let us note that verse 24 is as plainly FIGURATIVE as any figurative statement in the [Book of Job.]{.class_3759}

Now, the thinking of those who take literal passages [figuratively ]{.class_3759}(Mark 7:17--22) and figurative passages [literally ]{.class_3759}(Matt. 16:8, 26:28)---Catholics foremost---is that literal passages which deal with the Antichrist, Hell, the Lake of Fire, judgment, sin, death, and Israel are to be taken [figuratively ]{.class_3759}(or be "spiritualized") because they cross the grain of "positive Christianity." Then figurative passages are to be altered if they cannot be taken literally; that is, anything but the text, anything but the Bible.

(The school which originated this system of Bible perversion was the philosophical academy founded in Alexandria by Pantaenus, Clement, and Origen. It produced [Vaticanus ]{.class_3759}and [Sinaiticus, the manuscripts recommended by Dr. Custer ]{.class_3759}\[Bob Jones University\] for destroying the [Holy Bible]{.class_3759}.)

Let the reader note for himself some of the figurative references in Job which cannot be taken [literally]{.class_3759}, nor were they intended to be taken literally: Job just pulls them out (Job 19:12). If they were armed men [standing there]{.class_3759}, how come nobody else saw them? Job 20:13. Is wickedness (per se) something you can chew and swallow? Then why did the Lord Jesus Christ say it wasn't so (see Mark 7:15--23)? Job 20:16. Would anyone suck out snake's poison? And if they did (literally), would it [kill ]{.class_3759}them? Job 20:24. Does the [bow ]{.class_3759}strike Job through or the [arrow ]{.class_3759}that is shot from it? Job 28:2. Melted stones (literal stones) don't make [brass ]{.class_3759}([literal ]{.class_3759}brass). See also Job 29:3, 29:6, 29:17, and 30:22, etc.

The serious student of the Scripture, who knows that the "inspired original text" is certainly NOT the "final court of appeals" for anyone today but a lunatic-fringe nut, will notice that this is the approach used by skeptics and infidels to deny a literal Hell, a literal Heaven, a literal Incarnation, a literal resurrection, and a literal judgment. The line, therefore, between correct and incorrect exposition on scores of passages is so close that no amount of education or "training" in the "original languages" will ever enable a sinner to figure the right way, apart from [belief in the word of God ]{.class_3759}and reliance on the AUTHOR of the word of God.

Job 21:24 is so plainly a [figurative ]{.class_3759}reference to someone dying in good health, physically, that really no explanation should ever have had to be made in regard to the verse. But the maddening fanaticism of the Conservative "nuts" could not let the verse rest with a figurative application. Insisting that the [AV ]{.class_3759}translators were either stupid or else liars, the "qualified ethics" (any 40,000 of them) go to work tearing up the Bible like a pack of pack rats chewing up a newspaper.

1\. The Jehovah's Witnesses ([New World Translation]{.class_3759}) say that the man's "thighs are full of fat."

2\. The [Amplified "Bible"]{.class_3759} (Lockman Foundation) says "his pails are full of milk."

3\. The depraved [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) says "his [pails ]{.class_3759}are full of milk" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown go by this reading).

4\. The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---mercy, Lord!---[Bible ]{.class_3759}says "those who are\...fat."

5\. Dummelow says it is "milk[ pails."]{.class_3759}

6\. The [Vulgate ]{.class_3759}says "his intestines are full of fat."

7\. The [LXX ]{.class_3759}says "his intestines are full of fat" (Ditto Mr. Parkhurst).

8\. The [RV ]{.class_3759}(1885) says "HER breasts are\...etc."

9\. Umbreit has "stations or resting places of his [herds."]{.class_3759}

10\. Gesenius says it is "his [cattle ]{.class_3759}abound with milk."

11\. Kline and Heavenor, sensing the difficulty, omit any reference to the passage.

Now, what do you suppose brought on this mighty defection from the truth when there are scores of figurative passages in the Bible and no particular training or intelligence is required to spot them? The fire in Hell is plainly LITERAL (Matt. 13:40), and the "mass" is plainly [figurative ]{.class_3759}(1 Cor. 10:16--17) by Christ's own definitive use of terms (John 6:57). How then does some screwball get a passage like Job 21:24 so screwed up that it is unscrewable, when any corkscrew could see that the passage was figurative? "Milk pails?" What kind of accuracy is that? I thought that [bottles ]{.class_3759}were what they carried milk in (Judg. 4:19). Remember the "skin bottles" (Luke 5:37) everyone had such a fit over? Isn't that what Marshall raised all the stink about when he put the [King James ]{.class_3759}text alongside Nestle's text so that Nestle could keep on selling after God ditched him? Yes, that was the stink. And now you educated asses (yes, we know, you "object to to the language" because you are a born liar to start with) who went off the deep end in 1880 (and never got back up the bank) are going to tell us that it is "pails," are you? "His PAILS are full of milk"---referring to his[ cow's]{.class_3759} produce--- and "his bones are moistened with marrow"---referring to a [man's]{.class_3759} physical body? Aren't you the same bunch of twerps who altered the [Book of Proverbs ]{.class_3759}clear through [because the two poetic clauses didn't match? ]{.class_3759}Yes, there is no doubt about it! ["Ye are the people."]{.class_3760} We saw your HPD in Proverbs 18:12; 21:20; 28:11, etc., and no doubt ["wisdom shall die with you."]{.class_3760} And while you're at it---trying to force a literal interpretation into the passage---will you "recognized asses" explain why you didn't retranslate ["bones are moistened with marrow" ]{.class_3760}to "bones have marrow IN THEM"? Does ["bitterness of\...soul"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24) match ["pails]{.class_3759} full of milk" (vs. 24)? Then why not change that too? You won't do it? Kenneth Taylor did.

No, a thousand times no! Before we will follow you blind, lying, inconsistent, hypothetical "Christian" meddlers, we will go back to Moscow or Rome; at least they can enforce their authority with arrest, torture, and weapons. Verse 24 is clearly a [figurative ]{.class_3759}passage exactly as Psalms 75:10, 96:12, 107:26--28, 69:9, 73:9, 130:8, etc., and the thing is so common in Hebrew poetry (see Psa. 135:9, 137:5; Prov. 9:1, 22:14, 30:25, etc.; Eccl. 10:2; Isa. 34:3) that only a blind fool would have tampered with Job 21:24 to start with. Do you think that all of the smart people in Florida have their hearts on the [right side ]{.class_3759}of their chests? Well, that is what Ecclesiastes 10:2 says, and if you would leave [that ]{.class_3759}verse as it stands---and every critic of Job 21:24 left it that way---what in the name of "scientific research" are you doing trying to literalize Job 21:24?

The [Authorized Text ]{.class_3759}is right, and the day you get smart enough to alter it (like Stewart Custer and Robert Sumner) will be the day that your teaching and preaching will lose the fresh anointing oil of God's Holy Spirit and take on the professional whine of a Madison Avenue adman. When Custer, Neal, Gothard, Bob Jones, II, Machen, Davis, Warfield, Zodhiates, Thieme, and Wuest correct the [Authorized Text]{.class_3759}, anyone with any spiritual discernment can feel the breath of Death breezing over the audience with its killing blast withering the green stalk and the ripe fruit. [Death ministries ]{.class_3759}are quite common in America today, and they have nothing to do with the death life (Rom. 6) of the crucified saint (Rom. 7); they have to do with the fleshly flesh of the educated apostate lying, swearing, cheating, and killing to make Christian education the "final court of appeal." This is done by holding up as authoritative an UNAVAILABLE, unknowable, unread, and unseen "original." Since no one can check it to see if the scholar is lying, [he lies his way through]{.class_3759}, using [Vaticanus ]{.class_3759}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_3759}(Nestle's and Metzger's texts) to correct God Almighty.

21:27 Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices which ye wrongfully imagine against me.

28 For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?

29 Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,

30 That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath.

31 Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?

32 Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.

33 The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.

34 How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?

Again Job makes reference, doctrinally, to the Antichrist (vss. 28--33). He has proved (at least to himself) that anybody can drop in their tracks in any kind of condition (vss. 23, 25). Worms and dust (vs. 26), which are Job's present lot (17:14), do not prove that he is godless. ["Ye say, where is the house of the prince\...the wicked?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28). You say, "Well, Job, if you're a bigshot where is your house? We know you are wicked because your family and your dwelling place is torn all to smithereens." ["Have ye not asked them that go by the way"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29)? Of course they have, for Eliphaz (Job 15) and Bildad (Job 18) had just stated the course, destiny, and end of the wicked. But Job has something more in mind than Eliphaz and Bildad. Job says that the tokens of the wicked show that ["the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30), and he will be ["brought forth to the DAY OF WRATH"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30). This matches 2 Peter 3:4; 2 Peter 3:7; and Revelation 20:11--14 exactly. So, this is where Satan will be paid off (Matt. 25:41). Job's logic is sound: "Even if I was a wicked prince (vs. 28), like the Antichrist will be (Ezek. 21:25), my house might still be standing and my dwelling place might still be as well kept as the Taj Mahal." The homes and houses of the wicked are NOT always destroyed in this life (vs. 9), for the wicked have a reserved appointment later. God will not always do verse 17 to a wicked man in this life; sometimes his lot is verse 23. Appearances are deceptive.

"[Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 31). Observe how the Antichrist is spoken of as ["HIM"]{.class_3762} (vs. 31) and then ["THEY"]{.class_3762} (vs. 30) in the same breath, as in Job 40:15 (["Behemoth"]{.class_3762}). However, not all of the applications fit, for the Antichrist is cast [alive ]{.class_3761}into a Lake of Fire (Rev. 19:20), and he does [not ]{.class_3761}["remain in the tomb"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32). Job's questions (vs. 31) imply that no one in this life is able to recompense the correct measure of justice to the wicked man. Man is unable to comprehend the holiness of God in these matters; so man will always tend to "tone down" or dilute judgment against sin. For example, no man on earth in his right mind would sentence a man to death for stealing [one piece of fruit ]{.class_3761}from an orchard, but Adam was not only sentenced to death for this transgression, [his entire seed was sentenced ]{.class_3761}(Rom. 5). No one but God, at the "day of wrath," is really able to give the wicked their "just desserts" (Rom. 2:5--6).

"[Yet"]{.class_3760} even though judgment is delayed, ["he shall be brought to the grave"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32). No one gets out of that judgment (Ecc. 12:14), wicked or righteous. ["Every man shall draw after him"]{.class_3762} (vs. 33), wicked or righteous; and innumerable men before him (vs. 33) went to the same place, wicked or righteous (Ecc. 9:2, 11). ["How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 34). "How do you figure you can comfort me by lying about my condition? My present condition cannot be blamed on my wickedness, for what happened to me could have happened to anyone." And all of this is true, but for a [different reason]{.class_3761}. The Christian suffers to [make him sympathetic ]{.class_3761}(2 Cor. 1:1--4), to [make heaven more real to him ]{.class_3761}(Col. 3:1--3), [to make him more fruitful ]{.class_3761}(John 15:1--6), to keep him humble (2 Cor. 12:5--7), and to [show him that God's grace is sufficient ]{.class_3761}(Phil. 4:13). On the other hand, the unsaved man suffers for one reason and one reason only (Rom. 2:1--5): [God doesn't want him to suffer in Hell;]{.class_3761} so, what he goes through, he goes through to open his eyes to the fact that his righteousness cannot save him and that he needs God's free justification and salvation. Job is learning that although personal righteousness and moral integrity are great things, you cannot count on either of them when you get into a contest on "motives" between God and the Devil (Rom. 10:1--4). That is, he is learning something no Catholic Pope or Campbellite "elder" ever found out.

1\. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(Lockman Foundation) has messed up verses 12, 19, and 24.

2\. Kenneth Taylor (the Dead Bible) has messed up verses 12, 22, 31--32.

3\. The Jehovah's Witness Bible ([NWT]{.class_3759}) has messed up verses 9, 11, 13, 24, and 33.

4\. The [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition, same garbage) has messed up verses 19, 21, 24, and 32.

And it is a "crying shame" to waste paper, as expensive as it is these days, to list the endless perversions of the [Authorized Text ]{.class_3759}by these "qualified" change agents who could put the NKVD and the Gestapo to shame. Everything was perfectly clear in the English text. No one can teach God anything (vs. 22), and Paul affirms this to be so (Rom. 11:34). Those who ["go by the way" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 29) travel around the world and thus accumulate knowledge as "traveling salesmen." They know the day of reckoning is coming and God has set this truth instinctively in the heart and soul of every man born on this earth (John 1:9). All the evidence collected on this earth from every race, tribe, nation, and tongue shows that there is going to be a final Judgment somewhere. Verse 33 is figurative again, even though Taylor copies Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (p. 54) for his conjecture. The "sweet clods" and the "sweet worms" (Job 24:20) are to be understood within the context of Job's sufferings, for it is Job who uses both of these expressions. Both times the statements are made in hard and bitter irony, so the alteration of either of them (see how Origen and Eusebius messed them up in the [Septuagint]{.class_3759}) only reveals the scholar for what he is: He is an inexperienced novice with little or no knowledge of normal men or normal men's reactions to suffering.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1856.xhtml}

CHAPTER 22

22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

2 Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?

3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou are righteous? or is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?

4 Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?

5 Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?

Here comes Eliphaz around the track for the third time. He immediately classifies Job with the wicked people of Job 21:15 who were only interested in profit, for Eliphaz says, ["Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). That is, if you do use good judgment and do exercise wisdom and prudence in matters, it may profit you some (Ecc. 5:18--20), but what does this do for God? Does it increase His bank deposits? (Notice this refrain with Elihu in Job 34:9 and 35:7--8.)

"[Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). Well, God wants us to be that way, but He Himself has to arrange it (Rom. 3:20--26) for ["all OUR righteousnesses are as FILTHY RAGS"]{.class_3762} (Isa. 64:6). ["Is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). Well, not in the sense of material or physical gain, as men look at "profit." However, when faced with the Adversary (Deut. 4:5; Prov. 27:11), the Lord God is always more pleased when He can brag about us (1 Cor. 4:5) like he did Job (Job 1:8), than when He has to agree (2 Sam. 12:7) with the Adversary and Accuser (1 Cor. 10:6--12).

"[Will he reprove thee for fear of thee?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). Do you think God stays awake at night worrying about what you will do if He doesn't stop you with a rebuke or a reproof? ["Will he enter with thee into judgment?" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 4). This is what Job had asked for (13:18, 22--23) but what about Psalm 143:2? And what about Romans 2:16? ["Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). No, they weren't; however, any man's iniquities are "infinite" in the sense that his sins are committed against an infinite God, but this is not how Eliphaz is using the term. Eliphaz is using the term like the Pharisees used the term in John 9:24 and Luke 7:39. This is very evident in the charges which follow.

22:6 For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

7 Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.

8 But as for the mighty man, he had the earth; and the honourable man dwelt in it.

9 Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.

10 Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;

11 Or darkness, that thou canst not see; and abundance of waters cover thee.

Now, if anything was ever said about Job that was NOT true, it was this. Job was suffering for righteousness' sake and "well doing" (1 Pet. 3:14--17), and ["all manner of evil"]{.class_3760} was being said falsely against him (Matt. 5:11). Job had NOT taken anyone's ["pledge"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6) nor had he stripped anyone naked (vs. 6), for when God rebukes Job, not once does He charge Job with any of these sins (Job 31:13--21). Job had given water to the weary (vs. 7), and he [had ]{.class_3759}given bread to the hungry (vs. 7), as he so states the matter in Job 31:20. Obeying the Tribulation instructions of Matthew 25:35--38 and James 2:1--16 for Daniel's Seventieth Week, Job has lived a moral life above reproach, at least in the eyes of the community. He had never sent any widows away empty (vs. 9), nor had he broken the ["arms of the fatherless"]{.class_3760} (31:21) a day of his life. When Eliphaz says, ["THEREFORE snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10), he is lying like a dead flounder on cement. Job's immersion beneath God's wrath (vs. 11), like the Psalmist and Jonah (Psa. 69; Jonah 2:3), was not due to overt acts of wickedness, and God Himself testified to this on Job's behalf (Job 2:3).

Eliphaz has taken Satan's place as the false accuser and adversary, and when we accuse our brethren of overt acts of sin on the basis of rumour, hearsay, conjecture, Catholic tradition, or just downright meanness, we temporarily split the hoof (Ezek. 1:7, 28:14) and tune our pipes and tabrets (Ezek. 28:13) to Danse Macabre. When Elmer Rumminger (BJU) writes letters that "Ruckman is an enemy of Bob Jones University" after "Ruckman" has just [recommended a dozen students to go there]{.class_3759} (1970--1975) and then writes that "Ruckman" is [slandering ]{.class_3759}the position of BJU on "inspiration," and that he is teaching that the King James Bible is the "ORIGINAL MANUSCRIPTS" (see correspondence, 1975), he is simply lying and libeling like a dirty, two-faced Gehazi (2 Kings 5:25). Grown men who lie like that ought to be ashamed of themselves. The very idea of the "head of a Bible department" in a "bastion of orthodoxy" slandering the "brethren" that way. Tsk, tsk, tsk. We have never "misrepresented" BJU's position on the Bible. Their "position" is the position of John R. Rice, Guassen, Culbertson, Smith, Torrey, Machen, Davis, Trench, Vincent, Robertson, Thieme, Zodhiates, Westcott, Hort, etc. "Once upon a time God wrote a book [which He couldn't keep]{.class_3759}, so nobody has it. In the meantime, we will correct the [AV ]{.class_3759}of the Protestant reformation with the [Roman Catholic Greek text ]{.class_3759}of the Jesuit priests (1582)." If you don't believe it, [go talk to any man I just listed]{.class_3759}. Don't accuse [me ]{.class_3759}of slander you dirty, God-robbing liar. Every man in the list corrects the [AV ]{.class_3759}with the Greek text of [Vaticanus ]{.class_3759}and [Siniaticus]{.class_3759}. This is the Greek New Testament text of the Jesuit Rheims Bible of the Roman Catholic church (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_3759}), and no scholar, living or dead, has been able to prove otherwise, nor will he. There is "slander," and then there is "slander." The adversary is a "FALSE" accuser (John 8:40--44), and when he waves a Bible over his head and hollers, "If [that ]{.class_3759}Book isn't true (waving an [AV ]{.class_3759}in the air), we have nothing to preach to any sinner anywhere!" He will turn right about and say, "There is LITTLE room for mistakes in [the English\...but I can be sure that EVERY WORD IS ACCURATE]{.class_3759}," and then scream, "My father believed [it!]{.class_3759} My mother believed [it!]{.class_3759} I believe it, thank God!" And then quickly add, "ANY man who says there are mistakes in the Bible\...means it is NOT the word of God." And then, even more quickly: "But Acts 8:37 shouldn't be there, and Revelation 22:14 is wrong, and\...etc." And finally: "The BIBLE IS the very word of God\...[the VERY WORDS OF GOD"]{.class_3759} ([Sword of the Lord]{.class_3759}, Oct. 10, 1975). Sure, sonny; not all the [nuts ]{.class_3759}are in the trees or on the ground. Sure, the Bible "IS the word of God" and "Is the very words of God," which your "mother and father" believed, but it was NOT [really ]{.class_3759}the word of God because momma and daddy didn't have the "originals," and only the "originals" are the [very WORDS of God!]{.class_3759} Right? The Bible you are quoting is NOT the word of God nor is it the very words of God, but the Bible you are quoting is the one your momma and daddy read, and BELIEVED. ("NUTS", eh? Well, some of us squirrels know a real "nut" when we see one.)

22:12 Is not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars, how high they are!

13 And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?

14 Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.

15 Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?

16 Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood:

17 Which said unto God, Depart from us: and what can the Almighty do for them?

18 Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

19 The righteous see it, and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.

20 Whereas our substance is not cut down, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.

Eliphaz continues to slander Job, classifying him with people who think that God doesn't know what they are doing (vs. 13), and men who don't believe in God, unless it is "pragmatic" (vs. 17). That is, he classifies Job with Dewey, Russell, Epicurus, Sartre, Heidegger, Einstein, and the International Socialists who control the American educational system.

God is in the ["height of heaven"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) while the height and depth of His universe (Eph. 3:18 and comments) staggers the imagination of anyone who has consulted astronomy. ["The high and holy place"]{.class_3760} of Isaiah 57:15 is far beyond Alpha Draconis (Psa. 75:6--7), and God has made certain that no unsaved sinner will ever fly a rocket or shoot one (or anything like one) within 2,000,000 light years of the ["sea of glass"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 4:1--6). ["The height of the stars"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) will just about blow your mind if you give it much attention. The nearest star (Proxima Centauri) is just a hop over the way---25,000,000,000,000 miles. If you traveled at the speed of light for twenty-four hours a day, 365 days a year, it would take you four years and four months to get there. The most distant star in our galaxy could be reached if you traveled toward it at the speed of light (in a vacuum it is about 6,000,000,000,000 miles a year) for 75,000 years. In plainer words, if you and your family went 200 times faster than the fastest flying saucer ever seen or tracked, your great, great, grandchildren would die of old age before you got one tenth of the way there. But the trouble is, that little "jaunt" you took was only to the most distant star of OUR galaxy. We forgot to tell you that there are more than a million other galaxies. The remotest heavenly body visible to the naked eye is the great Galaxy of Andromeda. It is just a short trip of 13,000,000,000,000,000,000 miles. If you traveled at 670,000,000 miles per hour (much faster than the speed of light), your great, great, great, great grandchildren would all die of old age before they got [one quarter ]{.class_3759}of the distance there. It would be sort of like an ant traveling from here to the sun at the rate of one inch a day. (What a drag, man, what a drag!)

"[The dark cloud"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13) and the ["thick clouds"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14) refer to more than the clouds that are like Acts 1:9 and Matthew 17:5 (see Job 37:15--22 and 26:7--10). ["The circuit of heaven"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14) also refers to more than the "ancient Hebrew concept of the domed vault with the blah, blah, blah" in it (see any Liberal commentator in the [Interpretor's Bible]{.class_3761}). Job had marked ["the old way which wicked men"]{.class_3762} (vs. 15) had trodden, and he had just commented on it in Job 21:8--14. As we have stated (in this commentary), the flood of Noah's day is one of the main topics of conversation in the times of Job, Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar. ["Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood"]{.class_3762} (vs. 16) plainly ties Job 22:17 in with 21:14. In the days of Jabal, Jubal, and Tubalcain (Gen. 4:20 and comments), people lived "high on the hog" and "low on the chicken" (vs. 18), but they did not appreciate it (vs. 17).

Now Eliphaz has classified Job with this crowd. He says, ["OUR substance is not cut down"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20), which carries with it the obvious, "BUT YOURS IS!" ["The remnant off them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20) refers to the remnant of the wicked (vs. 18); the innocent and the righteous (vs. 19) are said to have laughed them to scorn (vs. 19). Notice how this last statement matches Proverbs 1:26; Psalm 2:4, and 50:2. The second clause in verse 18 is a sarcastic quoting of Job's own words in Job 21:16, and Eliphaz's purpose is clear; he is saying: "Who are you to go around claiming that the counsel of the wicked is not with you, when the same thing is happening to you that happened to the other people who told God to depart from them?" (See Job's unfortunate statement in 7:19.)

All is clear. Job is a dirty so-and-so, just like his wife said (2:9), and if the judgments of God cover him like the abundance of waters (vs. 11) covered the Ante-diluvians (vs. 16), it would serve him right. The ["mighty man"]{.class_3760} and the ["honourable man"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8) is probably a reference to the great hunter Nimrod, for the ["honourable"]{.class_3760} spoken of here is the kind of honor that ["cometh from God only"]{.class_3760} (John 5:44). Job, therefore, has been likened to the second type of Antichrist in the Bible, the first one being Cain (see [The Mark of the Beast, ]{.class_3759}1959).

22:22 Receive, I pray thee, the law from his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart.

23 If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.

24 Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.

25 Yea, the Almighty shall be thy defence, and thou shalt have plenty of silver.

26 For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up they face unto God.

27 Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.

28 Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.

29 When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.

30 He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.

The first verse in the section would be compared with Psalm 119:165, and especially Job 34:29. Everything that Eliphaz says in verses 23--30 is true, but, unfortunately, Job cannot return to the Lord (vs. 23) anymore than he already has, and he is already as well acquainted with the Lord (vs. 21) as any of his friends. The laws and words which God had given Job (vs. 22) were laid up in his heart (1:1), and he had put iniquity far from his tabernacles (vs. 23); so, really Eliphaz's advice goes by like a sandstorm on a cold day.

All of the passage is true, doctrinally, of Israel; they do return (vs. 23) to the Almighty during the Tribulation (Deut. 30:1--2); they do put away iniquity (vs. 23) at this time (Heb. 8:10; Jer. 31:34), and consequently they are delivered (vs. 30) by God (Joel 2:32), and the ["light"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28) will shine on them (Isa. 2:5, 10:17, 58:8, 10, 60:1, 3, etc.) when they ["lay up gold as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24). You see, the reign of Solomon (1 Kings 10:1--10) is a type of the Millennial reign (1 Kings 4:1) of Jesus Christ. That is, if you cannot find forty prophetic references in the Book of Job by the time you are halfway through it, you are not a "serious student" of the Bible, and don't ever try to kid any of us who are "seriously" have no more regard for the guesses of Neal, Keil, Vincent, Wuest, Payne, Custer, Anderson, Edesheim, Ladd, Bruce, Schaff, Ramm, Berkouwer, Kittel, Scholer, and Hoekema than they do for the ethical principles of UNICEF or UNESCO. A conservative, destructive critic who has gone bugs on his own learning and education is just as disastrous to correct Biblical interpretation as the worst Liberal or Modernist who ever lived.

Verse 26 is a repeat of Job 11:15. ["Ophir"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24) is a word derived from "gold dust." The natives of Malacca still call their gold mines "Ophirs" (see 1 Kings 10:11). Verses 26-28 are self-explanatory, and verse 27 is an obvious reference to Job's restoration as a judge (Job 29:7). The frantic attempts of the Scholars Union (see below) to write their own Bible at this point is, again, proof that none of them are "serious" Bible students. Verse 30 throws the scholars into even worse confusion. In spite of the fact that the same word (["island"]{.class_3760}) has been translated as "island" in the corrupt [ASV]{.class_3759} (1901) a score of times---see Isa. 11:11, 20:6, 23:2, 6, 41:1, 5, 42:4, 10, 12, 15; Ezek. 27:3, 6--7, 15, etc.---they have changed it here to "him," and then said that the "him" is NOT "innocent." (That is, they simply make a liar out of God Almighty and tell Him that He actually said [exactly what He did NOT say.]{.class_3759})

1\. The Jehovah's Witness Bible ([NWT]{.class_3759}) has written verse 2 as: "Can an able bodied man be of use to God himself, that anyone having insight should be of use toward him?" Then they have butchered verses 8 and 16 beyond all recognition, getting rid of the deluge references as neatly as the [New Scofield Bible]{.class_3759} got rid of the ordinance of Baptism in Acts chapter 8 (see footnote on page 1175 for this remarkable Roman Catholic alteration of the "Scofield" notes). Verse 29 has been so distorted you would not even connect it with the Bible if you saw the two laid side by side.

2\. The [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759} (Lockman Foundation, 1965) has altered the last two verses (29--30) to match the blind conjectures of Rawlinson, who said (nearly eighty years ago!): "aleph yod, in this place, is for alelph yod nun, as in 1 Samuel 4:21. The meaning [seems]{.class_3759} to be\..." (p. 373). Sure, doc, the "meaning SEEMS to be" whatever you and your blundering playmates want it to mean. We get the message; they got it in the Dark Ages, too, [every day]{.class_3759} (A.D. 600--1400).

3\. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759} tells us that verse 16 is not a reference to the deluge; that verse 25 should be changed to "thy treasure" so that it will match a change already made in vese 24 ("lay thy treasure in the dust"), and vese 15 and 25 should be changed to match the [RV]{.class_3759} of 1885.

4\. Dummelow says ["pleasure"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3) should be "advantage," and that verses 15, 20, 23--24, and 30 are all wrong in the [AV]{.class_3759} and should be adjusted by anywhere from five to ten changes. Dummelow refuses comment on the flood (vs. 16), and then alters verse 30 to a reading that will look like prophecy fulfilled in Job 42:8--9).

5\. The phony [LXX]{.class_3759} has written, "thy light has become darkness" for verse 11, and "thou hast established those that were already settled on the earth" (vs. 8), plus "the light has proved darkness to thee" (vs. 11), plus "has he not brought down the proud" (vs. 12), instead of the Biblical texts.

The additional corrections made on the Holy Scripture by Clarke, Lange, Williams, Bullinger, Peake, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Meyer, Wright, Ewald, and Dillmann are too many to mention. Adam Clarke and Kenneth Taylor have freely invented their own "bibles" for verses 29--30, but Taylor did not go as far as the corrupt [ASV]{.class_3759} of 1901, which has inserted a "not" into verse 30.

(In a recent, Methodist publication, some featherbrained "Christian" exhorted all of the readers to "thank God for the Phillips, the Weymouths, the Goodspeeds, and the Taylors, who had done such an "inspired job of reducing the scripture to UNDERSTANDABLE language." That interesting publication was the official organ of a religious group that had [rejected]{.class_3759} the Virgin Birth, the Deity of Christ, scriptural evangelism, the premillennial coming of Christ, the resurrection of the body, the Judgment Seat of Christ, the regenerating work of the Holy Ghost, the Bible doctrines of the Crucifixion, the Atonement, and a literal Hell. That is the kind of "Christians" who want to thank God for the new translations. My, what a testimony for their spirituality! There must be something terribly sterile and frightfully impotent about the mass of trivia between 18801980 which reduced the Bible to "understandable language." I wonder how it is that after reading all of these "understandable" versions, the Liberals who love them still [don't believe ANYTHING they didn't believe before they picked them up?]{.class_3759})

"[When men are cast down"]{.class_3760} (see Prov. 31:6--7 and Job 16:21), they need an advocate or intercessor (Prov. 24:11), exactly as Job needed one (9:33). Eliphaz is saying (vs. 29) that if Job "gets right" (vss. 23--28), he will be able to act as an advocate again (29:7), and tell the sufferers that they will be lifted up. And nothing could be as clear in view of Job 29:11--17---the Holy Spirit's own concordance, lexicon, and dictionary, in His own Book---unless you polished a plate glass window three hours with Windex.

The silly asses (yes, you don't "like the language," because you want your students to show respect for Satanic scholarship that passes off as "conservative") who went digging around in Hebrew and Arabic roots, to pervert verse 29, are no less irreverent and foolish than the "popish persons" who use John 2:3 to prove that saints should pray to Mary. (To tell the truth, they are [worse,]{.class_3759} for many of the popish persons were never saved and never had anything but an [ASV]{.class_3759} to start with: the [ASV]{.class_3759} \[any edition\] is the Roman Catholic Bible of the Jesuit priests \[1582\].)

"[The island of the innocent"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30) is just as plainly figurative as Job 21:24, you see. What Bible-believing Christian (who took his Bible studies "seriously," etc.) would fail to notice that "waters" represent the mass of unsaved mankind (Rev. 17:15); so, what God is looking for is an island in that mass of polluted water. Noah and his family wre a floating island in a [real ]{.class_3761}flood of waters, and [before]{.class_3761} that flood they were an island of isolated, segregated, unleavened REACTIONARIES in the middle of an ocean of integrated, united, "involved" corruption. Today, the saint who wishes to retain his power and fellowship (at the cost of his influence and reputation) will have to be an ISOLATIONIST (Isa. 5:8) of sorts. This "dirty word" (see any newspaper or magazine article by any major writer in the last twenty years) marks the real Christian as a [nonconformist]{.class_3761} (Rom. 12:1--3), and this is what the world system really has against him: Note John 17:16 and John 15:18 carefully.

The [King James]{.class_3759} text is correct, and the other 30,000 revisions, comments, commentaries, scholars, revisers, teachers, preachers, popes, bishops, students, linguists, and professors are wrong: as usual.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw1915.xhtml}

CHAPTER 23

23:1 Then Job answered and said,

2 Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

3 Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!

4 I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

5 I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.

6 Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.

7 There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.

Now, from here on through the [Book of Job]{.class_3759}, we will not go into a great deal of detail on the verses where they are limited to inspirational content only. By now, the reader can discern the tone of the Book and the nature of the discourses as the conversations pass back and forth between Job and his buddies. All of Chapter 23 is Job complaining about his misery and his inability to defend himself, and he has done this before. As we remarked in our Preface, the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}deals with the real issues of life---suffering and death---and if there is one thought that has passed through the heart and minds of men such as the thoughts of Job 21:7, it is Job 23:3. Man's "search for God" usually takes him everywhere except to the place where God has revealed Himself (Exod. 20:24 and comments)---in His word; but at this time, there is no written revelation, and Job is sweating it out like millions have, wanting and needing a real God who can comfort them and minister to them.

"[Oh that I knew where I might find HIM"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). Even in Job's agnostic dilemma he still has more spiritual discernment than the great truth-rejecting, Bible-denying, truth-perverting philosophers, scientists, educators and psychiatrists of the twentieth century. Notice that Job says, ["Oh that I knew where I might find HIM"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). Job is not looking for some neuter "IT"---the great "thinghood" of the "whatness in Itself." Mao Tse-tung and his kin are far behind Job in spiritual knowledge as Einstein was behind Moses. [Erich Fromm's ]{.class_3761}final answer is not "HIM." Erich Fromm's final answer is "LOVE"---a neuter IT. [Fyodor Dostryevsky's ]{.class_3761}final statement is not "HIM"; it is "life" and "nature"---two more neuter ITs. The ultimate answer for [Loren Eiseley ]{.class_3761}is not "HIM"; it is "spiritual transcendence"---a neuter IT. The [popes ]{.class_3761}and their Baalite priesthood simply turn "HIM" (Matt. 1:21) into a "sacrament"---a neuter IT. [E. B. Cherbonnier]{.class_3761} is not resting on "HIM"; he is resting on "the liberty and integrity of the individual"---two neuter ITs. When [Martin Buber ]{.class_3761}talks about "God or Idol," he simply overthrows the "I AM" of the universe ("HIM") and replaces Him with "the One"---a neuter IT. When [Paul Tillich ]{.class_3761}talks about the "concept of God," he is usually referring to the "power of powers"---some more great big neuter ITs.

And this is where all Baalite idolatry ends, no matter where it begins.

Take the first 30,000 books on science, religion, philosophy, and education (at random) and then turn to the last page on each work. Ninety-five percent of them will recommend a neuter "IT" as a solution or [the ]{.class_3759}solution to man's problems. The only exceptions will be the modern writers who recommend the superman from outer space (Rev. 13) whom the Bible identifies as [Satan ]{.class_3759}incarnate (2 Thess. 2). Idolatry is quite widespread among all of the major scientists and educators. They cannot conceive of [a person ]{.class_3759}being the answer to the problems that arise from human nature. They abandon Job's quest altogether since they are unable to see, feel, smell, touch, or hear God. All materialists are very much alike basically, and they have no "original thinkers" from Pythagoras to Sartre. They substitute a neuter "it" to replace God. And whether this "it" be a [golden rule ]{.class_3759}(a thing) or [church membership ]{.class_3759}(a thing) or [the sacraments ]{.class_3759}(things) or [education ]{.class_3759}(a thing) or "making the world a better place to live in" (something), they are all in the same boat: a waterlogged canoe going over Niagara Falls.

Verses 4 and 5 are self-explanatory (see 9:14). Job believes what Daniel experiences (Dan. 10:17--19). He believes that God would ["put strength"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6) into him and thereby give him a chance to "speak his piece." The ["righteous"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7) will not only have a chance to dispute with Him (watch old Simon do it in Matt. 16:22 and Acts 10:14!), but also the UNRIGHTEOUS (Rom. 3:4). ["So should I be delivered for ever from my judge"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). This is a strong intimation that Job suspects some other power than God is working on him. Notice the same intimation strongly voiced in Job 16:11. In perfect obedience to the word of God (Judge 9), Job does NOT bring a ["railing accusation"]{.class_3760} against the Devil, but in his "searchings of the heart" (Psa. 77:6), Job often came to the conclusion that some malevolent power was at work on him that was NOT the Creator. It is true that his ["judge"]{.class_3760} might have been his wife (2:9) or his friends (4:5), but Psalm 109:6--7 pretty well shows who the judge is. It was Absalom (2 Sam. 18:18), one of the greatest types of the Antichrist in the Bible, who wanted to "share his experience" with the people as "an instrument of peace" (2 Sam. 15:5) and "relate" in a judge's position (2 Sam. 15:4) by giving out the "kiss of peace."

Misters Good and Jerome (the [Vulgate]{.class_3759}) both avoid this cross-reference (vs. 7) carefully and then remove the word ["judge"]{.class_3760} from the text. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}changes the word ["from"]{.class_3760} to "by" so that the reference is not to the Devil. Kenneth Taylor also follows this blind lead. Since Dummelow suggested this change nearly seventy years ago, there is not much point in talking about the "latest scientific discoveries" and "better manuscripts" at this juncture. Really, this primitive alteration goes way back to the turn of the century and even beyond that, for Jamieson (1866) opted to make this change before the Franco-Prussian war (1870--1871). But if you are still not convinced, go back to Alexandria Egypt, between 100 and 300 years after the New Testament was completed and watch the old [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}mess up the Hebrew text with this: "He would forth MY JUDGMENT to an end." That is, what some of you educated Christians were suckered into accepting was not the product of "recent discoveries" at all, and it certainly had nothing to do with "older manuscripts," etc. It was a 1700-year-old stinking dead "shiner" washed up on the Nile Delta before Pope Gregory got around to changing the calendar. Many times, the "newer translations" recommended by Bob Jones II, III, IV, and V are just revived ashes that fell out of one of Pharaoh's furnaces.

23:8 Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:

9 On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:

10 But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.

11 My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.

12 Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.

Everything in the section is clear at a glance. Job is describing "man's search for God," the never-ending theme of Liberal and Modernistic theology. The Liberals and Communists are always "hung up" on this "kick," and many of them (see the first thirty in the [Interpreter's Bible]{.class_3759}) have gone so far as to say that the Bible is primarily a record of "man's search for God" or (Neo-Orthodoxy) that it is a history of "God's redemptive acts," etc. But like the modern Charismatic movement (Melodyland School of Theology---Harold Ockenga, David Du Plessis, Dave Wilkerson, Kathryn Kuhlman, Pat Boone, J. R. Williams, Robert Walker, Michael Esses, Maria Von Trapp, the Logos Publishing Company, and the "Charisciples"), they all avoid telling you that the Bible is the [infallible Authority of God Almighty]{.class_3759}. All Bible-denying Conservatives and Fundamentalists take the same position. While pretending to believe that the [final authority ]{.class_3759}is the Lord Jesus Christ, 40,000 [conflicting authorities ]{.class_3759}are resurrected until the Bible must be abandoned wherever it crosses the opinions of any of the 40,000 authorities whose "authority" is "the Lord Jesus Christ." In the face of this maelstrom of infidelity and Satanic confusion, the Fundamental scholars of 1900--1980 did the only thing they could do to save face: [They abandoned the Reformation Bible and set up a straw dummy called "the verbally, plenary, inspired originals," which could no more be CHECKED or CONSULTED than the men who wrote them]{.class_3759}. The twentieth century ushers in the great Cowardly Christianity of Machen, Robertson, Warfield, Gaussen, Rice, Wuest, etc. Not a "man jack of 'em" could face RIDICULE by unbelieving scholars. They were so afraid that people would think they really believed the Bible---the one by which they were saved and called to the ministry---that they fled to an unprovable, unknowable, unusable "original," and then posed as "champions of the faith." There isn't a Catholic, Atheist, Satanist, Liberal, Buddhist, Modernist, Neo-Orthodox, or Agnostic alive who is worried about any "originals," [no matter how they were written]{.class_3759}. They were never the issue ONE TIME in the history of infidelity. The issue, since 1611, has been the power and authority of [one book]{.class_3759}, and if you think anyone in the NCCC or RCC is concerned about some "original" that no one can produce, preach, check, or teach, you're about two minutes from the funny farm.

The final authority for the Bible-rejecting Fundamentalists and Conservatives of today is their own opinion which [somebody taught them]{.class_3759} or their own feeling about [their own emotional experiences]{.class_3759}. Their "authority" is no more the "Lord Jesus Christ" than it is the Holy Bible. (And that, ole' buddy, is sound doctrine, and if you don't like it, you are a reprobate who cannot stand "sound doctrine," and your telephone number is in 2 Tim. 4:1--5.)

Job is talking about finding God as a [person ]{.class_3759}(vs. 7) and talking with Him (vs. 8). He goes forward and backward, to the right and to the left, and still he does not see God (Matt. 5:8); so Job is in the exact position of Israel in this dispensation (Deut. 31:17, Matt. 13:44), and in verse 10, Job states Israel's position in the Tribulation: ["when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold"]{.class_3760} (Mal. 3:3). The faith of the Tribulation saints will be like ["gold tried in the fire"]{.class_3760} (1 Pet. 4:12--13). It will match ["the words of his mouth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12), for these words are ["tried in the furnace of earth"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 12:6) and ["purified seven times"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 12:6) so that they can well overrule your opinions or the opinions of the teachers who taught you or the people who wrote the textbooks which your teachers used. We may expect an epidemic of nervous breakdowns and pathological disorders at verse 12, where those fearful ["WORDS"]{.class_3760} of God---not "THE WORD"---appear again to distract and terrorize the "recognized Conservative scholars" whose "final court of appeals" (to cite Stewart Custer, BJU) is the unavailable, unread, unseen "original."

"[My foot hath held his steps"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). (See Psa. 44:18 and 119:133.) Job had not declined from God's ["way"]{.class_3762} (vs. 11), and the inclusion of this word (["way"]{.class_3762}) with ["commandment"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12) as a separate item from ["the words of his mouth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12) kicks the props from under the Conservative critics (1880--1980) who will not attempt to rid themselves of the ["WORDS of His mouth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 12). (See the exact procedure carried out again by the "Fundamentalists" in Acts 26:28.)

Job obeyed the admonition of the Charismatic "authority," for he did not live ["by bread alone, but by EVERY WORD"]{.class_3760} (Matt. 4:4) that proceeded out of the mouth of God. So, the surest proof that Charismatic "Charisciples" are Bible-rejecting reprobates (I didn't say they were lost, you old liar; I said "BIBLE-REJECTING REPROBATES.") is the fact that these people threw out ["BUT BY EVERY WORD OF GOD"]{.class_3760} (Luke 4:4) when they chose a "bible" to represent their "final authority"---supposedly "the Lord Jesus Christ." You see, the "Logos" Study Bible used by the Charismatics [is the second Bible that removed the authoritative statement of the Final Authority ]{.class_3759}("the Lord Jesus" from Luke 4:4). The "Study Bible," published by Logos Publishers, is the blasphemous [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition: same slop) recommended by many of the faculty members of Bob Jones, Midwestern, Mid-South, and Lynchburg. This corrupt publication is recommended by John R. Rice as a [superior translation ]{.class_3759}to the one from which Rice himself [preaches]{.class_3759}. (Strange isn't it? Here are hypocrites who state publicly that the [ASV ]{.class_3759}is better than the [AV ]{.class_3759}\[[Sword of the Lord]{.class_3759}, Oct. 10, 1975, p. 10\], [and they don't have the guts to USE it in their preaching]{.class_3759}. No wonder they are always upset about "Bible-believing Nuts." If I didn't have the courage to use in public the Bible I thought was the [best version]{.class_3759}, I would get out of the ministry tonight. What could be more gutless than a cowardly Christian, who is so afraid of the ridicule of scholarship that he cannot even [use ]{.class_3759}the Bible he thinks is the [best one]{.class_3759}?)

"[I have esteemed the WORDS of his mouth more than my necessary food"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). Well, that puts Job a good bit ahead of many a modern Premillennial, soul-winning, Fundamental, Christian "scholar," doesn't it? Could you say what Job said, with a clear conscience before God? And how is it that you could not say it when you have a complete written revelation (19:23) which Job did not have? If the word is a light to your path and a lamp to your feet (Psa. 119:105) and if it can cleanse your way (Psa. 119:130), help you to grow (1 Pet. 2:2), and build you up (Acts 20:32), how is it that you have esteemed your scholarship (or your ministry or your "testimony" or your reputation or your "attendance" more than ["THE WORDS OF HIS MOUTH"?]{.class_3762} (vs. 12).

Gonna let old Job beat you out, hey?

If Job reviewed God's words before he ate and valued them more than a meal---even more than his ["necessary food"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12), how is it that some of you have undertaken to correct the words of God every other verse, while esteeming His words as an "unclean thing"? If you had a dollar for every time one of these educated idiots said a verse in the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) was "corrupt," you could almost buy one of those Roman Catholic paintings at Bob Jones University.

Now we will only comment on verse 12 briefly. We have spoken of these matters at length in [Satan's Masterpiece, the NASV ]{.class_3761}(1973) and [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence ]{.class_3761}(1970), but there is no cure known for the saved sinner who makes a "hobby horse" out of correcting the word of God. If you will glance through the volumes of notes written by Trench, Vincent, Thayer, Rendall, Gaebelein, Culbertson, Feinberg, Mantey, Mason, McClain, Smith, Tischendorf, Weniger, Walvoord, Nestle, Neal, Custer, Metzger, Nutz, Bell, Souter, Mounce, Peake, Coder, Bickell, Keil, Marshall, Pyle, Wright, Duhm, Unger, Zodhiates, Ewald, and Delitzsch, you will finally understand what we are saying. These men were chosen (or called upon) to write books and articles because they were looked up to as "authorities." Their "bag" lay in the fact that every one of them [professed to be smart enough to correct the AUTHORIZED Version]{.class_3761}; this made them a higher "authority" than the Holy Bible. Hence, they were called upon to "help us poor, dumb Christians to understand our Bibles better." Ninety-nine percent of them were occupied with destroying the Reformation Bible when they were called upon, and many of them (in their classrooms, especially) devoted their lives to replacing the [Receptus ]{.class_3761}with the grossly corrupt Westcott and Hort text of the Roman Catholic Church. Their reputed "godliness" often lay in the fact that they lived a cloistered, sheltered life on tax exempt property where they had four to eight hours a day to attack the Bible (and those who believed it) behind closed doors. And if you eliminated from these monstrous libraries [all of the writings of these men where they altered the AV text]{.class_3761}, you wouldn't have enough Bible truth left in your hand (that wasn't already known [before ]{.class_3761}A.D. 500) to make a book thirty pages long. The twenty-two volumes of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3761}, for example, do not reveal ONE important Christian truth (spiritually, devotionally, doctrinally, or prophetically) that was not known by George Whitefield and Clarence Larkin, and so stated, [on less than fifty pages]{.class_3761}. The real "authorities" on Biblical revelation (in their alloted times) were never men who spent their lives correcting the Bible.

Chrysostom (347--407), in his day, used the Byzantine text. He could outclass the professional "professor" (Origen) as far as a Cadillac can outclass an Edsel. The Greek expositors of the Greek church (450--700 A.D.), who used the [Receptus]{.class_3759}, went by the Roman popes and priests like they were stuck in mud up to their hubcaps. When it came to Bible exposition in the Reformation, the men who used the [Receptus ]{.class_3759}of the [AV ]{.class_3759}(Calvin, Knox, Zwingli, Luther, Wesley, Whitefield, and Edwards) could keep pace with [any faculty member of any school ]{.class_3759}in their day, who used any other text, and when it came to practical application of the truth (evangelism and soul-winning), they passed those gentlemen by like they were stuck on a sandbar up to the water line. The men whom God used between 1800 and 1900 to "open the word" to His people were NEVER the Greek and Hebrew teachers of ANY school---Liberal or Conservative. What little Bible the faculty at these schools knew, they learned through the "grapevine." The grapevine, in this case, sprouted branches like Clarence Larkin, Darby, Bullinger, Cronin, Pember, Peters, Moody, Sunday, Norris, Scofield, Rimmer, Sauer, and Collett. Where [these men ]{.class_3759}referred to the [RV ]{.class_3759}or [ASV ]{.class_3759}(as occasionally they did), they added nothing to the body of scriptural revelation already known in A.D. 500. And if you hear (in the future) of some faculty member of some school showing you advanced revelation as "nuggets"---and these "nuggets" are actually TRUE---you can bet your last Federal Reserve "Note" that they got them [from a man (or men) who already got it from the Reformation text of the AV (1611). ]{.class_3759}God doesn't bother to bless "scholarship," where it esteems income and prestige more important than ["the WORDS OF HIS MOUTH."]{.class_3760} (vs. 12).

1\. ["The commandment of his lips"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) has been altered to "the [written Mosaic Law]{.class_3759}" (Clarke, p. 12) to enforce Clarke's opinion that the Book of Job was written after the time of Moses.

2\. The Jehovah's Witnesses ([NWT]{.class_3759}) have substituted "sayings" for ["WORDS."]{.class_3759}

3\. Kenneth Taylor has refused to paraphrase the verse in any fashion and has stuck in the word "them" for ["words,"]{.class_3760} thus making the ["words"]{.class_3760} refer to the "commandments"---not ["THE WORDS!"]{.class_3760} Nice "paraphrasing."

4\. The clumsy [LXX ]{.class_3759}and Dummelow have the words stored up in Job's "breast" so that he can still consider a [meal ]{.class_3759}as important, or more important, than the words of God.

5\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are still giving us the old song and dance about ["necessary"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) really meaning "appointed" (see Prov. 30:8 and comments), although the Hebrew Masoretic text is translated into English as the [AV]{.class_3759}.

6\. Meredith Kline cannot stand the thought of magnifying God's words above food; so, he says that Job "treasured" the words more "than his own [precepts]{.class_3759}".

7\. The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(written more than 200 years after the completion of the New Testament) erases all references to food of any kind so that a man can go right on living by "bread ALONE" (see corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}and [NASV ]{.class_3759}readings for Luke 4:4), as long as a "few words of God" are believed.

And no matter how far and fast you travel these days, you will get only one message from "recognized scholarship" of any kind. That message is [that scholarship is the final authority in all matters of faith and practice]{.class_3759}.
