Many of the brethren "prefer" the King James---see John Rice, Sumner, Bob Jones III, et al.--- because it pays off financially and keeps them preaching in Fundamental churches. If they "preferred" the [NASV]{.class_3759}, they would discard the [AV ]{.class_3759}as quickly as look at it. After all, their supreme authority is their "PREFERENCE," not God's word.")

3\. The serpent has already been "pierced" (not "formed") in the Jehovah's Witness Bible ([NWT]{.class_3759}). And since the [NWT ]{.class_3759}takes the same attitude toward the Deity of Christ that the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) took---see 1 Timothy 3:16 and John 9:35 in that obscene version, recommended by John R. Rice---the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) reads as they read it in the "Kingdom Halls."

4\. Umbreit thinks that the serpent was only the "northern constellation."

5\. The serpent is a "crocodile," according to the [New Bible Commentary ]{.class_3759}(p. 435). The only other alternative that Heavenor gives you is that the serpent is "popular mythology"---kind of like the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759}!

6\. The serpent is only "mythological imagery" according to the [Wycliffe Commentary ]{.class_3759}(p. 479), which borrowed the name of one of the greatest Christians who ever lived in order to deny the truths of God Almighty.

7\. The [LXX ]{.class_3759}has an "apostate dragon" [already slain]{.class_3759}, although he is still quite active in Christ's time (Rom. 16:20), and the twentieth century (Rev. 12:1--8). But John Milton's "mythological fable" of a [past fulfillment ]{.class_3759}of Revelation 12:9 was in vogue long before Herman whipped the Roman Legions.

8\. Dummelow ascribes the whole verse to a supposed "fable" that people used to believe (p. 310).

9\. Calmet says that the verse is a reference to the "production of FISHES."

10\. The Syriac and Arabic versions get rid of the serpent by saying that he was [already ]{.class_3759}slain, and although Adam Clarke finally connects the serpent with Genesis 3:1 (Scripture with Scripture---it's about time!), he cannot make one application of this truth after accepting it.

11\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown transform the serpent into a constellation or "eclipsed crescent" shape and leave you where you came in---in the dark.

The best procedure to follow (when faced with the mass of "recognized scholarship," "qualified authorities," and "godly, good men" whose "vast labors enable them" to blah, blah, blah) is simply to shelve 100 percent of the gentlemen where they have aborted the text.

Now when we do this, the "qualified authorities" (any puffed up, bloated, egotistical, stuffed shirt on the faculty of a Fundamental college or university) will start screaming, "Brother Ruckman thinks everyone is wrong who disagrees with HIM, and no one has it right but HIM!" (See Rice's correspondence with Fuller or Evans \[1973-1975\].) But this is because the "qualified authority" (who was never "qualified" to start with) has confused "Ruckman" with the Bible. We are not the same: I am fallible; the Bible is not. I am not without error; the Bible is. I did not write that Book; God wrote it. (The contemporary slander \[1970--1980\] carried on by this crowd is: "Ruckman thinks he knows more than all of the schools, and [he ]{.class_3759}is right and [they ]{.class_3759}are wrong." Shame on you, you old lying, two-faced reprobate! We have never corrected a Christian scholar with our opinion one time in 6,000 pages of print; [we corrected them with THE BOOK]{.class_3759}---remember?)

Well, the SERPENT, here, is in the heavens (vs. 13). He goes up and down through them (41:31--34) before walking ["to and fro" ]{.class_3760}through the earth (1:7), and the material in chapter 41 gives the details of his person. This "serpent" is the LEVIATHAN of Psalm 74:14 and Isaiah 27:1, for he has ["HEADS"]{.class_3760} (plural), not a "head" (singular). Therefore, the confused HPD which led the Conservatives and Liberals to make "whales" and "crocodiles" out of Leviathan, on the basis of Psalm 104:26, is the same type of HPD that caused the [NASV ]{.class_3759}to abort Matthew 16:18 and Hebrews 9:28. (Scriptural abortion is much more common than "birth control" abortion, but Fundamentalists rarely preach on the former.) God's hand ["HATH formed the crooket serpent"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13), and He will slay that dragon ["that is in the sea"]{.class_3760} (Isa 27:1--2) [later]{.class_3759}, not now; and certainly not in the past, as suggested by the Disneyland "bibles" mentioned above. The versions which put it in the past tense ([ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV]{.class_3759}---we corrected this last one before it was published since Bible perverters are quite consistent in their stupid errors) are handmade cover-ups for the Prince of Darkness, and they are allied to his hosts by a mutual bond of friendship; and even if someone who was really "godly" (like Dwight L. Moody or George Whitefield) had translated them, they wouldn't be worth the time it would take you to break in the binding.

"[Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). You betcha! So little that 40,000 saved scholars with 320,000 years of formal education behind them could no more expound the preceding passage than they could swallow and whistle at the same time.

"[But the thunder of his power who can understand?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14)., John wasn't allowed to write it if he did understand it (Rev. 10:3, 4), and nobody could get a [complete ]{.class_3761}portion of it (vs. 14) because God had John seal it up.

Following God's revelation of the structure and content of outer space, and the ["god of this world,"]{.class_3760} the Holy Spirit now tells the reader that Job's speech has been a ["parable"]{.class_3760} (27:1) exactly as the Old Testament was a parable (Psa. 78:1--6). And this should shut (forever) the mouths of the bungling, lying, Bible-perverting "Conservatives" who think THEY are right and GOD is wrong. A [parable ]{.class_3759}is clearly a parallel account of something else, and if ever a book in the Bible ran parallel to the Tribulation, the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}is it.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2182.xhtml}

CHAPTER 27

27:1 Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

2 As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;

3 All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;

4 My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.

5 God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.

6 My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.

Job is grimly dogmatic in his position; he is determined that his lips will not[ "speak wickedness"]{.class_3760} (vs.4) nor ["deceit"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) as long as he can breathe (vs. 3). Up to the day of his death (vs.5), he will not make a false confession (vs. 4), and he is not guilty of verbal trickery (vs. 5). He is determined that his heart will not reproach him (vs. 6) as long as he lives (1 John 3:21), and, like Paul, he will live with a ["conscience void of offence toward God, and toward men"]{.class_3760} (Acts 24:16). In the same breath, Job accuses God of vexing his soul (vs. 2) and refusing to give him a hearing in court. Adam Clarke's comment on the verse is remarkable for naivete and lack of spiritual understanding (see also his comments on Exod. 18:14--20). Clarke says the words show "a great want of reverence," and that Job speaks "with a degree of irritation, if not bitterness," which "cannot be justified."

Well, dog my cats.

Could Brother Clarke have done any better in the same position?

Before any commentator slings a big rock at Job (see comments under 1:21 and 2:10), he had better count to 10,000 ---slowly. Of course Job is wrong in justifying himself (vs. 5), and God makes this clear in Job 42:7; but that is the point: GOD MAKES IT CLEAR, not us. Not even Elihu (34:12) is able to convince Job. When Job says ["my righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6), he speaks for Israel (Rom. 10:1--4) and the eternal "fig leaf factor" (Gen. 3:7; Isa. 64:6): Every unsaved man in the world takes this position when presented with the demands of Calvary. There is no doubt about his self-righteousness, but Job is not speaking to GOD (see Clarke's misreading of the verse, p. 119), he is speaking to Bildad, Zophar, and Eliphaz:[ "God forbid that I should justify YOU"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5). Before Job will admit to the sins they charge him with, he will drop dead in his tracks (22:6 and 7).

And if Job is "irritated" and bitter (Clarke, p. 119), he has more right to those sins than Paul in jail, Peter in chains, Joshua on his face, or James on the chopping block. Consider: Boils, sores, ashes, maggots, garbage, insomnia, pains, fever, hallucinations, dirt, inflammation, and indigestion with no doctor or any medicine; money gone, wife gone, children gone, house gone, property gone, cattle gone, and reputation gone; the devil after him, and God indifferent to him; his friends against him, and no Bible, no indwelling Holy Spirit, and no Mediator at the Throne of God.

Let Adam Clarke sling the rocks---I'm getting outta here "as fast as I can git," if not FASTER. Here is "Uncle Bud" Robinson. He is hit by an automobile when he is fifty-nine years old. The car knocks him ten feet in the air, twenty feet down the street, and then runs over him again before it can stop. When the policeman runs out into traffic blowing his whistle and cursing, Bud is lying there with cuts and bruises, two compound fractures, his left foot turned around backward on his ankle, and an arm bone sticking through his skin into his shirt. Lying in his blood, Uncle Bud says to the cop, "Now, don't talk that way. I don't like that kinda talk."

They haul "Uncle Buddy" off to the hospital and put him in the emergency room. The interns dump hydrogen peroxide all over him while they're cutting off his clothes, and one of them says, "Whadya do for a livin', old man?" Bud says "I'm a preacher." "Well," says one of the interns, "you'll need all the patience you been preachin' about 'fore you get outta here."

They turn him over to a doctor who is an atheist. The doctor says that gangrene is in the bone of one arm and the arm will have to come off. Bud wants it saved. The doctor says, "It would pain you too much for me to work on it." Bud says, "I'm a Christian and God will give me the grace to stand the pain." The doctor snorts and says, "I don't know what a Christian is. I've been here twenty years and never seen one of [those]{.class_3759}, but if you're game, we'll try it." Then there followed three months of wrapping and unwrapping dressings and scraping raw bone with a scalpel. Bud would say, "Now, doctor, I love you, and I know you are doing the best you can for me even when it hurts. But I understand, and I'm praying for you, and I want you to live forever in heaven with me." The doctor would grunt and go on working.

Sometimes it got so bad that "Uncle Bud" would pass into a coma and dream about heaven. To keep his equilibrium, he would holler when it got hurting so bad that he couldn't stand it, "Glory! Well, glory to God!" Finally, when he said, "Now, doctor, I love you and\...etc." the doctor would blush and say, "All right, Bud, here we go again." To make a long story short, Bud got through the crisis, and when that atheist worked on the arm for the last time, he was sweating while he handled his doctor's tools. Suddenly he dropped his knife and said, "That's it! [GLORY TO GOD, Bud, we got it!"]{.class_3759} Bud Robinson led that doctor to Christ before leaving the hospital.

But "Uncle Bud" had not buried ten children before he went in, and he went into a hospital bed with[ clean sheets,]{.class_3759} not out to the city dump on an [ash heap!]{.class_3759}

I knew a real Christian woman in Memphis. Her husband was a saved sheriff, and he witnessed "in office" and refused the annual "liquor gifts" that all of the liquor dealers sent to politicians every Christmas (Jesus' "birthday," remember?). They were wealthy. They had four children, a fine house in the suburbs, and all of them were in good health. One day the Sheriff went down a steel ladder into their dry swimming pool to vacuum up about a foot of water that still lay on the bottom. His metal vacuum touched the water and he dropped over dead in the pool. (There was a bare wire loose somewhere in the vacuum.) A next-door neighbor (an orthodox Jew) had seen the accident, and thinking that the Sheriff might have had a heart attack, ran out of doors and to the pool; down the ladder he went, and reached out to pick up the body. He dropped dead into the pool.

By now, the Sheriff's wife had heard the commotion, and she came out of the back door; she saw both men lying in the pool. She was halfway down the ladder before something warned her to look at the metal vacuum extension. The tube still hung over the side of the pool and down into the water: The current was still on. That woman, struck with horror, slowly came back up the ladder and went into the house and turned the current off. She barely had the presence of mind, then, to phone for help and get the bodies taken away.

In the middle of that woman's ["garden"]{.class_3760} (John 19:41) was a ["sepulchre"]{.class_3760} (vs.41), but, even then, she had [friends ]{.class_3759}who stuck with her; Job did not. That Christian woman still had her [children]{.class_3759}; Job did not. And she had the [indwelling Comforter ]{.class_3759}of whom it was said, ["He may abide with you FOREVER"]{.class_3760} (John 14:16). Job had none of this. And if Job suddenly screams into your ears ["AS GOD LIVETH, who hath taken away my judgment"]{.class_3760} (vs.2), you had better sit in silence as his three friends sit and not open your mouth about his "bitterness" and "irritation." Frankly, I am surprised that Job did not say it the day his wife threw his religion in his face (Job 2:9, cf. Psa. 26:1, 11 for the "works" in the Tribulation period.)

27:7 Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.

8 For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?

9 Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?

10 Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?

11 I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.

12 Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?"

The ["hope of the hypocrite"]{.class_3760} (vs.8) is nothing, when God ["taketh away his soul."]{.class_3760} The rich man of Luke 12:20 is taken away that ["the hypocrite reign not"]{.class_3760} (Job 34:30), and where the hypocrites reign (the Caesars, the popes, the dictators, etc.), they are never allowed to reign forever; God takes away "Your Holiness," or "Holy Father" or "Comrade." The Lord says ["THOU FOOL, this night thy soul shall be required of thee"]{.class_3760} (Luke 12:20).

"[Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?"]{.class_3760} (vs.9). Not in the Tribulation (Prov. 1:24--32). ["Will he delight himself in the Almighty?"]{.class_3762} (vs.10). No, for he ["savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of MEN"]{.class_3762} (Matt. 16:23; Luke 16:15). The hypocrite's ["delight"]{.class_3762} is in the flesh (Phil. 3:3), and he couldn't imagine any greater time-waster than "worshipping God in spirit and in truth" (Psa. 103:1--2) like David did it.

Huey Pierce Long (1893--1935) blew 79 million dollars in one year (1931), and the next time he tried to tax the people of Louisiana, he faced something near an armed rebellion. The end came in 1935 when a certain Dr. Carl Weiss stepped out from behind a marble column in the skyscraper capitol of Baton Rouge and plugged Governor Huey Long in the stomach twice. Just before this happened, Governor Long had left a hotel lobby in Baton Rouge and was driven to the capitol. The last words anyone heard him say before he was assassinated were: "I couldn't give you five minutes of my time if you were Jesus Christ!" These words were spoken to a North Louisiana farmer who was trying to get Long's attention as he strode through the hotel lobby, surrounded by reporters, body guards, and politicians. This farmer was one of Long's old "home town" buddies from North Louisiana. He was trying to get the governor to go to bat for him and his friends in regard to a certain bill about to be passed. All he got from Long was: "I couldn't give you five minutes of my time if you were Jesus Christ!" (You'll give Jesus Christ more time than five minutes, I'll guarantee you---Rom. 3:4; Phil. 2:5--10).

"[Will he always call upon God?"]{.class_3760} (vs.10).

Arthur (Dutch Schultz) Flegenheimer was gunned down, in 1935, by Harlie (The Bug) Workman. He lay in a coma for fourteen hours, speaking to first one person and then another. In the entire transcript of Dutch Schultz's "last will and testament," the names of "God" or "Jesus Christ" do not appear [one single time]{.class_3759}. Dying, alone in the world (Eph. 2:12), without hope (Job 27:8), and without God (Eph. 2:12), Dutch Schultz called only on Henry, Max, Frankie, Jimmy, Dannie, and his own mother: God was ["NOT IN ALL his thoughts."]{.class_3760} As a matter of fact, God was not in any of his thoughts."

"[I will teach you by the hand of God"]{.class_3760} (vs.11). Job, at this point, is claiming something just short of inspiration (vs.3), exactly as Elihu claims it (32:8, 33:4) later. ["The hand of God"]{.class_3762} (Ezra 8:2; 2 Chron. 6:23; Psa. 102:25) has revealed certain things (Rom. 1:18--22) that cannot be mistaken. ["That which is with the Almighty" ]{.class_3762}(vs.11) is revealed, and Job will not go to the trouble that Robertson, Machen, Rice, Walvoord, and Weniger go in order to cover it up or conceal it (vs.11). Furthermore, his friends ["have seen it"]{.class_3762} (vs.12), so there is no need for them to play the hypocrite and pretend that they do not know what Job is talking about (vs.12). Job takes for granted that his auditors have seen Job 21:7--14 on scores of occasions, but, of course, this will not convince them. You see, as soon as Job starts discoursing on the ["wicked man"]{.class_3762} again (vs.13), he only repeats what they have already said (see Job 18, 20). The thing is this: The three auditors apply anything Job says about ["the wicked man"]{.class_3762} to him (Job) therefore, Job is really only wasting his breath.

27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.

14 If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.

15 Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.

16 Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;

17 He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

18 He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.

19 The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.

20 Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.

21 The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.

22 For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.

23 Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.

All that follows applies to the Antichrist, for he is the "wicked man" of 2 Thessalonians 2, called the ["man of sin."]{.class_3760} Again, notice the contrast between the [poor ]{.class_3759}righteous man (see 24:1--12 and remarks) and the [rich ]{.class_3759}wicked man (vss. 11--19). This is why we pointed out the case of the RICH MAN in Luke 12:20, for the Lord Jesus Christ told us that [that ]{.class_3759}rich man was in a PARABLE (Luke 12:16; see Job 27:1). (Although 100 percent of the commentators may have missed these cross- references, it would not nullify the doctrinal truths contained in them; you don't stay rich in the Tribulation without losing your soul. "The war on poverty" in the Tribulation is a war against the poverty-stricken.)

Everything in the passage is transparent: The wicked man may have children (vs.14), but the ["sword"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 19) finishes them off. While they are alive, his children are not ["satisfied with bread"]{.class_3760} (vs.14), because when the famine comes (Rev. 6:5, 6) it is accompanied by three and one-half years of drought (1 Kings 17, 18). The wicked man is not Job, for this wicked man has ["widows"]{.class_3760} (vs.15), and Job had only one wife (Job 31:1, 9), and she was not a "widow." ["Though he heap up silver as the dust"]{.class_3760} (vs.16---Shaikh Abdullah Al-Salime al Subah \[1895\] made \$7,000,000 a week), and ["prepare raiment as the clay"]{.class_3760} (vs.16---Haroldson Lafayette Hunt makes \$50,000, 000 a year), ["\...the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver"]{.class_3760} (vs.17). (See Prov. 13:22).

The ["rich man"]{.class_3760} (vs.19) may build his house, but it is mothy (vs.18) because a moth's house is temporary and though it be erected with great skill and industry, it is more fragile than a cardboard box. ["As a booth that the keepers maketh"]{.class_3760} (vs.18) is a reference to the shed erected near a vineyard or garden (see Matt. 21:33) where a watcher looks over the crops (Isa. 1:8). These sheds (as the "flat joints" on the Midways) are as flimsy as a berry basket.

When George Washington Vanderbilt died (1914), he left behind a 119,000 acre "yard," with a house on it valued at \$4,000,000; but he left it. When Cornelius Vanderbilt died, he said to his Christian wife and his Christian doctor (Dr. Deems), "Now won't you sit down by me and hold my hand and sing for me that old song 'Come Ye Sinners, Poor and Needy, Weak and Wounded, Sick and Sore'"? Vandy may have lived and died as a millionaire, but he was a spiritual pauper and alongside the Christians of his day and time---many of whom lived in tenement houses and made less than \$20.00 a week---he was a penniless bum.

One time Vanderbilt was eating his Melba toast (and an unseasoned salad) in a restaurant. Observing a young man bolting down meat, potatoes, gravy, ice cream, and biscuits, Vanderbilt said to his male secretary: "You know, I would give half of what I am worth just to be able to eat one meal like that!" ["A man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the THINGS which he possesseth"]{.class_3760} (Luke 12:15)!

"[The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered"]{.class_3760} (v. 19) in the first place to "his fathers" as the Old Testament saints were (see Gen. 49:33 and comments) and in the second place to a proper burial (see Ecc. 6:3). The corpses of Alexander the Great (356--323 B.C.) and Adolph Hitler (1889--1945)---who both controlled more than 2,000,000 square miles of land---have to lie alone on the dirt with no one caring whether they are buried or not (Ecc. 6:3). Alex lay three days on the ground before his own troops would fool with his carcass. Benito Mussolini (1883--1945) wasn't buried till he had hung two days, half naked by his feet, in the plaza at Rome. Furthermore, the wicked man is not "gathered to his fathers" as we mentioned before (2 Chron. 12:16). The rich man closes his eye (Luke 12:20) in the midst of comfort, luxury, and security (Luke 12:18--19), and when "he openeth his eyes" (vs. 19) he "IS NOT!" "A tempest stealeth him away in the NIGHT" (vs. 20), and terrors "take hold on him as waters" (vs. 20, cf. Psa. 69:2; 106:11.) ["The east wind carrieth him away\...as a storm hurleth him out of his place"]{.class_3762} (vs. 21). One can see Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar all nodding their heads in agreement. Only they are thinking of [Job's]{.class_3761} calamities (Job 1, 2) which match this confession, while Job is thinking of people like THEM.

The "wicked man" desires to run away from God (vs. 22) and get away from His judgments (Zech. 14:12), but it is no good; nobody runs from God (Jonah 1--2) because nobody can get to a location which God does not occupy (Psa. 139).

The clapping of verse 23 is the Oriental gesture of contempt (Lam. 2:15) as well as the sign of approbation (2 Kings 11:12). The wicked man, then, is "booed" off of the stage of history (Isa. 14:4--10). ("All of God's stories have happy endings" \[as a little girl once said in a Sunday School class\], and if you are on God's side, there is only one end to the story: eternal sinless GLORY. And if you are on the "other" side, there is only one end to the story: a Lake of Fire.)

And the enlightened Phenomenologists, the synthesized Integrationists, and permissive Relativists can "mediate," "communicate," "interrelate," "rethink," "re-evaluate," and "restructure," until their leavened adulteration amalgamates into one mammoth, homogenous, mongrel, indiscriminate BLOB, but it will still come out Heaven or Hell, saved or lost, up or down, right or wrong, and God or the Devil. Take your choice. If you split the difference you'll have twice as much trouble as if you hadn't tried to stand the coin on edge.

Before embarking on Chapter 28, we turn briefly (let us hope!) to the wretched and unholy works of the "majority of Conservative scholars." John R. Rice whined most of his latter life about our "lack of respect" and "lack of courtesy" in dealing with these destructive Bible-perverting egotists, but Rice was merely whining for more courtesy for himself. The Scholars Union is a self-protective Mafia which will make ANY adjustment to maintain its own authority over the Bible. You will notice (in the correspondence between Otis Fuller and Rice, 1973) a dozen real sick excuses for trying to justify the depraved [ASV ]{.class_3759}and [NASV]{.class_3759}. Among these is the ancient Roman Catholic alibi that the mass of common, "ignorant" (those are the words!) Christians who are "uneducated" cannot possibly understand the issues involved in recommending the [ASV ]{.class_3759}from the corrupt manuscripts of Westcott and Hort. Therefore, the [ASV ]{.class_3759}should be promoted as "equally reliable" with the [AV ]{.class_3759}1611, and any attempt to inform the dumb, stupid, ordinary Christians of the truth is a Satanic attempt to "SPLIT CHURCHES." The Roman popes took the same position for a thousand years before Rice was born; the same position exactly.

When "push comes to shove," there is no more difference between the Vatican hierarchy's way of stifling the truth, and the methods used by Rice and Sumner, than there is between the Vatican manuscript ([Vaticanus]{.class_3759}) and the college of Cardinals at Rome. If you or your friends---and I am talking to YOU, stupid---are afraid that the "common" or "dumb" mass of "uneducated Christians" will return to the authority of the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759} at the expense of the "opinions" and "scholarship" of those who [reject ]{.class_3759}it, then you \[and your friends\] are UNGODLY MEN, not "good men"; and you are only lying like a dog to yourself and your constituency. Don't you give us any gas about "godliness" being the standards of Luke 18:11. No "godly" man would get upset at the common mass of American Christians replacing the authority of Conservative apostates with the Protestant Bible of the Reformation. Who are you trying to kid, stupid? No man would resent that unless his prestige and income depended upon [competing with that authority]{.class_3759}. Don't woof us, sonny. Some of us have no more trouble spotting a jackass in a sheepfold than a serpent in a bath tub. Many of the so-called "scholars" who spent "hours of dedicated labor" trying to "restore the original text" were nothing but belly-worshippers (Phil. 3:19; Rom. 16:18).

1\. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(Lockman Foundation) has destroyed the sense of verses 6, 15, 19, and 22.

2\. The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---saints preserve us---[Bible ]{.class_3759}has altered verses 6--7, 17--18, and 15 with the worst possible "paraphrasing" of verse 6. The wording makes Job say, "I am not a sinner." Job never said that, and he clearly denies it in Job 7:20.

3\. The Jehovah's Witness Bible ([NWT]{.class_3759}) has "whistling" at the wicked (vs. 23) instead of ["hissing,"]{.class_3760} the hypocrite of verse 8 is altered to an "apostate" (like Russell and Rutherford, perhaps?), and Job's friends had seen nothing (vs. 12) but "visions."

4\. Adam Clarke and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown get through fairly well this time, and although they allow that several readings are "possibilities," they don't blatantly deny the proper text as it stands.

5\. Dummelow recommends the apostate [RV ]{.class_3759}of 1884 for verses 2 and 7 and suggests that the authority for verses 22 and 23 are "Persian books of mythology."

6\. Heavenor has the old tribal god "Yahweh" running around (see comments under Exod. 3--5 in that Commentary) and broadly implies that Chapter 27 is out of place. (When a Conservative does this he always covers his tracks with statements like: ["Some]{.class_3759} believe\..." or "Whatever [our ]{.class_3759}view of it is\..." or "One can take [either ]{.class_3759}view\..." or "[Some ]{.class_3759}hold that the passage\...," etc. The trick is to preserve your own reputation and integrity as a "godly" scholar who "respects" the opinions of those who "differ" with you---the other qualified kiddies, like [yourself]{.class_3759}, who are engaged in usurping the authority of the Holy Spirit.)

7\. The constant reoccurrence of the "spider's web" in verse 18 for "BOOTH" (see the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759} and the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}) comes from the grossly corrupt Septuagint of A.D. 330--500.

And there are, of course, many other alterations of the Holy Bible suggested by the depraved reprobates who demand "respect for their considered opinions" and "due regard for their laborious investigation to restore to us" a [Roman Catholic stink bomb.]{.class_3759}

Budde doesn't like verse 2, Ewald and Dillman change verse 3, Budde alters verse 3, Peake would change verse 8, Duhm doesn't care for verse 10, Schlottmann and Delitzsch don't care for verse 15, and Meyer and Budde would alter verses 19 and 20. Altogether, they could rewrite Job 27 till you wouldn't know whether it was Job 26 or Psalm 38. But all was clear to start with. If there were any difficulties in the text, one could study a dozen case histories from the morning newspapers about "rich men" who were wicked and whose children (vs. 14) were given to the sword, right and left. And the money these men heaped up could not buy them a "Mass" high enough to get their foot out of purgatory, nor could it prevent God from "hurling them out of their place" (Prov. 1).

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2250.xhtml}

CHAPTER 28

28:1 Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.

2 Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.

3 He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.

4 The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.

5 As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.

6 The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.

Job is still giving us a PARABLE (27:1 and 29:1); therefore, we are to look for more than vague generalities and pious maxims on "mankind." The subject of the passage is the value and the rarity of "the fear of the LORD." In reading the passage, the Bible believer should keep Proverbs 1:7 clearly in mind (see comments in that Commentary) and should never be seduced for a moment into accepting the Scofield reference note on the matter (see p. 609, footnote 2 in the ["New" Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3759}). Scofield (as Rice, McClain, Sumner, English, Robertson, and others) was just as liable to ["have men's persons in admiration because of advantage"]{.class_3760} (Jude 16) as Wilbur Smith, John Walvoord, Charles Feinberg, Pat Zondervan, William Culbertson, or E. S. P. Heavenor. "The fear of the LORD" is ["THE FEAR OF THE LORD,"]{.class_3760} and any attempt to make it a "reverential trust," by aborting 1 John 4, is just so much HPD.

Job begins by taking us below ground. Some of the expressions are figurative and some are not (see 20:16 and remarks); so it will take a believing heart and a receptive mind (and a sensitive spirit) to allow the Holy Spirit to lead us at this point. Anytime we allow any man to change the words in the passage to enforce his own interpretations (see above), we will grieve the author of the Book (Eph. 4:30), and when we "despise His prophesyings" (1 Thess. 5:20), we "quench the spirit" (1 Thess. 5:19). Let the bloated and egotistical puffed heads in the institutional monuments do this, but let us not do it.

"[There is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it"]{.class_3760} (vs. 1). The verse is immediately altered by Adam Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, Lange, the [ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV ]{.class_3761}(corrected before it was published), Orelli, Duhm, Budde, and the Scholars Union. ["Iron is taken out of the earth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2) is plainly a figurative statement like Deuteronomy 8:9. Brass is formed by copper being melted with stone ("calamine"---zinc), and only in this sense could it be ["molten out of stone"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2). The alteration of ["brass"]{.class_3762} to "copper" (see the last forty-six translations) has been commented on under Exodus 25:3, which see. The idea is that "zinc" is a [modern ]{.class_3761}invention; therefore the ancients could not have known, etc., because of their lack of scientific knowledge, etc., which modern man, etc., etc. Since we have already covered these matters twice (see Acts 8:9--11 and Exod. 28:17--19), it is not necessary to point out again that where the Bible says ["brass"]{.class_3762} it is to be taken as ["brass."]{.class_3762} However, this maniacal obsession for changing ["brass"]{.class_3762} to "copper" (talk to any professor who teaches "Old Testament" in most Fundamental schools) affords the serious student of the Bible a real opportunity to make the scholars "whistle Dixie." You see, the word for ["brass,"]{.class_3762} here, has been translated as ["BRASS"]{.class_3762} in Revelation 1:15 by the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901), the [NASV ]{.class_3761}(1953), the [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_3761}(1967), and many others. After correcting your New Testament more than 3,000 times with the corrupt "Septuagint manuscripts" ([Vaticanus ]{.class_3761}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_3761}---[Vaticanus ]{.class_3761}and [Siniaticus ]{.class_3761}are called "SEPTUAGINT MANUSCRIPTS" by Zondervan Publishing Company \[1970\]), the scholars (God have mercy on us!) forgot to tell you that the Greek word in Revelation 1:15 is the same one used here in Job 28:2---["BRASS!"]{.class_3762}

When in doubt, throw 100 percent of "recognized scholarship" OUT.

Verse 1 has been altered in 99 percent of the commentaries because "scholarship" (hail Mary!) decrees that all four clauses in verse 1 and 2 [must ]{.class_3759}match. That is, they all must make reference to WHERE the metal came from, not where it is "refined." Again, if the destructive critics of the [AV ]{.class_3759}text would be consistent (which they never have been through three centuries), they would point out to you that the word for ["gold,"]{.class_3760} here (Heb. ["zahab"]{.class_3759}), has no reference to gold in its raw state at all (see Job 28:17). This gold has been made clear and bright by refining and polishing (Clarke, p. 126). Since this is true, 99 percent of the scholars simply by-passed "the original Hebrew text" and then changed the Bible verse to suit themselves: typical.

"[Silver\...gold\...iron\...brass"]{.class_3760} (vss. 1--2). Wouldn't our Conservative mocking birds have done much better to refer the reader to Daniel chapter 2, where all four metals appear, [including the clay ]{.class_3761}(look at Job 28:5)? With the lion and the vulture both present (vss. 7--8) and Ethiopia and Ophir in the context (vss. 16, 19), how did our little "qualified" playmates miss the obvious connections with ["DESTRUCTION AND DEATH"]{.class_3762} (vs. 22), and the lightning (vs. 26) and thunder (vs. 26), of Daniel's Seventieth Week? They were just told that Job's discourse was a PARABLE (27:1). How then did they miss every reference? Job is refined as gold (23:10) exactly as Israel will be refined in the Tribulation (Isa. 48:10) when the Refiner (Mal. 3:2) sits in Judgment (Mal. 3:3). You see, the reference to ["gold"]{.class_3762} (vs. 1) had to do with the PLACE where gold is refined, and if one thing is clear (in spite of the blind leadership of Bob Jones University, Dallas Theological Seminary, the Lockman Foundation, the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_3761}, Moody Bible Institute, and Wheaton College), it is clear that Selah, Petra, in the land of Edom (Lam. 4:22), is the place where the[ "gold"]{.class_3762} will be refined (Zech. 13:9). And if the last thirty-five "dedicated, separated, godly, devoted" scholars altered verse 1 to match their pig-headedness, all that you should do about it is "phase 'em out."

"[He setteth an end to darkness\...the stones of darkness, and the SHADOW OF DEATH"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). (See comments under Job 10:21.)

Having botched up verse 1, the Scholars Union cannot resist the momentum of their downgrade movements. Verse 3 is now altered to mean that "man" has "set an end of darkness." This remarkable perversion which takes the glory from God (Rom. 1:21) and gives it to "man" is found in every major revision and commentary since 1800. Dummelow says that the ["HE"]{.class_3760} is a [miner]{.class_3759}, and so assents Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Lange, Williams, and the rest. The corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901), tolerated by Tom Malone, John R. Rice, Bob Jones III, et al., simply erases the Hebrew text (["He"]{.class_3760}) and writes "MAN." This blatant [lie ]{.class_3759}will also be found in the so-called ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759}, the [RSV]{.class_3759}, [NRSV]{.class_3759}, [NASV]{.class_3759}, and the Brand New, International, Standard, New Authorized, New Living Revised American DEAD SKUNK.

Now consider the calamity which must follow, if we accept the absurd interpolation of the Fundamental, Liberal, Catholic, and Conservative scholars who devoted their lifetimes to correcting the Bible:

1\. MAN does [not ]{.class_3759}overturn mountains by the roots (vs. 9).

2\. MAN does [not ]{.class_3759}see every precious thing (vs. 10).

3\. MAN does [not ]{.class_3759}["cutteth out rivers among the rocks"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10).

"Man" may [dynamite ]{.class_3761}his way [through ]{.class_3761}a small mountain, or he may blast a highway through [part ]{.class_3761}of a mountain. Man may see what God [allows ]{.class_3761}him to see, and man may find a river or spring that is [already there ]{.class_3761}["among the rocks"]{.class_3762}; but man [in his natural state ]{.class_3761}(according to the Holy Spirit, instead of "qualified authorities") is an [evil ]{.class_3761}(Luke 11:13), [unclean ]{.class_3761}(Job 25:4), [filthy ]{.class_3761}(Isa. 64:6) [JACKASS ]{.class_3761}(Job 11:12; Exod. 13:13), and if you think that God will bless translations that steal the glory from God (Job 28:3) and ascribe the glory to an evil, unclean, filthy JACKASS, you don't know enough about God or the Bible to discuss either with anyone, let alone look down your perverted nose at Christians who don't have "college educations." Hold your tongue instead, professor, and then at least people will THINK you're smart (Prov. 11:12).

Now it is not "man" who accomplishes the things which follow (vss. 3--9); it is [God ]{.class_3759}who ["searcheth out all perfection"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). No surer proof of depravity has ever been found in the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) or the [NASV ]{.class_3759}(1953) and t [Living Bible ]{.class_3759}(1971) than the perverse motives of their authors in making the word ["HE"]{.class_3760} in verse 3 refer to a human "miner." The day you find any miner who ["searcheth out ALL PERFECTIONS"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3) will be the day that the Kennedy family goes Premillennial. Jamieson knew this, so he quietly alters ["searcheth out all perfection"]{.class_3760} to "most thoroughly searches" (p. 66). (Ah yes, Jamey baby, we can count on YOU now, can't we!?)

The last time men refused to "give the glory to God" (Rom. 1:21), God gave them up, and when the old Easter-observing Roman, Herod (["EASTER,"]{.class_3760} Acts 12:4, not "passover" as in the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}and [NASV]{.class_3759}), refused to give God the glory, God decked him (Acts 12).

You see, it is God who does all the seeing and looking (vs. 24), not [man]{.class_3759}. If anyone searcheth anything out unto ["all perfection,"]{.class_3760} it certainly will not be "Man"---the Pagliacci of the solar system.

"[The flood breaketh out\...even the waters forgotten of the foot"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3761}) simply obliterates the whole verse because Adam Clarke said (one hundred years ago!) that "this passage is very difficult." The pitiful [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901) has miners "swinging by ropes" in "shafts" at this point (vs. 4), and the [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3761}(with Dummelow and the [NEB]{.class_3761}) picks up the same refrain. So, this is how you make the word ["HE"]{.class_3762} in verse 3 refer to a "miner." You just alter verse 4 so that it does refer to a miner. A miner "swinging on a rope in a shaft," and from there on it is easy: You just keep on altering [every word in the next ten verses ]{.class_3761}until the deceived reader thinks it [IS talking about a miner.]{.class_3761}

But why not make it a [paratrooper ]{.class_3759}swinging on a shroud line?

Or why not make it a [necktie party ]{.class_3759}with a [cowpoke ]{.class_3759}hanging on a limb in "Miller's" cave?

Or how about three astronauts trying to find the original manuscripts on Venus?

Or, to be "more accurate" and "clearer" (at the same time), why not just say: "The folks in the coal mines of West Virginia would have hung by their necks if old man Kennedy hadn't bought the State out with his bootleg money?" After all, if you can change eight words, what is wrong with changing eighty?

A. The FLOOD (vs. 4) [did ]{.class_3759}break out from the ["inhabitant"]{.class_3760} in Genesis 7:11, and we were told this so clearly that only R. A. Torrey, Sampey, Wuest, Delitzsch, Keil, Gesenius, Kolman, Harkavy, Dummelow, Budde, Dillman, Schlotmann, Ewald, Terry, Robertson, and Monser could have missed it.

B. The WATERS (vs. 4) [were ]{.class_3759}["forgotten of the foot,"]{.class_3760} for no foot walked where they lay for 370 days (Gen. 8:13); and you were told this so clearly that only the Old Testament faculty at Bob Jones, Clearwater, Trinity, Piedmont, San Francisco, Fort Worth, Dallas, Moody, Wheaton, and Pillsbury could have missed it.

C. These waters [did ]{.class_3759}dry up and went[ "away from men"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4), and you were told this so clearly in Genesis 8:1 and 2 that only the last 40,000 Fundamentalists who believed in the "plenary inspiration of the verbally inspired originals" would have had the nerve to botch the text, overthrow the Holy Ghost, [insult the Author of Scripture, sell out their birthright, make a liar out of God]{.class_3759}, and make a God-dishonoring shambles out of "Christian" education.

The New "INTERNATIONAL" Version is just more of the same, only a little worse.

[Always correct ANY translation with the Authorized English text. ]{.class_3759}Never worry about respecting Bible-rejecting oafs who think they "deserve" your respect (or are worthy of it) simply because they spent forty years studying Greek and Hebrew, as did Briggs and Robertson. The only claim these disgraceful reprobates have to fame is that they recognize that God once wrote a Book that He lost.

"[As for the earth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5)---showing us that verse 4 is also a veiled reference to the celestial phenomenon of 2 Peter 3:5---["out of it cometh BREAD"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). Notice again the [figurative ]{.class_3761}use: Bread only "comes out of the earth" (now) in the sense that bread is made from corn, barley, rye, and wheat, which grow on the earth. However, one must be careful, for "stones into bread" (Luke 4:4) could be a simple operation (Luke 4:3) at the Second Advent (John 6:32--50) when the Jews are in the wilderness (Rev. 12:14) [where Job was ]{.class_3761}(Job 1:1; Lam. 4:21).

"[And under it is turned up as it were fire"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5), showing that the core of the earth is ["set on fire of hell"]{.class_3762} (James 3:6). There are a variety of opinions about how far one can "dig" before the tools begin to melt, but the general consensus of opinion seems to be that when you get down to 1,500 miles deep, the temperature is about 3,000 degrees Centigrade which is not exactly the best weather in which to build snow men. There is fire, however, much closer to the surface than this, and there is plenty of fire in volcanoes that do not even lie deep as the gold, stones, and sapphire of the next verse.

"[The stones of it\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 6)---of the earth (see vs. 5)--- are connected with the ["stones of fire"]{.class_3762} (Ezek. 28:16), and, therefore, have some reference to Satan's original position beside the Seraphim (Isa. 6:1--6), but these ["stones of fire"]{.class_3762} (Ezek. 28:16) were on a mountain (Exod. 19--20 and comments) originally. Much about the passage, therefore, is still a mystery. When it is worked out, it will give information on "The Hollow Earth" and the beings which are there [at this moment]{.class_3761}. (The contemporary authors all take a POSITIVE view towards the ["principalities"]{.class_3762} and ["powers"]{.class_3762} of Ephesians chapter 6, although they were warned that these are subject to Satan. The authors, for example, of the last ten books on "visitors from outer space" and "our neighbors in neighboring planets" \[Frank Edwards, John Fuller, Von Daniken, R. L. Dione, Charles Berlitz, John Godwin, Vinecent Gaddis, and Erich Norman, et al.\] all take the positive view towards what the Bible calls fallen "ANGELS," half--breed "GIANTS," and monsters (Rev. 9:1--15). An unsaved man can no more detect unrighteousness in "scientific advancement" than a Roman Catholic can locate the Son of Perdition.)

28:7 There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture's eye hath not seen:

8 The lion's whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.

9 He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.

10 He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.

11 He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.

We shall confine ourselves mainly to the text, as it should be apparent by now (1978) that the majority of Bible teachers, college professors, revision committees, commentators, linguists, and expositors have junked the Reformation text for the radical fanaticism of the "majority of Conservative lunatics." This egotistical extremism can be spotted wherever it pops up by the following marks:

1\. Its practitioners always throw kisses at the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}while they are [destroying ]{.class_3759}or [correcting ]{.class_3759}it. (Obtain the one and onehalf hour tape put out by BJU---Panosian, Custer, Neal---in 1974.)

2\. They all subscribe to the Mickey Mouse scholarship of Westcott and Hort (see Custer's remarks on [Which Translation]{.class_3759}, a paperback from the BJU press).

3\. They all blabber, long and loud, about "VERBAL INSPIRATION" while writing or [rewriting ]{.class_3759}their own "bibles." (See the [NASV]{.class_3759}, recommended by Bob Jones University, or the [New International Version]{.class_3759}, recommended by other apostates.)

4\. They all USE the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}to get students or church members because they don't have enough spiritual guts or grace to follow their real convictions [publicly]{.class_3759}. (See the advertisements in [Faith for the Family ]{.class_3759}magazine published by the BJU press, or advertisements in the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_3759}.)

5\. They consider their own "preference" and "choice" of texts to be the final court of appeals and the determining factor in matters of [final authority]{.class_3759}: That is, they are HIPPIES AND HEDONISTS, regardless of their profession of faith (see any remarks by Kenneth Wuest or John R. Rice on mistranslations in the [King James Bible]{.class_3759}).

6\. They will accuse any Bible believer of worshipping the [AV]{.class_3759}, if he really believes it, and many of them will get infuriated (to the point of pounding, stamping, and screaming) when you put them on the spot about their attitude toward the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611).

7\. They will go to ANY length to prevent the average, "uneducated" Christian from finding out the FACTS that deal with [manuscript evidence ]{.class_3759}and the TRUTH about modern "bibles."

Take the first 4,000 that come to your mind (blindfolded), and you won't miss more than [ten]{.class_3759}.

It is one, huge, baloney, sausage factory from Origen to Zodhiates, turning out 3,000 graduates every year who would look as "at home" garnished with mustard and mayonnaise as they would with diplomas and degrees. This mammoth string of baloney sausage looks, feels, smells, tastes, and [costs ]{.class_3759}the same. No amount of historical facts, manuscript evidence, revival fruit, or chastening has ever (or ever will) convert ONE man in the crew. And the majority of them today will insist that "Ruckman thinks he's right and everyone else is wrong," even though they know they are lying like a dog when they say it. After all, what I think or what YOU think or what your professor thought doesn't amount to a hill of sand: The [Bible ]{.class_3759}is right, and where [anyone ]{.class_3759}disagrees with it, [they ]{.class_3759}are wrong. And when we say "BIBLE," we mean "BIBLE," not some mythological spook you are trying to "validate" by quoting the [King James Bible]{.class_3759}. We have the "Bible." If you don't have it, that is your problem.

"[There is a path which no fowl knoweth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7), and since nothing living knows about this path (vs. 21) and only God knows where it is (vs. 23), how could any "miner" find it---like the silly one in the [ASV]{.class_3761}, [NASV]{.class_3761}, and the [New International Version]{.class_3761}? The vulture's eyes (vs. 7) have not seen this path, nor have the lions ["passed by it"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). The One who puts forth His hand and removes rocks and overturns mountains (vs. 9) knows where this path is (vs. 23), and a "miner" hanging by a rope in a "shaft" (see the silly [ASV]{.class_3761}, 1901) could no more find this path than you could find Attila's left ear lobe.

It is GOD who ["cutteth out rivers among the rocks"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10); (Psa. 126:4; Isa. 33:21, and 35:6) literally, and you could no more "cut out" a river than you could "carve out" a tornado. You can cut out a [place ]{.class_3759}for a river to run, and you can cut out [channels ]{.class_3759}and [canals ]{.class_3759}so water can flow [through ]{.class_3759}them, but "cut out rivers"? Not on your life, "miner"! God's eye ["seeth every precious thing"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10), for the ["eyes of the Lord run to and fro"]{.class_3760} (2 Chron. 16:9), and ["there is no shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves"]{.class_3760} (Job 34:22). God knows where the gold, silver, copper, oil, and gas deposits are, and before a nation sides against Israel to get gas and oil, they had better take a long look at Psalm 83 and then let the God of the deposits "search things out" for them (vs. 3). After all, if that coin in your pocket says "In God We Trust," the government who minted it had better not trust [man ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 118:8).

"[He bindeth the floods from overflowing"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). (See Job 26:10, 30:10; Jer. 10:13, 51:16; Psa. 69:15, 104:6, and 136:6.) The waters cannot fall from the "great deeps" again (Gen. 1:1--7), nor will they overturn the earth (2 Pet. 3:6--7), as they did in Genesis 1:2 (yeah, man! That "gap theory" is about as "theoretical" as regeneration by the Holy Spirit). If God released the power of centrifugal force and gravity, don't you know there would be a mess of wet folks on this earth? ["And the thing that is hid bringeth HE forth to light" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 11). Again, the ["HE"]{.class_3762} is no more a [miner ]{.class_3761}than it is your Aunt Salley's housecat. \[You say: "How then did all these good, godly, dedicated Fundamentalists make such a horrible mistake in translating?" Simple: [They thought they were God Almighty when they sat down to correct the text]{.class_3761}. [Pride ]{.class_3761}and [jealousy ]{.class_3761}will make an ass out of a sheep every time, and if that sheep happens to believe in the Virgin Birth \[as the pope\] and the Deity of Christ \[as Bloody Mary\] and the Bodily Resurrection \[as Ignatius Loyola\], it has no more bearing on [his ability to translate ]{.class_3761}than on his ability to fix a car motor, if he seeks to replace the authority of God with his own stupidity.)

Luke 12:2 explains verse 11, and God not only brings these "hidden things" (words, deeds, thoughts, and motives) to ["light"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11), but He also brings up the occupants of Death and Hell (Rev. 6:8 and 9:1--15) where you can see them (Rev. 9:1--10) before they are exposed to the white heat (Rev. 20:13) of God's final judgment (1 Cor. 6:3). And the thirty corrections suggested by the [ASV, NASV, International, RSV]{.class_3759}, and [NRSV ]{.class_3759}on the text are about as helpful to proper exposition as a Pennsylvania Railroad timetable.

28:12 But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?

13 Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.

14 The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.

15 It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.

16 It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.

17 The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.

18 No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.

19 The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.

Verses 12 and 20 both ask the same question: The problem is "Where can a man get wisdom and understanding?" (vs. 12). The Greeks seek for "wisdom" (1 Cor. 1--3) by altering or ignoring the Wisdom Books (see comments in the Preface to the Book of Proverbs), which were written by God to give a man "wisdom." At the end of this chapter, Job defines ["wisdom"]{.class_3760} and ["understanding,"]{.class_3760} and one cannot fail to notice that "wisdom and understanding," as defined by the Scriptures (within their own context), are further removed from the modern American concept of "wisdom and understanding" than Winnipeg is removed from Tahiti.

Verse 13 is self-explanatory. Neither the ["depth"]{.class_3760} nor the ["sea"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14) contain the knowledge (see Job 26:5 and 41:21--32); therefore all attempts to "launch out into the deep" (Rom. 10:6--7) to "tap the hidden resources of the ocean bottom," to find the "lost continent of Atlantis," or to "adapt man to his watery environment" are just so much Moon-money (\$25,000,000,000) sunk into liquid darkness. The answer is not in the sea (Rom. 6:7), and if it was, it would cost you a fortune to find it (Deut. 30:13). The price of wisdom is too high for the gold standard (vs. 15). You can't buy it with silver (see Prov. 3:14, 8:10, and 16:16). Sapphires and onyx stones (vs. 16) cannot purchase it; jewels and crystal cannot purchase it (vs. 17). You cannot even speak in the same breath of WISDOM and such paltry items as coral, pearls, rubies, topaz, and GOLD (vss. 18--19). Alongside real WISDOM these items are just so much "flea market."

A wise man can turn \$15.00 into \$15,000, and a foolish man can easily turn \$15,000,000 into 15 cents. Even if it had taken the zoophytes and rhizopods 400 centuries to make a coral reef (some are 100 miles long and 100 feet deep), they would have gone to less work than it takes to obtain real wisdom. A jewel of gold (vs. 17) can be found in a swine's snout (Prov. 11:22), and pearls have often caressed the necks of demoniac monarchs and filthy queens who had the moral standards of an alley cat. A pearl bedecked crown or a pearl strewn necklace (like the ones that Bloody Mary, Marie Antoinette, King Charles II, Louis XIV, and Pope Urban wore) does not guarantee that a ruler will be anything more to a kingdom of people than a curse or a plague. (A description of ["gold\...onyx\...sapphire\...crystal\...fine gold\...coral\...pearls\...rubies\... topaz\...and pure gold"]{.class_3760} \[vss. 16--19\] will be found on page 126 of Clarke's commentary.)

28:20 Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?

21 Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.

22 Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.

23 God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.

24 For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;

25 To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.

26 When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:

27 Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.

28 And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.

The passage concludes with the Biblical definition of ["wisdom"]{.class_3760} and ["understanding."]{.class_3760} You will notice that it bears no resemblance whatsoever to the definitions offered to college students in the last 6,000 years by psychiatrists, educators, scientists, philosophers, and religio-political "bosses." (And don't give us this "Christian educators" bit, off the cuff. The Scofield Bible redefined ["the fear of the Lord"]{.class_3760} many years ago, and Christian educators, in general, accepted this substitution for the truth of God as quietly and as cheerfully as Bob Jones University accepted the [RSV ]{.class_3759}New Testament into their campus bookstore \[1950--1951\].) ["Whence\...where\...?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20). This is one of the hallmarks of the Book of Job (Job 3:23): "WHERE IS WISDOM?"

It is not at the roots of the mountains (vs. 9); it is not in the rocks (vs. 10); it is not under the water (vs. 11); it is not on top of the ground (vs. 7); and it is not in ["the land of the living"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13), for men ["DIE, EVEN WITHOUT WISDOM"]{.class_3760} (4:21)!

"[Destruction and death"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22) are plainly [figurative]{.class_3761}, although Death can be personified as in Revelation 6:8 and Destruction can be personified as in Revelation 9:11. Destruction and Death cannot "clue us in" on the secret of wisdom and understanding. Who can then? The "miner" of the [NASV ]{.class_3761}and the [International ]{.class_3761}muggle moggle? Surely that "miner"--- caught in his suspenders in a mine shaft---can tell us where wisdom is. After all, didn't he already "search out" every hidden thing to "all perfection?" [C'mon, you old coal hog, give us the pitch!]{.class_3761} The Lockman Foundation and the "Conservatives" on the International "Bible" Committee got us into the chapter; can't they get us [out?]{.class_3761} No, there is no miner, living or dead (on any continent), who knew the place of wisdom.

"[God understandeth the way thereof"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23).

And you can search the Library of Congress with a miner's lamp and a coal bucket till you are red, white, blue, and black in the White House; and you will not find "wisdom" and "understanding" with a pick and shovel unless God Almighty reveals them to you, and that goes for any of you or anyone that taught you or anyone you "read after," world without end, amen.

Tanchelm, the great Medieval preacher, was once pacing the steps of a cathedral. It was near midnight, and Tanchelm had been praying all day for God to send him someone to teach him something about the Bible. In those days, Bibles were almost impossible to get, and as the stygian darkness of the "qualified authorities" (who "believed in the fundamentals") settled down over Europe, there was a famine in the land (Amos 8:11). It was a cold winter night, and as Tanchelm paced the flagstones he shivered. At about one in the morning, he made a passing comment to a beggar who had huddled up by a huge torch on the leeward side of the cathedral.

"How goes it with thee, friend?" asked Tanchelm.

"It always goes well with me, sire," replied the beggar.

"Well, what if it pleased God to throw thee in Hell, how would that please thee?"

"Well, I'd be happier in Hell with Him than in Heaven without Him," responded the beggar.

Tanchelm quit pacing, forgot his unanswered prayer, and went over to the beggar. Stooping down he asked, "Who are you, anyway?"

The beggar looked out from under his cowl and said, "I'm a king."

"Where is your kingdom?" snapped Tanchelm.

"In here!" said the beggar, and he pointed to his own bosom.

Tanchelm stood up straight and said quietly, "When did you find THAT out?"

"When I ceased to go to men for wisdom," replied the beggar, "and went to God only."

That was Tanchelm's "Bible study" for that evening!

It is God (not some "coal miner") who ["looketh to the ends of the earth and seeth under the whole heaven"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24). It is God who makes the weight for the winds (see Psa. 135:7), and if the stupid scientists didn't find out until A.D. 1650 that air had weight, that only constituted one more testimony (among several thousand) that the man who rejects the Bible has neither wisdom nor understanding, no matter what his reputation may be or how many "degrees" he holds. ["He weigheth the waters by measure"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25) because this is the only way you can get the salt out of salt water and get it back up over the land to rain down on the earth again. Salt water weighs 800 times as much as the atmosphere, so how do you get it UP? Well, it turns to vapor under the sun (thus getting rid of the salt), and then it becomes one-eighth the weight of the atmosphere. It then rises in clouds; the clouds lose their heat over the land (see Ecc. 1:6--7, written 500 years before Archimedes or Aristotle cut their baby teeth) and drop fresh water back. (The Polar stream out of Baffin Bay goes under the Gulf Stream and then pops up in the West Indies to cool them off. These ["paths of the sea"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 8:8) were not even charted until the time of M. F. Maury \[nineteenth century A.D.\]. Ocean liners sail these "paths" today.)

The winds and waters have been weighed and put on this earth in their proper proportions; the proportion of nitrogen to oxygen is 79 to 21 at sea level or on top of the mountains. If this proportion were changed to NO ([Nitric Oxide]{.class_3761}), everyone on earth would be choking and strangling on gas. If these same two chemicals had accidently been combined (by "mother nature" and "evolution") so as to produce NO, everyone on earth would be hysterical all day long: NO is nitric acid! How would you like to live in that atmosphere? But, no. God [weighed ]{.class_3761}His chemicals out before He distributed them, and if Job knew about it before Archimedes knew a lever from a pulley, why would you think that a modern scientist knows as much as Bildad the Shuhite knew?

There IS a ["decree"]{.class_3760} for the rain, and the rain obeys God's decrees (see 1 Kings 18:45; James 5:17). There is a ["WAY"]{.class_3760} (not a "place") for the lightning and the thunder (vs. 26) just like there is a WAY (not a "place") for the ["light."]{.class_3760} When billions of positive charges, racing along on the ground under a thunderhead, make a "contact," the electrons from the thunderhead stream down making a tunnel (a ["way,"]{.class_3760} remember? not a "place") of ionized particles capable of conducting electricity. It takes about 1/1000th of a second for this tunnel to open, and then a stroke goes from the ground upward through the way (not a "place") 1,000 times faster. Then you see the contact as the stroke flashes down.

[God ]{.class_3759}has seen the place of wisdom (vs. 27); [He ]{.class_3759}has searched it out (vs. 27) to perfection (vs. 3) in spite of the bungling "miner" of the [NASV ]{.class_3759}trying to beat Him out of His inheritance, and God has declared it (vs. 27); so here it is!

1\. ["THE FEAR OF THE LORD\...is wisdom"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28).

2\. ["TO DEPART FROM EVIL is understanding"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28). (See Prov. 1; Deut. 4:6; Ecc. 12:13, etc.)

Now, for once, we are going to dispense with the "majority of qualified jugheads" who fancy they are smarter than God Almighty. You have gone far enough now through the Book of Job to know that these hobby horse riding fanatics cannot let ONE passage alone, [as it stands]{.class_3759}, in the greatest Book ever written, taught, or preached. And, as if their destructive nonsense were not enough abuse to God the Holy Spirit, they have, in addition, demanded that we Bible-believing people respect their "scholarship" ([whatever that is]{.class_3759}). Not content with mangling our Book, insulting our intelligence, and outraging the Author and Finisher of our faith, these discourteous oafs would have us commend them for their intellects, degrees, and labors and [respect ]{.class_3759}their efforts to overthrow our faith in the word of God. And what shall we say to these things? If God be for us (Rom. 8:31), who do these meatheads think they are? Let 'em rave! And if some of them think that we ourselves are rude, crude, coarse, vulgar, smelly, wicked, or ungodly for not bootlicking their Florsheims and buttering their bread, let them take a flying bite at a mud dauber's nest. God will settle with every one of them sooner or later (Rom. 2--3), and while their text books rot on the shelf, let us be up and about our Master's business: believing and putting into practice what He said.

(I mean, the very idea of anyone demanding that we "respect" the scholarship of some hair-brained idiot who thinks he is a BIBLE scholar just because he is a [saved sinner with forty years of formal education in Hebrew and Greek]{.class_3759}. Why, the very idea. When did God Almighty ever tell you in one verse, in either Testament, that [Biblical ]{.class_3759}truth and [Biblical ]{.class_3759}interpretation were dependent upon those things? [Where did you get that stuff from, anyway?]{.class_3759} From some saved sinner with forty years of formal education? Yes, that is about it, isn't it?)

"[The fear of the LORD"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28) means ["fear" ]{.class_3762}(Job 4:14), as in being flat scared (Exod. 20:20), after having the [tar scared out of you ]{.class_3761}(Exod. 23:27), when you were frightened [out of your wits ]{.class_3761}(2 Chron. 20:29). In this [frightful ]{.class_3761}condition (Matt. 14:26), you are [trembling ]{.class_3761}(Phil. 2:12) so that your [fear ]{.class_3761}(2 Cor. 7:11) is manifest to all (Rev. 11:11). ["THE FEAR OF THE LORD"]{.class_3762} means fearing God (Prov. 15:33), not man (Deut. 3:22), and it means being more worried about what God thinks (Psa. 25:12, 14) than what men think (Job 31:34). If you fear God (Psa. 119:74) more than you fear (Jer. 33:9) old age, cancer, excommunication, hospitalization, war, ridicule, famine, "recognized scholarship," poverty, or social ostracization, you will fear God properly (Job 1:1) and not improperly (Deut. 13:11). And the ["FEAR OF THE LORD"]{.class_3762} no more means "a reverential trust" (see any [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3761}) than it means "Little Orphan Annie."

"[To depart from evil"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28) shows ["understanding"]{.class_3762} because when a man sees the relationship of sin to God (Psa. 51:4), as the Prodigal saw it (Luke 15:18), he departs from it (Psa. 6:8; 14:1--5; 34:14, Prov. 14:16). When a man does NOT depart from evil (talk to any modern existentialist, phenomenologist, or pragmatist), it is because [he has no understanding ]{.class_3761}(see Prov. 13:19 and comments). Everyone who names the name of Christ is to ["depart from iniquity"]{.class_3762} (2 Tim. 2:19), and where a sinner fails to find wisdom and understanding, he is told, ["Depart from me, ye that WORK INIQUITY"]{.class_3762} (Matt. 7:23).

And as we have often said (and will continue to say, God giving us time and grace): "If your commentator, revisor, teacher, professor, or preacher didn't find THAT in the Book, he is either looking somewhere else for the truth or he is going out of his way to cover the truth up."

Exhibit A.

Here is a young sailor who has come out to have some "Christian Fellowship" at a young people's gathering on a Sunday evening after church. He himself is not a Christian. As a matter of fact, he is a professing atheist. I deal with him about forty-five minutes and get nowhere. Having decided that at least some response is better than none, I quietly suggest that Hobbes, Hume, Kant, Descartes, Heidegger, Sartre, Einstein, Huxley, Haeckel, Lorenz, Darwin, and Timothy O'Leary were a bunch of half-baked blockheads. I get my response. The young man pounds the dining room table hard enough to stop the conversations going on within ten feet of it and yells: "You're a hypocrite! No man could have read the books you say you've read and still believe the Bible."

I laughed at him and said, "Ah, nuts."

"You couldn't have read Darwin, Huxley, and Kant and believe what you say you believe."

"Okay," I said, "I believe the Bible from cover to cover, and if you don't think I've read Kant, Darwin, and Huxley, quiz me on them and see if I've read them."

He backed out; I closed in for the kill.

"Now I'll tell you what," I said, "I want to ask you two personal questions on the condition that you ask me the same two questions I ask you and that both of us answer them honestly without any fake humility or attempts to get out of the truth. That is, I'll ask them of you if you are man enough to answer them [honestly]{.class_3761}."

"Of course I'll do that," he said. "I'm man enough to answer [any ]{.class_3761}personal questions honestly!"

"All right," I went on, "You say that there is no God, no Ten Commandments, no heaven, no hell, no after life, and no absolute moral standards, right?"

"Right."

"Then, according to [you]{.class_3761}, you are the final judge [yourself ]{.class_3761}on matters of right and wrong, right?"

"Correct."

"Okay, I just wanted to get your position clear. Now here is the first question: Do you think---and answer honestly, no false modesty please!---[do you think that you have a pretty good mind]{.class_3761}?"

Without hesitation he smiled and said, "Yes, I am very intelligent." (Those near the table began to snicker and chuckle, but I got them quiet again.)

"All right," I went on, "question number two: Since you profess to have a good mind and you don't believe God gave it to you, how do you account for it? Where did your good mind [come from]{.class_3761}?"

He smiled again proudly and said, "It came with the equipment." (More chuckling from those nearby.)

I then bent forward and said, "All right, now you go on and ask me the same two questions."

"Oh but," he objected, "I already know what your answers will be."

"Doesn't make any difference," I said heatedly. "You said that you would ask the same questions if I would ask them, and I promised to answer them as candidly as you did; go on and ask."

"But," he insisted, "I know that you will say that\...."

"Whatsa matter?" I asked, ["scared?"]{.class_3761}

"Of course not!" he said. He then cleared his throat and smirking, in mock humility, he asked (in a very stilted fashion): "Do you think that YOU have a good mind?"

"[Yes,"]{.class_3759} I said, "Go on with the next question."

"But I know that\...."

"Go on and ask it if you don't have cold feet, fella."

"All right," he sighed. Then putting on his most effeminate air, he said, "And where did your good mind come from?"

"God gave it to me," I said loudly, ["and He could take it from me in fifteen seconds if He felt like it."]{.class_3761}

And you know what happened? There was a deathly silence for about five seconds, and that young buzzard turned as pale as a linen sheet. Then he excused himself from the table and left the party in thirty seconds flat. I found out later why he had made such a hasty exodus: He had been seeing a "shrink" at the Naval Air Station for about three months before he had come to that fellowship. He feared something; [he was scared stiff]{.class_3759}; he just didn't fear GOD, and the ["fear of the LORD, THAT is wisdom"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28).

Exhibit B.

I met another smart aleck from the Pensacola Naval Air Station---it's amazing how smart a young man becomes when he gets in a military installation 500 miles from his parents and his pastor---who was lecturing me on situation ethics, pragmatism, Malcolm Boyd, and Joe Fletcher, etc. He concluded an hour's wasted time with: "So you see, you Bible-believing people are [afraid ]{.class_3759}to face life. You 'Christians' must have absolutes to lean on, and thus you deny real religion; for it takes [faith ]{.class_3759}to face the [unknown future ]{.class_3759}without a crutch to lean on---like the 'Bible.'" (Ever hear that old pagan party line before?)

I had had enough for one afternoon (and besides, we were getting ready to go to a cornfield that day for a dove shoot, and the guy was holding me up); so I went into the bedroom and got out a .44 magnum revolver that one of my students had left there, and I came back into the room. I pulled back the hammer and said, "Now, there are no Ten Commandments, [right]{.class_3759}?" The brilliant young intellectual rared back on the sofa and said, "Of course not; what are you doing with that GUN!?" I stood in front of him and leveled the pistol and said, "Then you're just an animal, and I'm just an animal, and there are no absolute laws on killing, [right]{.class_3759}?" By now he was turning a little green around the gills, and he scootched up (a disyllabic, first ingressive, egotistical imperative from the riddcabobble particule "SQUINCHED") against the back of the sofa and hollered, "Put that away! It might be loaded1 Put it away! WHAT are you going to do?"

"Well," I said (lying like a rug), "since there is no judgment and no law and no heaven and no hell, I think I'll just blow your brains out. I don't like you anyway, and since I don't have a crutch to lean on anymore, I can just go ahead and shoot you down like a dog, and it'll come out all right." (And I think I had him convinced.) Listen! That [brave]{.class_3761}, bold, brilliant, enlightened, college educated, twentieth century, scientific, objective, pragmatic DUNCE began to shake like a maple leaf in a Northeaster. And I was only kidding. The gun wasn't even loaded---I hope it wasn't. I put the pistol away and gave him a gospel tract to read. He was glad to get it.

"[THE FEAR OF THE LORD"]{.class_3760} is not the fear of a gun or a bullet, and a man who is more afraid of a murderer or an assassin than he is of God lacks understanding.

Exhibit C.

When I first found the Lord Jesus Christ as my Saviour, I made a lot of mistakes in witnessing. I believe I made every mistake a man can make and made a few that he [couldn't. ]{.class_3759}I got "into the pool" and "got my feet wet," so to speak, and I committed every blunder in personal work a saved sinner could commit.

One time we were eating dinner in a wealthy home; there were about ten people around the table. In the middle of the meal the Christian lady of the house broke down crying. It seemed that her unsaved teenaged daughter (fifteen years old) had taken up company with a Roman Catholic girl (nineteen years old) whose father ran the only liquor store in a town of about 500 people. The two girls were, at this moment, out on the sun porch in their "halters" taking a sun bath. The mother had been praying for the salvation of her daughter for years. Suddenly the Holy Spirit got hold of her so badly that she broke down crying at the dining room table, and in front of her guests, she begged me to go out on the porch and deal with her daughter. I sized up the situation in about three seconds and tried to back out, but the mother insisted that I witness to the girl [then ]{.class_3759}and [there]{.class_3759}. The company (all of them saved people) began to eye me like I didn't have any "burden for souls," so finally (against my better judgment) I consented. I excused myself from the table and took my Bible out on the porch to deal with the girl. It was a "bad scene," man; like a "bummer."

Every five minutes the Catholic girl would interrupt the conversation: "Well, my mother was a Baptist, and she married a Catholic, and [she ]{.class_3759}says\...."

I would respond: "I'm not talking about Baptists and Catholics; I'm talking about getting [saved]{.class_3759}," and I would go on, showing the younger girl the plan of salvation.

"We've got a Bible too!" blurts out the Catholic, "and our Bible is not like [your ]{.class_3761}Bible!"

"You gotcher Bible on ya?"

"No, but\..."

"Fine Catholic you are, don't even carry a Bible on ya!" And on I went. Deeply under conviction, the younger girl began to cry, and as I was about to give her an invitation to receive Christ, the moonshiner's daughter yelled: "You see there! You've upset her now! You're not SOOTHING---[like the priest!"]{.class_3761}

Aarrggh! (Comic magazine cliché: 1950--1970.)

I'd had about enough; so I said something I should not have said, and it was certainly not a manifestation of any "wisdom" on my part. I turned on the Catlicker and said, "Well, sister, I don't know much about your religion (which was another lie! I knew more about her religion than she did!), but if my religion couldn't keep me out of the kind of clothes you're wearing, I wouldn't give you a dead horse for it. (She was dressed---if you could call it that---in about enough material to make a pair of "leggins" for a Jay bird.)

I turned on my heel and went back to the dinner.

Naturally, the "fish" got off the hook; I did not get a decision.

Now, if I had been one of those slick, smooth, professional, visitation experts, I would have certainly handled things better, wouldn't I? But you know something?
