Two months after that conversation, the liquor store shut down, the Catholic family pulled stakes, and that Christian mother's young daughter got saved. The last time I heard from them, her daughter was saved and had been teaching Bible in a local church for nearly fifteen years. She had heeded the word and broken up her fellowship with that liquor family, and God had honored her repentance: ["TO DEPART FROM EVIL IS UNDERSTANDING."]{.class_3760}

Exhibit D.

I preached in a pool hall in Greenville, S.C., one night. No one paid me much attention, which was rather unusual as these people usually listened to a preacher when he came in, at least they did back in the 1950s. When I left, I heard steps following me down the street. I stopped under a street lamp. A young man, who had been in the pool hall and had heard me, pulled up alongside me and said, "I heard you preach back there, and I want to apologize for those guys; they didn't treat you right."

"That's all right," I said, "I'm used to it; can't win 'em all."

"My mother is a Christian," he went on, "and she told me that before the Lord came back, it would be like this."

"Like what?" I asked.

"Oh, you know, preachin' in all kinds of places. The Bible says they will be preaching in pool halls before the second coming of Christ."

(Well, that was the first time I ever heard that eschatological "nugget," but I decided to let "well enough" alone.) We talked for a while about his own spiritual condition. There was a pause. He offered me a cigarette, which I refused. Then he said slowly, "You know somethin' preacher, I'm on my way to [kill ]{.class_3759}a woman tonight. Got mah gun right there!" He thumped his left chest and then pointed across the street straight westward.

"Well," I countered, "you better hadn't do it. No murderer has eternal life abiding in him, and it isn't worth anybody's hide to go to hell."

Then he talked openly. It was a familiar story: Estranged wife, boy friends around the house, she was suing him for child support, tried to break him, etc. I preached the gospel to him for about twenty minutes. He got under conviction, and he kept rubbing his left chest. Finally, he said (looking westward): "I'm going to prove that I'm a better man than that dirty so-and-so if I have to kill both of 'em!"

I said, "I know of a man who would make a better man dead than you would living."

"Who's that?" he asked belligerently.

"Jesus Christ," I said. I then took out my New Testament and showed him (under the street light) Luke 23:34--45. I read: ["Father, forgive them for they know not what they do"]{.class_3762} three times to him. "Now," I said, "you couldn't pray that prayer for [your ]{.class_3761}enemies because you're not man enough, and if it's the last thing you do, you ought to receive the Lord Jesus as your Saviour and let Him make a real man out of you."

To make a long story short, he claimed Romans 10:9--10, prayed out John 1:12 and Revelation 3:20, and when he got assurance from John 6:37 and John 5:24, he took out his loaded .38, emptied the cartridges from the cylinder, dumped them into my hand, and then walked off in the opposite direction (eastward).

"[To depart from evil is understanding."]{.class_3760}

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2419.xhtml}

CHAPTER 29

29:1 Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

2 Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;

3 When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;

4 As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;

5 When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;

6 When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock pured me out rivers of oil;

7 When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!

8 The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.

9 The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

10 The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.

11 When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:

12 Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.

13 The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.

Job continues his "parable" (vs. 1), showing that his life runs parallel to the life, or lives, of someone else (see Psa. 78). Chapter 29 is plainly a description of Job's better days when he lived "high on the hog and low on the chicken." Chapter 30 shows what happens when the "rubber meets the road," so to speak, and Chapter 31 is Job's justification of his moral conduct.

"[Oh that I were as in months past\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). And even though the Holy Spirit tells us that this is an unwise inquiry (Ecc. 7:10), in Job's case it is certainly true. Many of us only [pretend ]{.class_3761}that the "good old days" were better (see Exod. 14:12 and comments), but when real disaster strikes and tears us up, like it has riven Job, the former state (before the disaster) is always better in our way of thinking. The "old timer" is always a [relative ]{.class_3761}term because the "scoobydoobydoo" boys of the 1960's look at the hit tunes from 1950 as "Golden Oldies" and the hit tunes of the 1940s as "oldies but [goodies]{.class_3761}." The truth of the matter is that 1920 is not a long time back, and World War I largely determined the issues being discussed in the Near East by Kissinger (1976) and the Arabs. The "old days" are the days YOU have a hard time remembering. Some of you can remember when the "village SQUARE" was a place, not a person, when the "wonder drugs" were castor oil and mustard plasters, when a "bureau" was a piece of furniture, when people counted blessings instead of [calories]{.class_3761}, when you had to [marry ]{.class_3761}a dishwasher---not buy one, when a "car wash" was a bucket of soapy water, and when a "crystal set" was a radio receiver.

In the days of "The Shadow," "Burns and Allen," "Allen's Alley," "Backstage Wife," "Vic and Sade," "Ma Perkins," "Renfroe of the Mounted," and Jack Armstrong, a "compact" was what girls used to repair their faces with, and a window sill had a [flower box ]{.class_3759}in it instead of an air- conditioner. "On time" meant [punctuality]{.class_3759}---not ["time payments,"]{.class_3759} the "red threat" was long winter underwear, "delinquent" was a reference to a [library fine]{.class_3759}, a "trip" meant traveling by train or bus, and a "fix" meant to [repair ]{.class_3759}something. (My, my how times have changed. Terminology is "somethin' else," as they say down home.)

When Blake, Weigle, and Buttrick of the NCCC tell us that they are going to use the old Christian "terminology" ("Heaven," "Salvation," "Christ," and the "New Birth") without accepting [the Christian meaning of those terms]{.class_3759}, we can well believe them. The Logical Positivists at Vienna went through this years ago, and they decided that words can mean ANYTHING or NOTHING, depending upon how they wish to be used; for example, "Delousing Station" or "Shower Room" can mean "EXTERMINATION BY POISONOUS GAS"; "The Jewish Problem" can mean "Racial Genocide"; "Integration" can mean "enforced racial mixing by armed troops"; "Community sharing" can mean "confiscation of private property"; and "Environmental Ecology" can mean Federal confiscation of communications and industry. (See how it is done? You INVENT your own terminology to cover up SIN.)

But this is nothing new to those of us who have read the "creeds" being published by modern "Fundamental" Bible colleges and universities. We have seen this lying duplicity so many times in [Christian circles ]{.class_3759}that we are thoroughly adjusted to it. There are more than forty-five Bible schools in America whose articles of faith state that they believe the Bible IS the word of God, and in the same paragraph they will confess that the Bible WAS the word of God. They will change the [TENSE of a verb on you (while complaining about the translation of the aorist and the imperfections in the AV ]{.class_3759}1611) and go right on pretending that they believe what you and I believe. Well, some of us (and some of YOU) believe the Bible IS the word of God, and if you pin us to the mat like we pinned those lying scribes down, you won't get a recantation out of us; we have it---in our hands.

An "old timer," these days, remembers when "lights" (not people) were switched off-and-on, "rockets" were confined to the Fourth of July, only small boys feared the consequences of smoking, "to dig" meant to shovel dirt or sand, "neat" meant well organized or tidy, and a "pot" was something you cooked in. The real "old timers" remember when the idols were Maurice Chevalier, Clark Gable, Greta Garbo, Lillian Gish, Mary Pickford, Rudy Vallee, Rudolph Valentino, and Marlene Dietrich. Television has added somewhat in the reviving of these "gods," but time, taxes, tide, and apostasy wait for no man. They have long ago been replaced by Gene Hackman, Jane Fonda, Barbara Streisand, Carol Burnett, Johnny Cash, Jerry Lewis, Johnny Carson, Dean Martin, Liz Taylor, Lee Marvin, and Eva Gabor.

"[Oh that I were\...as in the days when God preserved me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). Well, the saints are [spiritually ]{.class_3761}preserved forever (Psa. 37:28, 29, 1 Thess. 5:23), but Job is speaking of [physical ]{.class_3761}and [material ]{.class_3761}welfare. No man knows when he is going to lose it all (see illustration above, Job 28:28), and any of us could lose our shirt, pants, and suspenders in twenty-four hours.

I once heard Ed Ames sing a lovely song called, "When the Dew is on the Roses." Knowing that the dew (Song of Sol. 5:2) and the roses (Song of Sol. 2:1) were borrowed from the Song of Songs (and that that song was written long before Tin Pan Alley began to bang on the tin pans), I decided to write my own words to the tune. They go like this:

"When the Spring of youth is in my bones, and my soul for sinful pleasure groans, I will plead with Christ my Saviour, who was tempted just like me; Let me not waste golden hours; Lord, I give my youth to thee."

"When the summer sun is shining high, and I have the power to sell and buy, I'll give thanks to Christ my Saviour for my wealth and health and breath, knowing all these joys were purchased through His poverty and Death."

"When my friends fade as the falling leaves, and my spirit sits alone and grieves, I'll remember Christ my Saviour, that Dear One who stands by me; how His dearest friends forsook Him when He hung on Calvary's tree."

"When the winter winds of death have blown, and my body lies as still as stone, I'll go home to Christ my Saviour, that Dear One who died for me, and I'll sing of death no longer, for I'll live eternally!"

God must preserve a man in all seasons, or he will rot.

"[When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3, cf. 30:26, 3:20, 18:6; Psa. 27:1, 43:3; and Prov. 4:18.) "AS I was in the days of my youth" (vs. 4, cf. 31:1--31 and Psa. 25:13--14.)

The words ["I"]{.class_3760} or ["ME"]{.class_3760} occur more than forty times in this chapter. Job is giving his autobiography to an unsympathetic audience. ["When the secret of God was upon my tabernacle"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) means that God's blessing was over his house, household, and his own tabernacle (i.e., his body---see 19:12 and 2 Cor. 5:1). ["When the Almighty was yet with me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5) in the sense of prosperity, blessing, and wealth, ["When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6). Notice again the figurative expressions as in Deuteronomy 32:13 and Genesis 49:11. Observe how the debauched [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901)---recommended for twenty years by Moody Bible Institute, Fuller Seminary, Wheaton College, Bob Jones University, and Mid-South---has left the reading (vs. 6) as it stands this time, [even though it is impossible to take literally]{.class_3759}. But they just finished altering Job 21:24 because they [couldn't take it literally]{.class_3759}! Typical "monumental scholarship" by "qualified authorities" whose "unquestioned loyalty" to the word of God would blah, blah, blah. ["Butter"]{.class_3760} shows that Job got plenty of cream from his herds, and ["oil"]{.class_3760} that he had the ["fatness of the earth"]{.class_3760} (Gen. 27:39) down in Edom, which was promised to Esau.

"[When I went out to the gate through the City"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). (See Gen. 19:1, 34:24; Deut. 22:15, 25; Josh. 8:29; Prov. 31:23, 31; and Amos 5:10, 12.)

Verses 8--13 are so clear that they need little comment. Job was a "big wheel" ("real boss," kingpin, top notch, wheeler-dealer, upper crust, dynamite brief, sheik, pasha, Mr. Big, etc.). Younger men stayed out of his way because his wisdom and experience overpowered them (vs. 8). Those of his own age stood up to honor him, exactly like a courtroom arises when a judge enters (vs. 8). Princes and nobles (vss. 8--10) who comprised the ["dukes of Edom"]{.class_3760} (Gen. 36) stopped their bull sessions when Job showed up, because they knew he didn't mince words, waste words, or misuse words (see "terminology" above). ["When the ear heard me, then it blessed me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). Notice the figurative language, again, which makes perfect allowance for the [King James ]{.class_3759}reading of Job 21:24. You don't think the nobles contracted literal "LOCK-JAW" in verse 10 simply because it says that their tongues ["cleaved to the roof of their mouth,"]{.class_3760} do you?

Job delivered the poor and the fatherless (vs. 12) and went to bat for them in court even at the cost of his own reputation (31:34). The widows and the condemned criminals (vs. 13) blessed his name because he helped the widows financially and probably pardoned criminals who were condemned to death (see Prov. 24:11 and comments). We understand, of course, that Job was not a B.C. Clarence Darrow or "Civil Liberties Union" lawyer: pardoning murderers (Num. 35) so they can get out and murder somebody else is one thing, and Bible mercy is another thing. Calling a rapist or a killer "mentally sick" so he can spend three years in hock and then be loose on the streets again is not the kind of "pardoning" the text is speaking of. Where a pardon is rejected, the sentence is carried out. There have been several cases where a governor pardoned a man on death row and the prisoner refused the pardon; he was executed. And we mention this because there are Calvinists, today, who are still deceiving their congregations into thinking that Christ's atonement was not sufficient enough for every sinner, on the grounds that if it WAS [everyone would eventually get saved]{.class_3759}. Wrong thinking. A pardon can be [available ]{.class_3759}and not be [accepted]{.class_3759}.

The [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) has changed ["candle"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3) to "lamp," ["youth"]{.class_3760} to "ripeness," ["secret"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) to "friendship," ["rivers"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6) to "streams," ["held their peace"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) to "voices were hushed," and ["judgment"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14) has been altered to "justice." (The grossly corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}made more than 30,000 changes in the Old Testament and New Testament as it followed the fradulent committee of 1881--1884 to its own destruction.)

The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---what a howl---[Bible ]{.class_3759}has erased the key to the whole chapter by refusing to translate or paraphrase (in a "paraphrase") the word ["PARABLE."]{.class_3760} The Scriptural word has simply been cut out of the chapter so that it is NOT a parable. There is no ["candle"]{.class_3760} in verse 3, no ["secret of God"]{.class_3760} in verse 4, no ["butter"]{.class_3760} in verse 6, no ["seat"]{.class_3760} in verse 7, no ["nobles"]{.class_3760} in verse 10, and no ["witness"]{.class_3760} in verse 11. That is, there ain't nothing but nothin' noway.

The [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_3759}(used by Bill Rice and others) has replaced the [Authorized Text ]{.class_3759}with the [ASV ]{.class_3759}reading of 1901 in verses 3--4 and 17, while still lying about their text. The text of the [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_3759}is NOT the [AV]{.class_3759}, and if the revision committee swore on a stack of grammars by Robertson, Davis, Trench, Moulton, Milligan, Mantley, and Machen that it was, [it still would not be.]{.class_3759}

The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(Lockman Foundation) has followed the blind leadership of the Bible-rejecting faculty at Bob Jones University and has altered Job's ["parable"]{.class_3760} to a "discussion." There is no such reading in any Hebrew or Greek manuscript discovered anywhere. The Hebrew word ["Mashil"]{.class_3759} has been translated as "parable" by the [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}in Psalm 49:4 and 78:2, exactly as Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3759}) translated it in Matthew 13:35.

The Scholars Union (Budde, Duhm, Peake, Lange, Dummelow, Williams, Kline, Heavenor, Feinberg, Fausset, Jamieson, Brown, and Bullinger) have about twenty more corrections they would like to make on the text. Kissinger, Schlessinger, E. C. Blake, Bishop Pike, Herman Reissig, Truman Douglass, Theodore Till, J. Carter Swaim, William A. Nogres, Dahlberg, and Robert Moon would have no objections to any of them. When it comes to resentment for the authority of the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759}, there is a happy spirit of "ecumenicism" between these leaders of the NCCC and the "godly" Conservatives of 1800--1989.

29:14 I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.

15 I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.

16 I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.

17 And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.

Job describes his work as a Circuit Court judge. He was clothed with honor (vs. 14) and righteousness (vs. 14), he went on errands for the crippled people (vs. 15), and he read information for blind people (vs. 15). He played the part of "father" to those who had no father (vs. 16), and he investigated cases until he knew the truth about them (see Deut. 17:4). Once he found out who the wicked were (vs. 17), he knocked their teeth out (legally speaking, vs. 17), and he restored to the innocent their lawful goods (vs. 17).

One cannot read the passage without seeing the exact duplicate in the New Testament---the Lord Jesus Christ. When Chapter 30 is reached (vss. 1, 9--10, 13, 17--20), the ["parable"]{.class_3762} has become so vivid that only a Bible-rejecting Conservative like Kenneth Taylor (recommended by Billy Graham) and the Lockman Foundation (recommended by Bob Jones III) would erase the key word ["PARABLE"]{.class_3762} from the verse (vs. 14). (That isn't slander, and it isn't rumour, and it isn't spite, and it isn't a "personality clash" or a "personal issue." That is the truth of God in black and white on [documented evidence ]{.class_3761}without any misrepresentation of anyone's position or intent. It is the truth of God as surely as Psalm 22 is the truth of God, and some of you Bible-perverting, Bible- rejecting Evangelicals will face Philippians 2:10 at the judgment and explain why you tried to erase references to the [person and work of the Lord Jesus ]{.class_3761}\[where they appeared in the Old Testament\] while shooting off your big, fat, lying mouth about "the Lord Jesus" being the "final authority." If He is the "Lord," why don't you hear His words \[John 8:47\]? And if He is the "Lord of Glory," were is His honor \[Mal. 1:6\]? And if He comes ahead of your church or school, or your income and your ministry, \[like you profess\], why do you elbow Him out of Job 29 and 30? Never mind. Don't answer me. You won't give account to me, anyway. You will give account to Him, you old liar.)

Observe, again, the figures of speech (vs. 17). Job did not reach into the mouth of the thief while he was chewing somebody's beefsteak and then pull it out and restore it to the guy who cooked it. The alibi, then, for altering Job 21:24 (see any Liberal or Conservative work) is a lame one if you ever limped.

Job was a good judge. Unlike Pilate, Job could not be corrupted with money or position (John 19:12), nor could his friends or "inlaws" (Mark 3:33) prejudice him for or against a case (Lev. 19:15). Like the Lord Jesus Christ (Mal. 3:1--4), he judged righteous judgment (John 7:24). The Lord Jesus was clothed (Isa. 59:17) with the accouterments of verse 14 (Isa. 61:10). He gave ["eyes to the blind"]{.class_3760} (Mark 10:52) and ["feet\...to the lame"]{.class_3760} (Mark 6:56), and he was a ["father to the poor"]{.class_3760} (Luke 7:14). Job's ["parable"]{.class_3760} is so parallel at this point that it remains a mystery for the ages how Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Kline, and Heavenor all failed to find mention of the One whom they profess to be exalting. But it is always amazing how many opportunities any commentator misses to exalt the Lord Jesus when he has, beforehand, determined to alter the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) to suit the fancy of the sinners who despised it.

29:18 Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.

19 My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.

20 My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.

21 Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.

22 After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.

23 And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.

24 If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.

25 I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.

Job continues his autobiography; he had said to himself that he would die a natural death of old age (vs. 18) with his children at his bedside (vs. 18, cf. Gen. 22:17 and Psa. 30:6--7.\] Like the ["blessed"]{.class_3760} man of Psalm 1:3 and Jeremiah 17:7--8, Job's root ["was spread out by waters, and the dew lay all night"]{.class_3760} upon his branches. (For men as "trees," see Ezek. 15, 17, 31; Matt. 7:17--19; plus Isa. 61:3.) Job's ["bow" ]{.class_3760}was renewed in his hand (vs. 20), meaning that Job's means of fighting for righteousness and protecting himself and his family was always in good shape; he got a brand new, 12 gauge, Browning semiautomatic every other year (see Gen. 49:24). Verses 21 and 22 are self-explanatory. Job's audience listened to him like they listened to Moses (Deut. 32:2), and they valued the refreshing powers of his speech as they valued a good rain in due season (Prov. 16:15). The "open mouth" of verse 23 is more than a "metaphor taken from ground chapped with long drought" (Clarke, p. 132), and if you doubt it, talk to anyone who has been adrift in a lifeboat or on a raft in the middle of the open ocean. Eddie Rickenbacker (1890--1973) could expound on the verse with much more clarity than Clarke or Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown. Louis Zamperini drifted on a raft in open seas for forty-seven days before being picked up by an enemy patrol ship and being thrown into a Japanese prison. If you think Louis didn't "open his mouth WIDE" (vs. 23) when a rain came down, you haven't read 1 Kings 18 too carefully. "Believe you me" (Yankee cliché: 1910--1950), when the rain finally comes (1 Kings 18:45) after three and one half years of drought (James 5:17), there will be people standing outdoors with their mouths open and their heads tipped backward at an eighty degree angle.

"[If I laughed on them, they believed it not"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24) has been altered to "I smiled on them when they had no confidence" in the miserable [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901). Umbreit has it as "I smiled kindly on those who trusted not." The word here for laughter is ["sachag"]{.class_3761} (Hebrew), and the word for "smile" is---the word for "smile" does not occur [in either Testament in any Greek or Hebrew manuscripts]{.class_3761}: "Smile" is the droll whimsy of Bible-rejecting Fundamentalists and Conservatives who have no more respect for the ["original]{.class_3761} Hebrew and Greek" (which they are shooting off their big, fat, stupid mouths about) than they have for a flea-bitten terrier. Every rascal who altered the God-given and God-preserved text to "smiled," translated the same word (Hebrew ["sachaq"]{.class_3761}) as ["LAUGH"]{.class_3762} in Psalm 2:4 and Psalm 52:6. Even Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3761}) and the Jehovah's Witnesses ([NWT]{.class_3761}) made the alteration. Why? Simple: Because those who rave and rant and roar about the "inferior text of the [AV]{.class_3761}" (see any nonsense written by F. F. Bruce, Gerald Hawthorne, Robert Mounce, John Skilton, Gerald Studer, J. Ramsey Michaels, William Sanford LaSor, or H. H. Ehrenstein) and the lack of knowledge of the [AV ]{.class_3761}translators of the "original languages" (see any bunko published by the "Conservatives" listed above) are some of the biggest, most bungling, most inconsistent hypocrites this world has ever seen in the realm of [BIBLICAL TRUTH]{.class_3761}. The reason for changing ["laughed"]{.class_3762} to "smiled" is that the modern Conservatives (see any baloney cut by the men listed above, especially the applesauce in [Eternity Magazine ]{.class_3761}on "Which Bible is best for YOU?") ["believed it not"]{.class_3762} (vs. 24), just like those who heard Job laugh. Isn't it a riot? The text defines the very unbeliever---oh sure, honey, they all believe in the "fundamentals;" they just don't believe the BOOK--- who didn't believe the text when it took place. How's that for "accidental placement" by the ignorant [King James ]{.class_3761}translators? You never saw Unitas, Tarkenton, Brodie, Jurgensen, Title, Baugh, or Bradshaw hit the target any better than that.

The truth is that nobody could alter Job's countenance when he felt like laughing; no matter what was going on ["the light of my countenance they cast not down"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24). Sometimes when Job laughed, he was [mocking ]{.class_3759}them (see Bullinger's paraphrase which is much better than Taylor's), but they couldn't believe it; people took for granted that Job (as God) would never laugh at anyone (see Prov. 1 and Psa. 2). So, the gullible translators (any version by anyone since 1800) have made Job "smile" so he won't offend the Christian Day-School-Parent-Teacher-Charismatic-Pink Lemonade-Iced Tea-Interdenominational-Bible Club-Kiddie-Car-leave us-your-money Club. Job DID laugh at the wicked exactly as God does (Psa. 2), because the Hebrew word here (["sachaq"]{.class_3759}) is a [scornful laughter]{.class_3759}. "Smiling" is out of the question, and nothing could be more irreverent or stupid than a man like William LaSor saying that versions should be compared with "THE ORIGINAL" and then telling you that the [RSV ]{.class_3759}is "exceptionally faithful to the Hebrew and the best Greek texts" ([Eternity Magazine]{.class_3759}). Why, any Hebrew text says LAUGH: [No Hebrew text says "smile." ]{.class_3759}Isaiah 37:22 and Psalm 37:13 describe the matter exactly, and the little devotional homilies of the Lockman Foundation and the ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759}, at this point, are about as Biblical and "accurate" and the [NASV's ]{.class_3759}dirty work on Mark 1:1--2 and Hebrew 9:27.

"[I chose out their way"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25) because Job passed judicial sentences that determined the future decisions of those who stood before him. A judge can decide the fate of a criminal for two to ninety years by merely opening his mouth.

J. Harold Smith had a woman come to him one time to confess as an accomplice in a killing. She and a man (both of them drunk) had killed a man in a barroom and had dragged the corpse to a tool shed beside an abandoned railroad track. No one had ever found the body, and the corpse had now rotted from age; it had been lying in the tool shed for nearly eight years. That woman said that she had just gotten saved a year ago, and since that time, her conscience had driven her to the brink of suicide. J. Harold said that he would tell no one of the matter, but he advised her to turn herself in; she did. She stood trial with her Christian husband standing beside her, both of them knowing that she might go to prison for life and that their plans for a happy Christian home would come to nothing. When the trial was over, it was discovered that the dead man was a derelict whom no one claimed, and the other accomplice had been killed in World War II. The judge had Brother Smith stand up with the man and his wife and said as follows: "Reverend, there is no one here to prosecute but the State, and the State's case shows that the killing was under circumstances that could not be called intentional murder. I am putting this woman into your custody, since I believe she is a genuinely converted Christian, and if she were not, she would not have turned herself over to the law. It is the opinion of this court that she be allowed her freedom, but that she be placed on probation for seven years. If at any time, in that time, she is involved in a major offense against the laws of the land, she will be committed to prison. If not, all charges will be dropped against her. Case dismissed!"

And that woman walked out of court with a Christian husband and lived (and is alive today) the rest of her life outside of the prison walls.

"[I chose out their way" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 25) Job says, and he is not "whistling Dixie."

Like Solomon, he dwelt ["as a king in the army" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 25; see David in 2 Sam. 18:3--4) and as a man who ["comforteth the mourners"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25). David and the Lord Jesus Christ both fulfill the last clause admirably (see 2 Sam. 9:1--10 and John 14:16, 26). Job had been the equivalent of a modern, morally righteous, respected, successful District Court Judge.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2485.xhtml}

CHAPTER 30

30:1 But now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.

2 Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?

The tables have turned; the "teeny boppers" and "hipsters" of the "drug culture" and the "street people" are making fun of Job. They point at him and wag their heads (Psa. 22:7) and probably thumb their noses at him. Job says that as far as their own character and quality goes, he wouldn't have paid their fathers to take care of his sheep dogs (vs. 1).

"[Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). That is, if Job hadn't bothered to fool with their fathers, would he fool with THEM? They wouldn't live to be "old" (see 36:14). ["Old age"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2) in them was ["perished"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2). While they went around beefing about the "older generation" and the uselessness of people "over thirty," they themselves were so profligate and undisciplined that most of them would be in the grave or a mental institution by the time they were twenty-nine.

30:3 For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.

4 Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.

5 They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)

6 To dwell in the cliffs of the valleys, in caves of earth, and in the rocks.

7 Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.

8 They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.

In what follows, there are veiled references to the "faithful" of Hebrews 11:36--39 who will, again, be on this earth in the Tribulation and will be hiding in the bushes (vs. 7) and cutting up ["juniper roots for their meat"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). Only this time, these outcasts and refugees are described from the viewpoint of Satan (see comments under vs. 7), at least until verse 9, and this is why we have divided the section as we have.

"[Want and famine"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3) are characteristic of the Great Tribulation (see Rev. 6:6; 13:17), and Israel does flee ["into the wilderness"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3) which was in former time ["desolate and waste"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3); see Revelation 12:14 and Hosea 2:14. The refugees of Matthew 24:16 will be ["driven forth from among men"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5), and they will ["dwell in the cliffs of the valleys, in the caves of the earth"]{.class_3762} (Rev. 6:16), ["and in the ROCKS" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 6). The passage is literally true where it touches the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week. The caves and rocks are in Selah, Petra, in the ["land of Uz"]{.class_3762} (Lam. 4:21; Job 1:1). (No, don't waste time with the material on this published by the major publishing companies; real "Bible believing study" would [bankrupt ]{.class_3761}the book selling business.)

"[Who cut up mallows"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). These are supposedly some herb or shrub like the "salsola" and they grow in a dry and sandy soil. \[The word for "mallow" here---see The [New English Bible]{.class_3761}---is NOT the word translated as "mallow" in Job 6:6 by the modern apostates. This word is ["malluach"]{.class_3761} (Hebrew) while the ["egg"]{.class_3762} in Job 6:6 is ["challamuth."]{.class_3761} Since the [AV ]{.class_3761}translators had a much better grasp of Hebrew than Bruce, Hawthorne, Ehrenstein, LaSor, Feinberg, Studer, Mounce, Michaels, or Skilton, we should not feel bad about rejecting any translation they recommend over the [AV ]{.class_3761}1611.)

"[Among the bushes they BRAYED"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7) is likening the cries of hungry fugitives to the braying of wild asses (see 24:5 and comments). These fugitives huddle together in the thorn bushes (vs. 7), and they are said to be ["viler than the earth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). And how similar all of this is to Hebrews 11:38. But [there ]{.class_3761}the fugitives and refugees are so much better than the "earth" [in God's sight,]{.class_3761} that it is said of them ["of whom the world was not worthy"]{.class_3762} (Heb. 11:38). If the people in Job 30:3--8 are the Antichrist's outfit---as they plainly are in verses 9--13---there is a strange double application here, for the saints of Matthew 24:22 will be spoken of in the same fashion by the Antichrist himself. Without getting involved so deeply that we cannot continue to plow in a straight line, let us make note that Lucifer was in Job's position (Ezek. 28:12, 15) before Isaiah 14:12--16. And the Antichrist's position at Revelation 20:1--4 will be that of Lucifer in Isaiah 14:15; this matches Job's description of himself in verses 29 and 30. And so closely does the Lord Jesus Christ resemble the ["serpent"]{.class_3762} (Num. 21:9, John 3:14) in His humiliation (Gal. 3:13; 2 Cor. 5:21), that it would take more than the Roman Catholic church has to offer to tell them apart (Psa. 22:6; Mark 9:46).

30:9 And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.

10 They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.

11 Because he that loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loss the bridle before me.

12 Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.13 They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.

14 They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.

15 Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud."

Job's position is not only the position of a man in Hell (see vss. 10, 18--19, and 30) and a picture of Christ in His sufferings (vss. 9--10, 12, and 20), he is also a perfect picture of Israel from A.D. 70 to the end of the Tribulation. Now, any man who was "at home" in the literature of Jewish history, or the history of the Third Reich, could not fail to see the similitudes. (Compare verse 9 with Ecc. 7:5 and Psa. 69:12.) The destruction of Jerusalem (Lam. 3:14) matches verse 9, and since this destruction (2 Kings 25) was a type of Roman destruction (Luke 21:24) and the United Nations destruction (Rev. 11:1--3), the ["byword"]{.class_3760} is the fulfillment of Deuteronomy 28:37, which see. ["They abhor me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) as they abhored the Lord Jesus Christ: ["They\...spare not to spit in my face"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) matching Isaiah 50:6 and Matthew 26:67. God did afflict (vs. 11) the Lord Jesus (Isa. 53:4), the Romans did ["push away"]{.class_3760} his feet (vs. 12 with Matt. 27:35), and they did mar his ["path"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13) when He had no helper; for they put nails through his feet (Col. 2:14).

When the "qualified authorities" (i.e., dunces who think they are smart enough to replace the word of God with a seminary "Christian" education) hit the ["cord"]{.class_3760} of verse 11, they "self-destruct" again and try "God's cord," "his cord," a "bow," a "bowstring," "my bowstring," "his bowstring," and God knows what, in an effort to explain what the Holy Spirit had hidden from them (see any translation recommended by anyone). Although the "yether" of Psalm 11:2 and 21:12 could be a bowstring, it could not be God's bowstring, and those contexts are NOT the context of Job 30:11. God's[ "cord"]{.class_3760} is the one that held his "TENT" in place, and if there were any doubt about this, observe:

1\. If it were "God's bowstring," no one shot anything, for you do not "loosen" a bowstring when you fire arrows: You loose the arrows.

2\. If it were Job's bowstring that was loosed, the passage would make more sense than No. 1, for Job did brag about a ["renewed bow"]{.class_3760} in Job 29:20; but here, Job is not discussing his "bow" or his "bowstring" or his means of defending himself (30:11). Job is talking about his FACE (vs. 10), his RIGHT HAND (vs. 12), his FEET (vs. 12), his PATH (vs. 13), his SOUL (vs. 15), his BONES (vs. 17), and his SINEWS (vs. 17). His ["cord,"]{.class_3760} then, obviously (yet not so obviously if you are as stupid as the [ASV, RSV]{.class_3759}, [NASV]{.class_3759}, [NIV]{.class_3759}, [NRSV]{.class_3759}, and the [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}translators) has something to do with his "EARTHLY TABERNACLE" (2 Cor. 5:1--10).

Now how do you explain the "recognized" scholars missing that? Why didn't they just consult the author of the Book to find out what the Author intended to say instead of running around like a bunch of rabid jackrabbits to invent a reading that had nothing to do with any "Bible"? I wonder how it is that 10 percent of them decided to close the lids of [the Bible ]{.class_3759}before seeing what [the Bible ]{.class_3759}had to say about [the Bible]{.class_3759}?

In Ecclesiastes 12:6 there will be found a cord that has been "loosed," and you could not have overlooked it unless you were reading a commentary instead of a Bible. Furthermore, the prophetic context of Ecclesiastes 12:1--2 is the Great Tribulation. If God has "loosed" Job's cord (notice God showing up as "HE" in the middle of Job 30:1--19, although He has not been mentioned by name for twenty verses), He did it preparatory to "breaking the golden bowl," the ["pitcher,"]{.class_3760} and the ["wheel"]{.class_3760} (Ecc. 12:6). Those who loosed their bridles and ran away from Job (vss. 10--11: ["flee far from me"]{.class_3760}) did so because Job was a man marked for death (see 6:21 and comments). The ["they"]{.class_3760} of verse 11 is the ["they"]{.class_3760} of verse 10, not the ["youth"]{.class_3760} of verse 12.

The tent cord of verse 11 is the "silver cord" that, hitherto, has been holding Job's tabernacle in place (2 Cor. 5:1--10); it has been "loosed," and the "tent" is now collapsing.

Now the ["youth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) of verse 1 rise up. In Job's life, this probably took place the year after God took his children, cattle, and property (see 1:13--22 and comments). The younger generation of Job's day pushed him out of society (vs. 12), blocked his progress with obstacles (vs. 13), contributed to his bankruptcy (vs. 13), overwhelming him together (vs. 14) like a tidal wave (vs. 14) pouring through a busted dam. ["Terrors are turned upon me"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). (See 7:9.) ["They pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). Job is being blown out of town just like the wicked man whom he described so vividly in Job 27:20--21, which matches this context exactly.

30:16 And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.

17 My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.

18 By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.

19 He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.

20 I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.

21 Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.

22 Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance.

23 For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.

24 Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.

As we have said before, the verses match the condition of a man in hell, of Christ during His atonement, and of the Tribulation saint.

"[The days of affliction"]{.class_3760} take hold on all three classes mentioned above (for Christ see Isa. 53:7, for Tribulation saints, Psa. 66:11, and for the Christ-rejecting sinner, Psa. 55:19). The pains of arthritis afflict Job at night (vs. 17), and he suffers from muscle spasms like those which accompany cholera. His ["garment"]{.class_3762} (vs. 18) has been changed from righteousness (29:14) to SHAME (Psa. 109:19), and it binds him so tightly about the throat that it is strangling him (Job 7:15). Job cries out to God like Jesus did on the cross (Matt. 27:46), but God doesn't regard him (vs. 20) any more than He did Cain (Gen. 4:13) or the rich man in Hell (Luke 16:24). Notice how Lamentations 3:1--16 (especially vss. 4--8) fit into Job's complaint, like Lamentations 5:10 and 3:49 fit into Job 7:5 and 16:20; we are dealing with Tribulation passages throughout.

"[Stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24) causes the Scholars Union to go to pieces like a hysterical woman, and in the higgledy piggledy derangement (HPD) which follows, all is "without form, and darkness is upon the face of the deep." Adam Clarke says that Job is saying, "though I suffer HERE I will not suffer HEREAFTER." Mr. Good says, "in its ruin is freedom." The absurd [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901), recommended by every dead orthodox apostate from A. T. Robertson to J. Ramsey Michaels, says, "stretch out the hand in his fall;" and The ["Living" Bible]{.class_3761} is about as faithful (and about as "living") to the Hebrew text as Mata Hari (German spy, 1916). Since Dummelow (p. 312) has already altered verses 2, 11--13, 15, and 22, it is no wonder that he adopts the ridiculous reading of Lightfoot, Endicott, Davidson, and the [RV ]{.class_3761}(1844) for verse 24: "Doth not a sinking man stretch out his hand and cry out in his calamity?" Which is nearly as good as: "Can't an X-ray technician collect compost if Flight 605 doesn't use ushers for trout fishing?"---but not quite as good! For a real "gasser" try the Jehovah's Witnesses "bible": "No one thrusts his hand out against a mere heap of ruins" ([NWT]{.class_3761}). But the gasser of gassers is the gaseous [Septuagint ]{.class_3761}recommended by Bruce, LaSor, Mounce, Custer, Studer, Hawthorne, and Robertson to correct your Bible. This perfidious production reads, "that I might lay hands upon myself, or at least ask another, and he should do this for me."

But why stop here? With three versions for three denominations and fifty for fifty heresies, what is there to stop us from all bowing down to Albert Einstein and saying, "All truth is relative, including Biblical truth; let us therefore each write our OWN Bible?" Try this updated version: "Rockefeller will not shake hands with Moshe Dayan even if Kissinger's oil wells in Saudi Arabia came from mashed fish!" Why, it doesn't take any talent or learning (or any ability or education) to rewrite the Bible in "updated" language! Any blank idiot can do it---and make money doing it. Hucksters have been doing it for 200 years. When Thomas Nelson and his buddies tried to con John R. Rice (and his buddies) into altering the [AV ]{.class_3759}again (1976)---after it had been "updated" to get rid of the "archaic language" twenty-five times in forty years---they said the motive behind the revision was to "clarify archaic words." Now what KIDDY doesn't know that every "archaic" word in the [AV ]{.class_3759}was "clarified" by twenty-five [translations ]{.class_3759}in the last forty years? Who but John R. Rice? Are any of you (reading this page) stupid enough to believe that the English language has become so "archaic" [since 1973 ]{.class_3759}that a "new translation" is [needed]{.class_3759}? Why, bless your soul, the "motive" given (on paper) for the [ASV, RSV, NASV, NRSV, New English, New Scofield, Jerusalem, International, Living Bible ]{.class_3759}and [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_3759}was to "get rid of the archaic words in the [King James Bible]{.class_3759}." Say daddy, who is going to get rid of the archaic words in ANY OTHER VERSION? Why is it always a Book that was supposed to have dropped out of style [300 years ago]{.class_3759}? Could it be that the Book never did "drop out of style"? Could it be that the money mad hucksters (and publicity mad "scholars") are only trying to get rid of a Book that they cannot get rid of? [Ever think about that?]{.class_3759}

Verse 24 is speaking about GOD---not some fool trying to shake hands with somebody at an ordination service. God ["will not stretch out HIS hand" ]{.class_3760}to the grave, according to Job, because Job, like David (Psa. 6:5 and 88:10), had never seen anybody pop up after they were "planted" (Ecc. 9:5). In the best Jehovah's Witness fashion (see Psa. 115:17), Job assumes that the grave stops life on earth, at least until the Last Judgment. All Jehovah's Witnesses teach this heresy ([Amillennialism]{.class_3759}), exactly as Calvin, Machen, Warfield, and A. T. Robertson taught it.

When the perishing sinner cries out to God in his destruction (vs. 24), he is not answered, and that is exactly where Job has placed himself in verses 11 and 20, in the context we just read. Therefore, any "serious student" of the Bible (who intelligently and prayerfully studies the word of God) should show no more respect for the "godly" scholarship of the "dedicated" men who produced the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) and the [NASV ]{.class_3759}(1973) than he should show for a barrel of dead fish. The deficiencies and bankruptcy of these destructive Bible critics is so manifest in scores of passages that only a Christian with an ulterior motive (like Thomas Nelson and Sons or John R. Rice) would tip his hat to them in passing. Where motives for service are generated by God (Phil. 2:13) and are God-honoring (Psa. 138:2), no kisses, kudos, or roses are ever thrown at ANY saved sinner who spends his time replacing the word of God with the trifling buffoonery of Vincent, Hort, Wuest, Zodhiates, Trench, Thayer, Robertson, Keil, Delitzsch, Rice, Weniger, Baxter, English, or the writers of The Pulpit Commentary. These men altered the word of God to suit their fancy because God the Holy Spirit refused to give them any light on the word, as it stood written, and if you had a dollar for every bungling, bithering, blabbering Conservative "scholar" who changed the Bible because he had no light on a passage, you could buy a round trip ticket to Tokyo on a 707.

30:25 Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?

26 When I looked for good, then evil came unto me: and when I waited for light, there came darkness.

27 My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.

28 I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, and I cried in the congregation.

29 I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.

30 My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.

31 My harp also is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.

Without taking time out again to follow the ["blind leaders of the blind"]{.class_3760} (Matt. 15:14) back into the morass of infidelity that spewed out of England and Germany (1880--1910), let us examine the truth.

Job did ["weep"]{.class_3760} for those in trouble, and his soul was ["grieved"]{.class_3760} for the poor (vs. 25); as a real believer, he "wept with those that wept" (Rom. 12:15), and none of the charges of 22:6--9 could be laid (justly) to his account. Because he lived a good life and "treated folks right," Job thought he was entitled to "look for good" (vs. 26), but while he was in the act of looking, ["evil came"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26). While Job waited nervously (see 3:26) for light (vs. 26), the darkness of pain, wrath, sickness, bereavement, and persecution enveloped him. ["My bowels boiled, and rested not"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27). The word is used in the Bible of the upper and lower viscera (see Gen. 15:4 and Psa. 71:6), although the word has been debased (1900) to a function of the lower vicera only (see Phil. 1:8 and Lam. 1:20). ["The days of affliction" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 27) come before Job can find ["good"]{.class_3760} or ["light"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26), and before he can rest (vs. 27), disaster has struck. ["I went mourning without the sun\...I cried in the congregation"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28). Not only did Job fail to "get light on his problem," he often failed to get sunlight; he mourned and wept in spiritual and physical darkness, exactly as the Tribulation remnant will mourn (Lam. 3). Job, as David (Psa. 38:1--11), is in real darkness. When he stood up in the assembly (vs. 28), he cried out in pain for help, and he didn't get it. (This must have happened somewhere after Job 1.) ["I am a brother to DRAGONS"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

At the bare mention of the name (["DRAGONS"]{.class_3760}) the Premillennialists, Liberals, Fundamentalists, Modernists, Catholics, Amillennialists, Neo-orthodox, and Conservatives dive for cover. In keeping with their muddled HPD that there cannot be plural "devils" or plural "dragons," the majority of whatever-you-want-to-call-them go to work like slaves under the lash.

It cannot possibly be "dragons," because there are no such things as "dragons," therefore, the word must be "JACKALS."

1\. [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901).

2\. [RSV ]{.class_3759}(1952).

3\. [NIV ]{.class_3759}(1978).

4\. The [Living Bible]{.class_3759}.

5\. [RV ]{.class_3759}(1885).

6\. [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3759}, plus Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Lange, the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, Kline, Heavenor, and the Jehovah's Witnesses.

7\. No, it is not "jackals," it is "howling brutes" (Bullinger).

8\. No, it isn't "brutes," it is "monsters" ([Septuagint]{.class_3759}).

9\. No, you are all wrong, it really was "jackals" all the time (the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759})!

Now, how is that for a majority?

Who left the text as it stood in the Protestant Reformation Bible?

Not ONE man among the last 3,000 who rejected the Holy Bible while blabbing about "God-breathed, inspired originals"! Not a man.

Do you at last see how a man becomes "recognized" as a "recognized Bible scholar?" Simple: All he has to do is correct the [King James ]{.class_3759}text, and he is "in the Union." Not one man in America or Europe can be a "recognized" scholar until he alters the [King James ]{.class_3759}text. Altering the [RSV ]{.class_3759}and the [ASV ]{.class_3759}is not considered to be "proof" of ability. The [King James ]{.class_3759}text must be attacked. John Rice will never be considered to be a Bible scholar until he alters the [King James ]{.class_3759}text. Then he will be "in"---in with Bloody Mary, Pope Paul, Mao Tse-tung, Castro, Hitler, Manson, Mrs. Roosevelt, Johnny Carson, Ford, Graf, Wellhausen, Fulton Sheen, Cronin, Oursler, Pike, Blake, Peale, Schaff, Machen, Davis, and Maharaji Guru: None of them would accept the KING JAMES text as it stood.

Now what was the source for rejecting the King James text as found above? Doesn't the Hebrew word here ("tannim") mean ["DRAGON"]{.class_3760} in Isaiah 27:1? How about Psalm 74:13 (see the remarks under Job 41:1--2)? How about Deuteronomy 32:33? Would you prefer Ezekiel 29:3? If that doesn't suit your exacting taste, did you sample Psalm 148:7? If the word was translated as ["DRAGON"]{.class_3760} in Isaiah 27:1 (see the [Living Bible]{.class_3759}, for example), how did the dragon suddenly shrink to a JACKAL? If the word was translated as "monster" in Isaiah 27:1 by the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) committee (and it was), how did the monster convert to a jackal here, and then back to a "serpent" in Deuteronomy 32:33? Such an indiscriminate juggling by inconsistent translators (the [ASV ]{.class_3759}committee of 1901) causes us to lose the cross-reference to Revelation 12:3--4, 7, 13, 16--17. So, what do you suppose was the source (I believe William LaSor naively called it "THE ORIGINAL" in his remarks in the [New English Bible]{.class_3759}) of this juggling? The word was certainly not aborted to "jackals" or "monsters" on the basis of Gesenius' Hebrew and Chaldean lexicon, for that work (p. 869) tells us that the word ["tannim"]{.class_3759} refers to a "great SERPENT" or "SEA monster" (see Job 41:1 and comments).

What then (you'll have to think for a while!) was the [source ]{.class_3759}of the scholarly "rip off" performed on the text by our "good, godly, dedicated" numbskulls who made the translations recommended at Bob Jones University and Hyles-Anderson College? As the text stood it pointed right to Isaiah 27:1 and Isaiah 13:21--22.

The trouble was this!

There is a TRADITION (Col. 2:8) among Premillennial, Fundamental "scholars" that is as strong as any superstitious tradition taught by Rome. This tradition comes in two installments which are as follows:

1\. There is only [one ]{.class_3759}DEVIL, therefore, there cannot be any ["devils"]{.class_3760} ([plural]{.class_3759}). The ["devils"]{.class_3760} of the [AV ]{.class_3759}have to be whittled down to "demons" (see any [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3759}).

2\. The ["wild beasts"]{.class_3760} of Isaiah 13:21 and Mark 1:13 cannot be ["devils"]{.class_3760} or ["dragons"]{.class_3760} ([plural]{.class_3759}); therefore, the owls (Isa. 13:21) and the satyrs (Isa. 13:21) are either natural animals or mythology. If there is the slightest doubt in your mind about this, observe how the Lockman Foundation and E. S. English obliterate Isaiah 13:21--22 as it stood in the Protestant Bible of the Reformation.

Hasn't it struck some of you as rather odd that every time the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}prepares to give us an advanced revelation about Satan (see Acts 12:4 or 19:35), that suddenly an army of "godly Fundamentalists" rise up in arms to guard the devil's person, and they use every "recognized" weapon they can get their hands on to do it!? And hasn't it struck some of you as even "odder" (a popael, hopael, spasmodic infinitive in the "boing!" case) that there are ANGELS (plural) and GODS (plural) and LORDS (plural), yet your Conservative conspirators do not allow DEVILS (plural) or DRAGONS (plural)?

There you got it: [Choke on it.]{.class_3759}

And you can shuffle "ostriches" and "jackals" around with "monsters" and "owls" till you have a zoo (Matt. 13:32) that would do justice to Barnum and Bailey, but it was Barnum who said there was a sucker born every minute. Once you make the mistake of RESPECTING the asinine leadership of any Conservative or Fundamentalist [who corrects the King James text]{.class_3759}, it will not be long before you will be carrying the torch and beating the drum (Gal. 5:9) for the institutions who have done more to destroy the authority of the Bible (1900--1980) than the Roman Catholic Church and the NCCC combined.

[DISRESPECT FOR SUCH "SCHOLARSHIP" IS THE WORK OF THE HOLY SPIRIT. ]{.class_3759}(See Jer. 23; Matt. 24; 2 Tim. 4; Ezek. 14; Rom. 16; and John 16:13.)

Job is a brother to devils (plural), for the ["king over all the children of pride"]{.class_3760} (Job 41:34) has him in the coils of self- righteousness (Job 27:5). His companions are the unclean "birds" of Revelation 18:2 and Matthew 13:32, and the OWL (Isa. 13) is an unclean bird (Lev. 11).

Now that is the meat of the text. That is the "deeper truth" of the passage which no amount of knowledge in any "original languages" will enable you to see. In dealing with these supernatural matters, a "working knowledge of the originals" is a positive HANDICAP to finding the truth. If your "Bible" has changed one word in verse 29, you know two things about the conmen who wrote that "Bible" and two things about the pitch- men that "tapped" you by recommending it:

1\. They were stuffed shirts.

2\. They were Christian idolators.

"[If the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch"]{.class_3760} (Matt. 15:14).

Job's skin is as black upon him as the Tribulation Jews (Lam. 4:8). His bones are burned with heat, for there is intense pain in the lumbar regions and the vertebral column in the disease of elephantiasis (The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, p. 485, 487). His ["harp"]{.class_3760} and his ["organ"]{.class_3760}---which normally caused people to dance (Job 17:6)---have degenerated into a basoon and a bass viol playing "The Death of Asa" (Peer Gynt Suite). The verse is obviously figurative (see 29:17), and there are no problems in the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text that call for any alterations or revisions by anyone. Changing the word ["organ"]{.class_3760} to "pipe" ([ASV, RV, RSV]{.class_3759}, etc.) just obliterated advanced revelation (see Acts 7:43 and 12:14 in that Commentary), and since an organ is composed of pipes anyway, there was no point in altering the text except to whet the jaded appetite of the ridiculous modern scholars who could say with Herbert Ehrenstein (of the [New English Bible]{.class_3759}): "Fresh, stimulating\...entirely new\... startling\...passages do come alive, etc." Yeah, like a puppet sticking his man-made stuffings out of a garbage can on Sesame Street.

CHAPTER 31

31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?

2 For what portion of God is there from above? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?

3 Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?

4 Doth not he see my ways, and count all my steps?

5 If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;

6 Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know mine integrity.

7 If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;

8 Then let me sow, and let another eat; yea, let my offspring be rooted out.

The entire chapter is Job's "summation for the defendant" where the "accused" is himself. Job attempts to vindicate himself before his three friends (and Elihu), and if they didn't exonerate him, at least he was justified in his own eyes (35:2 and 40:8). Before anyone shoves Job into a class with the Pharisees (Luke 18:11), let him read chapter 31 carefully. Job was not only outwardly righteous, but inwardly righteous as well (vss. 7, 24--25, 27, 29--30, and 33). On the other hand, before one classifies Job as being sinless or above correction, let him remember that the best man who ever lived couldn't keep up with Jesus Christ for twenty-four hours. Still Job is remarkable: no worldliness (vs. 24), no vengeance (vs. 29), no idolatry (vs. 26), no cursing (vs. 30), no deceit (vs. 34), no stinginess (vs. 32), no compromising (vs. 35), no stealing (vs. 39), and no wastefulness (vs. 38). That is, Job was at least 30 percent better off, morally, than the average born-again child of God in America today. If you ever want to see a Roman Catholic or a Liberal "tread water," make him read Job 31 and ask him if Job was good enough to get to heaven in that condition. He was moraly and spiritually better than any Roman Catholic priest in your town. If Job couldn't make it, who could? And Job was NOT good enough to get to heaven (Job 40:2, 6--14).

"[I made a covenant with mine eyes" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 1), because the eye (Gen. 3:6) is where the trouble usually starts (2 Sam. 11:2). Even a heathen king (Abimelech) knew this (Gen. 20:10) long before Achan SAW the spoils of Jericho (Josh. 7:21). Job had vowed to abide by Psalm 101:3 long before David wrote it. Since the eye affects the HEART (Lam. 3:51), a man had better be careful what he looks at (Matt. 6:22) and how long he looks (2 Pet. 2:14), because "out of the HEART are the issues of life" (see Prov. 4:23 and comments). Since Job only had one wife (vs. 10), he had made an agreement with his eyes not to look all over the pasture for something "greener" (Prov. 4:25 and comments). ["The portion of God"]{.class_3762} and the ["inheritance of the Almighty"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2) for the ["workers of iniquity"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3) is ["DESTRUCTION"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). If you look too long, you will think about it (vs. 1); if you think about it too long, you will lust (Matt. 5:28); if you lust too long, you will fornicate or ["burn"]{.class_3762} (1 Cor. 7:9). The trick, then, in modern fashion designing, TV programs, rock concerts, x-rated movies, rock festivals, and SIECUS educational material is to drive the teenager to fornicate or burn---one or the other or both. The "hurry up" pace of modern American civilization is geared to accelerate degeneration so that a mongrel race of passive automatons can be produced in time for Daniel's Seventieth Week. Two things are necessary to bring this about in the shortest time possible:

1\. Destruction of the American family: This can be accomplished by "children's RIGHTS" bills and by driving young people into marriage before they can work or have learned how to work. Being unable to live on a low income level, television and commercial advertising (the news media) will encourage them to overspend, over borrow, and waste money to live on a "high standard of living." The obvious terminus of this type of brainwashing will be BANKRUPTCY and subsequent dependence on a welfare state---like China and Russia.

2\. Disintegration of morals: This can be accomplished by encouraging young people to fornicate (premarital "sex") without getting married. To do this, SIECUS material should be used in the "enriched curriculum" (see how it's done?) to teach amoral "sexuality," free love, and toleration of sex perversion (called "GAY liberation"---see how it's done?). With the encouragement of the TV and the newspapers (the news media), this will produce a generation that feels no need to get married and no desire to take the responsibilities of married life. Rather than waste their time in "setting up" and "raising" families, they can "clarify their values" (see how it's done?) in a freewheeling position and spend their time [breaking up other families.]{.class_3759}

The quickest way to produce the effect described above is to magnify sex beyond all normal proportions, parade the marriage bed publicly to everyone, convert the word "love" to the sex act, and then force higher cultures to integrate with lower cultures where the emphasis is on what a person FEELS instead of what they THINK. Those entrusted with this unenviable task are the Fourth Estate---the news media. Neither Wallace nor Agnew (1960--1970) gave them one-third of their "dues." The motivating forces behind compulsory race mixing, federal busing, release of murderers from prison, abolition of capital punishment, and confiscation of firearms is not the Congress of the United States: [It is the news media putting pressure on politicians]{.class_3759}. No vote of any majority in the U.S.A. would back up ONE item mentioned in the list; it is the work of a minority, selling news. As many Europeans have observed, the news media in America controls both houses of Congress, the CIA, the FBI, both political parties, and the educational system in America. When Communism became the state religion of Russia and China, the first organ used to justify the arrest, imprisonment, torture, and murder of Christian pastors was the PRESS.

You say, "Explain yourself!" (All right, since you asked, we will.)

Before any Christian was imprisoned permanently, or was tortured into signing a false confession (or was killed), he was bracketed by the PRESS as a "reactionary" or a "right wing extremist" or a "lunatic fringe FANATIC." When the "People's Court" finally assembled, they would only "try" a case after they had been reading that rot in the press for anywhere from one to fifty years. The "purpose of the "press" was to [condition the people for a totalitarian state ]{.class_3759}under the guise of "helping THE PEOPLE" (see Acts 21:28 and comments). "The People's Court" finally assembled, and when they did, they were prepared to give "verdicts" before the accused entered the courtroom. They had been brainwashed by the press before the culprit ever showed up. Consequently, one will not be surprised to find in the court records of Russia and China (1930--1980) defendants with no lawyers (or lawyers hired by the government to ruin them), defendants who were not given a chance to speak in court, defendants whose families would have been tortured to death if they denied their "false confessions," and defendants who had been spied upon by the neighbors who themselves had been threatened with torture if they did not get something on the defendant for the "good of the people."

That is, and has been, the handwriting on the wall for the United States of America. The news media will not only "cooperate," it will build the mental pictures in the minds of its listeners, readers, and viewers. It has been lying steadily since World War I, and as a famous British statesman said in 1914: "I fear that from now on we'll never be able again to tell THE TRUTH." There isn't a single major daily newspaper in America that has told "THE TRUTH" about integration, social diseases, pornography, North Ireland, Vietnam, federal control, "detente," wholesale prices, crooked politicians, violence in the high schools, or Catholic control of tax money in the last seventy years. You never read TRUTH when you read a newspaper; you read leftwing [Roman Catholic Socialism ]{.class_3759}with a sprinkling of pornography, murder, and anti-Biblical propaganda on the side. "Truth" is as foreign to a daily newspaper as spirituality is to the World Council of Churches.

When Shechem "thought on a maid" (Gen. 34:1--8), it brought on him a "strange punishment": His whole village perished with him (Gen. 34:27--28).

"[Doth he not see my ways, and count all my steps?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). No, not according to Albert Einstein, Lorenz, the [Daily Worker]{.class_3761}, the American Civil Liberties Union, the Glide Memorial Methodist Church, FDR, Mick Jagger, Johnny Carson, the Beatles, and 5,000 ministerial students training for the "ministry" in denominational seminaries. Job is a good bit ahead of the Space Center in Houston (1980) when it comes to "God knowledge" (Theology). He, as David, knows that nothing escapes the observation of the One ["with whom we have to do"]{.class_3762} (Heb. 4:13). Job asks to be weighed in the balances (vs. 6); he is certain that he will come out in better shape than a man like Belshazzar (Dan. 5:27). But God already knew about Job's integrity (vs. 6), for He testified to it in Job 2:3. The trouble is that the ["even balance"]{.class_3762} (vs. 6) will have to place Job in one pan and GOD in the other. If the Lord had just put Buddha, Mao Tse-tung, Confucius, Daniel, Peter, St. Francis, Mohammed, John, Paul, Zoroaster, James, or Lao Tse in the opposite balance, Job could probably have outweighed them! But Job, here, is up against [God's righteousness ]{.class_3761}(see 27:6 and 40:8). The question here is: "Has God done RIGHT in allowing Job to go through what he has gone through?" Now that is the question! That is the same question we discussed in the Preface, and that is the question that you and I will have to face sometime in our lives when, to every appearance, it will seem that God has ERRED.

The atheists and agnostics of any generation simply study life and then decide that there is no God there, because "life" doesn't make "sense" from [their ]{.class_3759}standpoint, or if God is there, no one can find it out (since life doesn't reveal God to the SENSES of the agnostic). In plainer words, the basic root problem with man is SELF-RIGHTEOUSNESS (Gen. 3:12 and 4:9). He either thinks that he has more sense than God, or else he cancels God out (Rom. 1:19, 21) on the grounds that no God could run things as haphazardly as he (the sinner) thinks they are being run; by doing this, man can reign as the supreme deity in the universe. And it is most remarkable, in this age, to note that many modern Christian celebrities fit into one of these brackets, even though some of them profess to be "born again, soul-winning, premillennial, etc.," people. Any Christian who alters the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text with the befuddled "scholarship" of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries thinks that he has more sense than God, and when any "recognized scholar" sits in judgment as the final authority for Christians, he is taking God's place in regard to AUTHORITY. [Is that clear?]{.class_3759} Do you understand it? Why don't you, unless you draw your income from correcting the Bible?

Job has already been weighed in the balances, and he has already been found "wanting." Simon Peter was sifted in the Devil's sieve (Luke 22:31) before he got on the weighing machine, and he was also found "wanting" (Matt. 26:75). As a matter of fact, ["all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God"]{.class_3760} (Rom. 3:23). And the day that you see any man on this earth step into the scales with Jesus Christ and weigh out even---by putting his righteousness against Christ's righteousness---will be the day that Johnny Carson and Eva Gabor build a snow man on Mercury. When Paul tells you that the Lord Jesus Christ is ["IN"]{.class_3760} you (Col. 1:27), it means that if He is not, you will look like a feather placed on a scale to balance an ocean liner.

"[If my step hath turned out of the way\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). Observe the Scriptural "Ordo Peccados" laid out before us:

1\. The eyes look.

2\. The heart lusts after the eyes.

3\. The steps turn out of the way to carry out the dictates of the heart. The trick, then, is to get your EYE (Matt. 5:28); the news media (again, bless their little hearts!) will tend to this (see Prov. 12:12 and comments).

"[If any BLOT hath cleaved to mine hands"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). Job is speaking (figuratively) of a sinful deed (see Pilate, Matt. 27:24), but the text goes beyond this to the MARK of Revelation 13:17 where a black kiss (see [The Mark of the Beast]{.class_3761}, 1959) is implanted in the hand (Rev. 13:2, 16). ["Then let me sow, and let another eat"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). Job is willing to stake his crops and his children (vs. 8) on his own integrity, and you will notice how he speaks of his "offspring" as [still there]{.class_3761}, although they have already been ["rooted out."]{.class_3762} This is important in understanding Job 19:17, which see.

Adam Clarke botches the truth immediately and claims that all of Job's children are NOT wiped out. The ["Living"]{.class_3759} [Bible ]{.class_3759}confirms this blooper as quickly as possible by converting the ["offspring"]{.class_3760} into "CROPS." Just as anxious to overthrow the truth of God as Kenneth Taylor, the deluded fanatics of 1901 ([ASV]{.class_3759}: all "recognized scholars") turn the ["offspring"]{.class_3760} into "produce." And not to be outdone by these Bible-rejecting, Bible-adulterating, Bible-perverting apostates (who believe in "God-breathed originals," etc.), the Lockman Foundation (the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}) follows "in tow." All that Dummelow can get out of verse 6 is a mythological reference on Osiris in the Egyptian [Book of the Dead]{.class_3759}, and the Septuagint makes twenty alterations of the passage in the first eight verses; that's better than two changes per verse. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have to do what "Simon says," so they make the ["offspring"]{.class_3760} to read, "what I plant; my harvest." And, finally (let us hope so), Adam Clarke has converted the ["maid"]{.class_3760} of verse 1 to a "pile of anointed stones." The tradesmen's terminology for all of this blarney is "The Baithulia." The authority for the conjecture is [Sanchoniatho ]{.class_3759}and [Bochart]{.class_3759}. May we stick our oar in also and help paddle? How about the ["maid"]{.class_3760} being a "rebuilt carburetor"? Or how about the ["maid"]{.class_3760} being an "eight-track stereo"? Authority? Hans Heinrich Fullenstadten Weideraufen Von Duesenberger and Giuseppe Don Giovanni Mantenelli Dela Roncalli Pinnochio.

Now, this word for ["offspring"]{.class_3760} that all of the modern meddlers meddled with is ["tseetsaim"]{.class_3759} (Heb.). It means "children from a man's body" (Job 5:25, 21:8, 27:14; Isa. 22:24, 44:3, 48:19, 61:9, 65:23, etc.). In every one of the new "bibles" (God forgive us!), the word was translated as "offspring" in those references, or it was at least translated as "sons" or "children." What then was the point in altering it here, while pretending that the [AV ]{.class_3759}translators had an inferior "understanding of the ORIGINAL LANGUAGES" (F. F. Bruce, cited in "Good Thinking" from [Eternity ]{.class_3759}magazine)? What was the real point, kiddies?

Easy: [The conceited asses who altered it couldn't understand the word as it stood in the verse, in its own context]{.class_3759}. When a conceited ass cannot understand a verse in the Bible, in its own context (see ninety examples in the [Bible Believer's Commentaries on Genesis, Exodus, Proverbs, Matthew, Acts, Revelation]{.class_3759}, and [The Minor Prophets]{.class_3759}), he alters the verse to meet the demands of his ignorance. Since it took some of them forty years of study to get that stupid, it is often a very difficult operation in rearranging a verse to meet the "accredited standards" of [maximum stupidity]{.class_3759}. All Bible-rejecting Fundamentalists and Conservatives operate like atheists and agnostics when handling material above their intelligence or beyond their understanding; they just drag it down into the mud where it will be level with them.

Job is not saying that his children are still alive to be rooted out, and he certainly is not saying, "let my carrots and turnips get pulled up"! No, he is saying ["IF"]{.class_3760} (vss. 5, 7, 9, 13, 16) I have done so-and-so, ["THEN"]{.class_3760} (vss. 8, 10, 22) ["LET MY OFFSPRING BE ROOTED OUT."]{.class_3760} He is not saying "If I HAVEN'T DONE SO!" Sometimes the italicized words in the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text (["then"]{.class_3760}) shed a lot of light on the problems that confuse educated folks.

31:9 If mine heart have been deceived by a woman, or if I have laid wait at my neighbour's door;

10 Then let my wife grind unto another, and let others bow down upon her.

11 For this is an heinous crime; yea, it is an iniquity to be punished by the judges.

12 For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.

Not only has Job kept himself free from fornication (in relation to unmarried maidens), he has also kept himself free from adultery with married women. Job calls adultery a "heinous crime" (vs. 11), so you can well imagine that he would not be in very good standing with the editorial staffs of [Playboy, Esquire, Lampoon, Playmate, Oui, Playgirl]{.class_3759}, or the personnel of SIECUS and the NEA. The world has never cared for real righteousness (2 Pet. 2:5), and that is why Jews, Gentiles, disciples, Romans, rabbis, and "fathers" could all agree that Jesus Christ should be tortured to death (John 18:35). Job doesn't look at "adult consent" in adultery as "harmonious adjustment" to "cultural exchanges" (see how it's done?) in an "environmental situation" to "twentieth century VALUES CLARIFICATION" (see how it's done?). No, Job just says: ["CRIME!"]{.class_3760} Adultery in the Bible is criminal activity, and it is punished by capital punishment (Lev. 20:10).

David is an obvious case in point, for his heart WAS deceived by a woman, and he did "lay wait at his neighbor's door" (vs. 9); so, David's wives DID grind for another (vs. 10), and others did "bow down on them" (2 Sam. 16:22). This is actually what happens in a modern divorce where the Scriptural grounds of fornication have sundered a couple (see Matt. 19:9 and remarks). The judges (vs. 11) punish the crime in the Old Testament (Deut. 19:18); so, since David has no judge to punish him, his son becomes the ["judge"]{.class_3760} (2 Sam. 18:18)! \[You don't beat God out whether you live in 2000 B.C. or 2000 A.D.\] Again, the word ["increase,"]{.class_3760} in verse 12, is a reference to the ["offspring"]{.class_3760} of verse 8. In both cases, sins of sex are being discussed, and in both cases, the physical seed of the offender is affected exactly as in Genesis 9:25 and 49:4. For this reason, the "newer translations" altered verse 8 (see above) so that you would lose the obvious moral connections; this time the translators are not just covering up for the Devil (see 30:29), but for [sexual transgressions]{.class_3759}. (Oh, don't you know that they are a "godly" lot. I'll bet they would "take a stand" against abortion and second marriages as strong as a drunken Pope!)

31:13 If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant, when they contended with me;

14 What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?

15 Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?

16 If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;

17 Or have eaten my morsel myself alone, and the fatherless have not eaten thereof;

18 (For from my youth he was brought up with me, as with a father, and I have guided her from my mother's womb;

19 If I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or any poor without covering;

20 If his loins have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;

21 If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, when I saw my help in the gate:

22 Then let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone.

23 For destruction from God was a terror to me, and by reason of his highness I could not endure.

Job now switches to his dealings with poor people, and again, he justifies himself throughout.
