When Job's male and female servants took issue with him and brought a point of grievance to him (vs. 13), he didn't turn up his nose at them; he didn't curtly dismiss them with, "run on and get to work" (see Pharaoh in Exod. 5:5--9 and comments). He listened to their cause and sympathized with them. Job did this in obedience to Ephesians 6:9 and Colossians 4:1 long before Paul's mother gave him a Gentile home. Job knew that he himself would have to give an account to God some day (vs. 14), so he will have to answer some "grievances" himself (vs. 14 and 26:1--4). The same God who made blind Bartimaeus, Mephibosheth, and Lazarus, made Nicodemus, Abraham, Pilate, Herod, David, and Joseph of Arimathea.

The same God (Prov. 22:2) who put Joseph in jail, put him on a throne, and the same God who had His own Son born in a stable has put Him over the universe.

Job had not withheld the poor from anything they desired (vs. 16) when it came to the necessities of life (vss. 17, 19). No widow's eyes "failed" when they looked to him for help (vs. 16); her children ate at his table (vs. 17) when they had nowhere else to eat. Job was born (vs. 18) to guide widows (vs. 18) and act as a father to orphans (vs. 18). The ["loins"]{.class_3760} of a shivering wretch in the cold blessed Job because Job furnished him with a sheepskin coat (vs. 20) or a wool sweater. The figurative expression has been used because it is the ["loins"]{.class_3760} that need to be covered FIRST (see Gen. 3:7). Job had never raised his hand in a vote (or his voice in an accusation) against a fatherless child (vs. 21), even when the majority of the people would have backed him up (vs. 21) in an official ballot by the city elders. And if he had done any of these things, Job was perfectly willing for God to tear his arm out of his shoulder socket (vs. 22). Job will seal his testimony with his blood (see 27:5). (See comments under Prov. 13:13; Job 22:9, 24:7, 38:22; Gen. 2:12, and 14:2.)

Exegetically speaking, there is no use wasting time with the men who wrote the Bible study textbooks for the faculty members at Yale, Harvard, Princeton, Chicago University, San Francisco Conservative Baptist Theological Seminary, Bob Jones University, Luther Rice Seminary, and Dallas. Same crowd, same crew, same tune, same old infidelity. The only difference between one Mafia and the others is that some only change two verses [per chapter ]{.class_3759}and others change two words [per verse]{.class_3759}. The passage has been altered more than [four times per verse]{.class_3759}; and if you combine the destructive criticisms of Duhm, Budde, Peake, Bullinger, Taylor, Dodd, Lange, Dummelow, the [Septuagint]{.class_3759}, the NASV, and the old ASV, the display of reprobate apostasy will be double that found in the works of Voltaire, Rousseau, and Tom Paine. Since there wasn't one word (not ONE) in one verse (not ONE) in the entire passage that wasn't sufficiently clear in the [AV ]{.class_3759}(if the reader would believe God and read the rest of His Bible), there isn't any point in consulting any "scholar" listed above, or any of their pupils or teachers, to find out ANYTHING about the passage. Where God speaks let sinners shut up.

Verse 23 is absolutely clear in view of Job 31:1 and 18:11.

31:24 If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence;

25 If I rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because mine hand had gotten much;

26 If I beheld the sun when it shined, or the moon walking in brightness;

27 And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:

28 This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judge: for I should have denied the God that is above.

The next section is dealing with the relationship of money to idolatry (vss. 26, 27). Since "covetousness" IS "idolatry" (Col. 3:5) and the only idol we should have is the Lord Jesus Christ (1 John 5:20), we should be as wary of Capitalism as we are of Communism, Charismatics, Campbellites, or Catholicism. In spite of the fact that Job had plenty of gold (Job 1:1--5), he put no confidence in it (vs. 24). He could say with David ["And now, Lord, what wait I for? My hope is in THEE"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 39:7). Since ["riches profit not in the day of wrath"]{.class_3760} (see Prov. 11:4 and comments), Job does not make gold his ["hope."]{.class_3760} However, it is painfully apparent that Job's ["hope"]{.class_3760} was his own RIGHTEOUSNESS. He could not trust God's righteousness because, at this present moment, he thought that God was not treating him right. Our song must always be "My HOPE is built on nothing less than Jesus' blood and RIGHTEOUSNESS" (Rom. 10:1--5). (When America put her trust in gold \[1849\] and then trusted silver to buy friends for herself \[1933--1970\], she cultivated the most vicious enemies she ever had. You don't [buy ]{.class_3759}friends \[Prov. 17:17 and comments\].)

Job did not even rejoice when his wealth increased (vs. 25); so, there was a vast distance between him and the average pastor or teacher. ["If I beheld the sun\...the moon\...my heart\...my mouth\...my hand"]{.class_3760} (vss. 26--27). Notice again, that the moon does not "shine" (25:5); it only ["walks in brightness"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26).

The definitive book on "sun and moon worship" was written many years ago by Hislop ([The Two Babylons]{.class_3759}), and we do not have space to review the material here; it will suffice to say that the worship of the sun as a male deity and the moon as a female deity is as old as Genesis chapter 6. The references to this kind of worship---whether they refer to Baal, Ashtaroth, Remphan, Chiun, Ishtar, Diana, Molech, or Astarte---are found throughout the Bible (see Ezek. 8:16; Deut. 4:19; 2 Kings 23:5, 11; Acts 7:42; 2 Kings 21:5; Isa. 24:21; Judg. 2:11, 13; and 1 Sam. 12:10, etc.). We can lump them together without making the fine distinctions found in Greek, Roman, and Babylonian mythology. Actually, there were many "gods" before on this earth, and they were named by the people in the country where they showed up (Gen. 6:1--6). However, for theological purposes of deifying man as a "god," they can all be summed up in one pair (Gen. 3:8), typified by a fallen man and a fallen woman (Gen. 3:7) who questioned what God said so they could get an EDUCATION and be like the "gods" in superior knowledge. Their offspring was a MURDERER (John 8:44), and whether than fallen man be pictured as the "sun" and represented by a "Holy father" with a "Queen of Heaven" for a wife (the moon) and a fake Christ for a child (1 Thess. 2:2--6) or whether the sun is a Bull with a disk between his horns and his own mother for his wife (see Tammuz, Osiris, Seb, Isis, Nimrod, et al.) and his son (Apollos) in Hell as a fake Christ (see Abaddon, Apollos, Bacchus, Adonis, et al.), we come out where we went in: nature worship, with physical love as the supreme god, and fornication, sex perversion, and adultery as part of true "worship in spirit and truth." (See Rev. 2:20--21 and comments in that Commentary.)

The kiss blown from the hand (vs. 27) pictures the black kiss planted in the hand (John 17:12; 2 Thess. 2:2--4; Rev. 13:16--17; Hosea 13:2; and 2 Sam. 15:5), as the "KISS OF PEACE" (2 Sam. 15:5; Matt. 26:49) came from the "father of PEACE" (2 Sam. 18:18. That's what "ABSALOM" means, man!). Idolatry will be required of the Tribulation worshippers (Rev. 13:15), and it will deny the ["God that is above"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 13:6). We have stumbled into [four more prophetic references ]{.class_3759}which Payne and Biederwolf ([with their 400 authorities]{.class_3759}) missed, but you miss a lot of truth when you try to shred the Bible through a "made in Japan" blender you bought at K-mart. An ounce of real humility (not that old, fake, "pious" drivel about "respecting others who differ in opinions," etc.) is worth ten tons of planned humility when a man is trying to understand the Bible. The modern "humble, devoted" scholar is usually just some conman trying to tap some sinner for a fast buck--- some sinner just like himself.

31:29 If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:

30 Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.

31 If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.

32 The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doors to the traveller.

33 If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:

34 Did I fear a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrify me, that I kept silence, and went not out of the door?

The next section deals with matters of personal vengeance. Job did not even rejoice secretly (vss. 29, 33) when his enemies got "what they had coming to them." Knowing that the heart controls the mouth (Matt. 12:35), Job never took a chance on even a wish (vs. 30) getting to the door of his lips (Psa. 141:3). If one of his enemies was killed or contracted cancer or diabetes, Job was genuinely sorry for him (Luke 19:41) and probably prayed for him. He heeded James 3:9 before James was born, and he obeyed Proverbs 24:17 before Solomon could write anything. (God's laws on proper attitudes, conduct, motives, principles, and practices were written in the hearts of men \[Rom. 1--2\] many centuries before Moses received the Mosaic Law \[Rom. 2:14\], and the evidence of the Scripture is that "ancient man" was much better acquainted with absolute standards than the modern college professors at Harvard or Columbia.)

Further, Job forgave his own household servants, even when some of them desired retribution to fall on him (vs. 31). Most of them were satisfied (vs. 31) without "having his hide" (vs. 31), but if Job's household servants had NOT said, ["Oh, that we had of his flesh"]{.class_3760} he could be justified; so the obvious implication is that some of them [had ]{.class_3759}said it. To avoid this obvious construction, every Bible revision since 1800---including any recommended by [any Fundamentalist]{.class_3759}---has altered the verse. Whether we consult Dummelow or Lange or The [Interpreter's Bible ]{.class_3759}or Clarke, all we get is "rather it SHOULD READ\...." Or as the Wycliffe commentator so bluntly states it: "[ASV]{.class_3759}, not [AV]{.class_3759}." (p. 482). May we, as bluntly, suggest: "[AV]{.class_3759}, NOT [ASV]{.class_3759}"? The HPD on the passage came from connecting verse 31 with verse 32 instead of the previous verse (vs. 30). The Hebrew word for ["not"]{.class_3760} (vs. 31) has quietly dropped out of Jamieson's "exposition of the text," while the word has been put into a future statement in the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}. The [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) has added another "not" to the second clause to fix the Bible up the way they think it should be. Typical "godly" translating.

But the text, as it stands, states that a man who hated Job (vs. 29) could "get by" in Job's household even if he did say verse 31, and if a man even THOUGHT that, Job did not wish a curse on him (vs. 30). You see, when you "curse the king in your bedchamber" (Ecc. 10:20), it isn't long before he knows about it (Ecc. 10:20). The verse in Ecclesiastes said: ["Curse not the king, no not in thy THOUGHT!"]{.class_3760}

When in doubt throw the [ASV ]{.class_3759}and the [NASV ]{.class_3759}out the window.

"[The stranger did not lodge in the street"]{.class_3760} (vs. 32), but this had nothing to do with the prevous verse. All efforts by the commentators to make the ["stranger"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32) proof of the fact that everyone in [Job's house ]{.class_3761}knew that no one left there without being satisfied with a banquet (vs. 31) is piffle. Job opened his doors to the traveller, but the ["men"]{.class_3762} of verse 31 were ["THE MEN"]{.class_3762} of his ["tabernacle,"]{.class_3762} not wayfaring strangers.

The sudden appearance of Adam (vs. 33) is a little too sudden for the careless commentators and revisors, for two reasons: One, they wanted to remove the date of the writing of Job as far from Adam as they could, and two, they deeply resent Job saying that Adam had his iniquity in his ["bosom"]{.class_3760} (see comments of the heart under vss. 7 and 27). So the Lockman Foundation coyly adds "or like other men," the [ASV ]{.class_3759}suggests "after the manner of men," the [Living Bible ]{.class_3759}omits the word ["bosom,"]{.class_3760} the [NEB ]{.class_3759}inserts "as men do," and the corrupt [LXX ]{.class_3759}just takes the name of Adam smack out of the verse; others have altered it to read "common man" or "as men do." Anything but the text. Adam Clarke sees one more opportunity to put the writing of the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}after Exodus; therefore he says that Job had probably read about Adam from the "Mosaic account of Genesis." This brings up an interesting problem: Why does Job mention it (if he is acquainted with the "Mosaic account"), while no one in Judges, Joshua, or 2 Samuel mentions it? Weren't THEY acquainted with the "Mosaic account?" Obviously, Job's reason for recalling it is that Adam's sin was 600 years (or more) closer to Job's time than the time of the Judges and Samuel.

Adam DID cover his transgressions; he covered his nakedness with a fig leaf (Gen 3:7), and he HID his iniquities (vs. 33) not only in his heart (Prov. 6:18), but also behind some trees (Gen. 3:8). Job [confessed ]{.class_3759}and [forsook ]{.class_3759}his sins (Prov. 28:13). Furthermore, Adam committed transgressions (plural) in addition to partaking of the vine tree (oh yeah, man, the faculties of those schools you went to couldn't find that, and since they couldn't get another dollar out of it if they [had ]{.class_3759}found it, they just denied it). Adam also passed the buck (Gen. 3:12), blamed God for his partner (Gen. 3:12), hearkened to his wife (Gen. 3:17), broke his fellowship with God (Gen. 3:10), and lied about his motive for eating. These transgressions were "covered," or at least Adam made an [attempt ]{.class_3759}to cover them. Eventually, God had to make the covering (Gen. 3:21 and comments). All hidden iniquity is eventually hidden in the BOSOM (Exod. 4:6 and comments, also Prov. 6:25 and Mark 7:21). As a man ["thinketh in his heart, so is he"]{.class_3760} (see Prov. 23:7 and comments).

Did Job fear a multitude (vs. 34) like the legislators at Washington D.C. fear the Black Muslims, the Irish Republican Army, the prisoners in Attica, the NAACP, the Vatican, and the Kent State students? No. Did Job worry about the ["contempt of families" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 34) like the Kennedys, the Rockefellers, the Nixons, the Luces, the Proctors, the Fords, the Duponts, or the Astors? No. Did Job keep his mouth shut (vs. 34) and keep silence because he was worried about the support of the United Drive, the Full Gospel Fellowship, Campus Crusade, the Logos-Charismatic Lovey-Dovies, the Christian day schools, or the [Sword of the Lord ]{.class_3759}(Oh, brother!)? No. Did Job stay huddled up in his lil' ole office (vs. 34) like Peter, James, and John (Luke 24:36, John 20:19) simply because he was opposed by the NEA, the HEW, the qualified authorities, the "accredited scholars," the town fathers, the United Drive, the Roman priests, the Internal Revenue Service, and the faculty members of Christian schools? No. Job, like Micaiah (1 Kings 22), Paul (Acts 26), Jeremiah (Jer. 42), Jesus, and Nehemiah (Neh. 13) was about as cowed before contemporary society and religious and political power as a cougar would be in front of a chicken coop.

31:35 Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.

36 Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, and bind it as a crown to me.

37 I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.

38 If my land cry against me, or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;

39 If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:

40 Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and cockle instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.

Job is nearing the end of his defense. In approaching the climax (see Matt. 23:35 for comparison), his voice is getting louder and louder. Before he slumps back into grim silence (vs. 40), he is nearly crying again (vs. 35), and he only hardens himself just in time to keep from getting hysterical.

"[Oh that one would hear me!"]{.class_3760} (vs. 35). His friends are either yawning or looking piously up in the air at several buzzards flying over the city dump. Nothing is more boring (or more irritating) than hearing a sinner [like yourself ]{.class_3761}trying to prove that he is better than you are. Elihu is listening to him (32:11--12), but Elihu is not confirming Job's defense; He is just staring at him in silent anger.

"[My desire is, that the Almighty would ANSWER ME"]{.class_3760} (vs. 35), and the Almighty does answer him (38:1). ["And that mine adversary had written a book" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 35): both prayers get answered. Elihu was Job's adversary (see 34:7--8, 35--37), and Elihu wrote the Book. (See the remarks in the Preface and comments under 32:16 where all of the revisors and commentators have erased the authorship by changing the Hebrew and English words.)

"[Surely I would take it upon my shoulder"]{.class_3760} (vs. 36) like ancient criminals bore their accusations printed on wooden plaques hanging over their shoulders; sometimes a "pillory punishment" consisted of wearing a wooden yoke over the neck and shoulders for a certain number of weeks, with the crime written on the wood (Isa. 9:6; James 2:6). Verse 37 is self- explanatory. All that follows is bitter self-vindication; Job says if he has even mistreated his gardens and pastures, may God curse them and turn them over to thistles and cockle burs (vs. 40). Job kept "group insurance" for his employees (vs. 39), and he took protective measures to guard their safety while they worked (vs. 39). He had not even sown ["diverse seeds"]{.class_3762} (Deut. 22:9) or plowed with the wrong animals (Deut. 22:10). His farming is as faultless as his finances; his ground was as good as his gold. ["THE WORDS OF JOB ARE ENDED!"]{.class_3762}

Flump. And there he is.

There is old Job, head down, staring between his emaciated legs, clothed only in rotting rags; his hands drop listlessly from arms which rest on knee bones that are as easy to locate as two brass door knobs. Clothed in the filthy rags of his own righteousness (Isa. 64:6), Job, at this point, is a perfect picture of the self-righteous sinner in [Gehenna]{.class_3759}---the city dump. This is the man who said Job 9:20 and 9:28!

But Job wasn't the last man on earth to trust in his own righteousness.

Case No. 1. I stopped the manager of a large radio station in Pensacola (a man I used to work for before I got saved), and I asked him, "Are you SAVED?" He drew himself up stiffly, and, with a smile halfway between, "I'll bust your teeth in" and "of course, don't I look like it?" he stuck his hand up in my snoot and showed me a ring. Well frankly, I had never been interested enough in the Masonic Order to tell a 32nd Degree Mason from freezing temperature---perhaps both are the same. At any rate, I stupidly asked him the question again; whereupon he quit grinning and said angrily, "Of course!" and stomped out of the room. You see, I was supposed to have gathered ([from looking at a metal ring]{.class_3759}) that that human sinner had been justified by God. Now who could ever have guessed that?

Case No. 2. I was preaching on the street corner near a university town. About forty people, passing by, stayed to watch the chalk talk. At the conclusion I turned to the crowd and said, "If any man of you would like to receive the Lord Jesus Christ, step forward and take one of these tracts out of my hand. Don't do it if you are already saved, but if you will receive Christ today, the best way you know how, step forward. The tract is free." Three men accepted on this basis. I walked up close to the crowd, and in going from one man to another, I found myself looking at four young men with blue "beenies" on their heads. Each one had a beeny with a white "F" on it. I asked the young men: "Hey, are you guys saved?" And do you know what happened? So help me Nashville, all four of those birds---just like they were plugged into one transformer---raised their right hands and pointed with a forefinger to the "F" on their caps. Not one of them said a word.

(Now, if I had any sense I would have known that the "F" on the beeny stood for "FURMAN," and if you went to "Furman University," you just [had ]{.class_3759}to be saved. But I guess in my naivete I was a little confused.)

Case No. 3. I was dealing with a circus "strong man." Every year, at a certain festival in Panama City, Florida, he pulled a string of cars through the street (I think it was ten cars) by a harness which he pulled with his teeth. "Atlas" was unimpeachable. From his testimony I would have gathered that Job would have had a hard time eating at the same table with him. He didn't drink, he didn't smoke, he didn't curse, he went to church, he paid his debts, he\...you know. I put Ephesians 2:1--9 on him and then Romans 10:1--10 and John 3:16. After thirty minutes he said: "Do you mean to tell me that if a murderer who is sentenced to die and who gets into the hot seat, and then, five seconds before they throw the switch, receives Jesus Christ, that he can go to HEAVEN!?"

"That's correct," I said.

"Well, that's not fair," he said loudly.

"Why not?"

"Well, look at this! Here is this dirty so-and-so, living like the devil, and, then, at the last minute he gets in 'under the wire,' while a man that has lived a good life all his life, like ME, and has given up all kinds of things to live the right kind of life---ME---I've got to go to Hell because I don't believe on Jesus Christ?"

"If the Book is right," I said, "that's the ticket."

"But what about all the good I've done?! I don't believe that God would let me go to Hell and let a murderer go to heaven even if\...."

"Look," I said, "God knows if you're any good (Mark 10:21), and He won't overlook your goodness (Acts 9:36) so if you reject His Son (John 3:36), it won't go as tough with you (Matt. 23:14) as with Oswald, Dillinger, Karpis, Starkweather, Fulton Sheen, Manson, Pope Paul, and Bloody Mary (Matt. 23:14--15). You'll get Hell (Rev. 20), but not as much Hell (Matt. 11:22) as some will get (Deut. 32:22). How's that?"

Well, "that" wasn't any good at all! My "prospect" left the room grunting like he was press-lifting 250 pounds.

And there is no cure for a self-righteous man under heaven but a revelation from God (Job 38--40) which reveals man as an unrighteous rebel (Job 41--42). (The comments by Clarke and the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}on the passage are not worth reproducing.) There doesn't breathe a saved or a lost man on this earth who ever obeyed Matthew 22:37 twelve months a year, and the reason why millions of sinners think they will "make out all right" at the judgment is because their ethical standards are the standards of either a dog or a Pharisee.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2680.xhtml}

CHAPTER 32

32:1 So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

2 Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.

3 Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and ye had condemned Job.

4 Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.

5 When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.

(Symmachus, Kennicott, the Syriac, Arabic, and the LXX have all aborted verse 1, but we will not take time to discuss their foolishness.)

A silence has settled on the city dump like the silence which follows where someone has just nailed down the lid on a coffin. Job's three buddies cease to answer him, because ["he was righteous in his own eyes"]{.class_3760} (vs. 1). He had ["justified himself rather than God"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2); therefore it was useless to proceed any further.

Until now, Elihu has been sitting quietly by, listening and weighing the speeches of his friends; now the blood rushes to his temples (vs. 2), and he decides to stick his own cassette into the player. He has waited until Job is through (vs. 4), and he has honored the ages of Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar by remaining silent for them (vs. 4); but Elihu has come to the conclusion that nobody has really answered Job (vs. 5), so he will undertake to play the part of a ["daysman"]{.class_3760} (33:6--7) and deal with him.

32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.

7 I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

8 But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

9 Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.

10 Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.

11 Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.

12 Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, there was none of you that convinced Job, or that answered his words:

13 Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.

14 Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

Elihu is probably kin to Abraham (see 27:5--6 with Gen. 22:20--21). A city in Jeremiah 25:23 (["Buz"]{.class_3760}) is also connected with two towns in Edom---Dedan and Tema---connecting the ["Buzite"]{.class_3760} (vss. 5--6) with Abraham and Elihu.

Verses 6--7, and 9 are self-explanatory.

"[The inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8). This refers not only to the physical breath of God (33:4) that was breathed into Adam's nostrils (see 27:3), but the Spirit of God (33:4) giving a man spiritual understanding (vs. 8). Since ["all scripture is given by inspiration of God"]{.class_3762} (2 Tim. 3:16), Elihu's speech, which follows, is inspired in the truest sense.

"[Great men are not always wise"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). Socrates (469--399 B.C.) was given up to ["vile affections"]{.class_3762} (Rom. 1:26) because he rejected the truth of God (Rom. 1:25). Oscar Wilde (1854--1900) was in the same filthy class. Sigmund Freud (1856--1939) couldn't control his "complexes" after analyzing them, and he died a premature death after three operations on his lips for mouth cancer, contracted from smoking too many cigars. Napoleon (ruled 1804--1814), on the eve of Waterloo (1815), was stupid enough to tell one of his generals: "I make circumstances!" Neitzsche (1884--1900) could prophesy two World Wars fifty years before they were fought and could place the Jew and Russia in the focal positions in the late twentieth century, but he couldn't help making fun of God and calling the ["I AM"]{.class_3762} of Exodus 3:14 a "joke." So, the ["I AM"]{.class_3762} pulled a joke on Nietzsche: He took his sanity from him. Adolph Hitler (d. 1945) was crafty enough to capture half of Europe and smart enough to pull a whole nation out of bankruptcy, but he didn't have enough sense to believe Genesis 12:1--4; so Hitler lost everything, including his life and his soul: ["GREAT MEN ARE NOT ALWAYS WISE."]{.class_3762}

A little German Jew (1879--1955) came to America in the 1920's. He settled down at Princeton. In his youth, this Jew did very poorly in grade school, and before writing his work on "The Special Theory of Relativity" (1905), he worked as a clerk for about ten cents an hour in American money. But by 1921 he had received the Nobel Prize for Physics. (The Atom Bomb was built on the physics he demarked in his early work of 1905.) When this Jew died at the age of 76, he was classified with the greatest thinkers the world has ever seen. He was a world reknown celebrity. As to his "wisdom" (vs. 9), his whole system of thought was "make-believe," and now it is claimed (Von Daniken and others) that he was wrong on his major premise. The end product of his "thinking" and "wisdom" did not solve [ONE problem ]{.class_3759}of mankind, since it was all imaginary to start with. Just before Albert Einstein stepped out naked to the White Throne Judgment, clad in the rags of his own self- righteousness, he said to a minister (who had just tried to lead him to Christ): "I cannot believe in a God who is not a MATHEMATICAL FORMULA!" (Look at Job 21:15 and comments.)

"[Great men are not always wise."]{.class_3760} (vs. 9).

"[Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13). That is, the three companions have not found the answer, and they cannot say what is found in verse 13. None of them proved conclusively that God had thrust Job down because of his wickedness, for, after all, their accusations were based on imagination, pure and simple. With all the hot air in Job 3--30, not one witness had been produced to prove that Job had ever done anything wicked. It was "men" who were thrusting Job down (vs. 13) as far as the outward appearance went. Verse 14 is self-explanatory.

32:15 They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking. 16 When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, and answered no more;)

17 I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.

18 For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.

19 Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.

20 I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.

21 Let me not, I pray you, accept any man's person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.

22 For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away."

Now from here on, it is "too wet to plow." Just when the Holy Spirit is about to tell us who wrote the [Book of Job]{.class_3759}, the Fundamentalist, Catholic, Liberal, Conservative, Evangelical, Modernist, and Neo-orthodox scholars gather in one mighty ecumenical mob and attack the [AV ]{.class_3759}like a pack of mad dogs. Observe, first of all, how the Holy Spirit has preserved the truth of the matter, in spite of the bungling and stupid work of men like the Lockman Foundation and the faculty members of the University of Manchester, Wheaton Theological Seminary, Fuller Theological Seminary, Western Kentucky University, Westminister Theological Seminary, Dallas Theological Seminary, and Luther Rice Theological Seminary.

"[THEY were amazed"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). Third person: [The author is looking at THEM.]{.class_3761}

"[THEY left off speaking"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). Third person: [The author was listening to THEM.]{.class_3761}

"[When I had waited"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16). First person: [The speaker is the Author.]{.class_3761}

"[I said, I will answer\...I am full\...I will speak\...I will open my lips"]{.class_3760} (vss. 17--20). First person: The man who says that he will say it, [SAYS IT.]{.class_3761}

Now, to get rid of these Biblical truths as fast as they can operate, the "godly" (a John R. Rice cliché: 1940--1970) Conservative scholars of 1880--1980 have gone to the following lengths:

1\. Mr. Good makes the ["they"]{.class_3760} of verse 15 refer to the "speeches"---not the men.

2\. Adam Clarke throws out eleven words from verse 15 so that the first four words will match verse 11.

3\. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(Lockman Foundation) has inserted "Elihu reports" in verse 15, to put Elihu [into the THIRD person ]{.class_3759}with the ["they"]{.class_3760} of verse 15. (Oh boy, do we ever have some "good, godly" Christian scholars "laboring for years to restore the original dead cat.")

4\. The stinking [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901), recommended by Bob Jones University (1940--1960), has reworded verse 15 and verse 16 in a fanatical attempt (by a "crackpot extremist"---I believe that is BJU's nomenclature for people who believe the Bible) to get rid of the authorship. The [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) reads: "They ARE amazed\...and SHALL I wait because they stand still\...?"

5\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown say that at verse 15 Elihu "turns from the friends to Job." But this is not the sense at all; if Elihu had done this, verse 14 would have read: "Neither will I answer HIM (Job) with THEIR speeches." Instead, Elihu addressed the friends directly in verse 14: ["your speeches,"]{.class_3760} and, then, commenting on the silence that follows, he says: ["they were amazed\...they left off speaking."]{.class_3760}

6\. Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3759}) puts the verb for ["I had waited"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16) into the future and makes a question out of it: "Shall I continue to wait?" This also matches the abomination of 1901 ([ASV]{.class_3760}), recommended by all the faculty members of Moody Bible Institute.

Now these are the twentieth-century paths of Death and Hell. Not even the Roman Catholic Church in the heyday of its hellishness (A.D. 800--1500) went after the Biblical text like these fanatical extremists who would shoot God in the face if they didn't like His looks. Not even Dodd ([NEB]{.class_3759}) or Weigle ([RSV]{.class_3759}) would go to any more trouble than these men (and their kind) have gone to to overthrow the authority of God the Holy Spirit, and the serious student of the Bible should observe that nothing---absolutely NOTHING---is sacred with these destructive asses who think because they believe in ten things out of the Bible (and don't [drink ]{.class_3759}or [smoke]{.class_3759}), they have been called to play God for the body of Christ. Nothing is sacred to them but their [incomes ]{.class_3759}and their [prestige]{.class_3759}.

Now lest some of these snollygosters should think that our criticisms of them are based on an irratioal prejudice, let us for a moment consult the so-called "original Hebrew" in verse 16 and just see how "proficient" the Hebrew teachers are in the twentieth century as compared to the [AV ]{.class_3759}translators of 1611. Shall we? Let's!

There are no present tense verbs in verses 15 and 16. Every verb in the Hebrew text (Kittels) has been translated by the Greek [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}as a PAST AORIST: There isn't one future verb ("SHALL I wait\..." [ASV]{.class_3759}) or a present verb ("They ARE amazed\...they stand still\..." [ASV]{.class_3759}) in the passage. The [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(used by Bob Jones University and the Lockman Foundation to correct your [AV ]{.class_3759}Bible in 2,000 places in the New Testament---the Vatican manuscript IS the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}manuscript used to correct your New Testament, and it DOES contain the New Testament!) writes: "They were afraid" ([Heptoathasan]{.class_3759}), "They answered no longer" ([apekrithasan]{.class_3759}), "I WAITED" (Hupemenia), "Because they STOOD still" ([Estasan]{.class_3759}), "They ANSWERED not" ([apekrithasan]{.class_3759}). Now. What do you suppose happened when these "godly, dedicated, two-faced scoundrels" (who used the Vatican readings of Matthew-Revelation to correct your [AV ]{.class_3759}Receptus) saw the Vatican readings of Job 32:15 and 16 [matching the King James ]{.class_3759}text?

Simple: They threw them out.

You say, "Explain such conduct." Simple again: If you are a recognized, godly, dedicated, accredited, authoritative scholar of the "original languages" and you believe in the "verbal, plenary, inspiration of the original autographs," you qualify to alter any reading, any time you like, on no more grounds than whimsy or caprice. That's the name of the game, girlies. If you don't believe it, just check it out, chapter and verse in ANY language.

But let us not stop with the [Septuagint]{.class_3759}---the darling of the apostates---let us take a good look at the Hebrew text. ["They were amazed"]{.class_3760} ([AV]{.class_3759}, 1611)---Hebrew ["chatu." ]{.class_3759}The verb is in the PERFECT tense to be translated as [past completed action]{.class_3759}, exactly as the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}translated it. "They answered no more" ([AV]{.class_3759}, 1611)---Hebrew ["anah."]{.class_3759} The verb is in the PERFECT tense to be translated as past completed action, exactly as the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}translated it. ["They left off speaking"]{.class_3760} ([AV]{.class_3759}, 1611)---Hebrew "heatiku mehem milim" (Literally: "they were taken away from them their speech"). The verb (Hebrew ["heatiku"]{.class_3759} from ["athak"]{.class_3759}) is in the PERFECT TENSE to be translated as [past completed action]{.class_3759}, exactly as the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}translated it.

Then how were all three verbs in GREEK and HEBREW perverted to read in the [present tense ]{.class_3759}by the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) translators, thus destroying the location of the [author ]{.class_3759}of the Book of Job?

Now the Hiphil form (1 per. common singular) for ["I waited"]{.class_3760} in verse 16 could be translated as future since it is obviously the Perfect with waw consecutive (called also waw conversive). Normally when a verb in the perfect occurs with a waw consecutive, it is translated as IMPERFECT, in most cases in the future (see any first year Hebrew grammar.) But at this point the "qualified authorities" go to pieces, for the second half of the verse says ["they spake not"]{.class_3760} (imperfect, Hebrew[ "yidaberu"]{.class_3759}) and ["stood still"]{.class_3760} (perfect! Hebrew ["yamadu"]{.class_3759}). The [ASV ]{.class_3759}has translated the first [perfect ]{.class_3759}as [imperfect ]{.class_3759}(future), the [second imperfect ]{.class_3759}as [imperfect ]{.class_3759}(present: "they speak not"), and the [third perfect ]{.class_3759}as [imperfect ]{.class_3759}(present: "they stand still"). Now with such [consistency ]{.class_3759}in such a "valuable" translation for "serious Bible study, etc." (see how it's done?), why not just be perfectly consistent and translate: "I WAS WAITING" (past imperfect: the perfect with waw consecutive) because "THEY WERE NOT SPEAKING" (Past imperfect: the imperfect without waw consecutive) and because "THEY STOOD STILL" (past perfect as written).

So, the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) has much the best of the argument. The speaker is obviously speaking in the first person, on the spot, and commenting on the audience's reaction ("they," "them") to what he is doing.

Now we realize (gentle reader, etc.) that all of this grammatical excursion into syntax is a burden and a bore to the average reader. However, when dealing with a matter as important as the authorship of a Biblical Book, we should be more exacting than the bungling translators of the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition) or the Lockman Foundation (any publication). The "former theories" of Waw consecutive and Waw conjunctive in "parallel and sequence" for "Correlation and Coordination" is a heavy subject, and not even Gesenius (1815), August Muller (1882), W. R. Harper (1888), E. Kautsch (1909), and Dr. H. L. Eddleman could agree on the uniform translation of WAW with the imperfect and WAW with the perfect. J. W. Watts is of the opinion that translating the perfect with WAW as an imperfect every time is a mistake and that the perfect and imperfect never lose their basic meanings, even when preceded by WAW consecutive (or WAW conversive).

Observe, for example, the exact structure under discussion (["when I had waited"]{.class_3762} [AV]{.class_3761}) in Ecclesiastes 1:16: The [perfect with waw conjunctive ]{.class_3761}is translated as [past completed action]{.class_3761}. Again, Isaiah 1:2: A [perfect with waw conjunctive ]{.class_3761}is translated as past completed action. So, when we say that the [AV ]{.class_3761}translators were RIGHT and those who altered the text are WRONG, we are not just giving an "opinion." The truth of the matter is a "perfect" can be translated perfect with WAW to "correlate" it with an indicative imperfect or a participle or a prophetic perfect or subjunctive imperfect or an imperative or a "jussive." If it CAN BE, it is. That is, the [AV ]{.class_3761}text is innocent until proven guilty. It has NOT been proved guilty in Job 32:15--16.

But let us quit all the fancy "accredited, qualified, recognized" talk, shall we? Why not just take one cut straight to the bone: Job 32:16 is not an INTERROGATIVE as given in the corrupt ASV (any edition). Hebrew "questions" start with the ["he]{.class_3759} interrogative" or ["halo"]{.class_3759} (or adverbs such as [mathay, kammah, lammah, maddua]{.class_3759}, etc., or pronouns such as [me, mah]{.class_3759}, and [ezeh]{.class_3759}). The [ASV ]{.class_3759}reading ([Common Bible, RSV, NASV]{.class_3759}, etc.) is a fatuity. It is a fatuity designed to overthrow information on the inspired author of the Book of Job.

The reading, therefore, recommended by Bob Jones University (on the passage) is based on lying words (Jer. 7:4); it is not "out of the mouth of the Lord," and you are a fool to think that the sinners who recommended that kind of trash are any more "godly" or "separated" than the sinner who sold His Lord out for thirty pieces of silver: Judas didn't drink or smoke, and he stood for the "fundamentals," and he was [evangelistic ]{.class_3759}(Matt. 10)! Think about that awhile.

Elihu wrote the Book of Job; so he speaks in the first person in the intermission (note the parenthesis in verse 16), and this settles forever any talk about the Book of Job being written after the time of Moses or Solomon. If Moses did any publishing or "redactor's work" on it, he got it finished from the hand of Jethro (Exod. 2:16), and Jethro's great grandfather had been raised on it.

Elihu is about ["to burst"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19) as they say down home; he is going to curdle and go sour if he doesn't say something (vs. 18). When he opens his mouth, he promises not to flatter anyone (vs. 22), so his speech will please neither Job nor his friends (Prov. 29:5 and comments). Elihu is as unlike the average Christian FM "speaker" in the twentieth century as William Booth compared to Johnny Carson. Elihu will try to show "no respect of persons" (vs. 21) as his friends did (vss. 15, 18, 20), but he will show his opinion (vs. 17) about matters. The next five chapters are Elihu's, and he makes up for lost time, although, basically, there is not a great deal of difference between his outlook and the others. The only real variation is that Elihu recognizes that Job's main trouble is not overt acts of sin, but his attitude toward his own goodness; and furthermore, Elihu "solves the problem" by positing the truth that [no matter what God does ]{.class_3759}to anyone, He is under no moral obligation to [explain it ]{.class_3759}or [justify it]{.class_3759}. Anyone as holy and as powerful as God Almighty is not going to be accused of wrong-doing simply because one ant on an ant heap (Isa. 40:22) doesn't like the way he is being treated (Rom. 9:20). Whatever the appearance of Job's situation is (2 Cor. 10:7), God makes no mistakes, no errors, no false moves, no lies, no unjust rules, and no unjust judgments (Rom. 2:2).

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2744.xhtml}

CHAPTER 33

33:1 Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.

2 Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.

3 My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.

4 The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

5 If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.

6 Behold, I am according to thy wish in God's stead: I also am formed out of the clay.

7 Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.

Nothing in the passage is especially difficult. Elihu will speak clearly (vs. 3) from an upright heart (vs. 3); he challenges Job to answer him when he is finished (vs. 5), and he promises not to be a terror to Job like Job claimed God to be (vs. 7). (See Job 32:8--9, 6:10, 9:34--35, and 16:21.)

33:8 Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,

9 I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.

10 Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,

11 He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.

12 Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.

13 Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.

Although Job did not say verse 9 in so many words (see 10:7 and 9:17, 21), he at least implied it (see 32:1--2, and 34:5). Job had said verse 10---["Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy"]{.class_3760}---in his complaining (13:24) in several places. He had accused God of marking his paths (vs. 11) in the stocks (13:27;,14:16, and 31:4), and "in this" Job was not just. Elihu says: ["I will answer thee, that God is greater than man"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). Of course Job knows this (see Job 12), but again we are dealing with the difference between abstract knowledge of God's attributes and the actual responses to God's dealings with us in this life. Job knows that ["God is greater than man,"]{.class_3760} but not great enough to avoid being charged with false dealing under certain circumstances---his [own!]{.class_3759}

"[He giveth not account of any of his matters"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13). (See Isa. 45:9 and Job 9:12.) At least, he does not always give account of all His matters. God has given an account more than once (vs. 14) about some of his ["matters,"]{.class_3762} but as often as not, men have simply ignored God's account or altered it (see any "new translation" recommended by anyone). God is not obligated to give account of ["his matters,"]{.class_3762} but often He chooses to do so (Hosea 12:10; Deut. 29:29); when He does (1 Cor. 2:7--8), man had better "sit up and take notice."

33:14 For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.

15 In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;

16 Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,

17 That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.

18 He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

God's method of revelation before Mosaic times is indirect, by means of dreams and visions and occasional "theophanies" (Gen. 12:1--3). There is no chance at all that the author of the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}would be writing [after ]{.class_3759}Deuteronomy chapter 34. After Deuteronomy chapter 34, it is understood by everyone that God spoke face to face with Moses (Deut. 34:10) as a man speaks to his friend (Num. 12:6, 8), and that these words were reduced to [writing ]{.class_3759}(Deut. 31:24) in a BOOK (Heb. 9:19).

Verses 14--17, then, clearly refer to God's method of revealing Himself to the American Indian before 1492, to the Chinese and Japanese before A.D. 100, and to the "heathen" of Psalm 19:1--4 who once ["knew God"]{.class_3760} (Rom. 1:17--21). The time, therefore, that Joe Smith and Brigham Young took to protect the salvation of the American Indian from damnation was wasted time. God could appear to [anybody ]{.class_3759}whenever He felt like it (Heb. 12:19), and He could [converse ]{.class_3759}with anybody (Gen. 20:6) anytime He felt like it (Gen. 16:7--10) through any number of Theophanies. If you think the American Indians of 400 B.C. didn't know more about "God the Father" and the Spirit than Madam Curie did (A.D. 1868--1934), you haven't checked out the "Nobel Prize" winners very closely on their "God- knowledge."

God ["openeth the ears of men"]{.class_3760} at night (vs. 16) and then seals up this instruction so that the next day the man follows a course of action without even knowing what prompted him. In the Day of Judgment, unsaved men are going to be horrified to find out how much of their lives, plans, decisions, and actions were determined by SUBCONSCIOUS instruction that came as a result of their rejection of the truth (2 Thess. 2; Ezek. 14). In the same fashion, there is going to be some shouting over the revelation of the ["hidden things of darkness"]{.class_3760} (1 Cor. 4:5) when the Judgment Seat of Christ reveals how much God had to do with the right decisions and correct judgments made by His people in this life. Where truth is relished, sin is abhorred; where sin is relished, truth is abhorred, and you cannot get over those "polarities" with a Polar bear or a pole vaulter. A man reaps what he sows, and a man who deceives will be deceived (2 Thess. 2:9--12); that is the law of the universe (Ezek. 14:4--10) by the One who made it (1 Kings 22).

Where Christ has been rejected and the Bible perverted, there is no way any man on this earth can be sure of one right decision in twenty-four hours. If God can turn the bad things to good things (Rom. 8:28) for the believer, He can turn the good things to bad things for the sinner who rejects the truth. When God visited David ["in the night"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 17:3), he was determined that the Lord would not Xerox a recorded impression (John 4:18) of transgressions and sin. Who knows what happens while he is sleeping? The dream books and the legends of incubuses and succubuses, ESP, mental telepathy, and researches on "brain waves" are only the attempts of truth-rejecting sinners to neutralize work in the spirit world and get rid of the moral issues involved in that realm.

33:19 He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:

20 So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.

21 His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.

22 Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.

In what follows, Elihu describes (spiritually speaking) the ruin and redemption of a sinner as God describes the ruin (vs. 18) and then leads the sinner to repentance (vs. 19). In the description, Job sees his own condition like a mirror, for all that Elihu says in verses 19--22 is literally true of Job. God will go to great lengths to show a man his self-righteous pride (vs. 17) and prevent him from taking credit for his actions (vs. 17). God will put him in the Sick Bay (Infirmary, Hospital Ward, Dispensary, bone factory, etc.) and let him suffer (vs. 19). He will fix a man so he loses his appetite (vs. 20) for ordinary bread (vs. 20) and also for caviar, roast pheasant, and avocados (vs. 20). Pining away, the sick man will get so skinny his bones begin to stick out through his flesh (vs. 21), and his soul draws ["near unto the grave"]{.class_3760} as his life draws near to the undertaker, mortician, grave digger, and funeral orator (["the destroyers," ]{.class_3760}vs. 22). This is Job's condition. Elihu says that it has been brought about to ["hide pride"]{.class_3760} from Job (vs. 17) and defeat his ["purpose."]{.class_3760} And, so far, Elihu is right. There now follows one of the greatest types of New Testament salvation found anywhere in the Bible.

33:23 If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:

24 Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.

25 His flesh shall be fresher than a child's: he shall return to the days of his youth:

26 He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.

One cannot look at the passage without being reminded of the New Birth (vs. 25), the Blood ransom (Matt. 20:28), and the salvation of the soul (vs. 24).

The unsaved man is a wreck. And whether this wreck appears in broad daylight in the gutters of downtown New York or Detroit drunk in his own vomit or whether that wreck appears in a "Tux, tie, and tails" at a formal wedding in the White House, the wreckage is the same. Some men who are going to Hell broadcast it and some don't; some sinners broadcast their destination before they get there, while others camouflage it. That is, some garbage cans are polished, bright, clean, new aluminum, and some are weatherbeaten, rusty pieces of iron; they both contain GARBAGE (see Matt. 23:27 if you think I worded it too strongly; at least we didn't accuse you of being a putrefying [corpse ]{.class_3759}as the Lord Jesus Christ accused the established religious leaders of His day).

Before continuing, we shall print the closing verses which have application to the backslidden Christian as well as the unsaved man.

33:27 He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;

28 He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.

29 Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,

30 To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.

31 Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.

32 If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.

33 If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.

Now here we find RUIN (vs. 22), REVELATION (vs. 23), REPENTANCE (vs. 27), RANSOM (vs. 24), REGENERATION (vs. 25), RECONCILIATION (vs. 26), and RESURRECTION (vs. 30). And this old story has been told and enacted so many times in the history of sinful man that 3,000 volumes of 1,000 pages each would not tell the story. God's salvation has carried thousands of men (literally) from the "guttermost to the uttermost," and if the truth for many a life were posted over the door posts of that heart, it would read "RFD"--- Rescued From the Dump.

Here is John Gimenez, who began to fool with hallucinogens and lysergic acid diethylamine (LSD), and finally began to "take off" as a mainliner on heroin. Gimenez was a first-class junkie before he was twenty-five years old; he was "on the horse" when God rescued him from the dump. In his childhood, Gimenez knew more about Murphy men, hustlers, chickies, bulls, fences, horse parties, narcos, "booting it," skin pops, pushers, and muggers than most of you will ever know about your Bible. At Mountain Dale (a Christian rehabilitation center), Gimenez finally wound up "cold turkey," after touching the brink of insanity and suicide half a dozen times. A burnt-out wreck at thirty years of age, Gimenez had "had the treatment"; he had drained the barrel of life and found nothing in the bottom of it but gravel and broken glass. At Mountain Dale, a converted "knife" man (Jackie Dean) suddenly woke up the "barracks" one night, at three in the morning, hollering, "Devil get outta here! Leave this place right now; in the name of Jesus, GO!" Gimenez got up in a cold sweat and went outside the building while the rest of the men knelt inside to pray. In his own words, Gimenez was "crying, weeping way down deep inside." Gimenez said that God was shaking him like he had dumped a dirty rag into a washer and was banging it round and round. With the men inside praying for him, Gimenez staggered down a dirt road at 3:20 A.M. He began to weep and laugh at the same time. Suddenly all the heaviness left him and he began to sing: "Jesus, Jesus, praise your name! Thank you Lord! Come here, Jesus!"

At 7:00 A.M., that former addict (with nothing behind him but sin and nothing ahead of him but a harvest \[Gal. 6:7--8\] that would cause Gabriel to tremble) came back into the barracks and fell asleep. He didn't wake up till noon, and when he got up he was a new creature in Christ, filled with the Holy Spirit, born again, and burning with a desire to witness for Christ instead of smoking pot. Gimenez says: "Man, it was like an explosion!" In writing to one of his former friends (who was still "carrying the monkey"), Gimenez says, "Curtis, you'll make it man, with Jesus Christ. Don't you forget it, Jesus is HIGHER than HIGH!" (And for those of us who have read Col. 1:16--18, an "Amen" would be just as appropriate following this junkie expression as it would be following a reading of John 3:16.)

And over in this corner is Thomas Noah Carter, arrested in Arizona for dope and malpractice of medicine. Both of his grandfathers were preachers. Thomas Noah Carter studied at Mercer University and was the graduate of a Medical College, but he never received Christ, and he never believed the Bible. His mother prayed for him for more than twenty years. In jail, Carter cursed God and the Bible. A tubercular condition siezed him, and he went from 225 pounds to 118 pounds in less than six months. One time in his cell, he passed into a catalyptic state, and the prison doctor pronounced him dead. The prison officials notified Carter's aged mother, but she wrote them a letter saying: "I don't believe it. I've been praying for twenty years that God would make a preacher out of him, and I haven't heard him preach yet." He wasn't dead, but he was half dead. A week later Carter's voice failed him, and he could only curse God in a whisper. Carter then went to the chapel and came forward at an invitation, but the Chaplain told him that he was too wicked to be saved (Isa. 1:18); so, in despair, Carter returned to his cell, tore a blanket into strips (intending to hang himself), and prepared to die. But Carter's preparation miscarried. Just as he was tearing a third strip off of the blanket, a Bible fell out of it. Carter sat down on his bunk, in a sweat, and opened the Book: It opened at 1 John 1:7. As he read 1 John 1 and 2, the Lord said to him: "If you will teach and preach this Book, I'll save and heal you." Carter began to shout; he woke up all the prisoners in his cell block. The warden and two guards came in to give him some "special treatment," but the warden got converted coming through the door. (God had been dealing with the warden for nearly three months, and as he stepped into the cell block and heard Carter yelling, "Glory to God! Praise God for the Lord Jesus Christ!" the warden accepted Christ on the spot.) When they opened the cell, Carter rushed to him and hugged him and yelled, "Warden, I've just been saved! I've just been saved!" and before the body guards could interfere, the warden was yelling, "Me too, Carter, me too!" Thomas Noah Carter was given an unconditional pardon after serving four years of a ten-year sentence, and he preached the truth of God till the day that he died: RFD---"Rescued From the Dump!"

And who could forget old "Bulldog" Charlie Wyman from Kent, Kentucky? You could "read the wallpaper" on the walls of the house where he was born; it had a ground sandstone floor. When Charlie was a boy his mother whipped him three times because of three fist fights he had with an older boy at school who had challenged him. Each time she whipped him, Charlie would say, "Mother, if you whip me again, I'll beat that boy up worse than the last time." The third time (after he had put the older boy in the hospital) he said, "Mother, I'm going back and whip that kid again, and if you whip me one more time, I'll kill him." So Charlie's "home discipline" (without a father) ended there at the age of fifteen. The rest of Charlie Wyman's life reads like a Zane Grey novel or a Hollywood "Western." He shot out all the street lights in his home town as fast as they were put in, and he whipped every deputy sheriff that came to town to arrest him. He took the guns out of the holster of a sheriff and told him to "be a good boy and go home before you get into trouble." He crippled a man for life in one gun fight, killed two men in another, and shot all the fingers off a man's hand except one; and in a bloody shotgun versus .38 duel, one man went insane, one died of wounds, and Charlie caused the death of a clean-cut youngster (named Foss) who was not even involved in the fight. Running a liquor store and pool hall, "Bulldog" Charlie Wyman ran that town.

Then, in 1913, a small, circuit-riding, country preacher came to town. His name was "Night Hawk" Tom, and he was called that because it was rumored that no man could know as much about the sins of his congregation as "Night Hawk" knew unless he was abroad at night "window peeping." The first time Wyman saw Tom going down the street to the "meetin' house," he said, "ah, that little rabbit! Ah ain't going to kill him; ah'll jus run him outta town." The next day Night Hawk Tom crossed the street to Charlie's pool hall, introduced himself, took Charlie's hand, and invited him to the revival meeting. When he left, Charlie shook his head and said to himself, "Now I wonder why a good man like that would take time out to talk to a rascal like ME?" Well, the ["steps of a good man are ordered by]{.class_3760} [the Lord"]{.class_3760}; and Charlie didn't know it, but he wasn't about to run Night Hawk Tom out of [anywhere]{.class_3759}.

The next night (after the revival had been running for a week), Wyman got terribly sick. He closed down the pool hall early, and in a drizzling rain, he walked home down the dark streets of the town. As the downpour increased, he stepped into a front yard and took temporary shelter under the eaves of a large two-story house. The house had belonged to a young woman (Gaussey) whose husband was serving time for killing a man during a gunfight with Wyman. Leaning on the gate post near the window, Charlie Wyman wound up his "career." You see, the townspeople at the meeting had been praying for Charlie Wyman in public and private devotions for nearly a month, and as Wyman huddled against the house below a bay window, he saw Alice Gaussey---the wife of the man who was in jail---come into the room with her two small children. They knelt, facing the window, and as the storm slacked and the rain stopped, Wyman heard that woman and her children praying aloud: "Oh God, please save the godless sinner, Charlie Wyman! Oh Lord, please save us and our town from that murderer! Don't let him burn in Hell, Lord, please save him!"

That prayer struck old "Bulldog" Charlie through like an arrow that pierces to the marrow of the bones. Charlie said to himself: "I am the man for whom she is praying. Oh my God!"

To make a long story short, Charlie was in church the next night. Before an amazed congregation, he literally ran to the altar at the invitation and knelt there, as he said, with "blood dripping off my hands." At the altar, that old-time Methodist circuit rider told him to repent and make restitution before asking God for any favors---that's how they did it in those days. Charlie got up and went to the banker and apologized to him for some bad checks; he then turned himself in to the sheriff whom he had made "dance" with bullets when the sheriff came to arrest him. When asked for someone to sign his bond, Charlie moaned, "Nobody in this country would sign a bond for me."

"Well," said the sheriff, "do you mean business for God?"

With tears raining down his face, Wyman said, "Oh I want to do [right]{.class_3759}. I want to be a GOOD man! I want to go to Heaven; if I have to go to the penitentiary the rest of my life, I must make it to Heaven."

The sheriff bonded him out.

"Bulldog" Charlie preached the old-time gospel for nearly fifty years after God rescued him from the dump (Job 33:24, 27--28). And time would fail us to tell of those legions of "mouldy saints" with "unwashed breath" whom God salvaged for His glory and made their "harps" (Job 30:31) sound forth the praises of Zion.

On the 14th day of March, 1949, a drunken bum, working as a disc-jockey at a radio station (WEAR, Pensacola, Florida), received Christ as his Saviour. He made the decision in the record room of the station surrounded by Hank Williams, Patti Page, Tony Martin, Tex Ritter, Doris Day, Tommy and Jimmy Dorsey, Dizzie Gillespie, Artie Shaw, Peggy Lee, Benny Goodman, and Stan Kenton. At twenty-seven years of age this DJ had "had the treatment." Raised as an Episcopalian with a vestryman for a father, educated as a college graduate with a major in Radio and Psychology, trained as an Infantry officer with forty-six months active duty as a Rifle Platoon leader, catechized as a Roman Catholic proselyte by a Jesuit priest from Loyola, and acquainted with the world system through jobs as a lifeguard, bartender, beach boy, radio announcer, newspaper cartoonist, and dance band drummer, this wreck had reached the end of the rope. Liquor had not solved his problems, lust and dissipation had failed to provide any happiness, education and science had failed miserably, religion and mysticism had accomplished nothing of a permanent nature, and three years of "TM" and Zen Buddhism had failed to still the voice of outraged conscience and guilt. At twenty-seven, this junk heap was on his way to the junk yard---lost, drunk, ruined, disillusioned, bitter, broke, and alone in the world ["having no hope, and without God"]{.class_3760} (Eph. 2:12).

A country Baptist preacher led him to Christ in the radio station that morning. In less than a month, he was studying the Bible. In less than a year, he was preparing for the ministry, and that depraved sinner (who had tried everything short of murder and suicide to attain his ends) has been preaching and teaching the living words of the living God for more than forty-eight years.

What he could not find in two universities, three religious systems, four years of Government service, and every hell hole from Bourbon Street (New Orleans) to Hell's Half-acre (Honolulu, Hawaii), he found in God's Book! RFD---Rescued From the Dump. ["His flesh shall be fresher than a child's: he shall return to the days of his youth\...he shall pray unto God\...he shall see his face with joy\...He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life SHALL SEE THE LIGHT"]{.class_3760} (Job 33:25--28). And God works these things ["oftentimes"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29) with man.

Many a man who had worn out his constitution is living on his by-laws, or as an octogenarian said: "If I'da known I was gonna live this long, I would have taken better care of myself!" There are physical wrecks on this earth, social wrecks, financial wrecks, and spiritual wrecks; but our God is a "junk specialist," and He can take a physical wreck, like the man in Mark 5:5 or the woman in Mark 5:26, and restore their health. He can take a religious wreck like Paul and make a flaming evangelist out of him. He can take a scholarly wreck like R. A. Torrey or B. H. Carroll and make them preach the Virgin Birth, the Blood Atonement, and the physical Resurrection. He can take a social wreck like the Samaritan woman of John 4:18 (or Mary Magdalene) and make her fit to dine with princes and angels (Rev. 19:9). And there has been many a "wreck" salvaged by God, who went on to greater service in the Lord's vineyard than the smooth, slick, flawless, kept-and-cared-for "late models," like the "elder brother" of Luke 15. Many of these "cultured" Christians (Luke 7:39) thought that because they had morals, money, property, education, and prestige that God was obligated to use them; sometimes God will use an INSTITUTION when He can't find a man in it that He can do anything with by himself. "Corporative Christianity" and Communism are very popular these days, and when you hear many of these smooth, slick, professional motivators bragging about "THEIR" work, they are often referring to 10--200 Christians who are doing the work in their stead, paying the bills in their stead, teaching the converts in their stead, and working on slave wages to promote the "work." First Corinthians 3:13 will iron a lot of things out.

Well, there is a ["messenger"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23) who is a messenger of reconciliation, and there is an ["interpreter"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23) who is the Author of the message (Gen. 40:8). The Holy Spirit is the One who shows a ["man his uprightness"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23) in no uncertain terms (Luke 18:14), and when the Holy Spirit gets through with a man like Job or Paul (see Job 42:6), there aren't enough pieces of "self" left to sweep up in a vacuum cleaner. God has found a ransom (Matt. 20:28) for the hell-bound sinner (Rom. 3:26), and he can now be delivered (vs. 24). In type, the New Birth creates a baby (vs. 25) with skin as fresh as Naaman's (2 Kings 5), and what is said here (especially verses 26, 28, and 30) applies to the Tribulation saints in Israel who will ["see"]{.class_3760} God's face (Heb. 12:4) literally (Psa. 50:2, 3). \[See Job 11:15.\]

In the context, God is rendering to the man his own righteousness---not the Righteousness of God (see v. 26), and, again, the modern Fundamental expositors are faced with that terrible truth of "FAITH PLUS WORKS" for the Tribulation saints. Rather than submit to this Biblical truth (Rev. 22:14) which is taught in either Testament (James 5:18--19), the modern "Fundamentalist" does two things:

1\. He insists that everyone who was ever saved in [either Testament ]{.class_3759}was saved by faith [plus nothing]{.class_3759}. Having done this, he is unable to exposit Matthew chapter 25, Zechariah 13:2--3, or Hebrews chapter 8. Further, he must then insist that the examples given in Hebrews 11:1--30 refer to faith in the completed work of Christ, for this is the "faith" of Ephesians 2:1--9. Forgetting that the first twenty-four verses of Hebrews 11 deal with saints BEFORE the Law, the modern expositor simply makes hash out of the Bible to preserve one doctrine which he knows to be true [in this age:]{.class_3759} Typical.

2\. He will then devote his time to expositions of the Hebrew Christian Epistles (called "General" or "Catholic"), since these are the ones where the faith-plus-works set up is most manifest (see for exmaple Heb. 3:14, 6:6, 10:30; James 2, etc.). These expositions constitute volumes of works on Greek grammar and exegesis published by modern Fundamental scholars (who have already rejected the [AV ]{.class_3759}text). What it amounts to is this: Since they do not believe the Bible as it stands, because "as it stands" it often goes contrary to ONE doctrine in this age, they will alter [every word ]{.class_3759}or [every verse ]{.class_3759}dealing with that doctrine to make it conform to their own stupidity and then claim (publicly) that it was "light from the Original Greek text" that made the passages "understandable;" and without this knowledge of the "original Greek," poor, common, ordinary, dumb Christians (like some of YOU!) cannot "understand the passage." This demoniac horseplay goes on six hours a day in nearly every Bible college and university in America.

The thinking behind this ungodly, apostate nonsense is as follows:

A. I know my Greek; therefore, I have access to more truth than my students who don't know "Greek." (See [How to Teach the "Original" Greek, ]{.class_3759}1993.)

B. Therefore, "apparent contradictions" in the Hebrew Christian epistles can be ''ironed out" with the GREEK, because if my students knew Greek [like I know it ]{.class_3759}(see how it's done, Eve?), they would see that there is "really no contradiction at all." (See Custer's comment on Matthew 23:35.)

C. Therefore, I will become the "savior of Orthodoxy" and the "benefactor of Conservatism" by proving that there are no "contradictions" between Hebrews 3, 6, 10; James 2; and Romans 4--5; with Ephesians 2.

D. To do this, I will alter every word and every verse in the [AV ]{.class_3759}text that seems to contradict the Pauline Epistles. This will "resolve" the apparent contradictions, and it will make me a god in the eyes of my classroom (see Custer on Matt. 23:24). By obliterating the truth of God, preserved in the Protestant Reformation Bible, I will make God eternally grateful to me for helping [Him ]{.class_3759}out of the mess the [AV ]{.class_3759}translators got Him into (you bet'cher boots, sonny), and my students will be eternally grateful to me for magnifying their EDUCATION so [they ]{.class_3759}can browbeat uneducated Christians with their knowledge.

Now, that is how the snow drifts. And if that infuriates some of you, why don't you go out and get drunk or try pot? You would do less damage to the body of Christ than with your demoniacal foolishness.

The truth of the matter is that the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) text of Hebrews, James, Peter, John, and Jude is correct and is far superior to any Greek text available to anyone. Your linguistic ability (if you have any) never gave you the right to alter the Bible to suit your ignorance, nor does it give you the right to call people "nuts" and "heretics" because they [believe ]{.class_3759}the text. Your problem is simple: You want to force your alterations of text on the body of Christ so they will look to YOU as the final authority. To do this you will condemn every voice raised against your changes, and you will be just as bigoted and dogmatic about it (and just as stupid and intolerant) as the worst Pope who ever reigned in the "pornocracy" (tenth century). And let us give you a final lecture to prevent you from your madness: If you cannot alter your theology to meet the demands of the Biblical text, don't ever get out in the theological traffic with some of us, you're liable to get run over.

"[If any say, I have sinned\...perverted\...profited me not"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27). See Isa. 57:15; 66:2---this is what God is looking for.) And if you have perverted that which was RIGHT like Robertson, Weniger, McGee, Schaff, Trench, Vincent, Thayer, Westcott, Nestle, Hort, Peake, Budde, Duhm, Wuest, Thieme, Feinberg, Smith, Archer, Neal, Custer, Cleason, Reuben, Coder, LaSor, and Dummelow---see above---the correct response is not: "I have enabled God's people to arrive at a clearer understanding of the 'originals.'" The correct response is: ["I have sinned"]{.class_3762} (vs. 27).

When Pharaoh says this, it is a temporary expedient (see Exod. 10:16 and comments). When Balaam says it, it is to avoid getting clobbered (Num. 22:34). When Saul says it (1 Sam. 15:24), he says it to regain lost ground. Judas says it to relieve himself of guilt (Matt. 27:4); none of this amounts to anything. But when David says it (Psa. 51:4), he means business. Like the prodigal son who said it (Luke 15:18), David is under the lash of godly sorrow (2 Cor. 7:10) which ["worketh repentance\...not to be repented of."]{.class_3760} Simon Peter's repentance (Matt. 26:75) did not lead him to a gallows (Matt. 27:5); it led him to Pentecost (Acts 2:1--5) through Gethsemane (Matt. 26:41). Repent! Repent! That's the ticket! Peter preached it to Israel (Acts 2:38), Paul preached it to the Gentiles (Acts 20:21), and the Lord Jesus Christ recommended it for anyone (Luke 13:1--5).

Elihu, like God, desires to justify Job (vs. 32), but there is no way any sinner can be justified in his natural state (John 3:1--3). A greater righteousness than Job's is needed (Matt. 5:20) if God is going to justify you. The terrible truth of the matter is that God cannot justify any man apart from perfect righteousness (Rom. 3:21--22), and you will never ask for this gift (Rom. 6:23) or receive it (Rom. 10:9--10) until you HUNGER for it (Matt. 5:6; Rev. 22:17). Repent! As Sam Jones used to say: "Quit your meanness! Cease to do evil; learn to do good!"

A man who mistreated his wife and children regularly had been going to several psychiatrists for help. It was of no avail; the headshrinkers convinced him that moderate adultery and moderate drinking and moderate lying were moderately normal, and that as long as they didn't lead to a moderate divorce, he was neutrally "moderate." Still in misery, the man wound up in the pastorium of an old-time Southern Methodist preacher. The "Bible thumper" didn't waste five minutes with him; no sooner had he confessed his ill treatment of his family to the pastor than the pastor said, "Tell that to the Lord, mister--- right now!" And wham! They got down on their knees. While that distraught sinner was fumbling around for words to pray with, the preacher began to holler: "Fix him, Jesus, fix him!" (Well, he got "fixed," brother, and he got fixed without a "fix" and fixed better than you can get your car fixed at any garage in town these days.)

"[Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

"[Mark well, O Job"]{.class_3760} (vs.31).

If Job will "hold his peace" (vs. 33), he will learn something, and as surely as Naaman's big mouth (2 Kings 5) temporarily prevented him from being healed (2 Kings 5:12), many a doctor, lawyer, and college professor has blabbed his way into Hell trying to find mistakes in the Bible because it cramped his style of life. If some of you would just shut your big yapping mouths for one month, you would find Christ (Isa. 55:6--8). How is God going to talk to some of you, anyway, when you are so full of yourself you'd make a peacock look like a groundhog? Repent! Turn from your sacraments, your religion, your opinions, your traditions, your news media "information," and your "education." Shut up a minute and maybe God will show you something you can't find in a university.

I never read Job 33:17 without thinking of a certain night in Mobile, Alabama, back in 1955. I was preaching at the rescue mission on Government Street (Reynolds was the superintendent). When I finished the message (I believe it was on Luke 16:19--25), I turned to the song leader and said, "Let's stand and sing number\...." But before I could finish, there was a commotion in the audience; I turned and saw a young man about twenty-five struggling to get to his feet. His face was wet with tears, and as he stood he stammered, "P-p-preacher! Preacher!" Someone tried to help him into the aisle, but he brushed him aside. He stepped into the aisle (without an invitation hymn!) and staggered toward me; he was walking like a drunken man, but he was cold sober. It was the painful walk of a condemned criminal heading for an execution. The tears now began to rain and he began to cry out: "Oh God! Oh God!" Then he fell on his knees in the aisle. Again, someone tried to help him, but he pushed him back and began to crawl to the altar, choking and weeping as he came. I came down from the pulpit to meet him at the prayer rail, but he had fallen flat on his face four feet short of the altar. All I could hear him say was, "I have sinned! I have sinned!" over and over again. Two men lifted him up and brought him to the prayer rail, and there he passed from death to life (vs. 28), receiving God's provision for guilty sinners---the Lord Jesus Christ.

When the service was over, another wreck of a man (an old drunk about sixty years old) came up to me and said (between "hics"): "Preacher, d-d-do you rem-re-rember me?"

"No," I said, "who are you?"

"Well, preacher I am one of your (hic) converts. I got s-s-saved under your ministry here l-l-l-last year!"

"Well," said, "you aren't doin' too good, are you?"

"Nossir, preacher, I ain't. B-but I'll tell you one thing (hic)! You know somethin', preacher? I missed a (hic) freight train outta here tonight jes to hear you preach!!"
