(Among my preaching "souvenirs" I relish that one night more than many a night teaching and preaching to 3,000 people in a large church.)

It is useless to review the destructive work done on the passage by the Lockman Foundation (supported by Bob Jones University). Never satisfied with the heavenly manna, never able to believe what God said as He said it, never thankful for the greatest book ever written, and never content to submit to it, the Scholars Union has butchered the truth of the passage till you couldn't even call it a Bible. Adam Clarke has inserted the word "head" into verse 23; "my soul" has been stuck into verse 28 ([Jerusalem Targum ]{.class_3759}and the [Vulgate]{.class_3759}); DeRossi and Kennicott have "shall be AS the light" for verse 28; ["living" ]{.class_3760}is "life" according to Peace (vs. 30); the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) restores to a man his righteousness (vs. 26); "twice, yea thrice" has been put into verse 29 by the [New Scofield Reference "Bible"]{.class_3759}; the interpreter (vs. 23) is an "angel" in the [NEB]{.class_3759}; the ransom (vs. 24) is a "substitute" in the [Living Bible]{.class_3759}; the interpreter (vs. 23) is a "spokesman" for Jehovah's Witnesses ([NWT]{.class_3759}), and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown contribute about fifteen more changes. The [NASV]{.class_3759}, in comparison with the mass of trivia, shows about as much difference as the difference between a dead rat and a dead squirrel.

Clarke and Henry make some good spiritual application to the text, but of course the New Testament prefiguring is so obvious here that not even Clarke and Matthew Henry could miss it.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2838.xhtml}

CHAPTER 34

34:1 Furthermore Elihu answered and said,

2 Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.

3 For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.

4 Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.

5 For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.

6 Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.

7 What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?

8 Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.

9 For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.

There is nothing particularly difficult about Elihu's speech. ["Words"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2) are like food (vs. 3), so the mouth tastes them exactly as it tastes any kind of food. Since the Bible is likened to apples, bread, milk, meat, and honey, the words of the Bible can furnish a "balanced diet" for any man who esteems them "more than his necessary food" (Job 23:12). Ezekiel, in the Old Testament, is told to eat the words of God (Ezek. 3:2), and so is the Apostle John in the New Testament (Rev. 10:10). The outstanding characteristic, therefore, of the modern American "Christian" is MALNUTRITION. To make sure that the Laodicean generation will come out with spiritual rickets, scurvy, diarrhea, low blood pressure, diabetes, leukemia, blurred vision, arthritis, palegra, Parkinson's disease, and chronic headaches the "leading Christian scholars" and educators have served up the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) and the [NASV ]{.class_3759}(1963); the Liberals have cooked up the [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}and the [NRSV ]{.class_3759}(["Common" Bible]{.class_3759}), and the apostate blubber-blabber Charismatic Interdenominationalists (and Ecumenical Socialists) have set the table with The ["LIVING"]{.class_3759}---God help us---["Bible. ]{.class_3759}A tree is known by its fruits, and [food ]{.class_3759}is known by the [type of constitution ]{.class_3759}it produces; you become what you EAT (see Exod. 12:15 and comments). The modern Christian in America (1933--1978) was fed and nourished by a diet that would gag a buzzard. After the revolution (1979--1982) when America becomes a Roman-Communist country, a different type of Christian will surface, but until then, the type of Christian being produced by most Christian schools is an EFFEMINATE IDOLATOR. One can usually spot these sophisticated gourmets by the passages in the Bible which they reject or refused to preach on openly. You see, some passages in the Bible are:

1\. SALTY (Ezek. 16, 23; Prov. 3--8; Judg. 16--18).

2\. TENDER (Song of Sol.; 1 Cor. 13; Luke 2).

3\. SWEET (Psa. 8, 23, 32; Hosea 2).

4\. TOUGH (Jude, 1 Pet.; Heb. 10; 2 Thess. 2; Ezek. 14).

5\. DRY (1 Chron. 1--12; Num. 1--9).

6\. SOUR (Lam. 1--5; Jer. 11, 23).

7\. LUSCIOUS (John 17; Rom. 8; Eph. 2).

8\. FIERY (Matt. 23; John 9; Rev. 22).

9\. COOL (Prov. 1; Ecc. 1--9).

What the modern Christian wants is a BLAND DIET to match Melba toast (without butter) and bran flakes sopped in milk. This will be a SAFE diet, and safety is the first and primary concern of the modern Christian celebrity whose "ministry" or "work" depends upon staying in good with the "right" people. "Safety First" is the honest slogan of many a dishonest "defender of the faith" who has no more guts than a butterfly. "Milk sop" is the best descriptive word for the average Christian in America between 1940--1980. "Hell" is too FIERY for the palate of the effeminate Lockman Foundation and Bob Jones University; so they refused to translate the Greek word ["hades"]{.class_3759} in the New Testament. ["Devils"]{.class_3760} is too tough for the [NASV ]{.class_3759}and the [NRSV]{.class_3759}; so they leave the word "daimon" untranslated in the New Testament. ["BLOOD"]{.class_3760} is too salty for the taste buds of "Good News"; so it has been altered to "death" in a dozen places. As the cool, refined, intellectual breath of pantywaist seminarians breathes over the words of Divine authority, it waters down or leavens or contaminates (or obliterates) every major passage that would offend SOMEONE. For example: Notice how Bob Jones University and the NCCC are in perfect agreement on the alterations made in Romans 1:18,21, and 25, [where the Holy Spirit aimed those words at Bible teachers and revisors]{.class_3759}. (And imagine some little, flea-bitten, Orthodox mutt growling about "Modernism" and "Neo-evangelicalism" after siding with the Liberals ten dozen times---see any edition of any [ASV ]{.class_3759}recommended by any graduate of BJU---against the Holy Bible. Stay in the barn, sonny; the chickens are liable to eat you.)

"[For Job hath said, I am righteous"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5); well, he had come mighty close to it in Job 27:2--7 and 19:7, and if he had not said it outrightly, he had, at least, given a fifteen minute demonstration (Chapter 31) of his righteousness where all four of them could hear it. Verse 6 is a free quotation from Job meaning: "Should I pretend that I deserve this punishment when I didn't deserve it? I have been wounded by God with no reason for it." Elihu not only accuses Job of drinking [iniquity ]{.class_3761}like water (see 15:16), but [scorning ]{.class_3761}as well. The idea is that Job fills his belly with scorn just like a man fills it full of water. But Job has only "gone in the company with the workers of iniquity" in the sense that he has taken their side in the argument that comes up between God and men (see vs. 36). Elihu seats Job smack down in the middle of Psalm 1:1, which see. ["For he hath said\..."]{.class_3762} (vs. 9) shows what is meant by ["walketh with wicked men"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). Elihu then removes Job from Psalm 1:1 and puts him back down into Job 21:15 by saying: ["For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9). Now Job had NOT said that.

(Sensing the incongruity of the charge, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Henry, Lange, Ellicott, Dummelow, Kline, Heavenor, and the [LXX ]{.class_3759}all bypass the verse and fail to give a cross-reference to explain it, or (the [LXX]{.class_3759}) simply alter the wording so Elihu does not say what he said. The [Cross Reference Bible]{.class_3759}, compiled by 200 scholars (including Monser, Price, Terry, Robertson, Dungan, Sampey, Eiselen, and Zenos), refers its readers to Job 35:3. Once the reader follows this blind lead into a dead end, he arrives at the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition) which alters verse 3 so that it makes absolute nonsense.)

Now the sense of Elihu's remark is both profound and bitter. What Elihu is saying is that where a man delights himself in God (as Job had done) and obeys God (as Job had done), he is bound to profit (1 Tim. 6:5). Job's railing accusations (37:2) and grievous complaints (Job 30) are proof, therefore, that Job has not been delighting himself in God (although he did) and has not been serving God (although he had been). That is, Elihu is in the same trap in which Bildad, Zophar, and Eliphaz were ensnared. Absolute proof of this is found in what immediately follows (vss. 11, 21--22, and 26). Elihu accuses Job of refusing to get right (vs. 37) after being caught in sin and punished (vs. 37); in this respect, he [would ]{.class_3759}be going in company with the ["workers of iniquity"]{.class_3760} and walking with ["wicked men"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8).

It is not until the New Testament that we find an ordinary man who can pass through a valley nearly as bad as Job's and still ["delight himself with God."]{.class_3760} Not even Jeremiah could refuse the temptation to call God a liar when times got tough (Jer. 15:18). David comes as close to delighting himself in God (profit or not) as any Old Testament saint who ever lived, but again, we find complaints in David's prayers of praise (Psa. 73:1--10) that are entirely missing from the Pauline epistles. When God told Paul ["My grace is sufficient for thee"]{.class_3760} (2 Cor. 12:9), he demanded no further outward proofs of God's blessing, no matter what his enemies thought or did (see Phil. 1:28 and comments). Beaten and half blind, chained and imprisoned, bereft of property, securities, buildings, family, and friends (2 Tim. 4:16), you don't hear one word out of Paul which would match Job's lament in Job 31. Paul simply counted the ["loss"]{.class_3760} (Phil. 3:8) as good riddance and threw in ["all things"]{.class_3760} in case Rev. Salmond or Rev. Rendall (or the scholars that taught them) went to the "original Greek" in order to make a liar out of him.

34:10 Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.

11 For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.

12 Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.

13 Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?

14 If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;

15 All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.

Verse 10 is perfectly clear, and the two verses that follow it need no exposition; God's holiness and righteousness are self-evident by virtue of His essential being. If God is not absolutely holy and absolutely righteous, He is not "the Alpha and Omega, the Beginning and the End," and substitute gods are in order for a variety of idolators. The Judge of the whole earth will ["do RIGHT"]{.class_3760} (Gen. 18:25). He will not pervert judgment (vs. 12) even where we suppose that He has. If you had a dollar for every G.I. who became a practical Atheist during a war, you would be rich. One unregenerated look at the suffering that goes on in Africa, China, Viet Nam, Korea, and Siberia would convince nearly any unsaved "intellectual" that God was either dead or "unemployed" (to cite Walt Kelley).

But ["who hath given HIM a charge over the earth?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13)? YOU? Did you? Did "mother nature?" Did the "cosmic laws" of the nebular dust clouds? Where were Democritus, Bacon, Hobbes, Hume, Huxley, Einstein, and Kant (and all of their monkey retinue) when God created the galaxies? Did any of your forebears tell God who He could give power to and who He could keep it from? If any one of you (or any 30,000,000 of you) wanted to stop poverty, death, war, hell, famine, or torture tonight, could you do it? None of your forebears in six millenniums could do it. God disposes dispensations as He sees fit (Rom. 13:1--5), and you could no more prevent Daniel's Seventieth Week and the Millennium from arriving on schedule than you could make a Bible-believer out of Kenneth Wuest (see Prov. 19:21 and comments).

"[If he set his heart upon man\...gather unto himself his spirit\...man shall turn again unto dust"]{.class_3760} (vss. 14 and 15). (See Ecc. 12:7.)

This time the heart of God would not be set on man in the sense of Psalm 91:14 and John 10:15, but in the sense of Genesis 6:5 and Psalm 104:29 and 30 where all flesh did ["perish together"]{.class_3760} (Gen. 6:13). If God gathers man's spirit (Gen. 2:7) to Himself (Ecc. 3:21) and man's breath from his body, man sinks down into the dust from whence he came (Gen. 3:19), and that is the end of his physical life.

34:16 If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.

17 Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?

18 Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked? and to princes, Ye are ungodly?

19 How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all are the work of his hands.

20 In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.

21 For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.

22 There is no darkness, nor shadow of death where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

23 For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.

24 He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.

25 Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.

26 He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

27 Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:

28 So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.

The proposition that Elihu puts to Job is the one warned about in Jude 9 and 1 Peter 2:17---railing on and reviling gods and rulers. In a Democracy, this business is little understood, if understood at all, for in a Democracy (the lowest form of government---see Dan. 2:41---before the Military Dictatorship of Satan) you can make Spiro Agnew watches to match Mickey Mouse, you can put a balloon over the picture of a President's mouth making him say, "Dear Abby\...," and you can urinate on an American flag while it is hanging upside down from a fishing pole.

Harry Truman often referred to anyone who was exposing Communism in the government as a "Demagogue." It became quite popular in the 1950s to refer to any pro-Communist as a "civil rights leader" (see how it's done?). "Life line" (Melvin Mund, 1960--1974) is the best example of constructive criticism of the "powers that be" within a Christian context of "honour to whom honour is due." (Of course, one can go overboard with it; men like Rice, Custer, Bob Jones III, Wilbur Smith, and Merrill Unger would always prompt us to render the honor "due" to the cowardly apostate Conservatives of 1881--1980 on the grounds of the time and money they wasted trying to destroy the Reformation Text. Our answer to these promptings is simple: Go sit on a tack. The day that any born-again, Bible-believing child of God has to salute Robertson, Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Souter, Tischendorf, Hort, or the Vatican flag will be the day he will have to salute a picture of Lenin. You won't get us to do any of that without torture or imprisonment or both.)

"Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked?" (vs. 18). Well, John Knox told Mary Queen of Scots where to head-in; Luther told Henry VIII where to sell his potatoes; Gen. Andrew Jackson wouldn't have nodded at the King of England if he had bumped into him on the street; Raymond Lully denounced Mohammed as a false prophet right on the streets of downtown Algiers (1250); Elijah and John the Baptist would just as soon tell a king off as a thief. Many a prince has been ["ungodly"]{.class_3762} (vs. 18) whether anyone ever said so or not; many a king like Pharaoh and Nebuchadnezzar has been ["wicked" ]{.class_3762}(vs. 18) whether he got "called on the carpet" or not. But you will notice that the Lord Jesus Christ doesn't waste much time in attacking the princes (Herod and Pilate) or the king (Caesar). His main blast is at the RELIGIOUS CELEBRITIES of His day whose authority rested on their education, tradition, reputation, and political ties. That is, if you have to ferret out all of the CFR, the CIA, the International Bankers, the Illuminati, the Bilderburgers, the Federal Reserve System, and the NEA, don't waste too much time on it. There is bigger and more legitimate game afoot than the Rothschilds, Warburgs, and Rockefellers: These are the college professors and ministerial graduates that these men use to ruin your country and to set up International Socialism.

"[How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 19). Now this is absolutely true: God is no respector of persons---prince or beggar, thief or bishop---["for they are all the work of HIS HANDS"]{.class_3762} (vs. 19). Whatever accusations you make (and Paul made some: Acts 13:10) and whatever vilifications you have to throw (and Jesus threw plenty: Matt. 23:24--33) and whatever negative criticisms you have to give (and Peter gave a carload: 2 Pet. 2--3), avoid, at all costs, [the grumbling and murmuring of 1 Corinthians 10:1--12 ]{.class_3761}which is aimed at the only Infallible Critic and Judge who ever lived. In Job's position this is hard to do. And as we have stated before, only when you have gone through what Job went through would you be able to heap on Him what Elihu heaped, but not before then; mark that, God does NOT commend Elihu for this speech (Job 42:7) even though it was RIGHT.

Verses 20 and 28 have a Tribulation application, and the student of prophecy should pay special attention to the timing of ["midnight"]{.class_3760} as it is found in Ruth 3:8; Matthew 25:6; Acts 16:25; and Judges 16:3. Verse 21 is one of those bombshells like Romans 6:23; John 3:6; Ephesians 2:8; Romans 4:5; Numbers 32:23; Ecclesiastes 8:11; Hebrews 9:27; and Luke 16:16. God sees all (vs. 21), knows all (2 Chron. 16:9), comprehends all (Psa. 139:12); and God will hold a man accountable for every decision he makes (Rom. 2:16; Ecc. 12:14). In the Bible there is no leeway given to Heidegger, Joe Fletcher, Sartre, Einstein, Hefner, Bertrand Russell, Dewey, Hoffman, or any of the "Rubins" (or "Reubens") when it comes to individual accountability to God for individual sins against God. The Lord does not need any fool "United Nations" or "Council on Foreign Relations" telling Him anything about ANYTHING that anyone has done, [is doing]{.class_3759}, or [will do ]{.class_3759}(vs. 13).

Before you can arrest a murderer, God has already judged him (1 John 3:15), sentenced him (John 3:36), and fixed the day of his execution (Rev. 22:15). God made "man" so ["He knoweth their works"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25). Where they "turned back from him" (vs. 27) and refuse to ["consider any of his ways"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27), He strikes them down (vs. 26) and breaks them to pieces (vs. 24). You will notice that all of these descriptions match the Antichrist of Psalm 52 and Daniel chapter 2, and the ["shadow of death"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22) appears again in the Tribulation context. ["The mighty"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20) are ["taken away without hand"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20), for this happens literally (["without hands" ]{.class_3760}Dan. 2:45) to the ten demoniac kings of Revelation chapter 17 and Daniel chapter 7 at the Second Advent (Rev. 19:19). They are "overturned in the night" (vs. 25) because they have caused ["the cry of the poor"]{.class_3760} to come up to God, and God ["heareth the cry of the afflicted"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28) in the Tribulation (see James 5, Matt. 24 and remarks under Job 30:1--8). God will not lay on a man more than what is right (vs. 23), although one might gather that He did if one spent a great deal of time in concentration camps, torture chambers, solitary confinement, "old folk's homes," and cancer wards. At times, it is nearly impossible to resist the temptation either to deny the goodness of God or else deny that God is actively [concerned ]{.class_3759}with the affairs of men. Satan had been trying to get Job to do this, and if you had a dollar for every man or woman who yielded to this Satanic pressure, you could buy a Swiss bank.

Over there, for example, is Don Baker (Pensacola, Florida) confined to a wheelchair for life, and he loved hunting and fishing as well as Bing Crosby or Phil Harris ever loved it. Baker was a saved man who loved God, attended church, witnessed for Christ, and loved his family. In an auto accident he was crippled for life, and his young sons had to go to work to support their own mother. Did God lay more upon Don Baker than that which was RIGHT? But there are scores of "Don Bakers" in America and abroad! Any issue of [Grit ]{.class_3759}or [The Enquirer ]{.class_3759}will document one to five cases like Don's, or worse. In a world filled with cleft palates, harelips, clubbed feet, congenital syphillis, Brite's disease, Hodgkin's disease, mongoloids, water brains, double amputees, starving children, and people damaged for life by torturing, who has not been tempted to say, "There is no God" or "God is dead" or "God kicked it off, but man has to work it out"?

I preached one night to some children in a spastic clinic in Gastonia, N.C. At the time (1950), I had only been saved about a year. Although I was deeply grateful for Calvary and deeply thankful that Christ had saved my soul from a burning Hell (which I deserved), there was much about the providential grace of God that had not yet penetrated my "brow of brass." But on this night some of it began to "get through." I preached thirty minutes to an audience made up of abandoned and spastic children, about forty in number. My "congregation" had feet growing out of their knees, hands growing out of their elbows, faces burned so black by fire that they looked like burnt out coals, double cleft palates, and heads that lolled or flopped grotesquely on their shoulders and chests. When I finished my chalk-talk message, I didn't wait for the evangelistic group I was with to finish dealing with the children and the nurses. I folded up my easel, ran out through the hallways, dumped my stuff in the trunk of a car, and ran across the street (in the dark) to a golf course which lay adjacent to the spastic clinic. I remember falling on my face on one of those greens and trying to form some kind of a prayer, but all I could think of was: "Years I spent in vanity and pride, caring not my Lord was crucified! Knowing not it was for ME He died, on Calvary!" The words of that song (which I had sung many times in the last year) were etched on my heart and mind like a steel engraving. Here I was, twenty-eight years old, still in good health in spite of abusing every faculty of mind, body, and soul that God ever gave a man; still able to read, write, preach, draw, sing, and work after breaking every commandment in the Book and destroying the lives and souls of others while I was doing it; still able to fish, hunt, swim, play football, and travel after doing everything short of cursing God to His face. And while I had gone my merry way, eating, cursing, drinking, sinning, gambling, fighting, and raising Hell, these little children I had just preached to had been growing up in a clinic in the condition I had just seen. Ah, my good Christian brother. That will "exercise your soul!" What had any of those little ones done to deserve their lot in life? The criminal wasn't in the clinic; he was out on the golf green, and the [innocents ]{.class_3759}were in the clinic. Many of those children had even been abandoned by parents who could not face the reality of raising a child in that condition.

"[For he will not lay upon man more than RIGHT"]{.class_3760} (vs. 23)?

Do you see yet what Job sees?

Do you see how Karl Marx and the Fabian Society got into the mess they got into? It would be easy to get into, wouldn't it, if you were a lost man? You see, all International Socialists adopt the view that things are not right, and that God (if He is there) does not know what He is doing or is unable to do it, then "WE THE PEOPLE" (see Acts 21:28 and comments) must fix things up and adjust things so that God (if He is there) won't make so many mistakes and miscarriages in justice. All International Socialists are basically Bible-rejecting "do-gooders" who imagine they have more sense than God, and every one of them would justify his self-righteousness on the grounds that Job did (Job 31); i.e., that he worked to help human beings out so that the "world would be a better place to live in."

But God is not particularly interested in a Bible-rejecting, God-defying, Hell-bound reprobate doing anything with a world that He (God) had made (Gen. 1:1) and owns (Rom. 9:20--21) and intends to rule (Rev. 20). God's plan for "making the world a better place to live in" (Jer. 25:15--31) includes three World Wars in the future and a world-wide famine (Rev. 6:6) with demons turned loose on "the United Nations" (Rev. 6:9, 14). Princes (vs. 19) mean no more to Him than beggars, and where a [beggar ]{.class_3759}will listen (Mark 10:47), he will get something from God that a [prince ]{.class_3759}couldn't find (Mark 10:52) if he studied Greek and Hebrew for thirty-five years.

The world has always had its heroes, and God has always had His; they are never harnessed in the same stable.

A good friend of mine in Blythesville, Arkansas, got saved. Before his conversion he was a card shark, midway barker and con-man. At thirty-seven years of age (after wasting a lifetime in fist fights, knife fights, extortions, and swindles) he found the Lord Jesus Christ. He was so anxious to learn about Christ that he woke a preacher up at two in the morning and demanded that the preacher teach him the Bible. After thirty minutes of explanation, the preacher finally convinced him that he could not learn the Bible between 2 A.M. and 7 A.M. in one morning. Instead, the preacher told him to come back once a week at eight in the morning, and he would try to teach him about 30--40 minutes of the Bible, a little at a time. My friend (whose name is "Paul") promised to do this; the preacher then handed him two colportage books by Dwight L. Moody. "Here," he said. "Take these. Read them; they will help you get started. They were written by Dwight L. Moody."

"Who's [Dwight L. Moody?]{.class_3761}" asked Paul.

"You never heard of Dwight L. Moody?" exclaimed the preacher.

"Naw," said Paul, "who is Dwight L. Moody?"

"Why [everybody ]{.class_3761}knows about Dwight L. Moody!" said the preacher. "He was the greatest evangelist of the nineteenth century. Everybody knows about Dwight L. Moody!"

"Listen," said Paul, "did you ever hear of Titanic Slim?"

"Who?"

"Titanic Slim!"

"Titanic Slim?" exclaimed the preacher. "Who in the world is Titanic Slim?"

"Why anybody that knows [anything ]{.class_3761}knows Titanic Slim," said Paul. "Titanic Slim was he greatest oddsmaker east of the Mississippi. He picked a mark, tapped the guy five to one on a thousand that he could hit a golf ball 500 yards with no hitches. He went out on Lake Michigan in the winter when it was frozen, hit that ball with a northeast wind behind it, and it didn't stop rolling for 650 yards. Everybody knows about!"

God knew about Titanic Slim and Dwight Moody, but there is many a Christian on earth who fancies that his own heroes are the only important people who ever lived. In response to a pile of correspondence between John R. Rice and Herbert Evans (on the subject of the authority of the [AV ]{.class_3759}Bible), a famous pastor in America said to Mr. Evans: "Please don't send me any more of this \[the documented evidence of the [AV ]{.class_3759}Bible\]. John R. Rice has been a very dear friend of mine for many years." You see, that pastor was a RELIGIOUS LIBERAL even though he believed in the "Fundamentals." When it came to a showdown between his friends and the truth of God's word, he would not tolerate factual evidence from the Bible or anywhere else: He was a religious Liberal and didn't know it.

Did you know that some of us old Bull Mooses, who came in out of the howling wilderness, knew no more about Bob Jones, Lee Roberson, Dallas Billington, Dwight Moody, Billy Sunday, and Frank Norris than some of you slicked up girlies knew about Homer Van Meter, Gene Tunney, Art Wermuth, Artie Shaw, Legs Diamond, and Peter Lorre? And when many of us got saved, we were already without illusions about "dear friends" and "godly men" and "dedicated, separated Christians." We had experienced a first hand account of dealings with human nature through a period of years, and although the maxims and epigrams we learned in those years may not have been "Christian principles" (see any hogwash published by BJU while trying to alibi their lack of belief in the Bible) or "Christian philosophy," honey, let me tell you something: We could flat\
locate BALONEY and spot a gaffed act before you could tie your shoelaces. And many of us old dogs who were dragged in out of the back alley by the scruff of our necks, with scars all over us, have never been convinced ONCE that any Christian "hero" or "friend" is worth trusting where he (or she) corrects the word of God. We know who to trust (Psa. 118:8)---God! And we know WHAT to trust (John 17:17)---God's words. And where it comes to Christian celebrities who sit in judgment on the Bible, we harness them up with Goering, Pope Paul, Charles Manson, Pope John, Huey Long, Bloody Mary, Ted Kennedy, and A. T. Robertson. Square count, neighbor. It's tap city for the whole crew.

Perhaps our manners are uncouth, and our language is deplorable, and our attire could not pass at a rummage sale; but none of you will fool us with any rubbish about AUTHORITY. We know who runs things, and we know where the final authority is: It is certainly not in any set of Hebrew or Greek grammars used by any faculty member of Bethel St. Paul, Heston College, Grace Bible Institute, Pacific Bible College, Taylor University, San Francisco Seminary, Malone College, Oakland City, Nyack, Messiah, Manchester, Eastern, Dordt, Concordia, Augustana, Pillsbury, Dallas, Toccoa Falls, Beulah Heights, Theiel College, Westmont, Tabor, Simpson, Mid-South, Pikeville, Mercer, Howard, Judson, Stetson, Bob Jones, Columbia, or the next 3,000 you might drum up from somewhere else. You pull that flim-flam off on "marks" who never had to deal with Titanic Slim for a "touch." A gaffed act is a gaffed act no matter who is handling the props.

34:29 When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:

30 That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.

31 Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more:

32 That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.

33 Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.

Verse 29 is one of those great verses in the Bible which occasionally pops up to show us a doctrinal truth with so many applications that a volume of sermons would not cover it "in depth." Nobody can make trouble where God gives quietness (vs. 29), and when ["He hideth his face"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29), no one can behold Him (Song of Sol. 5:6), whether it be the nation of Israel (Deut. 31:18; Matt. 13:44) or an individual (Psa. 104:29). [Foxes' Book of Martyrs]{.class_3759} well illustrates the first half of verse 29. Paul is a beautiful example of the ["peace of God, which passeth all understanding"]{.class_3760} (Phil. 4:7) abiding, even in the presence of hatred (Acts 20:24), persecution (Acts 23:11), whippings, stonings (2 Cor. 11:25), calamities (2 Cor. 11:26), and death (2 Tim. 4:18). Where God gives peace, the world cannot take it (John 16:33), and where God will not give peace (Matt. 24:6), the world cannot obtain it (1 Thess. 5:3). To look for ["on earth, peace"]{.class_3760} (Luke 2:14) without giving ["glory to God in the highest"]{.class_3760} (Luke 2:14) is like looking for a bumper harvest without plowing or planting.

Here is a Huguenot martyr (1600); a Roman Catholic king says to him: "We will take your property." He replies that he has treasure in heaven. "We will throw you in prison." He replies that he is seated in heavenly places in Christ Jesus. "We will run you out of the country!" He replies that his real home is in heaven. "All right then, we will cut off your head!" He answers that he will be absent from the body and present with the Lord. "We will put you to the torture then, and not let you die!" He replies: ["I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_3760} Well, what can you do with a fanatic like that? ["When he giveth quietness, WHO then can make trouble?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

Here is the English martyr kissing the stake where he is to be burned. He says: "Welcome thou cross of Christ, welcome everlasting life!" Here comes another one, clapping his hands and yelling, "Blessed be God the day that I was born for this preferment!" Here is another one (a woman this time); she is burning in the flames lit by the Vatican priests, and as she burns she says: "Behold you papists. You're always looking for [miracles]{.class_3759}. Look at me! I feel no pain; this fire to me is as [sweet as a bed of roses]{.class_3759}." Here is yet another one, roasting slowly on a griddle in front of his family and friends; the papists have been "barbecuing" him for over an hour; he looks up at the "cooks" and says (with a smile): "You can turn me over now; I'm done on this side!" ["When He giveth quietness, who then can make trouble?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

There goes a Christian missionary in China out to dig his own grave, with the Communist troops behind him to bury him when he falls into it. As he shovels he is smiling and saying, "This is the will of God. This is the place I was born to be buried." There is another Christian lying in the dungeon, and the priest's flunkies bring in the bloody head of his first born son (age 10) and dangle it by the hair before his eyes. He says to his Roman Catholic murderers: "It is the will of God; let him do what seemeth Him good." Here is Zwingli lying on the ground (1531) mortally wounded; a Roman Catholic soldier kneels by him and places the point of a dagger on his cheek and says: "Pray to Mary!" Unable to speak, Zwingli simply shakes his head; the knife goes into his eyes. Here is Harlan Popov, standing at "attention" before the NKVD goon squad after thirteen months of starvation rations and constant torture. "Go on," says Popov, "SHOOT!" ["When He giveth quietness, who then can make TROUBLE?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

Here is a Scotch Covenanter leading his men to battle and certain death against the majority "Scottish REPUBLICAN army" (sixteenth century) and saying "All right, Gentlemen, I would rather die as a Christian than live as a Catholic. Whoever goes first goes cleanest. Follow me!" Here is Ian Paisley preaching the truth of God, with the British Broadcasting System against him, the Irish Catholic (Republican) Army against him, the Associated Press against him, [Life ]{.class_3759}and [Time ]{.class_3759}against him, the National Council of "Christian" Churches against him, but---on he goes. ["When He giveth quietness, who then can make trouble?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

But what in the name of God is THIS?!

Here are twentieth-century American Christians selling out their Reformation heritage quicker than they can find it, without anyone imprisoning or torturing them. Here are the most luxury-ridden people on earth selling God out, and so troubled with financial, social, moral, and religious problems that they have to have one new "bible" every five years to calm their ulcers. With God refusing to show His face (vs. 29) or reveal any truth through the sickening, revolting, nauseating [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) and [NASV ]{.class_3759}(1963), etc., the American Christian has no quietness: Anything can trouble him. Some Christians are more upset about the words ["let"]{.class_3760} and ["whit"]{.class_3760} and ["wot"]{.class_3760} in the Bible than they are about the Roman Catholic Bibles recommended by Bob Jones University.

And where does the real Bible believer stand in all of this mess? On one hand is the Illuminati, Rockefeller, the International Bankers, and the Bilderburgers; on the other hand is the Roman Catholic Church, the Jesuit Priests, the Charismatic movement, and Catholic Action; behind him is the Communist Party with Gus Hall, Walter Reuther, Alger Hiss, Whitaker Chambers, and the National Education Association; and before him is the NAACP, the Black Muslims, a famine, a race war, a depression, and flying saucers. Isn't that enough of a "cloud bank" (Ecc. 11:4) to prevent the Bible believer from sowing the word (Ecc. 11:6)? Not at all. ["When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29).

Nobody.

"[Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 26:3). Let the humanoids show up, let the bombs drop, let the Socialists stuff the mental wards and prisons with sane people, let all Hell break loose (which it will---Rev. 9:11); our God is just beginning to warm up.

Why does God "hide his face" (vs. 29) from the nation of Israel (Deut. 32:20)? So they will repent (vs. 31) and stop their iniquity (vs. 32). If God continually blessed the ["nation"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29) with the prosperity of His countenance (see Psa. 24:6, 31:16, and 27:8) while they were rejecting His prophets and His words (2 Chron. 20:20), ["the hypocrite"]{.class_3760} would reign (vs. 30) and the people (Isa. 30:28) would be ensnared (see Prov. 28:15 and 29:12). Practically, Elihu intends this as a rebuke to Job, the idea being that God has hid his face from Job so that Job will repent and "get right." But verse 30 is too plainly a reference to the ruler of Revelation 13:1--6 and Daniel 7:8; so naturally, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Heavenor, Kline, Dummelow, Clarke, Lange, Bullinger, and Williams completely miss the application.

"[Surely it is meet to be said unto God\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 31). (See Gen. 2:18 and comments on the "help meet.") ["I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more"]{.class_3762} (vs. 31). (See Psa. 119:18 and 73:14.) The penitent sinner who truly turns back to God should say: ["That which I see not teach thou me"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32) exactly as David said it in Psalms 27:11 and 119:33, and like Isaiah said it in Isaiah 2:3, and like Solomon said it in Proverbs 4:11 and 9:9. ["If I have done iniquity, I will do no more"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32), where the ["if"]{.class_3762} is a fulfilled condition: He HAS done iniquity. A perfect example is the great homecoming of the prodigal son in Luke 15 where he confesses through his sobs, ["I HAVE SINNED AGAINST HEAVEN!"]{.class_3762} The prodigal returned because repentance (in essence) is returning to God, while sin (in essence) is departing from God.

Then Elihu puts a block of vicious realism on top of his discourse as a capstone to a pyramid of accusations: ["Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose"]{.class_3760} (vs. 33). Now this is the kind of thing that the unregenerated nature of man revolts against: [Authority]{.class_3759}. The statement would arouse fury in the heart of nearly any man other than Job, but Job has been beaten to the ground; he has been whipped until he can take the rebuke with his mouth shut. A normal man in good health (especially an educated man!) simply cannot stand to be spoken to in this fashion (see 2 Tim. 4:3). You see the fiber of Elihu's capstone is this:

"You can choose to do right or wrong, and it won't make any difference. You can get right or drop dead, and it won't affect anything; and [you'll]{.class_3761} still be the one to pay the piper whether you want to or not. Disregard what I say or heed it, whatever you like. I don't care [what ]{.class_3761}you do with it, because [your opinion ]{.class_3761}doesn't matter one way or another."

Human nature will forgive almost any sin but [calculated indifference ]{.class_3759}or [deliberate neglect]{.class_3759}. When Congreve said, "Hell hath no fury like a woman scorned," he was summing up the results of indifference and neglect; and if you want to see the Liberal or the Modernist get upset---I mean those educated gentlemen who are always bragging about their "broad-mindedness" and "tolerance"---just treat their education and scholarship like a [broken commode ]{.class_3759}and watch how "tolerant" they get. As we have already pointed out and proved (see Prov. 27:4 and comments), ENVY is behind nearly every sin in the catalogue of sins. Although the ["love of money"]{.class_3760} (1 Tim. 6:10) is certainly the ROOT of all sin in the world system, ENVY and PRIDE lie at the base of individual transgressions against God and man, for envy and pride unseated the [fifth cherub ]{.class_3759}(Ezek. 28:17; Isa. 14:13) before Eve was tempted to be "as one of the gods" (Gen. 3:5).

When Marie Antoinette replies, "Then let them eat cake," after being told that the poor people of France have no bread to eat, she accomplishes the same results that a man would accomplish who says, "The Bible will come through on schedule whether you believe it or not," to a man who has just said, "You think you're right and everyone else is wrong." Human nature (in the saved man or the lost man) cannot tolerate any absolute, dogmatic authority [which overrules his own "feelings" or personal likes and dislikes; ]{.class_3759}God's Authority does just that, and if some of your "dear friends" don't like it (see above), they can go take a flying jump at their left leg. In matters of Biblical authority, for example, you can choose or refuse (vs. 33) the [AV ]{.class_3759}text of the Reformation (in the universal language of today, preserved by the church who kept the ["word"]{.class_3760}---Rev. 3:8), and it won't have anymore bearing on the authority and infallibility of that text than if you never made a choice to start with. If your "choice" happens to line up with the "choice" ("preference," I believe, is the contemporary clap-trap) of 4,000 "authorities," it would no more affect the authority of the [AV ]{.class_3759}text than if you hadn't decided to choose---[one way or another]{.class_3759}. That Book will override your teachers, all of your versions, all of your education, all of your library, and eventually the entire world system. If you don't "prefer" it or don't "believe" it, I couldn't care less: It's your [funeral]{.class_3759}, not mine; enjoy yourself.

Along these lines, the believer will notice that the Bible faculties of the Conservative schools will have a sudden "reviving" of Biblical knowledge between 1974--1980, due to their being forced (against their wills) to profess faith in the AV text. Students at these schools will not be surprised to find scores of things found in the [Bible Believer's Commentary Series ]{.class_3759}being taught as [fact ]{.class_3759}by the professors of these schools, while the same men are still kidding the students into thinking that these new revelations and truths were obtained by mastering the Greek and Hebrew. Scholarly hypocrites in church history are very consistent in these matters; wherever advanced revelation and truth are revealed by the Holy Spirit (and these truths prove to be [absolute truth ]{.class_3759}with the passage of time), they are espoused by the Scholars Union while the Union (at the same time) keeps propagating the lie that [EDUCATION and SCHOLARSHIP were responsible for their discoveries]{.class_3759}. Thus, you will find scores of faculty members who teach the truths revealed to Chrysostom, Ignatius, Polycarp, Tanchelm, Burgon, Berthold, Luther, Knox, Wesley, Whitefield, Sunday, Moody, Larkin, Pember, Collet, Cronin, and Darby; taking credit for obtaining these truths by telling the student that "a knowledge of the originals" revealed these truths. Thus, credit for real BIBLICAL scholarship is transferred from men [who believed and preached ]{.class_3759}the Greek, Latin, German, and English Receptus to men who [hated and attacked ]{.class_3759}the same text (Aland, Metzger, Schaff, Robertson, Nestle, et al.). Where real believers occasioally altered the text (as Torrey, Spurgeon, and others did several times in their writings), they revealed NOTHING that was not known [before ]{.class_3759}they changed the text. A good rule to go by is this: Correct all teachers, translators, revisors, preachers, principals, founders, students, professors, chancellors, writers, and linguists by the [King James ]{.class_3759}text. Where they [agree ]{.class_3759}with any particular verse, they have a limited amount of knowledge which ANYONE could obtain; where they disagree with any verse, they are foundering in the mire of their own infidelity. Learned stupidity is quite characteristic of the Laodicean church.

"[Therefore speak what thou knowest"]{.class_3760} (vs. 33). In the final analysis Job must answer, not Elihu (see the same verse: ["and not I"]{.class_3762}). Job cannot answer for the way that God governs the world (vss. 17--19); he must only answer why he has charged God with unrighteousness. Elihu is probably speaking in irony when he says, ["Speak what thou knowest"]{.class_3762} (vs. 33); for he has just implied that no matter what Job says (vss. 31--33), God will bring the ship into port on schedule, and Job will have to unload it at the dock.

34:34 Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.

35 Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were without wisdom.

36 My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.

37 For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.

Since Job does NOT speak (vs. 33), Elihu turns to his buddies and appeals to them (vs. 34). He says that Job has ["spoken without knowledge"]{.class_3760} (vs. 35), and this is exactly what the Lord says to Job in Job 38:2. Job's trial ["unto the end"]{.class_3760} (vs. 36) is so clearly a cross-reference to Matthew 24:13, Hebrews 3:14, and Daniel 12:9 that every Premillennial expositor from Tertullian to Pentecost missed it completely. The nation of Israel is ["tried unto the end"]{.class_3760} (vs. 36), but it is the end of Daniel's Seventieth Week (Dan. 9:26) and all the attempts by Custer and Panosian (BJU) and Spiros Zodhiates to alter the texts of Hebrews 3:14 and 6:4 to preserve [Baptist ]{.class_3759}doctrine, [for this age]{.class_3759}, is wasted paper, wasted ink, wasted time, and a God-dishonoring expenditure of intellectual stupidity. If you have ever wondered why the hobbyhorse riders of the "original Greek" spend so much time in Hebrews-Jude, that is why. Unable to understand the English text, unable to believe it, daring not to expound it, fearing the wrath of the "majority of professional jackasses," they simply cover the Tribulation truths in the passage by [aborting the texts.]{.class_3759}

Verse 37 is self-explanatory, and the clapping of the hands is illustrated in Ezekiel 21:17 and Job 27:23. Job's words have been "multiplied" against God (vs. 37), although God only knows what would have happened if the four "comforters" had not showed up to "help" Job "endure to the end." Job got by for a week (2:13) or more (3:1) without saying anything against God, and that is probably six and one half days better than Elihu could have done with half of Job's afflictions.

For the "benefit of clergy" we shall very briefly list the critical and exegetical blunders of the Lockman Foundation, the translating committees of the newer translations, and the major commentators.

1\. Dummelow has altered verses 4, 19, 23, 25, 30, 33, 34, and 36 to match the translation of 1881--1884 ([RV]{.class_3759}).

2\. The [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3759}---professing to be the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text (ho ho)---has altered verses 3, 4, 5, 12, 31, and 36 to match the grossly corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901 (especially in verses 2 and 12) while still professing to be the [King James ]{.class_3759}text. (You can get away with a lot on the "Bill Rice Ranch" that you couldn't get away with in court. The [NSRB ]{.class_3759}is no more the [AV ]{.class_3759}than Archie Bunker's tomcat.)

3\. The [New English Bible ]{.class_3759}(same text as the [RSV ]{.class_3759}and [ASV ]{.class_3759}in the Greek) substitutes "palate" for ["mouth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3) to make the translation "clearer." There is no ["wound"]{.class_3760} in verse 6; there is no ["scorning"]{.class_3760} in verse 7, and no one should ["delight"]{.class_3760} themselves with God in verse 9. Further, the words ["commit iniquity"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) have been toned down; the words ["most just"]{.class_3760} (vs. 17)---a reference to God---have been "clarified" by "disparage a sovereign whose rule is so fair." The other changes (forty in twenty verses) are too numerous to list.

4\. The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---three Novenas and four Hail Marys---[Bible ]{.class_3759}completely destroys the sense of verses 2, 18, 22, 24, 29, and 33. Then, in addition, the paraphrasing of verses 6, 9, 30, 31, 34, and 35 have been so loose that attaching the word "Bible" to Taylor's private interpretation is about as far as you can go without calling [Peyton Place ]{.class_3759}an "epic poem."

5\. The absurd [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901) has altered verses 3, 5--7, 14, and 18. Verse 14 has God setting His heart upon Himself instead of [man]{.class_3761}, and the cross-reference to the Tribulation, found in verse 22, has been neatly removed. The acid realism of verse 33 had been turned into a bowl of mush, and verse 31 has been turned into a [question ]{.class_3761}instead of sound advice. Typical "reliable" translating. (The [ASV ]{.class_3761}\[1901\] has also followed the [Roman Catholic Vulgate ]{.class_3761}in verse 18.)

6\. Adam Clarke cannot get the sense of verse 30; therefore he alters it to match the [Vulgate ]{.class_3759}(which didn't get it either) and then refuses to comment on it or apply it. Verse 33 is a "very obscure original" to Clarke; so he recommends a paraphrase by Mr. Good. Mr. Good promptly erases the Tribulation reference in verse 36 (["unto the end"]{.class_3760}) and causes Clarke to consider substituting: "My father, let Job be tried" which is the [Vulgate ]{.class_3759}reading for verse 36.

7\. Duhm doesn't like verse 4, Delitzsch and Ewald don't like verse 6, Duhm doesn't like verse 10 and 13, Schlottmann and Hitzig don't like verse 13, Peake doesn't like verse 14, Ewald and Dillman don't like verse 18, Duhm and Budde don't like verses 20, 23, 25--26, 30, 33, and 36. Theodotian, Julius, Ley, and Wright don't like some other verses; neither does Pharaoh, [Balaam]{.class_3759}, Pope Paul, Marx, Lenin, [Satan]{.class_3759}, Voltaire, Bullinger, Tom Paine, and Calvin. So what?

8\. Origen's corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(written one hundred years after the completion of the New Testament) has altered verses 9, 16, 19, 20, 22, 25, 26, 31, 33, and 37 so that they no longer resemble even a paraphrase of the truth, and words have been added to verses 11, 19, and 20 in spite of the warning found in Proverbs 30:6.

9\. The [Amplified "Bible" ]{.class_3759}follows the false lead of the [ASV ]{.class_3759}in verse 31 and the false lead of the [Vulgate ]{.class_3759}in verse 18.

10\. Bullinger (in his marginal notes) suggests ten changes.

11\. The [RSV ]{.class_3759}and [NRSV ]{.class_3759}(with [Good News]{.class_3759}) make about twenty changes in the text, most of them in line with the apostate Bibles recommended by Bob Jones III and John R. Rice (the old [ASV ]{.class_3759}and the [NASV]{.class_3759}).

The best thing to do with the writings listed above is blow your nose on them when you run out of Kleenexes.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw2959.xhtml}

CHAPTER 35

35:1 Elihu spake moreover, and said,

2 Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God's?

3 For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? and, What profit shall I have, if I be cleansed from my sin?

4 I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.

5 Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds which are higher than thou.

6 If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or if thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?

7 If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?

8 Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.

9 By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.

10 But none saith, Where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;

11 Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?

12 There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.

Elihu continues to lay the heaviest load on Job that anyone so far has dumped, even though his intentions are good and most of his charges are true; but the truth always cuts harder than the lie, and the truth that Elihu has hold of---which is quite easy to lay hold of when things are going well---is that no man really has a right to complain about God's treatment of him no matter what God does.

"[Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is MORE than God's."]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). Now, there is another atomic explosion; Job had not actually said it, for his own confession of his unrighteousness (9:29--31) is apparent. However, there is something inside Job that is insisting this, for God Himself backs up Elihu's speech in Job 40:8. What Job [had ]{.class_3761}said is found in the next verse (vs. 3), and this was found back in 21:15 and 22:2. By saying that the wicked do NOT suffer for their sins and then by saying that he himself (Job) was suffering with "no profit" after obeying God (vs. 3), Job had practically said, "I AM RIGHT AND GOD IS WRONG."

Now, this is what every American in this century is saying if he accepts his church, sacraments, ideology, philosophy, religion, tradition, good works, or moral standards (or "beliefs") as a basis of justification before God. You see, Romans 10:1--4 makes it quite clear that the morally righteous man, who is sincerely and zealously seeking to "be good," is actually a man who is engaged in making a LIAR out of God by pitting the frail, puny, fallible "goodness" of his own human nature against the goodness of a holy, omnipotent Lord. If Christ is [God's righteousness ]{.class_3759}(Rom. 10:1--4), what is YOUR righteousness? Answer: [filthy rags ]{.class_3759}(Isa. 64:6). You couldn't be as righteous as God (let alone MORE righteous) if you could charge to your credit every good deed committed by St. Francis, Florence Nightingale, Churchill, Kagawa, Peale, Gandhi, Mann, Madam Curie, Father Damien, and Livingstone in their lifetimes. (See [Tell It Like It Is]{.class_3759}, 1969.) Although Job, as any modern Campbellite elder or Catholic priest, has been very careful not to say: "God [you're]{.class_3759} wrong and [I'm]{.class_3759} right," he has clearly done three things:

1\. Stated that God has no real cause for punishing him.

2\. Stated how good he was (Job 31) to show how he deserved better treatment.

3\. Stated that God blessed and prospered wicked people while persecuting good people: i.e., HIM!

Was God wrong in doing what He did to Job?

Now there is that question again! And when our time comes ("our," not "your"), will we sit as cooly as Elihu does and judge it as objectively as Bildad did, or will we take Satan's position (Job 1:9, 10) against our Redeemer? Oh, THAT is a question.

Elihu's answer to Job (vs. 4) and to his three friends (vs. 4) is:

1\. God is higher than man [positionally]{.class_3759}, as well as [morally ]{.class_3759}(vs. 5: Compare Isa. 55:8).

2\. God is immune from actual injury whether a man lives right or not (vs. 6: see 34:33).

3\. No man could give God anything that would profit Him, for God is the author of all life and all gifts (vs. 7: See Rom. 11:36).

4\. Although sin is against God (Psa. 51:4), it does the damage to the man who commits it, not God (vs. 8).

5\. Men often cry out under oppression (vs. 9) like Judas cried out (Matt. 27:4) and Pharaoh cried out (Exod. 9:27), but "crying out" because of material and economic trouble is not true worship. True worship consists of seeking after God and loving Him [no matter what He does to us ]{.class_3759}(John 4:24), and by such a standard, the ["true worshippers"]{.class_3760} (John 4:23) in any age are probably reduced to a pitiful two percent of the Body of Christ. ["Songs in the night"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) are more often sung in jail (Acts 16:25) than in Bellingrath Gardens (Mobile, Alabama).

It is [God ]{.class_3759}who gave man the wisdom that is denied to the ["beasts of the earth, and\...the fowls of heaven"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11), not "mother nature" or "evolution." Since all progressive education is aimed at an [animal culture]{.class_3759}, God is eliminated in the federal school system as the Author of man's wisdom. In the speeches by Bertrand Russell ([The Ostrich Code of Moral's Forum]{.class_3759}, July, 1928) and the works of Lester Ward ([Dynamic Sociology]{.class_3759}) and the theories of Dr. Franz Boas (Columbia University) and Professor Leonard (University of Wisconsin), one will find the typical position of the National Education Association and the Health and Rehabilitation Services departments, backed by the International Communist party. To put across this great jungle society of animal culture, the Junior High School and High School students are subjected to a ceaseless barrage of "leading authorities say," "the majority of scientists believe," and "all the major scholars believe," etc. \[Sounds almost like the faculty of Bob Jones University and Dallas, doesn't it?\] However, in Biology, [James D. Dana, Lord Kelvi]{.class_3759}, and [Agasszi ]{.class_3759}did not "agree"; [Bateson ]{.class_3759}(Cambridge) did not "agree"; [Thomson ]{.class_3759}(University of Aberdeen) did not agree; [Virchow ]{.class_3759}(Berlin) did not agree, nor did [A. R. Wallace,]{.class_3759} [Tinkler, Chance, Murray, Kellog, Pfaff, Gerg,]{.class_3759} or [Goldschmidt]{.class_3759}: None of these men agreed with Darwin's animal culture. In Geology, [Sedgwick ]{.class_3759}(Cambridge), [Dawson ]{.class_3759}(McGill University), [McReady, Price]{.class_3759}, and [G. F. Wright]{.class_3759} (Oberlin) did not "agree"; and in Physics and Astronomy Kelvin, Kepler, Sooner, P. J. Wiseman, Paton, Velikovsky, Smith, J. H. Jeans, Byron Nelson, Morris, and Lt. Col. Davies did not agree with Darwin.

But International Socialists do agree with Darwin. \[It's kinda like the [AV ]{.class_3759}controversy, ain't it? All the scholars who want to "play god" agree with Westcott and Hort---temporarily called "Aland and Metzger"---but Burgon, Miller, Hills, Fuller, Ruckman, Scrivener, Ray, and Phillpot don't "agree" with Westcott and Hort. But all "good, godly, recognized, dedicated scholars" do [agree ]{.class_3759}with Westcott and Hort.)

"[Evil men"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) will prevent the ["oppressed"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9) from getting a hearing. And this is especially true in the Tribulation when ["the mighty"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9) will be in control (Job 20:5--25). The great spiritual truth of the passage is apparent: "Worship" must come to a great deal more than worship services, proper living (Job 31), and proper dealing with our "fellow man." Violating the First Commandment (Matt. 22:38), while engaged in socialist humanitarian enterprises (to fulfill the Second Commandment), will land a "do-gooder" nowhere but in a Lake of Fire. Job lived as good a moral, social, political, and economic life as any Christian leader in America has lived in the last fifty years, but that didn't solve the problem of the First Commandment.

I had the privilege, once, of leading a seventy-six year old man to Christ. He lived in New Buffalo, Michigan. His decision was preceded by a solid hour of unconvincing Scriptural arguments for needing the new birth and God's righteousness. At the end of this time, I was about worn out with things. Finally, I said, "Pop, you told me that you are seventy-six years old; now tell me something. In that lifetime---seventy-six years--- can you name me ONE thing you have done for the Lord Jesus Christ?"

"Why, of course, preacher, I told you. I attended church regularly all my life."

"But I thought you said you did that because you thought a man ought to do it. You didn't say you did it just for the Lord Jesus Christ. Tell me, what have you ever done in seventy-six years just because you [loved Him ]{.class_3761}and for no other reason?"

"Well, I told you, Reverend, I take the sacraments. I haven't missed more than ten Sundays in\...."

"Wait a minute Pop. You told me that you took the sacraments because you were [taught ]{.class_3761}to take them; that was the way you were raised. Think now for a minute. In seventy-six years (that's a long time, dad) what have you done just for God and no one else?"

And to make a long story short (as we have already done ten times), after three more of those questions that old soul was crying. Finally, he said, "Well, preacher, I guess the truth is\...I guess the truth is I ain't ever done nothin' [just for Jesus Christ.]{.class_3759}" I said, "Okay, Pop, why don't you do what you know is right, right now? Let's kneel here on your living room rug, and you give your heart to Jesus and let Him save you and cleanse you, and then you can do something for Him."

And he did, and seventy-six years of wasted life were over.

I wonder when we kindle the bonfire (1 Cor. 3:15) and "cash in our chips" (2 Cor. 5:10) how much of anything will go through? I wonder if God removed from our "ministries" everything that was done to establish records, everything that was done to get publicity, everything that was done to make a show, everything that was said to get money from people, everything that was arranged to influence rich Christians, everything that was written to save face, and everything that was preached to get "positive results," how much would survive a fire that tries the QUALITY (1 Cor. 3:13) of every man's work?

"[But none saith, Where is God my maker\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 10).

Although this is an overstatement, it is not grossly overstated. Under extreme torture and persecution some turn to God, but even in the middle of the hell of artillery shelling or saturation bombing, many sinners cry only, "Where are my children?" or "Where is my house?" or "Where is the water?" The rich man in Hell (Luke 16:24) didn't say "Give me God!" He said, "Give me WATER!" The chances of your cursing and blaspheming when you die in the desert from an intestinal wound are just as good as the chances that you will seek God on an operating table in an airconditioned hospital. Where is the money? Where is the good fishing? Where will Miami play next year? Where is a good pickup? Where can I get a fix? Where can I make a living? Where are the best jobs? Where can I retire? Any question but the ultimate one: ["WHERE IS GOD MY MAKER?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10).

God alone can give a sinner a song in the nighttime as well as the daytime (Exod. 15 and comments), for the "nighttime" of this passage is the nighttime of pain, sorrow, suffering, bereavement, persecution, loneliness, and misery. God can fill the darkest midnight with music (Psa. 119:54). The song of Moses (Deut. 32) will be sung at midnight in the darkest part of the world's night (Rev. 14:1--3); a night so black that it will make the Dark Ages look like an evangelistic Crusade.

35:13 Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.

14 Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet judgment is before him; therefore trust thou in him.

15 But now, because it is not so, he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth it not in great extremity:

16 Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.

Nothing is hard about the passage except verse 15, which says in effect: "Because the Great White Throne Judgment has not yet taken place, Job has only had a taste of God's anger, not the great extremity that will take place when sinners SEE God" (vs. 14: ["thou sayest thou shalt not see him."]{.class_3760}). The verse has been butchered beyond recognition by the Scholars Union, which began (in their usual negative fashion) by insisting the English text (["great extremity"]{.class_3760}) was wrong. Having done this, the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) has to deny that God [did ]{.class_3759}visit in His anger (vs. 15) and has to tell the chump (who was stupid enough to buy the book on the recommendation of some "Bible" teacher) that God had "NOT visited in anger." The [Living Bible ]{.class_3759}also picks up this refrain with the [Amplified Bible, RV ]{.class_3759}(1884), [NEB]{.class_3759}, and every apostate revision by every Liberal since 1880. One must never forget that the [ASV ]{.class_3759}of 1901, still recommended by [NASV ]{.class_3759}fanatics, is a depraved, liberal product made to compromise with the tenants of Higher Criticism and the Roman Catholic Church. (If Torrey, Machen, Rice, Weniger, Warfield, Malone, and Robertson were stupid enough to recommend it, that was their problem, not yours. And you have no business thumbing your nose at the Bible simply because some "dear friends" or "godly Conservatives" messed up the truth, occasionally.)

"[Judgment is before him"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14) is a reference to a future judgment (Prov. 20:8) which is perfectly apparent by the statement: ["Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet\..."]{.class_3762} (vs. 14). However, Job had stated that he would see God (Job 19:27 and comments); the only trouble was Job was trusting in his own righteousness (Job 31) to save him in that day, instead of God's righteousness. Elihu says, "You had better trust HIM" (vs. 14). Observe that Elihu speaks of Job in the [third person ]{.class_3761}(vs. 16) to match the ["he"]{.class_3762} of ["yet HE knoweth it not in great extremity."]{.class_3762} The error of the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(and other apostate corruptions) in making the "he" of the last clause (vs. 15) refer to God is quite manifest. The [ASV ]{.class_3761}had to change the Hebrew ("yada" in any text) for ["knoweth"]{.class_3762} to "regard," to produce their erroneous "funnyism."

Since the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901) was promoted, recommended, and pushed by men who were basically man-pleasers and man-fearers in heart, we should not be surprised to find that they have translated the same word (["yada"]{.class_3759}) as "KNOW" in the other 400 times it occurs. The [NASV ]{.class_3759}and the [New, NEW ASV ]{.class_3759}all carry on with the same type of "reliable translating." Consistent translating (or "updating the archaic words") was never a real issue with Christian educators to start with. That was merely the hypocritical alibi they used for altering the Bible (see any issue of [The Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_3759}, BJU press).

Elihu's charge is clear; he is accusing Job of not believing he will face God in judgment, or he would be trusting God's righteousness instead of his own. He is further stating that even though the judgment has not taken place (vs. 15), God has already shown Job (by His anger) that He is displeased with Job's "righteousness" and will not accept it. Job has only gotten a foretaste of the wrath to come (Rom. 2:5) and does not yet know how "extreme" it can get. (Now that is the sense of the "original English," as common sense can "sense" the sense of it, and the reading of the [NASV ]{.class_3759}\[any edition\] is no more "reliable" than a rotten plank. If some "dear friend" or "godly Fundamentalist" recommend you walk on this rotten plank, I would suggest that you carry a survival kit, a first aid kit, a life preserver, and an inflatable raft with you.)

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw3013.xhtml}

CHAPTER 36

36:1 Elihu also proceeded, and said,

2 Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet to speak on God's behalf.

3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.

4 For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.

5 Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.

6 He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.

7 He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.

Elihu now launches into a great discourse which demonstrates the difference in the way that God treats His own children (vss. 6--12) and the unregenerate (vss. 13 and 14). Although there are no [regenerated sinners in the Old Testament ]{.class_3759}who can be properly called "the sons of God" (as the angels are in Job 1, 2, and 38), the [spiritual lessons ]{.class_3759}are still very appropriate. Elihu states that he will ["speak on God's behalf" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 2) as God's ambassador (2 Cor. 5:20), and he will ["fetch"]{.class_3760} (Southern term) knowledge from afar and write down righteousness for his Maker (vs. 3). The One who is mighty and perfect (vss. 4 and 5) is with him in this, so Elihu claims that his words will not be false, and from here on, they are not. Elihu gets so taken up with giving glory to God (Job 37) that he nearly forgets Job. With only one more reference to his intended audience (27:14), Elihu "raves" about God after Job 36:21.

Observe again, how the ["poor"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6) are equated with the ["righteous"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7), and recall our comments under Job 30, especially the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition, same garbage) boo-boo on Proverbs 14:9. In the Tribulation, no righteous man is RICH, and no rich man is righteous. The International Socialists, the HEW, UNESCO, the International Bankers, Swiss banks, Bilderburgers, UN, the Communist Party, and the Roman Church State (same bunch; different names) will take care of this so that no man can buy or sell unless he has "the number." The paving that leads to this "interstate" has already been laid, and it consists in the gradual loss of freedoms in the "free world." Among many freedoms lost in America already are the freedom to buy and sell, trade and transport, educate, improve structures, and build or redecorate. Personal income taxes are a violation of the Constitution, forced busing is a violation of the Bill of Rights, the FCC "equal time" law is a violation of the rights of free speech, demanding building and wiring permits are a violation of the constitution, and Federal laws favorable to muggers, perverts, hustlers, wire tappers, house breakers, rapists, and trespassers are all unconstitutional: So what?

So, the Constitution will be revised between 1979 and 1984 to match the demands of the [Communist Manifesto]{.class_3759}, with a qualifying clause that Roman Catholicism will be allowed as a "religion."

"[The righteous"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7) is a doctrinal reference to Tribulation saints whose righteousness (Rev. 12:17, 14:12, and 22:14) entitles them to the title. However, a spiritual application can be made to those saved by grace through faith, who have Christ's righteousness; for they shall be ["on the throne"]{.class_3762} (vs. 7) in the Millennium (Rev. 20:1--4), and they will be established forever (Eph. 1:10). What follows is a spiritual description of God's dealings with ["the righteous."]{.class_3762}

36:8 And if they be bound in fetters, and be holden in cords of affliction;

9 Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.

10 He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.

11 If they obey and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

12 But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.

We should observe that whether "righteous" or "unrighteous," no man escapes affliction or death (vss. 8 and 12). Christians can die (Rom. 8:13) for "walking after the flesh," even though the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition, same trash) denies this in Romans 8:1. To begin with, in the Old Testament we have two outstanding examples of the passage set before us: One is [Manasseh ]{.class_3759}(2 Chron. 33), a perfect matchmate to verses 8 and 11. Most of the commentators have failed to observe that although Manasseh was the worst king Judah ever had (see 1 Kings 21:9, 11, 16, 17, 18, and 20), he finally repented and returned to the Lord (2 Chron. 33:13--18), but first, Manasseh was ["bound"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8) in ["fetters"]{.class_3760} (2 Chron. 33:11). God shows Manasseh his evil works (vs. 9) just as our text states it (2 Chron. 33:12 and 13), and he returned from iniquity (vs. 10) when he returned to Jerusalem (2 Chron. 33:13). Manasseh then obeyed God and served Him (vs. 11) by taking away the strange gods, altars, and idols and by repairing the altar of the Lord (2 Chron. 33:15--16).

The other is [Ahab]{.class_3759}. Ahab's experience is a good example of what takes place in verse 12. Ahab, as Manasseh, repented and got right with the Lord (1 Kings 21:29), but then Ahab refused to obey the word of the Lord (1 Kings 22:16), so what happened to him? He perishes by the sword (1 Kings 22:34), [exactly as Elihu states the matter ]{.class_3759}(vs. 12).

Now all of this should be very instructive to the Christian who looks to the Old Testament for "present truth" (Stamite cliché). (The Old Testament (Rom. 15:4) was "present truth" (1 Cor. 10:11) for the One Body of 1 Corinthians and Ephesians 4, [according to the author of Romans and Corinthians]{.class_3759}.) Even though a Christian is called to be a king (Rev. 5:10) and called to inherit a [Kingdom ]{.class_3759}(Rom. 8:17), he can transgress (Gal. 5:16--21) and ["be holden with the cords of his sins"]{.class_3760} (Prov. 5:22). Although he is free from the law ("oh happy condition!") and not under the dominion of sin (Rom. 6:14), he can temporarily come under the condemnation of the law (see Rom. 8:1 in the Holy Bible: It will [not ]{.class_3759}be found in any edition of the ridiculous [NASV]{.class_3759}), when he walks after the flesh. In this condition, he is BOUND and HELD (Job 36:8). In this condition, God uses the Bible to show the Christian his work and transgressions (Psa. 119:9, Eph. 5:1--6) and opens his ear to discipline (Heb. 12:5). Once instructed, the Christian is commanded (vs. 10) to ["return from iniquity"]{.class_3760} (2 Tim. 2:19), and if he [does]{.class_3759}, verse 11 follows. If not, then verse 12 follows. It is as simple as that, and no amount of "serious study" in the "original languages" will make it any clearer, no matter what knucklehead is trying to show off for the grandstand.

Now I have (by the grace of God) seen the Lord "open the ear" of many a Christian young person and command them to ["return from iniquity."]{.class_3760} In nearly twenty-five years of preaching in youth camps (numbering anywhere from 100 to 1,200 young people), I have seen the tears flow and the fists clench and the chests heave and the lips quiver as God the Holy Spirit fulfilled verse 9 to His children. I preached at a camp (Keystone Heights, Florida) where I challenged the teenagers to ask me any question they could think of and I would try to give them a verse of Scripture in answer to it within twenty seconds. This open "Bible Forum" went from 7:00 P.M. to 8:30 P.M. At 8:30 P.M. I tried to quit, but they wanted to go on; we went to 11:30. By 11:45 I was getting tired from standing and suggested that we stop; we didn't. At 12:00 midnight, there were teenagers crying at the altar, praying around the tabernacle, rushing back to their cabins and literally dragging some of their unsaved bunkmates with them back to the tabernacle. At 12:30, we finally "knocked off." That old black- backed, 66 caliber, [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}(1611) literally blew that group of "Atomic Age" teenagers into Kingdom come without one reference to [Hebrew ]{.class_3759}or [Greek and without one quotation from ANY translation printed by ANYONE since 1700. ]{.class_3759}How do you explain that?

A half dozen times I have been in the middle of this scene: A young man comes up at the end of a service, and he is blubbering so loud that he is incoherent. After he has wept awhile, the story comes out: He has just been saved, but before his conversion he was pushing drugs. Now that he is saved, he has turned himself over (and all of his "contacts") to the police. The authorities and the law have absolved him, but his conscience [hasn't. ]{.class_3759}I can hear it now like I heard it yesterday: "But Brother Ruckman, I damned those kids. I ruined them. They got hooked. And I'm saved, but it's too late for them. A lot of them are hooked, and I did it. I DID IT. How can I ever undo it?"

Well, you can't.

When Paul told Timothy to get there "before winter comes" (2 Tim. 4:21), he was preaching a first rate message. When the north wind strips the trees bare of their clothing and the freezing blast of winter shrouds nature in a coffin of ice, it is too late for some things. The best you can do about some things is to vow not to do them again. Get away from the old company, sell out to God, and spend the rest of your life [ministering ]{.class_3759}to the kind of people you [ruined]{.class_3759}. Some opportunities never come back.

I think of Sergeant Stewart---a fifteen-year-hitch, Marine DI on the island of Oahu. He got saved; the next day he lined up his platoon at parade rest, and then pacing up and down in front of them, he said: "Gentlemen, I have an announcement to make. I just got saved." Then he preached to them for fifteen minutes. The C.O. called him in and told him he could not curtail the "religious liberty" of the Atheists, Jews, and Communists in his platoon by witnessing for Jesus Christ. "Captain," said Sgt. Stewart (standing at attention). "Sir, you were commissioned by the government in Washington, D.C. I received this commission from the Lord Jesus Christ." On he went. You think they didn't give him a fit? A month later, the Sgt. Major sicked the head-shrinker on him.
