Two months later a lieutenant said, "You cannot keep saying the name of Jesus Christ to those men." "Sir," said Stewart, "I hear you take the name of God in vain every day in front of those troops; I'll make a deal with you: Every time you use 'Jesus Christ' as a cuss word, I get to witness for Him."

But I will get to the point. The night before I left Honolulu after a Bible conference (1973), I saw Marine Sergeant Stewart leaning on the front door post of the church; tears were running down his face. I put my arm around him and said, "It gets tough, don't it, man?" Stewart fixed his watery eyes on me and said, "Preacher, I can't stand it; I just can't stand any more of it. I'm getting out. I can't stand the cursing and the dirty stories and the filthy language; it's killin' me. I don't know how I can finish my hitch." "Well, we reap what we sow, my good brother," I said, "now you see how it goes. Remember the fifteen years you filled those barracks with the same kind of stuff?" Stewart nodded his head miserably. "Well, we reap what we sow, my good brother, and you're just beginning to see your work and your transgressions" (Job 36:9).

Sergeant Stewart is learning something right now that you couldn't learn with thirty years study in the "original languages," and when he "graduates," he won't forget his "curriculum."

36:13 But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.

14 They die in youth, and their like is among the unclean.

15 He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.

16 Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table should be full of fatness.

17 But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.

18 Because there is wrath, beware lest he take thee away with his stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.

19 Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.

20 Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.

21 Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.

Now, there is a contrast given immediately; the "hypocrites" of verse 14 are likened to Onan and Er (Gen. 38:7--9), for ["they die in youth, and their life is among the unclean"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). These hypocrites are mentioned again in Luke 12:46 and 56, and you will notice that the reference (for the "umpteenth time") is to the Second Advent. This makes more than [sixty references ]{.class_3759}to the Second Coming before the material is given on the rapture (37:1--4), the advent (38:10), and the Antichrist (40:15). The [Encyclopedia of Prophecy ]{.class_3759}(Dr. Barton Payne) and the [Second Coming Bible ]{.class_3759}(Biederwolf) are not to be taken seriously within the category of [Biblical ]{.class_3759}studies, by [Biblical ]{.class_3759}scholars, by any means. No real "serious" student of the Scriptures would waste five minutes with such compendiums in spite of the fact that they make use of the works of over [400 Christian ]{.class_3759}scholars with [8,000 years]{.class_3759} of education. However, such "labor" and "effort" would not qualify any of those listed to comment intelligently on the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}in these matters. Eight thousand hours of study by "Fundamentalists" do not amount to enough evidence for us to consider seriously, when these men can't find twenty references to the Second Advent in the [Book of Job]{.class_3759}. Let the kiddies play in the sand pile.

"[The hypocrites in heart heap up wrath"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13), because they pile it up like a treasure (Rom. 2:5). When God binds them, they do NOT cry out to God for help; they go to the HEW or the NEA or the AMA or the NAACP or the RCC or the SDA or UNESCO or UNICEF or NATO or they start burning and looting in the streets or stabbing high school students with icepicks and "combs." The hypocrites in ["heart"]{.class_3762} fulfill ["the judgment of the wicked"]{.class_3762} (vs. 17), and God's stroke (vs. 18) will place them where a great ransom (Matt. 20:28) cannot deliver them (vs. 18). To this day we say, "He had a STROKE," and the elimination of the twentieth-century, updated, modern word from the archaic bibles---[Living Bible ]{.class_3761}(1971), [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901), the Dark Age [NASV ]{.class_3761}(1963), the outdated [NEB ]{.class_3761}(1961), and other ancient fossils, shows clearly the intention of the twentieth century translators: They resent the REAL [Living Bible ]{.class_3761}([AV]{.class_3761}, 1611), and wish to replace it with dead magazines that have neither power nor authority. The great ["stroke" ]{.class_3762}of verse 18 is not the "stroke" of Dave Gardner and the scat drummers; the One whose right foot (Matt. 5:35, oh yeah, man) sets the tempo for this dance (Psa. 150:4) strikes in the open (Job 34:26), and those who fall under this "stroke" (vs. 18) are BURIED (Ezek. 24:16).

"[Will he esteem thy riches? No, not gold\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 19). ["Riches profit not in the day of wrath"]{.class_3762} (see Prov. 11:4 and comments), and all the forces of strength (vs. 19) who worship the "God of forces" (Dan. 11:38)---no, it is not "munitions" silly; the duffers who gave you that guff when they gaffed you delighted in diddling with duds---cannot get a sinner out of Hell once he gets in. You won't buy off the Judge of Revelation chapter 20 like some mannequin on the Supreme Court or one of Mao's stooges; if you don't have "connections" with this Judge's Son (1 John 5:12), ["judgment and justice"]{.class_3762} will ["take hold on thee"]{.class_3762} (vs. 17).

Notice how the Bible defines certain things (and people) as being ["unclean"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14), ["filthy"]{.class_3760} (15:16), and "dirty" (Judg. 3:22). The perennial superstition in America (cultivated by educators, publishers, movie houses, and psychiatrists) is that pornography is not "dirty" unless somebody [thinks ]{.class_3759}it is dirty. This is quite similar to placing ten gallons of bourbon before a chronic alcoholic and then telling him it is [his fault ]{.class_3759}if he gets drunk. You can't mess with human nature in this fashion (Gen. 6:5) unless you are ignorant of human nature; those who mess with it reap what their "mess" produces (Gal. 6:7). Only a deluded "positive thinker" would ignore 6,000 years of recorded history where that history deals with the results of questionable practises, questionable art, questionable music, questionable morals, and questionable political alliances. If it's [doubtful ]{.class_3759}(Rom. 14:23), it's [dirty]{.class_3759}. The ["unclean"]{.class_3760} of verse 14 includes the great mass of lesbians, homosexuals, queers, gays, sodomites, and practitioners of pornography, bestiality, and incest; that is, the Old Testament defines the ["unclean"]{.class_3760} as being [this group]{.class_3759}. A "GAY" church is a "church" in the sense of the Masons, Elks, Moose, Delta Delta Deltas, and Knights of Columbus (see Acts 19:37 and comments). As far as a [Christian ]{.class_3759}church goes (or a Biblical church), a "gay" church is no more a "church" than the Vatican is a Bible institute. That is, you are going to have to take the NEGATIVE view of human nature as stated by the Author of life, or you will have to reject the Author of life to take the POSITIVE view propagated by unregenerate mankind.

The ["stroke" ]{.class_3760}of verse 18 can come in a number of ways.

There is a man mowing his yard in South Carolina. He turns off the lawnmower as J. Harold Smith approaches him. Smith witnesses to him, and the man curses Smith and tells him to "get the H--- outta here." Smith goes back to the curb and gets into his car. The man at the mower turns his machine back on and then screams and drops dead across the mower with a heart attack.

There is a young lady leaving a Youth For Christ meeting in Tuscaloosa, Alabama, (1950) and saying, "I'll go to Hell before you see me down at that altar." In thirty minutes she fulfills her own prophecy: A head-on car collision at forty miles per hour.

There is a young Air Force Lieutenant in Panama City (1956); he is stepping out with the wife of a Christian man. After giving up his cigarettes and laying his whole life on the altar, the Christian man spends one whole night in prayer on his knees in his living room. The next day there is a loud explosion about 20,000 feet over the city, and the Air Force recovers about twenty-five pounds of the Lieutenant's body.

Then there was a profligate, young married man who stepped out on his wife ten or eleven times before their first baby. When the first baby was delivered, he was "shacking up" ("making out," etc.) with another man's wife in a motel. On the way back to the motel (after a short visit to the hospital), a drunk hit him head-on: The steering column slammed up between his legs. He is still alive, but he will father no more children.

There is a young man that a friend of mine witnessed to in Pensacola (1964). He laughed at this friend of mine and told him to "go to H---." Two nights later, the police caught this young fella trying to rob a gas station. In the fight that ensued one policeman discharged a shotgun at him from about fifteen feet away. They picked up the body of that Christ-rejecting young man in two pieces. ["Beware lest he take thee away with his stroke"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18).

I am preaching in a small town in South Alabama (1952). An old town drunk hears about the meetings and decides to come out to church (for the first time in thirty years). Two hours before the service, a summer thunderstorm comes up; lightning runs over the house, finds him on the back porch, slaps him between the eyes, and he dies in his wife's arms with blood running out of his nose and ears.

Way back in 1887 an infidel in a small Southern town dared God to kill him. To prove his contempt for the Bible, he strode out into the road, in the middle of a rainstorm, and shook his fist at God and dared God to strike him dead. A bolt of lightning hit the porch of his house and struck his seven-year-old girl dead. After the rain stopped, the infidel prepared hot water and grave clothes for his little girl, while the neighbors watched. Still cursing God, he grew more rabid by the minute, finally screaming: "You dirty Bully! Pick on my little girl, will you! Couldn't kill [me]{.class_3759}. Had to pick on a seven-year-old girl, didja!? Come on, I dare you to strike ME! Gowan, [strike me!!]{.class_3759}" Two hours later he was dead. A black widow spider under the woodpile bit his hand while he was getting kindling for the fire, and everyone in the neighborhood was so scared by his hysterical screaming that no one would come and help him. God "struck" him; He just decided to use something smaller than a mouse to do it with. No need to be spectacular about it all the time. An infected tonsil or toenail can kill you just as good as a firing squad when it comes to eternity. It just depends on how the Lord God Almighty wants to do it; hours are not a big issue with Him.

I witnessed to an "inlaw" in Greensboro, Alabama, (1950). He lied his way through a profession of faith and went back to his gambling, smoking, drinking, lying, and stealing. In less than a year they found him dead, in a parked car, near his \$90,000 home. You don't mess with God.

Monroe ("Monk") Parker witnessed to a young man in a drug store in a small town where he was holding a tent meeting. Earlier, the young buck had tried to pick a fight with Monk by bumping him into a table. Monk said, "You did that on purpose." The young man said, "So what? What of it?"

"I'm not afraid of you," said Monk Parker.

"Well, step outside and take off your Christianity about five minutes, and we'll see whether you're afraid of me or not," challenged the young man.

"I haven't got the kind of Christianity I can take off for five minutes," retorted Parker, "and besides that, I know who you are. You're so-and-so, and your daddy has been down at the tent praying for you all week."

The young man apologized and promised that he would come out to the revival "one night" that week. "Maybe," said Parker, "you and I ought to go back in one of these booths and settle the issue with Christ NOW." "Naw," said the young man, "but I promise you, I will come out to the meeting [tonight]{.class_3759}."

"You might not make it," said Parker.

"What do you think I am, a liar?" said the young man.

"No, but something might come up; you never can tell. Why don't you and I have a word of prayer, and you can receive Christ here, right in the store?"

"I'll be down tonight," said the young man, and he turned on his heel and left. Fifty minutes later he was dead. He died screaming, holding on to the bars of a cheap iron bed in a cheap hotel. Not twenty minutes after he left the drugstore a car had run over him. When Monk Parker got to the bed, the doctor was already shrugging his shoulders. Monk yelled John 3:16 into the dying man's ears. With one mighty effort that young buck opened his eyes, stared wildly, and then cursed God in four letter words you couldn't possibly have misinterpreted. ["Beware, lest he take thee away with HIS STROKE."]{.class_3762}

Some of you will mess around with John 3:16 and Romans 10:9 and 10 in a dozen "new" translations too long, and about the time you make up your mind that you are "ready to live it," God will just quietly drop your soul into Hell; you will abide by [His ]{.class_3759}timetable, not yours.

Glenn Schunk (Greenville, S.C.) made it a practice to witness consistently for Christ while he was in the Infantry (1944--1945); he didn't always bat .500, but he won a few. One G.I. (whom he was unable to win to Christ) died about forty- eight hours after Glenn preached the gospel to him. The troops were in a damp cave in Italy, early in the morning, waiting for the "jump off" at 6 A.M. Someone had built a fire. With thirty or forty men lying around and sitting around the blaze staring dumbly at it, this particular G.I. suddenly got up and walked toward the fire; he almost stood in it, and he remained there about ten minutes gazing into the coals. Finally (as though talking to himself) he said, "You know something? I'm going to have to change my way of living one of these days." Then he walked to the back of the cave and sat down. Two hours later a booby-trapped grenade went off between his legs; he died in terrible agony. You don't mess with that Bible no matter how good you think you are, and that applies to you "nuts" like John R. Rice, Weniger, Walvoord, and Custer who think you are smart enough to mess with it. [You have the same old nature ANY unsaved man has.]{.class_3759}

(Observe the Tribulation references in verses 15 and 20 and the reference to the restoration of Israel in verse 16.) Elihu accuses Job of choosing inquity instead of affliction (vs. 21), and as we have already stated on four occasions, in the Tribulation you will either be afflicted, or you will have to commit iniquity; there will be no middle ground (as there is now) in Daniel's Seventieth Week. Moses chose affliction rather than iniquity (Heb. 11:25).

One is reminded of "Father" Nash (1830) who, more than any other man, was responsible for the powerful anointing that accompanied the preaching of Charles G. Finney (1792--1875). One night a snoopy Christian decided to find out what Nash's secret of prayer was; he hid under the bed in Nash's hotel room. "Father" Nash came in, undressed, turned down the gas lamp, and went to bed, without kneeling to pray. Thirty minutes later, the eavesdropper under the bed heard him say, "Goodnight Jesus." But that wasn't the worst of it. Two hours later, his arthritis got so bad that "Father" Nash could not sleep. The old man got up and began to walk the floor, holding on to his arthritic arms. As he paced the room, the spy under the bed heard him exclaiming: "Oh, my God, how much better this is than sin." \[Every head bowed, every eye shut, etc.\]

36:22 Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?

23 Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?

24 Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.

25 Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.

26 Behold, God is great, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.

No one can teach like God (vs. 22); no one can supervise Him while He is "on the job" (vs. 23: ["who hath ENJOINED him"]{.class_3760}); no one can accuse Him of error (vs. 23); and woe be to the evolutionist who gives the credit for the glories of creation (vs. 24) to "nature" or "natural processes" (vs. 25). Any fool looking straight over his head, day or night (Psa. 19:1--6), knows that "nature" and "processes" create nothing (Rom. 1:19--21). You have to be an educated fool (and a hypocritical one at that: Look at the First Law of Thermodynamics) to digest that kind of slop. You have to be somebody like La Mettrie (1748) or Holbach (1770) or Moleschott (1852) or Vogt (1855) to think that [material ]{.class_3759}things came from [material ]{.class_3759}things, or even from [electronic ]{.class_3759}things.

Exactly as an American Christian has to be EDUCATED into denying the [AV ]{.class_3759}(see any of John R. Rice's lame alibis for setting "godly men" up as the final authority---while refusing to mention Hills, Ray, Philpot, Burgon, Miller, Scrivener, and Fuller) so an American sinner has to be EDUCATED into accepting the religious superstitions of Darwin and Huxley. The men today, who are working night and day to make a liar out of Moses (Gen. 1--3), are all EDUCATED men---H. L. Frankel, Conrat, Kornberg (1957), Spiegelman (1965), Hayakawa (1955), Stuart Chase, Alfred Korzybski, Norbert Wiener (1964), Hans Reichebach (1938), and Professor Margenau (1963). No pagan in his natural habitat, raised by the laws of nature and the "survival of the fittest," has to be told that there is a God or that God has [laws that must not be broken]{.class_3759}. You have to learn that crap from a high school or college or university, or books written by crap-slingers who went to one.

"[Man may behold it afar off"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25). ["Afar off"]{.class_3762} is the right term because the revelation of God's work (vs. 24) often appears, at distances of 30,000,000 light year (see 22:12 and notes). ["Behold, God is GREAT, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out"]{.class_3762} (vs. 26). But Job knew this. If Zophar knew it (see 11:7 and remarks), Job certainly did. Observe, however, that when Elihu says, ["we know him not"]{.class_3762} (vs. 20), the remark is made before the Old Testament is written, before the revelation of God in Jesus Christ, before the Advent of the Holy Spirit, and before any absolute assurance that ["we know him"]{.class_3762} (1 John 5:10--13). Although none of us can know everything about God there is to know (Rom. 11:34), we certainly can know Him as well as we know our best friends on this earth. We have knowledge of His gospel (2 Cor. 3,5), His Spirit (John 14--16), His presence (John 14--16), His words (John 17:17), His prophecies (Rev. 6--22), His birth, life, and death (Mark, Matthew, Luke, etc.), His enemies (Acts 4--26), His apostles (2 Cor. 11), His likes and dislikes (Eph. 4--6, Gal. 6:1--21), His attitude about the world (John 17), the church (Eph. 5), science (1 Tim. 6:20), philosophy (Col. 2:8), the Jew (Rom. 9--11), worldly wisdom (1 Cor. 12), and the destinations of those who accept Him (Rev. 21) and those who reject Him (Rev. 22). Now that may not be "batting 1,000," but you won't do much better with your best friends!

36:27 For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:

28 Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.

29 Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, or the noise of his tabernacle?

30 Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.

31 For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.

32 With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.

33 The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.

Now we come upon one of those unexplored passages which defies thorough exegesis. We know what to say about it [in general]{.class_3759}, and the obvious truths in it lie apparent on the surface; but when we compare the passage with Job 37:15--16, 37:21--22; Psalm 104:3; and Isaiah 19:1, we realize we are surveying ground that Delitzsch, Duhm, Budde, Linton, Kantzer, Dillmann, Reuben, Olson, Custer, Lehman, Feinberg, Muntz, Pember, Larkin, Scofield, Pettingill, Culbertson, Keil, Gesenius, and S. I. Johnson never "paced off." (People like John R. Rice, of course, are out of the question. "Biblical scholarship" was never their field and never will be. Promoting your own revival meetings through literature does not come under the category of serious Biblical scholarship. Rice got eighty percent of his theology right because he followed the "mightiest pens of the eighteenth and nineteenth century"---Wesley, Spurgeon, Cronin, Pember, Clarence Larkin, Andrew Murray, R. A. Torrey, and Charles G. Finney. [Anyone could get the same]{.class_3759} reading any of the works named above, without consulting Rice for anything.)

We are not going to stick our necks way out on something we know nothing about. Not once in twenty-eight years of Christian service have we ever even intimated that we knew the Bible from "kiver to kiver." We have only professed to know it well enough to know that it is able to correct any "Fundamentalist nut" who is arrogant and stupid enough to think he can correct it. Like Sam Jones (1847--1906), we are, and have always been, of the opinion that if anyone understood all of it, it would mean that someone wrote it who didn't have any more sense than they had. Unlike John R. Rice (see [I Am a Fundamentalist]{.class_3761}) and his "good" and "godly" friends (who sit in judgment on the word of God every time they read it), we have never made the fatal mistake of thinking that we knew enough about the Bible to correct it (see any of Rice's "nice" Christians or "good" Christians who were sanctified enough to correct the Holy Scriptures). The stupid asses who correct it with Greek and Hebrew have never mastered it in their own language--- ENGLISH---and you can bet your soul and your religion on that. (Yes, I know; you don't like the [terminology]{.class_3761}. Well, you pie-eyed saphead, you didn't like the [pure]{.class_3761}, [unadulterated word of the Living God]{.class_3761}; why should I worry about you not liking MY terminology? Do you think I am as unbalanced as YOU are? Nobody but a fanatical, critical, hell-raising CRACKPOT would find fault with God Almighty and then whine because you didn't respect them or [their ]{.class_3761}scholarship or [their ]{.class_3761}teachers. Go soak your head, son; you need a good stiff drink.)

Instead, we will list the implications and inferences that surround these passages. It will take someone with a great deal more time than we have had to plumb the depths of the material. (For a similar situation see Exodus chapter 25 and comments in that Commentary.)

1\. The ordinary clouds which pass over your head may contain something more than vapor and dust; [they may shield UFOs.]{.class_3759}

2\. The ordinary clouds which pass over your head may be connected with [interstellar observation ]{.class_3759}by the ["principalities and powers"]{.class_3760} of Ephesians 6:11--13.

3\. The constant association of clouds with nearly every major event in Biblical history---the flood, the tabernacle, the giving of the Law, the Ascension, the Second Advent, etc.---is not accidental. (At the crucifixion darkness covers the land, and this is followed by an earthquake; certainly clouds are connected with these events in some way.) ["He cometh with CLOUDS"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 1:7) and Wuest's "myriads" are about as enlightening on the passage as a mud plaster.

4\. Clouds are connected in some way with God's judgments on a people or country (vs. 31).

5\. "Invisible UFOs" may have learned to bend light rays long before the U. S. Navy experimented (with disastrous results) with the matter during World War II.

6\. "Noises" are not merely "lightning" in clouds; they are the ["noise of HIS TABERNACLE"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29), and ["HIS tabernacle"]{.class_3760} is clearly the universe (see Exod. 25 and notes), not a tent up in the third heaven.

7\. The bright light of 36:15 and 37:15 cannot be [lightning ]{.class_3759}(as it is so stated in every Commentary in print), for ["men see NOT the bright light which is in the clouds"]{.class_3760} (Job 37:21). That is some ["bright light"]{.class_3760} you have there girls, if it is lightning that no one can [see]{.class_3759}! You can see lightning when you can't see your hand in front of your face, or you can see it in broad daylight. The text obviously contains more information than one can find in the last 300 commentaries written on it. If the "majority of scholars who AGREE" (cliché borrowed by John R. Rice from the liberal faculty at Chicago University---1900--1940) had spent as much time praying over the English text as they spent in altering it with the "original Hebrew," there might be some "bright light" on the passage that we could see. As it is, 300 years of commenting on the Scripture never revealed anything. God doesn't show things to silly asses (Fundamentalists or otherwise) who think that because [one saved sinner ]{.class_3759}altered a few words (see Torrey, Larkin, DeHaan, et al.) that [any saved sinner ]{.class_3759}could do the same thing (see Rice, Custer, Bob Jones III, et al.).

Practically speaking, we may say that God does make ["small the drops of water"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27), for it is vapour that begats rain (vs. 27).

The clouds do drop this vapour as it does ["distil"]{.class_3760} (vs. 28) upon man abundantly. When water is distilled in a "still" it comes out of the copper tube in drops, exactly as the verse states. The ["spreadings"]{.class_3760} of the clouds include the Cirrus, Altostratus, Nimbus, Cumulus, etc. The light "spread on the clouds" is not only sunlight and flashes of lightning, but also the bright colors that appear at sunrise and sunset which are caused by selective absorption and reflection of light by dust particles or vapor haze in the atmosphere.

"[The noise of his tabernacle"]{.class_3760} (vs. 29) may have a practical application of the thunder which proceeds from the lower part of the Tabernacle (understanding, of course, that the Tabernacle is the universe: Heb. 8, 9; Exod. 25--26:30 and comments).

God covers ["the bottom of the sea" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 30), practically, in the sense that the bottom of the sea is covered with light when the lightning streaks over the surface (see Psa. 18:14--15), but this application is farfetched. ["The bottom of the sea,"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30) in this passage, is aimed at the ["sea"]{.class_3760} of Job 41:31 and Revelation 21:1--2 and the ["deep"]{.class_3760} of Job 41:32 and Genesis 1:1--3. The ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 31) could be applied to storms, tempests, rain, thunder, and lightning, and the "judging" of the verse (vs. 31) could be in the sense that [rain at the wrong time ]{.class_3759}(1 Sam. 12:17) is a disaster, while rain at the [right time ]{.class_3759}(Deut. 32:2) is one of the greatest blessings a man can get on this earth (1 Kings 18:45). ["He giveth meat in abundance"]{.class_3760} (vs. 31) when the rain comes as a blessing, and conversely (Job 37:13), there is no ["meat"]{.class_3760} at all where there is no rain (Zech. 14:17).

"[With clouds he covereth the light"]{.class_3760} (vs. 32) in the sense that lightning is shielded in a thunderhead till it strikes, or in the sense that sunlight is often blotted out by clouds. The ["cloud that cometh betwixt"]{.class_3762} (vs. 32) comes between the observer and the sun so that it shades his eyes. However, the next verse would point to lightning, not sunlight: ["THE NOISE thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour"]{.class_3762} (vs. 33). We are on difficult ground (see above), and this is apparent by what follows; for what follows is a direct reference to the RAPTURE, before the Second Advent, where lightning and thunder are the ["noise" ]{.class_3762}of verse 33. This ["noise"]{.class_3762} is called ["the voice of the Lord"]{.class_3762} in Psalm 29, and this voice also ["maketh the hinds to calve"]{.class_3762} as it is ["upon many waters"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 29:3 and 9). This voice would certainly ["spread light over the bottom of the sea"]{.class_3762} (vs. 30), for it ["divideth the flames of fire" and "discovereth the forests"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 29:7, 9).

At this important passage, [prophetically]{.class_3759}, the Scholars' Union (one hundred percent of any publications by Zondervan, Eerdmans, Baker, [Sword of the Lord]{.class_3759}, BJU Press, Logos, etc., where they deal with the passage) defects and pretends that any verse [they ]{.class_3759}cannot make sense out of has been written [incorrectly ]{.class_3759}(see Heb. 9:27 in the [NASV ]{.class_3759}for example). Clarke (p. 161) says that verse 32 is "no translation of the ORIGINAL." "The mending of the text by conjecture" is necessary according to Mr. Good and Mr. Reiske. Clarke is unwilling to alter the text, but with an "extreme difficulty" (compare this with Rice's baloney about the "difficulty" people have with the "archaic words" in the [AV ]{.class_3759}1611), he suggests that the clouds are really [the MOON eclipsing the sun]{.class_3759} and that it has no connection with ["the noise"]{.class_3760} of verse 33.

Since that ["noise"]{.class_3760} is connected with an "eclipse," Clarke says that the next verse (vs. 33) is a "very UNHAPPY translation." Sure it is, sonny; [now it won't match your private translation]{.class_3759}. (Ain't these billy goats a flip. What is "unhappy" about the translation except the miserable commentator who sat in judgment on the Scripture because he didn't believe in the Rapture? Clarke, as Matthew Henry, rejected the Premillennial truth of both Testaments, and now he is "unhappy" with his inability to figure out the truth and blames this "unhappiness" on the King James translators. No, don't shake your head; it goes on 300 times a day in every Christian college and university in America, and Fundamentalists are just as guilty of it as the worst Liberals and Modernists who ever lived.) To make sure that verse 33 comes out "happily," Adam Clarke obliterates the words[ "sheweth," "it," "cattle,"]{.class_3760} and ["vapour,"]{.class_3760} and then rewrites his own "happy" version which is in "strict connection with the context."

The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}has God covering His hands (vs. 32) and adds "to strike the mark" in the same verse. The [Living "Bible"]{.class_3759} (Taylor) has "blankets" stuck into verse 30 and "fantastic powers" stuck into verse 31 and "may all sinners be warned" stuck into verse 33. (The last five-word addition is not a "paraphrase" of anything; it is a private conjecture based on irrational guesswork.) Dummelow tries to drag the Christian back to the apostate committee of 1881--1884 by recommending its readings for verses 3, 7, 9, 13, 17, 22--23, 27, 29, and 32. The changes suggested by Duhm, Peake, Budde, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Lange, and the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}are (quite normally and naturally) not worth listing.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw3114.xhtml}

CHAPTER 37

37:1 At this also my heart trembleth, and is moved out of his place.

2 Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound that goeth out of his mouth.

3 He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.

4 After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.

5 God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.

6 For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.

7 He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.

8 Then the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places.

9 Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.

10 By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.

11 Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:

12 And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.

13 He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.

The passage is one of the greatest in the Old Testament on the rapture of Tribulation (or Church Age) saints. Naturally, Dr. Barton Payne and Dr. Biederwolf failed to find it when listing their compendiums on "Prophecy" and the "Second Coming." Both men named are [Premillennial Fundamentalists]{.class_3759}; so once again, we are faced with the violent and terrible truth that being a "Fundamentalist" doesn't guarantee that a Christian is a [BIBLE BELIEVER]{.class_3759}. Several things are of note in the passage which were overlooked by the educated Conservatives who sat in judgment on the [King James Bible:]{.class_3759}

1\. Someone's heart is not only [trembling ]{.class_3759}(vs. 1), but ["is moved out of his place"]{.class_3760} (vs. 1), as the ["day star"]{.class_3760} arises in the "heart" of the Christian (2 Pet. 1:19) when he rises at the first resurrection.

2\. The noise of God's ["voice"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2) is THUNDER (vs. 4), exactly as it is written in John 12:28,29. The lightning is directed ["unto the ends of the earth" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 3), clearly matching Matthew 24:27 where a Post-tribulation rapture of tribulation saints is in the context.

3\. Somebody is sent on their way when this blast goes off, for the wording states: ["He will not stay THEM when his voice is heard"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4).

Now what is the answer of the majority of "recognized scholars" (who "agree," etc.) to this obvious, [Scriptural ]{.class_3759}evidence concerning the phenomena of 1 Corinthians 15:5--55 and 1 Thessalonians 4:13--18? Here is a perfect test case (among several dozen already listed) to show the Bible-believing preacher, teacher, or student the difference between Bible believing [Scholarship ]{.class_3759}and Bible-rejecting [Fundamentalism]{.class_3759}, the difference between Christian [education ]{.class_3759}and Christian [understanding]{.class_3759}, the difference between reverent, serious Bible study and the obscene and blasphemous nonsense carried on by the faculty members of Biola, Midwestern, Southeastern, Northwestern, Mid-South, Southwest by Northeast, and South- southwest by North-northeast, etc.

1\. The ["them"]{.class_3760} of verse 4 is a "misprint." It should have been "who can assign the limits to thunder and lightning?" (Clarke, p. 162).

2\. The word ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) is a reference to snow (!). This is the work of Williams (p. 293), a premillennial hyper-dispensationalist.

3\. The word ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) is lightning (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown and the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}).

4\. The word ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) is the "voice of his majesty" ([Living Bible]{.class_3759}).

5\. The word ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) was a reference to men DYING ([Septuagint]{.class_3759}).

6\. The word ["them"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) was completely removed from the [NEB ]{.class_3759}(Dodd).

7\. Kline and Heavenor give up any attempt to even explain the verse.

8\. The word ["THEM"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4) has been converted to "lightnings" in the grossly corrupt [NASV]{.class_3759}, recommended by John R. Rice and Bob Jones III. Dummelow reads "flashings and thunderclaps."

There, now, how is that!? We have listed two postmillennial [Conservatives]{.class_3759}, two apostate [Liberals]{.class_3759}, two dead orthodox [Conservatives]{.class_3759}, two [premillennialists]{.class_3759}, and two Bibles recommended as "reliable" translations by John R. Rice, Stewart Custer, Reuben Olson, [Bob Jones III, Billy Graham]{.class_3759}, and the [Lockman Foundation]{.class_3759}. Where could one find a more "happy" spirit of ecumenicism, as all seek to be "all things to all men that by all means" (see the baloney in Rice's book on [I Am a Fundamentalist]{.class_3759}, where he uses the same alibi for cooperating with Modernists) they may replace the God-breathed words of divine authority with the man-made nonsense of superstitious idiots.

Let us approach this crowd (inclusive of Fundamentalists and Conservatives as well as Liberals and Apostates) exactly as God the Holy Spirit would have us do it; that is, let us begin by throwing out 100 percent of all their interpretations, no matter [who ]{.class_3759}suggested them or [who ]{.class_3759}believed them. If "dedicated, godly, good men," whose "vast labors" and "hours of research," produced these interpretations, let us handle them properly: Let us discard them like a marked deck. And let us say, as reverently as we know how (of their "unquestioned loyalty to the word of God"), the only thing that a real believer could say prayerfully: PIFFLE.

[Observe:]{.class_3759} Rapture (Job 37), advent (Job 38).

[Observe:]{.class_3759} The man of Sin (Job 40), the Son of Perdition (Job 41).

[Observe:]{.class_3759} When the Lord speaks (John 12:28), it is mistaken for [thunder ]{.class_3759}or the voice of the archangel (John 12:29)---"Mah Lawd is inna dey thundah, de trumpet sound withinna mah soul! Ah ain't got long to be heah!"

[Observe:]{.class_3759} Thunder can "speak" (Rev. 10); and you can explain rain as an "electronic action" or lightning as "positive and negative charges" (or ionized particles) till you are Black (Muslim) and Blue (Angel) in the face, and it will never over-throw the words of truth. With the [direction of the Advent ]{.class_3759}in the chapter (vs. 22), with the [manna falling to feed Israel ]{.class_3759}(vs. 6, Job 38:22), with the [Tribulation and Millennium ]{.class_3759}related to Israel (vs. 13), and the "latter rain" (vs. 6, Joel 2:23: Oh yeah, "man." What punk told you that was the Holy Spirit?) right in the context, 100 percent of the "recognized," [Premillennial Conservatives, ]{.class_3759}who "agree" that they are "good" and "godly," are just as blind as an unsaved Christ-rejecting sinner, dead in trespasses and sin. (Typical "recognized" scholarship as found in the Lockman Foundation, promoted by Moody Bible Institute).

"[He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work"]{.class_3760} (vs. 7), because the absolute surety of the Divine hand is found in the [human hand. No two sets of fingerprints are exactly alike]{.class_3761}. To add vengeance to condemnation, the Lord has just mentioned the ["snow"]{.class_3762} (vs. 6) in the previous verse. You see, when 180,000,000,000 flakes are falling every day, somewhere, no two of [them ]{.class_3761}are alike. Any man can know God's work (vs. 7), for every man ["may behold it afar off"]{.class_3762} (36:25). You and your political constituents are blank fools if you think that "mother nature" or "evolutionary processes" could produce such phenomena under the flexible and unpredictable "laws" of chance and "statistical probability." A random evolution that is so careful that it [refuses to make two sets of fingerprints alike ]{.class_3761}and, at the same time, is so careless that it [can't keep a dinosaur alive]{.class_3761} is a pretty haphazard affair, you will have to admit. If no two snowflakes match, the "evolutionary processes" have certainly gone a long way around left end to accomplish NOTHING. If one millionth of that care and energy had been expended in keeping vitamins and minerals in the ground, anyone could live in good health for 200 years on bread and beans.

When the great rain of God's strength comes (vs. 6), or the great snow (vs. 6), then ["the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8); the bears hibernate, and the cats and dogs head for shelter.

"[Out of the south cometh the whirlwind"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9), except in the case of the Second Advent (Ezek. 1:4), where it comes out of the North (in relation to the [planet]{.class_3761}). However, even this whirlwind will move South to North (Mt. Sinai to Mt. Olivet) after the Lord descends into our atmosphere. Since these truths are completely hidden to Hal Lindsey and Kirban (and any other modern "prophetic" writer), they usually do what Clarke does with the verse: He denies that the whirlwind (vs. 9) is a [real ]{.class_3761}whirlwind and then alters the word ["south"]{.class_3762} to "chambers." (Nice work; if you're too stupid to know what you're talking about, then attack the Bible. It goes on all the time in every classroom in America, from Pensacola Christian College to Hyles-Anderson.)

The cold wind that produces ["frost"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) comes from the North (vs. 9), and it is equated with ["the breath of God."]{.class_3760} And the ["breadth of the waters is straitened" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 10) in the sense that when water freezes, it [expands ]{.class_3759}till it covers the waters and prevents them from moving. Now notice, throughout all of this, the constant equating of [nature's forces with God's actions ]{.class_3759}(see Psa. 147:8, 9, 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18), and wherever the Bible does this, the modern response is one of two assumptions:

1\. The writers are presumed to be using [figurative expressions ]{.class_3759}in [poetic form ]{.class_3759}(see the [ASV ]{.class_3759}or the [NASV]{.class_3759}, and notice how the form matches the [NRSV ]{.class_3759}and the [New English Bible]{.class_3759}. No! Check it. Don't holler about "terminology," you prejudiced bigot. CHECK IT.) That is, the writers are [pretending ]{.class_3759}that God is responsible for these things, where He really is [not]{.class_3759}.

2\. Being "primitive" and "unacquainted with the inductive scientific method," they wrongly attributed to God things that were nothing more than the natural reactions of materials and chemicals which showed up [gradually ]{.class_3759}(and naturally) without any supernatural intervention.

We will discard both of these modern religious dogmas (or creedal statements) as being little more than panicky attempts by superstitious pagans to cut God out of time, life, and history. If any of us knew how much God really had to do with frost, rain, snow, hurricanes, floods, crops, famines, heat waves, typhoons, tidal waves, and "dry spells," we might go back to praying for our groceries instead of looking to the "great white father" in the "White" House, and the Committee for "price controls." If you believe that God controls life and death (see Job 1--2)---and what Christian doesn't believe this?---why have you suddenly decided that certain elements which produce life and death (crops, rain, hurricanes, earthquakes, famines, etc.) are incidentals, or "ACCIDENTALS," determined by the caprice of the natural elements? Isn't this position rather inconsistent? Doesn't the Communist or Atheist have a much more tenable position when he just cancels out God altogether?

If God can number the "hairs on my head" (Matt. 10:30) and count the sparrows as they fall (Matt. 10:29), am I to believe that the "cold front" and the "low pressure area" are produced by "the weather"? On a morning so cold that the squirrels are pushing the jack rabbits to get them started, am I to assume that God had nothing to do with the reading of the thermometer?

When it is so hot that the dogs chasing the cats are walking, am I to assume that God is not the author of the temperature? Do I assume that the foggy weather over Dunkirk (May, 1940)---which saved the BEF from annihilation---was "mother nature at work?" And look at this one: ["Who can stand before his cold?"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 147:17). Napoleon (Russia, 1812) couldn't; Adolph Hitler (1944) couldn't. Are we to believe that it was an "accident" that made Napoleon and Hitler attack West to East with a Russian winter coming on? And if it would have snowed "anyway" and the temperatures would have dropped below zero "anyway" (yes, we've been to college too), why didn't Hitler [cross the channel ]{.class_3759}instead of attacking Russia, or why didn't he begin operations in [April ]{.class_3759}instead of [June]{.class_3759}, or why didn't the "lend lease" tanks from the U.S.A. to Russia sink in the Atlantic, or why didn't Napoleon sign an alliance with Russia against Germany, or\...? You can't overrule God's providential hand on time and history with all of the depraved "rational" powers of the degenerate "scientific" mind.

Men in a flooded mine shaft have seen water rise steadily to a mark on a beam, and upon claiming a Scriptural promise and crying out to God, they have seen the water [stop ]{.class_3759}at the mark and, an hour later, [recede]{.class_3759}. Men have raised their sails in the doldrums without a breath of wind to encourage them, and in answer to prayer, they have seen the sails billow and the ship take off at three knots. POW's have run for their lives in fresh fallen snow and had a snow storm cover their tracks from their pursuers within an hour of their flight. Men adrift on rafts in an open sea have prayed for rain and have had a thundershower come up from a [clear horizon ]{.class_3759}in less than ten hours. Men have prayed for rain to stop when two more inches on the level of a lake or river would have meant a flood disaster; the rain stopped. The rain turns off and on, many times, in answer to prayer (1 Kings 18; Rev. 11:6), and you are a credulous dupe indeed to believe that rainfalls and droughts can be explained apart from the One who causes them to "correct" His land (vs. 13). It is His land, His rain, His sun, His clouds, and His atmosphere (Rom. 11:36), and if He says "drought," you couldn't make rain with five witchdoctors, a rain dance, ice pellets, and a government ecological handout of \$12,000,000. If God says, "Pour!" you couldn't stop that rain with a battery of artillery, two cobalt bombs, a solar plant, and the Space Center at Houston (see Job 38:16, 27). Elihu continues his lecture to Job.

37:14 Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.

15 Dost thou know when God disposed them, and caused the light of his cloud to shine?

16 Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?

17 How thy garments are warm, when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?

18 Hast thou with him spread out the sky, which is strong, and as a molten looking glass?

The phenomena we have been discussing are called ["the wondrous works of God"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14), not the "marvels of evaporation and condensation." No man knows [when ]{.class_3759}God disposes the clouds, for there were no clouds before Genesis 9:13, as far as anyone can tell; and since 95 percent of the "qualified scholars" don't believe in a universal flood (Gen. 6--8), they can only guess as to when "clouds" showed up. These "guesses" vary from 900,000,000 years (on earth) to 400,000,000,000,000 years (dust clouds in outer space). But again, these "clouds" are more than those which a man can see over his head; observe: ["the light of HIS cloud"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15) [is in reference to a particular cloud]{.class_3759}, as it is found in Revelation 14:14 and Ezekiel 1:4.

Now, we have commented on our own insufficiency in these matters (see 36:29--30) as well as the matters of Exodus 25, which see. Verse 18 is plainly a reference to the [top of the great deep ]{.class_3759}(Gen. 1:1--3) which is FROZEN (38:30), and this ["deep"]{.class_3760} is no more the Atlantic or Pacific ocean than it is your front yard birdbath. The crystalized (Rev. 4:6) ["sea of glass"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 15:2) is NORTH (37:22) beyond Alpha Draconis, and if the "Space Centers" at Houston and Canaveral cannot discern this, they are merely shooting par for the course. No major scientist ever lived who believed Genesis 1 literally, and that includes the entire group of Christian scholars engaged in writing apologetic works on catastrophic geology (Rimmer, Price, Morris, Reid, Pattern, Nelson, Davidheiser, et al.). The trouble with ["his cloud"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15) is that it hearkens back to Job 26:7 and 8, which see. Practically, we may marvel at the ["balancings of the clouds."]{.class_3760} However, we may marvel even where these ["clouds" ]{.class_3760}are the ones we see. (See Job 28:25 and comments on the "balance.")

Job's garments are ["warm"]{.class_3760} (vs. 17) when God quiets the earth by the South wind, which is sometimes called the "Sirocco." The sky that is ["spread out\...as a molten looking glass"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18) is plainly the "firmament" of Genesis 1:6--8, not the ionosphere or exosphere. This is the ["glass"]{.class_3760} through which we now seek ["darkly"]{.class_3760} (1 Cor. 13:12). (No, it's not just a mirror, even though it reflects back more than it magnifies. Never mind the Lockman Foundation, just read your Bible and believe it.) It is ["molten"]{.class_3760} (vs. 18) because it was poured out by God (Gen. 1:6) as a solid cast (38:30), and the "ancient Hebrews" (to cite the modern Liberal) had the concept right while the modern astronauts have it wrong---as usual.

37:19 Teach us what we shall say unto him; for we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness.

20 Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.

21 And now men see not the bright light which is in the clouds: but the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.

22 Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God is terrible majesty.

23 Touching the Almighty, we cannot find him out: he is excellent in power, and in judgment, and in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.

24 Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.

Verses 23--24 are self-evident and every word in them is true.

Verses 19--20 hearken back to Job's desire to appear before God "in court" and speak his piece (23:3--7). Elihu's thinking is this: "You have been telling us that you knew what you would say if you could address God. Well, [we ]{.class_3759}don't have that much sense." ["Teach us what we shall say unto him,"]{.class_3760} if you know it all (vs. 19). Elihu says that "we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness" (vs. 19). (See 38:2 for the ethical meaning of this.) God dwells in the "thick darkness" because the "sea of glass" and the clouds that cover His throne (Job 26) are not visible to a telescope; radar signals could not reach them in less than 40,000,000 years at a minimum. We are in the bottom of the "great deeps," and here, in our sink hole (the back end of an S-shaped Nebula), we cannot see God unless He withdraws the veil (Acts 7:55) and reveals Himself (Acts 9:5, 6). ["Shall it be told him that I speak?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20). Will someone step into God's presence and say, "Lord, here comes Elihu and he wants to justify himself." The irony in all of it is that with the finished work of God's Son (John 19:30), there is Someone who can stand for us in the "presence" (John 1:4) and tell God that we speak (Rom. 8:26). ["If a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20), the idea being that his words will be his condemnation (Matt. 12:37), for they will "swallow" him up. But the Lord Himself is said to swallow sinners in Psalm 21:9, exactly as the Devil swallows them in Jeremiah 51:44. Job's words [were ]{.class_3759}swallowed up in Job 6:3, and he admitted it; not only this, but the earth (Psa. 106:17), the deep (Psa. 69:15), the grave (Prov. 1:12), and the ["lips of a fool"]{.class_3760} (Ecc. 10:12) are able to "swallow" men up.

Verses 21--22 again take us forward to the Second Advent. We can say (practically) that the light ["which is in the clouds"]{.class_3760} is lightning or that no man can look at the sun when it is shining (Clarke, p. 166). But all of this has an air about it of "avoid the doctrinal import as quickly as possible." [This ]{.class_3759}wind cleans the cloud; it does not blow it away. Furthermore, this wind can be cleaning MEN (vs. 21), not ["clouds."]{.class_3760} \[For the skeptical let us recommend a careful reading of Psa. 1:4, 35:5; Prov. 25:23; Isa. 11:15, 57:13; Jer. 18:17; Hos. 13:15; and Acts 2:2.\] ["Fair weather cometh out of the north"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22, cf. Prov. 25:23.) ["With God is terrible majesty"]{.class_3760} (vs. 22).

Gesenius' (who wrote the text books for the professors who taught the faculty at Bob Jones and Dallas) note on the word ["north"]{.class_3760} is a "beaut" (cliché 1940--1950). Since he had no idea what he was saying to start with, Gesenius says: "Prop. hidden, obscure, inasmuch as the [ancients ]{.class_3759}regarded the North as obscure and dark" ([Hebrew and Chaldean Lexicon]{.class_3759}, p. 715). Yes, they certainly did, and it certainly is. It is too bad that "moderns" (not "ancients") don't have access to the scientific knowledge of the "ancients" (not "moderns"). And it is too bad that neither the Dead Sea Scrolls nor the Bodmer Papyrus nor the Vatican manuscript (see any baloney published on the texts of Aland, Metzger, and Nestle) couldn't give any Hebrew or Greek Scholar any light on[ "the north"]{.class_3760} that wasn't in print in 1611. The [RV ]{.class_3759}(1884) has another "beaut," and since they didn't know what they were doing either, they say: "Out of the north comes a GOLDEN SPLENDOR." (You bet it will. Ezek. 1.) But no one on the [RV ]{.class_3759}committee believed the verse to start with so what was the point in altering it? The socalled "Living" Bible makes no attempt to paraphrase any of the first clause; Taylor simply pretends it wasn't there. Instead, he converts ["fair weather"]{.class_3760} into the sun "shining brightly." I do not remember seeing the sun "shining brightly" [in the NORTH]{.class_3759} since I have been on earth; but perhaps my education has been neglected, and I have not been to all the places that the "qualified meatheads" have been. Evidently the 10,000,000 suckers who bought the [Living Bible ]{.class_3759}(after listening to Billy Graham) have access to scientific information that is still unknown to astronomers in the twentieth century. (248,000,000 copies of the [AV ]{.class_3759}\[1611\] were in circulation before Taylor sat down to mess the Bible up with a "paraphrase.") No one in Job's area saw the sun [in the NORTH ]{.class_3759}one day in his lifetime. The "golden splendor" of the [ASV ]{.class_3759}(any edition; same bunko) must have been a yellow [Aurora Borealis]{.class_3759}---a unique "American Standard Version" of Metaphysics if you ever saw it, buster.

Again---carefully placing all of the revisions and commentaries in the wastebasket---let us turn to the AV (1611) to see why a compass needle points NORTH.

1\. The city of the Great King (Psa. 48:2) is on ["the sides of the north,"]{.class_3760} and the ["sides of the north"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 14:13) are above the galaxies (Isa. 14:12--15).

2\. The ["sides of the north"]{.class_3760} have to do with a PYRAMID structure (Heb. 12) called a ["Mount"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 14), and the sea of glass is on top of this ["mount"]{.class_3760} (Rev. 4, 15); it is called a ["holy hill"]{.class_3760} (Psa. 26:3, 4, 24:3, and 43:3). The confounding of this mount (every time) with the [earthly ]{.class_3759}Mt. Zion at Jerusalem is quite typical of the Bible-denying "Fundamentalists" who blab so much about "serious Bible study." These allegorizing Alexandrians are about as "serious" in their Bible studies as Bob Newhart giving a monologue.

3\. When the Lord Jesus returns, He returns from the NORTH, past an ["image of jealousy"]{.class_3760} (Ezek. 8:3) which is between YOU and the ["door"]{.class_3760} (John 10). This image of jealousy (Rev. 13:1--18) is clearly identified in Job 41, which see. It blocks a [sea entrance ]{.class_3759}(Job 41) which is NORTHWARD (Ezek. 8:1--5).

Directions through the universe, then, are measured [South to North ]{.class_3759}when going UP and [North to South ]{.class_3759}when going DOWN. The milk-and-bran-flakes [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_3759}was so anxious to obliterate this truth (in order to get along with unsaved apostates) that it tells us, on page 1261 (footnote 1), that "the first century cosmology" was so outdated and archaic that it had to use the "phenomenal language common to men." (See how it's done?) Now, kiddies, there is nothing "phenomenal" about North or South unless you are an unsaved Jew like Einstein or a Zen Buddhist like Suzuki. South is DOWN and North is UP in the universe, according to a [King James Authorized Version ]{.class_3759}(1611), and if one hundred percent of the saved (or lost) physicists and astronomers don't believe [that]{.class_3759}, it would be of no more significance than a pope denying eternal security.

Having rejected Ephesians 3:18 thumbs down, having turned the Devil into a crocodile (see Job 41:1), and having turned the Son of God (Dan. 3:25) into "one of the gods," the [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_3759}has given up Bible cosmology for the apologetics of Rimmer and Smith; and the pity is that you cannot buy one book on the market printed by Zondervan, Baker, or Eerdmans (or the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_3759}) that does not waste paper and ink talking about the ["recent]{.class_3759} discoveries which have given us NEW LIGHT on the Bible [since ]{.class_3759}the [AV ]{.class_3759}was translated." Why, you simpering liar, when did anything anyone ever discovered, [aside from the AV text itself]{.class_3759}, enable you to know what "North" means or what it stood for [in the Bible]{.class_3759}? And when did [any ]{.class_3759}archaeological discovery of [anything]{.class_3759}, by [anyone ]{.class_3759}since 1611, give [any ]{.class_3759}real light on salvation, prophecy, the new birth, the blood atonement, the Deity of Christ, the religious history of the world (past, present, or future), or the unseen world (demoniac or otherwise) that isn't found in the [King James ]{.class_3759}text? (See some more of this flim-flam in Acts 1:10 in the [NASV]{.class_3759}, tolerated by Pensacola Christian College.)

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw3186.xhtml}

CHAPTER 38

38:1 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

2 Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?

3 Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.

4 Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.

5 Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?

6 Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;

7 When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?

Now the Lord puts in an appearance for the first time since Job 2. It is important to notice that when He shows up He reveals Himself primarily to [Job ]{.class_3759}(38:1), a type of Israel. Observe, further, that it is a WHIRLWIND (vs. 1), and it comes from the NORTH (Ezek. 1) without consulting anyone on the Scofield Board of Editors about the "cosmology" of any scholar in any century (see Nah. 1:3; Amos 1:14; Zech. 9:14, and comments). This whirlwind (Jer. 23:19, 30:23) comes north to south in relation to the globe, and then it moves south to north up the ["king's highway"]{.class_3760} (Num. 20--21) from Bozrah ("Petra" in Edom: Hab. 3) to Mt. Olivet (Zech. 14) via Rabbath of the Ammonites (Num. 20--24), Ar of Moab (Jer. 48), and Gilgal (1 Sam. 15:33).

The Lord has either ignored Elihu's speech, or else Elihu (as the author of the Book) has not recorded what God said to him. Observe the same thing in Job 42:7 where he again omits any reference to himself. The Lord now speaks through the next four chapters, and the questions which He asks Job in this chapter (38) could not be answered by any group of scientists in 1980. As a matter of fact, Harry Rimmer (at death) left a standing offer of \$1,000 to anyone who could prove a scientific error in the Bible. During his lifetime, Harry was taken to court by a "plaintiff" on [eight counts ]{.class_3759}in regard to this offer; the plaintiff's case was thrown out of court, after boring an unsaved judge to tears. At a scientific convention, Rimmer got together with a bunch of scientists in a hotel lobby and challenged them to answer the thirty-five questions in Job chapter 38 which God asks Job; there wasn't a man there who could answer more than twenty-five of them correctly. You see, the average scientist is a man who "studies more and more about less and less till he knows [everything ]{.class_3759}about [nothing]{.class_3759}"; so these scientists were called "specialists." Once they got out of their "field," you had [them ]{.class_3759}where they thought they had the [Bible]{.class_3759}. The thirty-five questions in Job chapter 38 cover questions about physics, phonetics, tonal values, light rays, oceanography, biology, weather, astronomy, philosophy, and geography. Where a man could get three fields correctly, he could not get eleven, and the prodigy is even more marvelous when we consider that the author of Job is writing more than 3,100 years before Christopher Columbus set sail.

"[Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). Job knew nothing of the celestial judgment hall of Job 1 and 2. He knew nothing of Satan's charge, what prompted it, how God answered it, or why God allowed the Devil to go only "so far." God is going to justify Job and bless him, eventually, but first a good "tongue lashing" (Heb. 12:5--8) is in order; and if we can get out with a verbal rebuke like this (Heb. 12:6--9), we are fortunate indeed. Job is accused of obscuring (["darkeneth"]{.class_3762}) the truth about God's dealings with men by his tirade of Chapters 21 and 31.

"[Gird up now thy loins like a man\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). Whew! Don't you know something went through Job, then, like it coursed through Adam when God said,[ "WHO told thee thou wast naked?"]{.class_3762} (Gen. 3). ["For I will demand of thee"]{.class_3762} (as in Romans 3:2--6) and ["answer thou me."]{.class_3762} Job's prayer has been answered. He is going to get "his day in court," and he is going to get a chance to deal with God directly. But, brother, what a "chance." It is kind of like the "chance" that unsaved men get in Revelation 20:11--15.

And now the questioning starts:

1\. ["Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth?"]{.class_3760} No answer. There is no way that any man can answer such a question. A Zen Buddhist master could answer, "WE KNEW," but that doesn't really answer it. A Zen master with Satori can play with the mind and the imaginations as a professional news writer can play with words, but it still doesn't answer the question: ["Where wast thou?"]{.class_3760} You weren't ["IN Christ"]{.class_3760} if you were ["chosen"]{.class_3760} (see Eph. 1:4 and comments), for John Calvin forgot that 4,000,000 (or more) Old Testament saints weren't "in Christ" even after they were "elected," or [before ]{.class_3759}they were "chosen." You weren't "in Adam" because Adam wasn't there, and if you were in "the mind of God," you couldn't prove it and wouldn't know it. ["Declare, if thou hast understanding"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). A silence so silent follows you could hear a watch ticking in an empty tomb.

2\. ["Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5). No answer. You didn't, that's for sure; none of your kin folk did, that's for sure. A "demiurge" or "created God" (see the apostate [NASV ]{.class_3759}promoted by Bob Jones University) didn't; the gods and devils didn't (Jer. 10:11). Who did? Ninety-eight percent of the scientists would answer: "Nobody did; they just [happened]{.class_3759}." And that kind of an answer would be enough evidence to discard "science" without a second trial run. Nothing with measurements, genetic codes, balances, rotation, substance, shape, and harmony "just happens." Technically speaking, "science" is supposed to be a "correlated body of absolute knowledge." If it is, then science is unable to deal with [origins ]{.class_3759}of any kind; the origins of the earth or the solar system are beyond "scientific scope," and always have been and always will be, apart from believing the revelation of God in the Bible. What a scientist calls "evolution" is only development. As to the origins of the earth or the origin of man, there is no body of scientific knowledge anywhere outside of Genesis chapter 1; Job chapters 26, 38; and Exodus chapters 23--30. The Bible is still the [only scientific textbook in print ]{.class_3759}on the origin of man and the universe; [there are no others written by anyone.]{.class_3759}

One of the fundamental laws of physics is that all transformations of matter which result in the production of energy are the results of DEGENERATION: A breakdown from the complex to the simple (not vice versa). The "simple" [never ]{.class_3759}evolves to the complex where transformations of this nature occur.

3\. ["Who hath stretched the line upon it?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5). You stretch out a tape measure when laying a brick wall, a sidewalk, a driveway, a foundation, or a fence. Who decided that the earth should be 7,927 miles in diameter (at the Equator), with a circumference of 24,894 miles? Who decided that it would weigh 5,883,000,000,000,000,000,000 tons, with a total land surface of 197,272,000 square miles and an average ocean depth of 12,500 feet? Who decided that the earth should spin at a speed of 1,000 miles per hour at the equator, whirl in an oval path around the sun at twenty miles per second, at a distance of around 93,000,000 miles out? "Declare, if thou hast understanding." (vs. 4).

4\. ["Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6). You would have had a tough time finding them, for He ["hangeth the earth upon nothing"]{.class_3760} (26:7). Still, this may be a reference to the foundations which underlie the continents; to what are they fastened? A metal core? A molten core? Did you ever dig one up and look at it? Do you think if no one did it in A.D. 1970 that anyone sitting near Job would have done it in 1800 B.C.? To quote the [Associated Press Almanac ]{.class_3759}of 1973 (p. 343): "The core at the earth's center is the [least known ]{.class_3759}part of the interior; our knowledge of it stems mostly from study of seismic waves\...."

5\. ["Who laid the corner stone thereof; When the morning stars sang together, and all the SONS OF GOD shouted for joy?"]{.class_3760} (vss. 6--7). What was the first thing (cornerstone) the Creator did when He created the earth (Gen 1:1) in "the beginning"? Let's see if the scientists of 1970--1980 can tell us, shall we? "About 150,000,000,000 years ago, [most scientists ]{.class_3759}believe there were clouds of gas and dust\...the clusters of chemicals began to spin\...within it lumps were formed, as in a pudding\...as the millions of years passed these large lumps became planets\...slowly the planets cooled\...some stars were formed like the sun\...after 100,000,000,000 years our solar system evolved\...slowly the crust of the earth cooled\... [perhaps ]{.class_3759}it took millions of years\...its heat was radiated out into space\...the hot matter from inside came bubbling and boiling\...as this cooled the heavier matter settled back into place, sealing the fiery heat in the center of the planet\...so the center of the earth is [probably ]{.class_3759}a gigantic ball of molten iron\...the mantle is [perhaps ]{.class_3759}1,800 miles thick, it is probably made of iron rich rock\...perhaps where the crust below was weakest these outpourings kept piling up\...most geologists [think ]{.class_3759}that a floating island of lighter granite began to harden on the still molten sea, or [perhaps ]{.class_3759}the granite gathered in great pools\...they [may have ]{.class_3759}hardened where they rest today\...[perhaps ]{.class_3759}the\...."

Sure, doc, we understand; [things are tough all over.]{.class_3759}

But there is more in the question than just this dead end for a modern "scientist." Stars [do ]{.class_3759}have tonal values as well as phonetic values, and the morning stars [can ]{.class_3759}"sing" if they want to, for their rays emit sounds on certain frequencies. However, the ["morning stars,"]{.class_3760} here, are plainly "stars" like the seven stars of Revelation 1, and they were with the heavenly "choir conductor" (Ezek. 28) on the morning of creation (Isa. 14:12); for Lucifer was the "son of the MORNING" in Genesis 1.1. (See Pember, [Earth's Earliest Ages]{.class_3759}.) The ["sons of God"]{.class_3760} are present in Genesis 1:2 (see vss 4, 5, 6) before Adam's wife gave birth to Seth.

38:8 Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?

9 When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,

10 And brake up for it my decreed place, and set bars and doors,

11 And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?

12 Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;

13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?

14 It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.

15 And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.

6\. ["Who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 8). When the seas were formed, what stopped them from overflowing the land, Job? Gravitation? What is "gravity," Job? "Well, a portion of matter attracts other portions with a force directly proportional to the product of the two masses, and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them, because lines of force radiate so that even though the masses are only related to attracting bodies, and have no relation to chemical or physical conditions of the acting masses or intervening mediums\...."

Yes, Job, but what is GRAVITY?

"Well, the independence of gravitation of any quality but mass, bars the way to [any explanation of its NATURE or SOURCE." ]{.class_3761}(Thank you, Job, for loaning us the Encyclopedia Americana, Vol. 13, p. 164. It is amazing how much men have learned since you sat on the ash-heap). If the men who wrote the [Encyclopedia Americana ]{.class_3761}in 1969 couldn't answer the question, do you suppose Job could have? The ["proud waves"]{.class_3762} (vs. 11) go only to a certain point, and then they stop. A man said that it was because of the "gravitational pull of the moon." What is "gravitation," doctor? "Gravity is the linear acceleration downward of falling bodies due to the GRAVITATION of the earth." Okay. One more time, doc: [What is gravitation]{.class_3761}? \[Verse 9 is probably the cloud problem we mentioned under 36:29, which see. Bars and doors may not be as "figurative" as they look when one remembers the ["windows of heaven" ]{.class_3762}(Gen. 6) and the "lattice work" of Song of Solomon 2. Furthermore, a ["cloud"]{.class_3762} (vs. 9) is the garment of this ["sea"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8), and it wraps this sea up like swaddling clothes wrap up a new baby (vs. 9). The reference is probably to Gen. 1 again.

7\. ["Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days]{.class_3760}\...[?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). No, you haven't. No scientists, living or dead, ever told the sun what time to rise and when not to rise (see Exod. 10). No scientist could slow the course of the sun in the heavens as Joshua did (Josh. 10), and no scientist could delay the sun from rising a fraction of a second.

8\. Have you ["caused the dayspring to know his place; That it might take hold of the ends of the earth\..."]{.class_3760} (vss. 12 and 13)? The ["dayspring"]{.class_3760} in the passage is said to be ["turned as clay to the seal"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14), and ["they stand as a garment."]{.class_3760} The verse is so difficult that Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3759}) simply strikes out all of verse 14 and invents a reading that is not a paraphrase of any Hebrew manuscripts; like many of the readings in the ["Living"]{.class_3759}---saints preserve us---[Bible,]{.class_3759} it is a piece of imaginative fiction, inserted at the caprice of the author.

How can he ["take hold of the ends of the earth?"]{.class_3760} Well, from the divine viewpoint it was simple: The sun can do it, because the earth declines north and south between March 21st and December 22nd so that the sun's rays reach the Arctic and the Antarctic continents. Furthermore, the earth is like a [seal ]{.class_3759}turning against the sun, which represents [clay;]{.class_3759} a steel roller, rolling over wax on an envelope, will leave the imprint everywhere on the wax. Every inch of the steel roller is exposed to the wax, and, thus, every mile of this earth is exposed to the sun as it rotates in passing. Since none of the Bible readers for the last 3,000 years were able to believe this passage in Job, none of them believed Galileo (1546--1642) when he began to talk like a Bible believer instead of a Roman Catholic.

"[And they stand as a garment"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). The reference was to the ["ends of the earth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 13), and the ["ends,"]{.class_3762} in the context, are the northern and southern extremities. We should not be surprised, then, to find an Aurora Borealis standing ["as a garment"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14) at the ends of the earth. One can tone down the interpretation and say that as the earth turns against the sun, the spreading rays of the morning light make the things on the earth "stand out" like a beautiful garment draped on something. However, ["their light"]{.class_3762} (vs. 15) is a reference to the [dayspring ]{.class_3761}(vs. 12) and the [sun ]{.class_3761}(vs. 14). We are in exegetical trouble, and before we alter the text to match our stupidity (see the [Septuagint ]{.class_3761}here), let us notice the "broken arm" of verse 15. You see, the Antichrist has a bad RIGHT ARM (Zech. 11:17) because he is a Syrian Jew from Moab (Jer. 48:25) who gets the ["deadly wound"]{.class_3762} of Revelation 13 that puts out his RIGHT EYE (Zech. 11:17).

(Man, did you hear the howl of protest that went up just then from the faculty members of Moody Bible Institute, Fuller Theological Seminary, Dallas Theological Seminary, Bob Jones University, and Pensacola Christian College? Don't those dudes get upset when you find something deep in the Scripture that they couldn't find in the "original text"? But you can't really blame them. After all, they have been occupied (according to their own profession) in trying to show you revelation from the word of God, and being unable to do it---because God would show them nothing---they must feel pretty miserable to see "new light" continually coming on the Bible, [without any relationship to them or their books, studies, abilities, or talent]{.class_3759}.)

This matter is mentioned in Psalm 10:15; Jeremiah 48:25; Ezekiel 30:21--22, 24; and Zechariah 11:17. Furthermore, we slammed through verse 12 a little too quickly in view of the fact that the literal sun (Psa. 19:3--6) is a type of the bridegroom who is the "Sun of righteousness" (Mal. 4:1--3) at the Advent. After all, the "dayspring" (vs. 12) is ["from on high"]{.class_3760} in Luke 1:78, and He comes ["To give light to them that sit in darkness and in the SHADOW OF DEATH"]{.class_3760} (Luke 1:79). (Ahhh. There it is!) This explains the statement in the context (Job 38:13): ["That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the WICKED might be shaken out of it"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13). We are back on Second Advent passages again. The rising sun of our passage is the one found in Nahum 3:17, Psalm 83:11, and James 1:11. So we read, ["from the WICKED their light is withholden"]{.class_3760} (see Acts 9:4--7 for the mid-tribulation appearance of the Lord God to Israel).

9\. Can you withhold the sunlight from the wicked and bust their arms at the same time (vs. 15)? All of them? (Don't forget Job 40:12.)

38:16 Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?

17 Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?

18 Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.

19 Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof,

20 That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?

21 Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?

22 Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,

23 Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?

24 By what way is the light parted, which scattereth the east wind upon the earth?

25 Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;

26 To cause it to rain on the earth, where no man is; on the wilderness, wherein there is no man;

27 To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?

28 Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?

29 Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?

30 The waters are hid as with a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.

31 Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?

32 Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?

33 Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?

34 Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?

35 Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go and say unto thee, Here we are?

The greatest non-commercial quiz program ever presented for "week-day morning viewers" continues with no "jackpot prize," but only the right to repent and apologize. The "panel of experts" this time is one man, and, so far, he has missed nine out of nine. (Almost like watching the League of Nations and the United Nations, isn't it?)

10\. ["Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16). Several of Harry Rimmer's friends (see comments under 38:1--4) missed this one and insisted there were no springs of water in the ["sea,"]{.class_3760} but there are. It is true that they were not discovered, for certain, until after 1910, but when one is fickle and naive enough to follow "the latest scientific discoveries," he will always be 100 to 1,000 years behind Biblical knowledge.
