2\. [Gutbehomothmakakalaleka]{.class_3759}: German-Hebrew-Tagalog for "Good animal that takes it easy."

3\. [Akathartosaurus]{.class_3759}: Greek-Latin for "Unclean dinosaur."

4\. [Sehrrachabacaballo]{.class_3759}: German-Hebrew-Spanish for "The very large horse."

5\. [Eingrossekuova Avecquerenim]{.class_3759}: German-Greek-French-Hebrew for "A large dog with horns."

Make up your own list. Ph.D.'s these days are no competition, and M.A.'s wouldn't give a cockroach a run for his money. Make up your own list and then charge some teenage kid (that doesn't have any better sense) \$2,000 a semester to make him memorize the list. When the kid "graduates," he'll think he knows as much as Albert Einstein, when neither he nor Albert had the sense that God gave to a Welsh peat digger.

"[Will the UNICORN be willing to serve thee\...?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). You don't find many tame rhinoceroses (rhinoceri, rhinocerisis, rhinoceroses?) eating out of a crib (vs. 9) after plowing a field (vs. 10). If you left your work up to him (vs. 11), you might as well leave it to a wild water buffalo. People who count on a rhinoceros to plow their fields (vs. 10) don't "bring home" any seed, and they will gather nothing into the barn (vs. 12).

39:13 Gavest thou the goodly wings unto the peacocks? or wings and feathers unto the ostrich?

14 Which leaveth her eggs in the earth, and warmeth them in dust,

15 And forgetteth that the foot may crush them, or that the wild beast may break them.

16 She is hardened against her young ones, as though they were not her's: her labour is in vain without fear;

17 Because God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he imparted to her understanding.

18 What time she lifteth up herself on high, she scorneth the horse and his rider.

The peacock and the ostrich are included in one section, and since the commentators cannot see the similarity between the birds, they immediately alter ["peacocks"]{.class_3760} to "ostriches" (see any edition of the atrocious ASV recommended by Bob Jones University); and then, seeing that this alteration destroys the sense of the verse, they alter ["feathers" ]{.class_3760}into "hawk" or "pelican" (Adam Clarke, Ibid., p. 177). The ["Living"]{.class_3759}---Lord have mercy on Thy people!---[Bible]{.class_3759} says that the ostrich "flaps her wings grandly," while the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}(A.D. 300--500) has flown a stork in on the formation at verse 13. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(Lockman Foundation) has inserted "but are they the pinion and plummage" to the wreckage of verse 13, after altering the ["peacocks"]{.class_3760} to an "ostrich." The word for ["peacocks"]{.class_3760} in the Hebrew is a PLURAL; therefore, to reduce it to one ostrich ([ASV]{.class_3759}, [NASV, RSV, NRSV,]{.class_3759} and all other Communist and Catholic "Bibles") is fudging a little. But this gang of Bible-perverting, Bible-denying, Bible-rejecting, truth-despising Fundamentalists would never hesitate to translate a plural as a singular, while complaining in the same breath of the [AV ]{.class_3759}translators doing the same thing. Situation normal.

Since we have not mentioned the [New Scofield Reference "Bible"]{.class_3759} for some time, we might take note here that it has made twenty changes in the [Authorized Text ]{.class_3759}in the last three chapters while still professing to be "the Authorized Version." And who could fool with this kind of perversity without being a crook at heart to start with? The insertion of the "wild ox" at verse 10 for ["unicorn"]{.class_3760} (New Scofield Reference Bible) is quite typical of John Rice's "Fundamentalism," which bends over backwards to meet the demands of "modern scientific investigations" and "provable data." Some of these faculty members at Columbia, Maranatha, Mid-South, Wheaton, Fuller, and San Francisco are so afraid that scientists and "scholars" will think they are old fashioned bigots that they would sell your Bible down the river in ten seconds to prove they are "well read" and "highly knowledgeable." Wild oxen do NOT have one horn, and the Bible text said ["UNICORN."]{.class_3760} But as far as that goes, most of the rhinocerosi (see? ssis?) have two horns, one behind another. The lame alibi that this means only a "buffalo" or only a "rhinoceros" will not do at all, for the text says in Isaiah 34:7 that ["the unicorns shall come down with them."]{.class_3760} I wonder what Isaiah 34 is talking about? Do you suppose that in a passage which dealt with the Advent (vs. 2), the destruction of the Antichrist's armies (vs. 3), the catastrophic signs which accompany both (vs. 4) when the Lord goes from Edom (vs. 5) up the King's highway with His sword (vs. 6) and turns Edom into a Lake of fire (vv. 9--10) for the Millennium (Matt. 13:42), that the UNICORN (vs. 7) and the BULLOCK (vs. 7) are references to [four-legged animals?!]{.class_3759}

Wanna bet?

If the Bible speaks of a DRAGON (see Job 41:1 and comments) with TEN horns, who are you to put in a "wild ox" ([NSRB]{.class_3759}) simply because you never really believed the Book to start with? Is that the kind of "dedicated scholarship" we Christians are supposed to "respect"? Well, sonny, why don't you go out in the front yard and play in the traffic so we won't have to step on you every time we try to get some truth from God's word?

The peacock [does ]{.class_3761}have ["goodly wings"]{.class_3762} and the ostrich does NOT. All the ostrich has is ["wings and feathers"]{.class_3762} (vs. 13) exactly as the text states it. And the reason for mentioning both animals is obvious to any reader of the [Encyclopedia Britannica ]{.class_3761}(or [Americana]{.class_3761}), although it might be a little "obscure" to Dummelow, Clarke, Bullinger, Lange, Heavenor, Line, and the Lockman Foundation. You see, peacocks (as ostriches) lay their eggs in ["the dust"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14), and the peacock's ground nest, on the ground, is no better than the rampart of earth that surrounds an ostrich nest of twenty to fifty eggs. Furthermore, neither animal is a high flyer like the hawk and the eagle mentioned in the same chapter (vss. 26--27). God obviously has picked two extremes among our feathered friends to show us, again, the variety of His wisdom and power.

The peafowl (Genus Pavo from the Sub-family [Pavoninae]{.class_3759}) is called "PEACOCK" from the "eyes" on the tail coverts, which are the peacock's chief glory. A peacock, as a pheasant, can fly, but you normally do not think of flocks of peacocks "heading south for the winter." Neither peacocks nor ostriches major in flying.

"[Which leaveth her eggs in the earth, and warmeth them in dust"]{.class_3760} (vs. 14). All that follows is true. Since God has "deprived her of wisdom" (see comments under 38:36), ["she is hardened against her young ones as though they were not her's"]{.class_3762} (vs. 16). Most of the eggs an ostrich lays are stolen and eaten by animals, or they are crushed by heavy objects.

When an ostrich runs from a horseman her paces are twenty- five feet apart, and it often takes a relay of fresh horses to catch one. ["She scorneth the horse and his rider"]{.class_3760} (vss. 17--18). Xenophon says that Cyrus offered a thousand ducats or a hundred camels for a horse that could equal the speed of an ostrich. To make up for his inability to soar in the heavenlies, the ostrich majors in [sprinting]{.class_3759}, and to make up for his inability to compete with the swallows and the doves, the peacock struts a wardrobe that would make Liberace look like one of the world's "worst dressed men." Fausset's note that it should have been "goodly tail feathers" instead of ["wings"]{.class_3760} (if the correct text was on the peacock), and his subsequent alteration of the text to match the fanatics at Oxford, Westminster, and York (1800--1869), is not to be taken seriously. The words for "wing" in the Old Testament (Hebrew: [eber, ebrah, gaph, kanaph, ]{.class_3759}and [tsits]{.class_3759}) are the only words used to describe the appendages on a bird. The word for tail (Hebrew: [zanab]{.class_3759}) is never used for a bird, but only for snakes (Exod. 4:4), foxes (Judg. 15:4), Leviathan (Job 40:17), and scorpions (Rev. 9:10). Birds and fowls only have wings in the Bible since the tail is counted as a third wing, exactly as the horizontal stabilizer and the tail flaps on an airplane perform the same function as a wing. (When in doubt, you know what.)

But again, we are dealing with more than ostriches running around in Africa or peacocks flying around in India. Observe: ["her labour is in vain without fear"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16). Now how do you suppose 100 percent of the commentators failed to notice that this was also said of Satan in Job 41:9? ["The hope of him is in vain,"]{.class_3760} and he is ["made without fear"]{.class_3760} (Job 41:33)? In view of the fact that a woman who cares nothing about her offspring is mentioned in connection with the Devil (Rev. 17:1--9), and in view of the fact that 95 percent of the commentators called the ostrich a "stork" (see margin of [ASV]{.class_3759}, 1901), how did they miss the two STORK-WINGED WOMEN of Zechariah 5:9 who set Babylon up on her base (Zech 5:11) in preparation for Revelation 17:1--9? I mean, after all of this pious gas about "interpreting Scripture with Scripture" (see pamphlet published by the board of the [NSRB]{.class_3759}) and "stating clearly" what the Scriptures say (Ibid.), and then admitting that they modified the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text so that it would be "more accurate" (Ibid.) and in line with what they [thought ]{.class_3759}(!), how did Wilbur Smith, Walvoord, Feinberg, English, MacCrae, and company think for a minute that they were engaged in anything but destructive criticism of the word of God? And what is this ["horse and his rider" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 18) doing when we know that one ["horse and his rider"]{.class_3760} (Exod. 15:1) is the Antichrist and the other one is the [Lord Jesus Christ ]{.class_3759}(Rev. 19:11)? Why would any Christian in his sound mind (2 Tim. 1:7) consult Gesenius, Garstang, Wooley, Ramsay, Keil, Delitzsch, Harkavy, Peake, Budde, Duhm, or ANY of their friends for correct "interpretation," when the scriptures have already interpreted themselves independently of the "unquestioned scholarship" of any "change agent" disguised as a Christian "scholar?"

Now do you know how these Bible-perverting Christian agnostics get away with their devilment? It's really quite simple: When they got saved (if they are saved at all!), they got saved through reading (or the teaching or preaching of) a [King James Bible]{.class_3759}, and where there is an exceptional conversion through the preaching or reading of an [ASV ]{.class_3759}or an [RV ]{.class_3759}(or a [Douay Rheims ]{.class_3759}for that matter; you can find the plan of salvation in [any translation ]{.class_3759}if you plow through enough muck), the new convert never questioned the authority of the word until he was [taught ]{.class_3759}to by critics who were taught by critics, who were taught by critics, who learned from critics, who taught critics.

At the root of all Conservative scholarship in 1980---where it corrects the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text---is NOT the enlightening power of the Holy Spirit. To the contrary, it is the [PHILOSOPHICAL THEORIES ]{.class_3759}of unsaved British and German Deists (1700--1900). Now the saved scholars on these Conservative faculties know [some things ]{.class_3759}about the Bible that are true (who doesn't!?), but the amazing thing about this is that none of them ever know anything about the Bible that some other man didn't know BEFORE them, [who believed and accepted the AV (1611) as it stood]{.class_3759}. For example, the faculty members of Pensacola Christian College or San Francisco Theological Seminary or Clearwater College might be able to find the Virgin Birth; the Premillennial coming, the Bodily Resurrection, or the doctrine of Scriptural Inspiration from any Bible, but what saved sinner who believed the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}couldn't find the same things in it 100 to 300 years before the other English "bibles" show up? What was the purpose in putting out forty-five translations in "clearer language" to make the Scripture "easier to understand" when the men who translated them couldn't figure out [ANYTHING ]{.class_3759}that ANYONE couldn't figure out from the 1611 text!?

All Catholics and Episcopalians between 1200--1800 believed in the Virgin Birth and the Bodily Resurrection and the Last Judgment. There is nothing NEW or "revealing" about the last 500 works printed on these matters by Hebrew or Greek scholars. The "Fundamentals" were all known (and professed) in A.D. 325 before the Vatican manuscript ([ASV, NASV RSV, NRSV]{.class_3759}, etc.) was ever written. [Belief in the "fundamentals" never guaranteed that any Christian would be a Bible BELIEVER.]{.class_3759}

Now, since 325 A.D., the Lord has given His body a great deal of light from the Scripture, and the modern faculty can obtain this knowledge and make use of it, but the catch is this: The light which God gave on salvation by faith came through a Syrian Receptus Greek text and a rude, crude, nasty, negative Hell-raiser---Martin Luther. The light which God gave on holiness, He gave through the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}in the hand of a man who did not even believe in Eternal Security---John Wesley. The light which God gave on Premillennialism, He gave through Bible students who were studying [the English text of 1611]{.class_3759}---Cronin, Darby, and the Plymouth Brethren. The light God gave on Dispensationalism, He gave through Larkin, Pember, Scofield, and Stam. [No Greek text is necessary to follow the teaching of one man in the list]{.class_3759}. The light which God gave on the modern Sunday School and "visitation" came through Spurgeon and J. Frank Norris; neither man had to appeal to ANY Greek text to back up any teaching concerning either kind of work. And the light which God is now giving on the authority of the Authorized text (given to men like J.J. Ray, Hills, Fuller, Pickering, Bynum, Clarke, Cimino, Evans, and Bonam), He is withholding from the Hebrew and Greek professors of every Christian college and Christian university in the United States and Europe. [He does not need any Greek text to back the teaching of any man just listed]{.class_3759}. So what happens? A young sucker, who has just been saved and called to preach, is not quite mature enough in the Lord to recognize the presence of the "old man" in himself, still clamoring for "recognition." He is quietly ushered into a classroom of a "bastion of orthodoxy" that "stands without apology for the [absolute authority ]{.class_3759}of The Bible," and there he has the authority of God Almighty (in his own life!) knocked slap out of his hands by conmen who can pass off as Bible believers because [they have read the material we just listed]{.class_3759}.

Now that's the "lay of the land," boys and girls: If you had a dollar for every [advanced ]{.class_3759}revelation (that was TRUE) which God gave to a Greek or Hebrew scholar since 1700, you wouldn't have enough money to take a cab down to the corner store and back. Young men make a terrible mistake when they assume that because a professor has read Gordon, G. Campbell Morgan, Halley, Pember, Maxwell, Nee, Murray, Pink, Sauer, Larkin, Pieters, Darby, Stam, Bullinger, Sam Jones, Bob Jones, Frank Norris, Spurgeon, and Edward Hills, that God gave him this knowledge through understanding an original language; God never did. God never has, and He never will. The Author of scripture is the Revealer of scripture (Deut. 29:29) and the Preserver of Scripture (Psa. 12:6--7) and the Interpreter of Scripture (Luke 24:45); so, any fool with a grade school education can get more real truth from the Author of the Scriptures in five minutes than he can from any Greek or Hebrew lexicon published by anyone with fifty years of formal education behind them.

39:19 Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?

20 Canst thou make him afraid as a grasshopper? the glory of his nostrils is terrible.

21 He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in his strength: he goeth on to meet the armed men.

22 He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from the sword.

23 The quiver rattleth against him, the glittering spear and the shield.

24 He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage: neither believeth he that it is the sound of the trumpet.

25 He saith among the trumpets, Ha, ha; and he smelleth the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.

Now there follows a picture of the battle of Armageddon (see Matt. 24:28; Luke 17:37, and compare with Job 39:30,) where the ["horse and his rider"]{.class_3760} (see Exod. 15:1 and comments) meet with disaster (Rev. 19:11--18). This horse[ "paweth in the valley"]{.class_3760} (vs. 21), and ["the valley of "decision"]{.class_3760} (Joel 3:14) is so apparent in every line that only the faculty members who stopped with Larkin, Pember, Scofield, DeHaan, Weniger, Coynebeare, Ramsay, Luther, Calvin, Baxter, Kirban, Wuest, Thieme, Berkhof, Schaffer, and Unger could miss it.

The animal described is the famous war horse of a thousand battles. The description is that of a beast who is eager to get into the thick of the battle and is absolutely fearless in combat (vss. 21--24). The remarkable thing about the passage is its truthfulness, for the horse, by nature, is sensitive, skittish, shy, and nervous. The horse, trained for combat, assumes something of the dual nature found in German Shepherds; the German Shepherd is so sensitive that he will crouch and lower his ears if his master glares at him, and yet, he is so tough that he will run through machine gun fire to deliver a message (I've seen this---1944). A German Shepherd is so sensitive that his eyes will follow the slightest movement in a room from the opening of a book to the adjusting of a tie, but the same dog will stalk a burglar at night and leap for his throat and tear his jugular vein out. A horse that you can't get to leave the hay in the barn will take off like a runaway locomotive when he feels like it. Secretariat, Gallant Fox, Sea Biscuit, Whirlaway, and Twenty Grand---and don't forget old "Man O'War"!---didn't gallop: They flew.

The men who rode with Nathaniel Bedford Forrest (1821--1877) said that every charge was like a [steeple-chase. ]{.class_3759}Somehow or another, the horses don't seem to care any more about the consequences of the charge than the rider. The Death Valley riders of Balaclava (1854), the charge of the Scot Grays at Waterloo (1815), the annihilation of Ney's Calvary in the sunken ditch at Ohain (1815), the madcap raids of Jeb Stuart and Colonel Mosby (1862--1865), and the thundering tons of metal during the Hundred Years War (1338--1453) tell us again and again that the horse can thirst for killing, death, mutilation, and wounds exactly as a man like Richard the Lion-Hearted or Alexander the Great.

Every major battle fought on this earth from 2000 B.C. till World War I used cavalry, and in World War II Cossack riders were still charging their mounts blindly into automatic weapons fire. What possesses a horse to charge into a hail of arrows shot from 100-pound bows (Crecy, 1346) or steel darts fired from crossbows or kneeling pikemen with their weapons braced or counter-charging horses of other horsemen is a great mystery.

Mohammed had five horses out of 3,000 that turned to charge an enemy who had attacked his troops at a water hole. The thoroughbred Arabian steed is supposed to have been sired from those five horses. When the barbarian Celts overran western Europe (2500--1200 B.C.), they came on horseback. When the waves of Hittites and Elamites, Sumerians, and Chaldeans attacked each other (2500--1000 B.C.), they used the horse. When Thutmose III conquered Syria and part of Mesopotamia (1496 B.C.), the horse was there, right in the thick of it. When David and Joab went out to fight, they fought with horses, chariots, and cavalrymen (1050--1000 B.C.). When the Aryans invaded India and destroyed the Indus Valley (1500--500 B.C.), they came with their horses. When the legions of Rome and the armies of Alexander swept across Europe and Asia Minor like a scourge, they brought their cavalrymen with them (600--100 B.C.). When the Teutonic tribes invaded western Europe, and the Saxons, Thuringians, Slavs, Alemanni, Franks, Lombards, Goths, Visigoths, Vandals, and Huns were running all over each other (and everybody else), the horses were doing most of the running (A.D. 200--600). Cyrus, Artaxerxes, Xerxes, and Darius sported some of the finest cavalry in the world (550--330 B.C.), and time would fail to read the epitaphs of the bravest soldier of them all---the cavalry charger. As a Negro said when asked what the name of his horse was: "Ah doan really know, but we jus' calls him 'Jim'."

When the Crusaders rode them (A.D. 100--1200), they were so weighed down with armor they could hardly make ten miles an hour at top speed. When the Saracens rode them, they cut Europeans out of the herd like a quarter horse doing his job on the range. When the Germans marched them into battle, they put kettledrums on either side of their shoulders (see vs. 19) and wrote tunes for the horses by which they could canter, trot, and gallop. In the classic charge before World War I, the trooper lay down on the mane of the horse and thrust a saber straight over the horse's head. When he hit the enemy lines, he went in like a 1,200 pound guided missile, at thirty-five miles an hour---iron hoofs, steel stirrups, and blade. Horses have completed a charge when their rider had been unhorsed, or shot off, a mile back in the charge. When they finally captured Field Marshall Ney (Waterloo, June 18, 1815), he had already had four horses shot out from under him, and he was riding the fifth one, with saber slashes all over his own boots, gun powder all over his face, and one eye put out completely. Rising in the sitrrups of that noble animal, the "bravest of the brave" roared, "Come and see how a Field Marshall of France can die on the battlefield!" (["he saith among the trumpets, Ha, ha\...!"]{.class_3762})

Many years after Waterloo the people who lived near the stables of the Duke of Wellington were treated to an unusual (but regular) sight; every time a thunderstorm came up, the retired war horses in the pasture would group automatically and wheel into line, pawing the ground (vs. 21) and shaking their manes (vs. 19). Like Eleazer, the son of Dodo the Ahohite (2 Sam. 23:10), they had been in the battle for so long, and their discipline was so exact, that their "hands cleaved to the sword." If those horses had been flat on their sides, crippled, and dying, they would have tried to kick themselves to their feet.

Well, God gave the horse strength (vs. 19), so you still measure power today by "horse power!" (Whether or not you believe the [AV ]{.class_3759}Bible at this point is immaterial. The [AV ]{.class_3759}will set the pace, and you will keep up by the grace of God,[ if you are able]{.class_3759}.) The neck of the horse is ["clothed\...with thunder,"]{.class_3760} not only in the poetic sense (see any shallow commentator), but by the scientific, absolute, infallible, literal, provable FACT that some horses are FIRE (2 Kings 2:11, 6:17), and literal THUNDER attends the thunder of their hoofs (Rev. 10:3--7). (Yes, we know some of you can't believe that, but after four years of "higher" Christian education, how could some of you ever believe ANYTHING about the Bible again that wasn't given to you on a silver spoon by a wet nurse?)

The substitution of "locust" for ["grasshopper"]{.class_3760} (see any atrocious corruption since 1800---[ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV,]{.class_3759} etc.) is gaffing the act again. The faculties at Pensacola Christian College and BJU, who kidded you into thinking that ["arbeh"]{.class_3759} (Heb.) always meant "locust," took for granted that you wouldn't check them out anyway---so, we did: The [Arbeh]{.class_3759}, here, is "specially of a particular kind, probably the GRYLLUS GREGARIOUS of Leviticus 11:22" (Delitzsch, p. 75). Well, the ["gryluss gregarious"]{.class_3759} of Leviticus 11:22 is an ["chagab"]{.class_3759} (Heb.)---a GRASSHOPPER. \[See exhaustive comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on the Minor Prophets]{.class_3759}, Joel 1:4.\] You see, in all of this we are to assume that the poor, dumb, stupid [AV ]{.class_3759}translators did not have the "benefit of recent archaeological discoveries" (see puff sheet for the [RV]{.class_3759} or the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3759}: Same crew, same regatta), so you are to discard their writings, and, instead, accept the "unquestioned scholarship" of "godly men whose opinions are to be respected for their\...." (See any of the baloney printed by John R. Rice.)

The idea in the verse is: "Can you [scare ]{.class_3759}the horse so that he will jump like a grasshopper?" Well, a horse will jump like that when something rolls between his legs. He will also jump like that at a flying piece of paper or a leaf or at the sound of a rattlesnake, but you will not make a war horse jump like a grasshopper by [threatening him]{.class_3759}. He will jump when he is [told ]{.class_3759}to jump, and [where ]{.class_3759}he is told to jump, at the command of his trainer. ["The glory of his nostrils is terrible"]{.class_3760} (vs. 20), especially if they are breathing FIRE (2 Kings 2:11). (Virgil counterfeited this expression in one of his pagan, mythological works by saying, "and in his nostrils rolls collected fire"---cited by Clarke, p. 179.)

Verses 21--24 are self-explanatory. ["The sound of the trumpet"]{.class_3760} (vs. 24)---again points forward to the trumpet of Numbers 10:1--9 and Revelation 11:15. The "horse laugh" of verse 25 is just as "living" today as it was when it was penned in 1611, for, after all, the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}always sets the pace for any translation. The battle can be "smelled" (vs. 25) by its blood (Isa. 9:5) or its engines (Isa. 9:5) or its smoke (Josh. 8:20). ["The thunder of the captains"]{.class_3760} (vs. 25) has been heard on every battlefield since Cain killed Abel, and to this thunder has been added the thunder of muskets, muzzle loaders, cannons, artillery pieces, buzz bombs, rockets, land mines, grenades, bangalore torpedoes, tank guns, mortars, pistols, automatic rifles, rifles, machine guns, and machine pistols. A horse will still charge right through the whole mess if his rider drives him that way!

39:26 Doth the hawk fly by thy wisdom, and stretch her wings toward the south?

27 Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make her nest on high?

28 She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, upon the crag of the rock, and the strong place.

29 From thence she seeketh the prey, and her eyes behold afar off.

30 Her young ones also suck up blood: and where the slain are, there is she.

The hawk does not fly by ["THY wisdom,"]{.class_3760} for she flies by God's wisdom. She stretches out her wings and flies ["towards the south"]{.class_3760} (vs. 26) in the autumn, for the hawk (as the stork, crane and swallow: Jer. 8:7) knows her ["appointed times."]{.class_3760}

"[Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make her nest on high?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 27). Not normally. You can [train ]{.class_3761}falcons and eagles to mount up on command if you have brought up the bird in captivity and chained it, but they don't do it naturally. ["She dwelleth and abideth on the ROCK, upon the crag of the ROCK"]{.class_3762} (vs. 28). With Selah, Petra, in the land of Uz, right before their faces (in the Hebrew text), one hundred percent of the Conservative and Fundamental scholars sail right by the Second Advent reference like they were lost in a fog. Adam Clarke is so anxious to erase any references to the Second Advent\
that he cites Matthew 24:28 and then says quickly, "It is likely\...that this was a PROVERBIAL MODE of expression\...."

Well, it is a "mode of expression" at least 30,000 times more accurate, historically, than any of the men who taught Adam Clarke. (Revelation 19:17 and 18 is the fulfillment of Job 39:29 and 30. Clarke missed the reference (as did Matthew Henry and Dummelow) because he followed the private interpretation of the Roman Catholic Popes---[Postmillennialism]{.class_3759}.)

The word ["sela"]{.class_3759} (Heb.) is the ["Rock,"]{.class_3760} as found in Psalms 18:2, 31:3, 40:2, 42:9, 71:3, and 78:16. (Notice the ["Rock"]{.class_3760} in Exod. 17:6 and 1 Cor. 10:4.) The word indicates the name of a city in Edom, called ["Petra."]{.class_3759} It is in the valley of Wady Musi between the Dead Sea and the Gulf of Aquaba, and it was called ["Joktheel"]{.class_3760} by Amaziah after 822 B.C. "Sela" is in Uz, where Job lived, and it is to this place ([Sela Hammehlekoth]{.class_3759}: "the rock of escapes or separations") that Israel will "fly" (Matt. 24:20) in the Tribulation (Rev. 12:14).

The musical notation ["Selah"]{.class_3760} in the Psalms (Psa. 49--50, 52, 55, 57, 59--60, 62, 67--68, 75--77, 81--82, etc.) is simply the substitution of ["He"]{.class_3759} for ["Ayin"]{.class_3759} (Heb.) in the last radical of "Sela." Wherever this word (Selah) occurs in the Psalms, there will be found a reference to the Second Advent of Christ within four verses of it. \[No, don't run to Delitzsch, Keil, Gesenius, Driver, Ewald, Briggs, Davidson, Bathgen, Duhm, Davies, Kay, Kimchi, Oehler, Nowack, Hengstenberg, or Kittel for help: They are helpless. Where the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text of the Protestant reformation is rejected for the lifetime researches and studies of post reformation CRITICS, God lowers the blinds, turns out the lights, shuts off the gas, closes down the store, and lets the pump lose its prime.

If 20,000 born-again, "dedicated, godly scholars" with an accumulated backlog of 200,000 years of education (in Christian institutions), could not locate Job 39:26--30 [prophetically ]{.class_3759}and would deny that YOU could because of your "lack of education," they would be just as [wrong ]{.class_3759}and just as [wicked ]{.class_3759}as any 20,000 Christ-rejecting Atheists in the Soviet Union. When God blinds the Conservative (or Orthodox) scholar because that scholar has rejected Revelation, that scholar has no business telling you (or anyone else) anything about the word of God. [You will find that anything he knows is already in print by a man who believed the word of God]{.class_3759}, and what he doesn't know, he cannot find out, for he has rejected the source of revealed truth.)

The eagle and the hawk of Job (vss. 26, 27) are present at Armageddon, flying from Selah, Petra, in Edom. ["Where the slain are, there is she"]{.class_3760} (vs. 30). The eagle does not normally feed her young with carrion; so she takes her "young 'uns" (vs. 30) with her, and they suck the blood out of the [wounded ]{.class_3759}and [dying ]{.class_3759}of the United Nations World Liberation Army (Rev. 19:17--18) which was "dedicated to the preservation of humanity and the freedom of all men everywhere, etc., etc." The hawk and the eagle here are females, as are the bloodsucking daughters of the LEECH (see Prov. 30:15 and comments). Therefore, the ostrich and the peacock (with the hawk and the eagle and the horse) are all indirect references to things present at the Second Advent and the outcome of the battle of Armageddon. The words are spoken to Job by the Author of Scripture; they are recorded without error by Elihu, and they are preserved to this day as God intended for you to read them. If the "majority of Conservative change agents" don't like it, they can go chew oats.

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw3432.xhtml}

CHAPTER 40

40:1 Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said,

2 Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.

3 Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

4 Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.

5 Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.

You can well imagine the state that Job is in by now; he has been asked more than forty questions, and he couldn't get an answer in edgewise. Now the Lord says to him: ["Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2). Job's answer is the only answer a man could give under these conditions, and this answer will pop up so many times at the White Throne Judgment you will think that 50,000,000,000 people got "Xeroxed."

"[Behold I am vile\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). That's it, Job. That's the ticket. There is nothing like [negative thinking ]{.class_3761}to get the true picture. Never mind Kenneth Taylor and all of the Campfire Girls trying to tone down your vileness to "I am nothing." Never mind the [ASV ]{.class_3761}and the girlies at BJU trying to slip you through with "I am of small account." Never mind the [NEB ]{.class_3761}and the freaks of the "Gay Liberators" saying that you "carry no weight." Just sack the whole bunch of effeminate apostates into one freezer bag. Never mind the [NASV ]{.class_3761}fixing up Philippians so that nothing comes out ["vile."]{.class_3762} Never mind the [RSV ]{.class_3761}doing the same thing, to set the pace for the faculty at Bob Jones University. Just stack the whole pile of rotten junk in one garbage can and tell the Disposal Unit to put it back where God left the Septuagint of A.D. 350 and the [ASV ]{.class_3761}of 1901: In Gehenna, the city dump.

"[What shall I answer thee?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). A man can't answer one out of a thousand (Job 9:3), and God has only put about forty-five on Job so far. ["I will not answer\...I will proceed no further"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). And that is how it always turns out.

When Isaiah hits the Lord head-on, he suddenly begins to holler about [his own ]{.class_3759}unclean lips instead of[ God's]{.class_3759} mistakes. When Peter sideswipes the Lord (Luke 5:8), he suddenly decides that ["two cannot walk together except they be agreed"]{.class_3760} (John 6:68); so Peter decides that the trouble is with [himself]{.class_3759}. When Daniel bumps into the Angel of the Lord, he falls flat on his face, and when Moses gets his fuel injection (Exod. 33:18), he is stumbling all over himself to get on the right side (Exod. 34:9). You don't alter ["vile"]{.class_3760} to "nothing" or "small account" when you've had a personal encounter with God Almighty. That stuff is for psychopathic amateurs who think that the Alpha and Omega was an "astronaut" in a "flying saucer." If you ever get the glimpse that Moses, David (Psa. 51:5), Paul (Acts 9:5, 6), Isaiah, or Job got, you will settle for ["vile"]{.class_3760} and be thankful you didn't have to "update" the word to ["CURSED"]{.class_3760} (Matt. 25:41). Job is through talking. He has become one of the most interested [listeners ]{.class_3759}in the entire Bible; so the Lord proceeds:

40:6 Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

7 Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

8 Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?

9 Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?

10 Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.

11 Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.

12 Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.

13 Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.

14 Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.

Now Job's real trouble is brought forward and presented by the One who ["searcheth all hearts"]{.class_3760} and ["understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts"]{.class_3760} (1 Chron. 28:9). The master psychiatrist, whose x-ray vision pierces heaven, earth, and hell (Psa. 139:1--2), gives His comment on Job's "sins" (Heb. 4:12--13); and for fifty-two verses Eliphaz, Bildad, Zophar, and Elihu have no further contributions to make. ["The Judge of all the earth"]{.class_3760} (Gen. 18:25), ["who will render to every man according to his deeds"]{.class_3760} (Rom. 2:6), always judges ["according to truth"]{.class_3760} (Rom. 2:2), and Job's fig leaf (see Chapter 31) has no more covered his self-righteousness than the original model (Adam's "apron") covered Adam's nakedness.

"[Gird up thy loins now like a man\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 7); like a man about to enter combat (Eph. 6:10; 1 Kings 20:11) or a man called upon to do a hard, dirty task (Jer. 1:17; John 13:4). Job has challenged God (see Rom. 9:20); so God now challenges Job. He puts four questions to Job that no man on earth could answer in the [affirmative]{.class_3761}, and whether that man was a contemporary of Job (1800 B.C.) or a contemporary of Rockefeller (A.D. 1980), he could not answer one of the questions with a "Yea."

"[Wilt thou also disannul my judgment\...?"]{.class_3760}

"[Wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?"]{.class_3760}

Well, the Pharisees tried it (Matt. 22:15), Pilate and Herod tried it (John 19:9), but can it really be done? Could any sinner, of any degree of attainment, overrule God in a contest of "judging right and wrong"? ("Values Clarification" if you are an unregenerate socialist.) Unable even to detect the ["proud"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11), how on this earth could any man ["abase"]{.class_3760} them (vs. 11) and ["bring him low"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12)? How do you ["tread down the wicked in their place"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12) when you can't even find them, let alone their "places?" If the FBI (1930), the CIA (1970), the Gestapo (1940), the NKVD (1950), and the secret police (1960) never got all of them located, how can YOU do it? God can do it. And the Lord Jesus Christ (Matt. 23:12; Luke 14:11) states that it will be done as smoothly and as naturally as the law of gravity. (See lengthy comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_3759}, Prov. 15:3.)

Job's problem is [self-righteousness ]{.class_3759}(see Rom. 10:1--4).

Every sinner who ever pitted the Golden Rule or the Ten Commandments against Christ's finished Blood Atonement should know something of Job's "problem." Every Catholic, Charismatic, Communist, and Campbellite who fled to James 2:24 or the Second Commandment (in order to reject the grace of God) should understand Job's position. Every sinner who ever tried to sneak into New Jerusalem through Acts 2:38 and the "Sermon the Mount" should know what is going on; Job is righteous, but he is SELF-RIGHTEOUS. This is the righteousness which the Holy Spirit calls ["filthy rags"]{.class_3760} in Isaiah 64:6. Paul called it religious ["dung,"]{.class_3760} even though the [NASV ]{.class_3759}(recommended by Bob Jones University) fixed the word up so that it wouldn't hurt its "testimony" too much.

Job is a perfect picture of: The sinner in Hell, the Jew in the Tribulation, Christ suffering the wrath of God, and (finally) the living sinner himself, covered with the filthy rags of his own self-righteousness, talking about "living it," and "doing good to his fellowmen." Job is the President of the United States, the United Nations, and the Soviet Union; and he is pope, priest, king, and "chairman" of every major nation in the world. All Communists, Catholics, and Capitalists are counting on their "lives" to justify their deeds.

"[Hast thou an arm like God?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). Well, if you cannot turn dust into lice, water into blood, seas into dry land, and kill all the firstborn in a country in one night (see Exod. 6:6, and 15:6), you aren't "armed" properly. The "stretched out" arm of God (Deut 4:34, 5:15) stretches out so far that it becomes an arm of flesh (John 12:38) [on this earth ]{.class_3761}(Luke 1:51), and then it heals sick people, casts out demons, gives sight to the blind, speech to the dumb, hearing to the deaf, power to the impotent, and then tops the performance off by hauling dead people out of caskets (Luke 7:14) and cemeteries (John 11).

"[Hast thou an arm like God?"]{.class_3760}

Science, philosophy, education, and religion---the great tragic buffoons of history---do not have an arm like God. The 500 greatest minds who ever lived on this earth could not do (with their combined labors and discoveries), in 4,000 years what the "arm of God" (John 12) accomplished in one year of His earthly ministry.

"[Canst thou thunder with a VOICE like him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). Of course not; not even the manmade thunder of a Cobalt bomb or a four-megaton "firecracker" can match the thunder of God; for the thunder of God says something (see 37:1--5), and the greatest linguist in the world cannot decipher that celestial code (Rev. 10) apart from the direct revelation of the Holy Spirit (Job 26:14).

"[Deck thyself now with majesty\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 10). What a thing to say to a sick man on an [ash heap ]{.class_3761}who is covered with [running sores ]{.class_3761}and [worms ]{.class_3761}(Job 1--2). ["Array thyself with glory and beauty"]{.class_3762} (vs. 10). Talk about sarcasm. Talk about sheer meanness and "rubbing it in." If Job thought that his three friends were hard on him, he changed his mind about here.

What a contrast there is between the most righteous man on earth (in this case, Job) and the man Christ Jesus (1 Tim. 2:1--10). Arrayed in garments that smell of myrrh, aloes, and cassia, annointed with the ["oil of gladness" ]{.class_3760}above His fellows, the chiefest of ten thousand is altogether ["lovely"]{.class_3760} (Song of Sol. 6; Psa. 45); and in His preincarnate glory which He had before the world began (John 17:24), He was pavilioned in splendor and girded with praise. On creation's morning He put on the universe like a king dons his robes (Heb. 1:12) and decked Himself not only with [Majesty ]{.class_3759}(vs. 10) and [Strength ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 93:1) but with [Honor ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 104:1) and the [Light ]{.class_3759}(Psa. 104:2). The universe is a demonstration of God's ability as a tailor, and before he folds it up as a ["garment"]{.class_3760} (Heb. 1:12), He will return to this battleground clothed with a garment and a vesture dipped in blood (Rev. 19:13), annihilate His enemies, and then sit down in BEAUTY (Zech. 11:7, 10) on the throne of His GLORY (Matt. 25:31).

What a challenge for a worm-covered sinner!

"Clothe yourself!"

You can't do it. Every stitch of clothing you have on this minute came from a plant or an animal. [No man can grow his own clothes]{.class_3759}. Education, social image, political status, worldly honor, social recognition, income, and I.Q. have no bearing on the problem at all; [no man can grow his own clothing]{.class_3759}. Solomon (in all his glory) could not produce what a lily of the field could produce (Matt. 6:28, 29), and imagine a self- righteous sinner like YOU, for example (or any of your acquaintances), thinking for a minute that you are going to compete with God when it comes to glory, beauty, and righteousness!

"[Every one that is proud, and abase him"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). (See Dan. 4:37 and the comments under Prov. 16:18 and 19:23.) ["Bring him low,"]{.class_3762} like God brought Nebuchadnezzar when he was brought to his knees on the front lawn. ["Bind their faces,"]{.class_3762} like Lazarus' face was bound with bandages when they ["wound him up"]{.class_3762} (see Acts 5:6). ["Hide them in the dust together,"]{.class_3762} like Moses slew the Egyptian overseer and hid him in the sand. ["THEN will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee"]{.class_3762} (vs. 14).

"[Salvation is of the LORD"]{.class_3760} and ["Salvation is of the Jews"]{.class_3762} (Jonah 2:9; John 4:22); it has no source in a Gentile "do-gooder" trying to batter down the gates of New Jerusalem with Ecumenical Socialism and a "golden rule" or

["Living" Bible]{.class_3761}. All you have to do in this age to gain heaven by works is keep Deuteronomy and Leviticus to the letter, worship at Jerusalem, [live a sinless life]{.class_3761}, die for every other sinner in the world, and then [pull yourself up out of the grave by your bootstraps ]{.class_3761}three days after they bury you: Simple, isn't it? And to hear some of you Charismatics, Communists, and Catholics talk, one would surmise that you thought you could pull it off without a hitch. Until you do, don't be surprised if some of us Bible believing people fail to take you seriously when you preach about your "religion" or "sharing your experience." You have your right to be heard, but not to be taken seriously by anyone who knows the word of God.

Now it would seem to be a waste of time, at this point, to backtrack in our exposition and pick up the "majority of qualified scholars" whose "unquestioned loyalty to the Bible, etc., etc.," equipped them to "so forth and so on." These Madison Avenue quacks, whose eternal theme song is "[recognized ]{.class_3759}scholarship," "[godly]{.class_3759}, dedicated scholars," "[erudite ]{.class_3759}scholarship," and "[unquestioned ]{.class_3759}loyalty," remind us of the so-called "infallible" bishops of Rome when it comes to "loyalty" to the Bible. We not only question their "loyalty," we question their scholarship, their sources, their motives for attacking the [AV]{.class_3759}, and their penchant for open, deliberate, and continual LYING. Just because a saved sinner slips into the flock talking about the "Virgin Birth" and the "verbal inspiration of the originals," it doesn't mean that he has any calling (or any right) to talk about "loyalty to the Bible." After all, the Devil never attacked either of the above doctrines when he first spoke: When he first spoke, he questioned something that God had SAID. [He questioned what God said to someone who had heard Him say it. ]{.class_3759}There was no discussion of some fundamental that Neo-evangelicals or "Liberals" were attacking; no, that's just the straw dummy that modern Fundamentalists erect to cover their own hatred for the AV's authority. There was no discussion over "how" the originals were "inspired." Satan is a [realist]{.class_3759}, and he is no more concerned about unavailable "original" manuscripts than he is about last year's soybean crop.

When we speak about the "unquestioned loyalty" of Robertson, Trench, Thayer, Vincent, Gregory, Farrar, Cheyne, Sanday, Driver, Lightfoot, Ellicott, Hort, Westcott, Thieme, Wuest, Rendall, Salmon, Machen, Warfield, Nestle, Aland, and Metzger to the "word of God," we are talking about the [cover-up work ]{.class_3759}of the meanest bunch of [destructive critics ]{.class_3759}the world has even seen; no atheist or papist could step into their shoes for twenty-four hours. And this is evident by the fact that neither Rome nor Communism were able to replace the [Authorized Version]{.class_3759}; Christian scholarship did it (A.D. 1880-1980). We not only "question" the scholarship of these egotistical upstarts (and their "loyalty" to the word), we also question their sincerity, their methods, their attitude, and their duplicity through 100 years of deliberate falsification of the truth and intentional substitution of their bungling researches for the authority of the living words of the living God. As Billy Sunday so aptly put it: "Where the Bible says one thing and scholarship says another, scholarship can go plumb to the devil." Exactly.

1\. The archaic [ASV ]{.class_3761}of 1901---recommended for fifty years by every Fundamental scholar in the country---has written "cavilleth" for ["contendeth"]{.class_3762} in verse 2. The Hebrew word (["yakach"]{.class_3761}), translated as ["reproveth"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2), is a Hiphil, singular, masculine participle. It has been mistranslated as "argueth" by the clumsy, infidelic revisors of the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901), whose "unquestioned loyalty to the word of God, etc.," is about as unquestioned as Genesis 3:1. The bungling [ASV ]{.class_3761}committee (all composed of orthodox Conservatives) translated the same word as ["reprove"]{.class_3762} in Job 6:25, 26 and 13:10 and then as "rebuke" in 2 Kings 19:4 and then as "judge" in Isaiah 11:3. (You say: "But the translators thought it best to bring out the sense of\..." Sure, kid, we know. So did the translators in 1611. They just had God's blessing on them, and the 1901 committee didn't.)

Now we would not make so much of these matters if these blind critics ("recognized scholars") had kept their yaps shut in regard to the [Authorized ]{.class_3759}text, but if there is one main thing these inconsistent hypocrites yapped about from 1800--1900, it was the "lack of uniform translation" of the Hebrew and Greek words in the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611), wasn't it? Yes, it was. Well, how does the kettle call the pot "black" when the kettle ([NASV]{.class_3759}, for example) translated articles where there were none in any manuscript (see 1 Pet. 1--2), translated the same Greek word three different ways (see Acts 1, 14), left out articles that were in all the texts (see Luke 4--6), and then invented words that are found in NO Greek text (1 Cor. 13)?

May we humbly suggest that until Johnson, Gleason, Weniger, Custer, McClain, Panosian, Cheyne, Olson, Lehman, Kantzger, Binton, Anderson, Culbertson, Baxter, Smith, Muntz, Wedge, Coder, Feinberg, Robertson, Wuest, and English know what they are talking about, they would do well to keep their mouths shut?

2\. The [NEB ]{.class_3759}(Dodd Version) inserts "to be stubborn" into verse 1, which is found in no Hebrew text. Then it tones the ["vile"]{.class_3760} down to "carry no weight" (vs. 4) and then coyly removes the Biblical terms ["condemn," "righteous"]{.class_3760} and ["judgment"]{.class_3760} from verse 8.

3\. The scholars Budde and Duhm don't like verses 1--12, so they make twelve changes in as many verses.

4\. The [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}naturally adds over forty words to the passage.

5\. Bullinger offers a "new metrical version" to replace the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611).

6\. The Jehovah's Witnesses follow the Arian Bible of 1901 ([ASV]{.class_3759}) and tone Job's "vileness" (vs. 4) down to where he can appear almost respectable, even if he cannot ["array"]{.class_3760} himself with ["glory and beauty."]{.class_3760} The words ["majesty"]{.class_3760} and ["excellency"]{.class_3760} have been run off the boards and replaced with "superiority and highness," which is a very crummy substitution.

7\. Since the Hebrew word for ["answered"]{.class_3760} has been used in verse 1 (["annah"]{.class_3759}), Adam Clarke decides the whole passage has been [misplaced]{.class_3759}; so he tells us that it should be on the end of the Book of Job, [following Job 41]{.class_3759}. To perform this double amputation on the [AV ]{.class_3759}text, Dr. Clarke (whose "loyalty to the word of God is unquestioned, etc., etc.") cites a certain Dr. Heath briefly: Dr. Heath says we are to subjoin the fifteenth verse of the forty-second chapter to the end of the thirty-ninth chapter and then place the first fourteen verses of the fortieth chapter after the sixth verse of the forty-second chapter, and things will be "hunky-dory." But why stop now with such "unquestioned loyalty to the word of God?" Why not place the first fifteen verses of Chapter 20 after the last ten verses of Chapter 30 and then take the middle five verses of Chapter 4 and place them after the first ten verses of Chapter 22 and then\...! I mean if our "loyalty" is "unquestioned," what is to prevent us from just tearing the grand old Book all to pieces and reassembling it after any fashion and make it our own book? Who would "question" our "loyalty" if we were "recognized"?

8\. A. R. Fausset (Commentary, p. 98) says that "answer" (vs. 5) should be "not plead against." (The Hebrew word "anah" means "answer" in Gen. 18:27; 23:10; 31:14, 36; 40:18; 45:3, Exod. 4:1; 15:21; 19:8, Num. 11:28; 22:18; 32:31, Deut. 1:14, 41; 21:7, Josh. 1:16; 7:20, 1 Sam. 1:15, 17; 9:8, 12, 19, 21, and the other 95 passages.)

9\. The ["Living" Bible]{.class_3759} (Taylor's Version) makes a number of changes which are not worth discussing.

But Job 40:1-4 doesn't really test the metal of our destructive critics (Fundamentalists foremost), nor does it reveal their true colors. If one really wants to see the unholy apostates leading the stumbling parade of blasphemous nonsense (from Origen to Custer), one must read what now follows:

40:15 Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.

16 Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.

17 He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.

18 His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.

19 He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.

20 Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.

21 He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.

22 The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.

23 Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.

24 He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.

Now students of Manuscript Evidence, who have given careful attention to the works of Burgon, Miller, Hills, Clarke, Scrivener, Mauro, Miller, and the Trinitarian Bible Society of London, are familiar with the "apostolic succession" of blunders that proceed automatically from one Christian college (or university) to another one. By taking a man's word for something without checking it---especially if that man is a "godly" man whose scholarship is "you know what"---an accumulation of errors has been incorporated into Christian education and Conservative scholarship with such zeal and earnestness that when one tries to shed light on them he finds himself the target of every type of villification and misrepresentation known to Christendom. The [New International Version ]{.class_3759}and the [NASV ]{.class_3759}both represent the accumulation of nineteen centuries of pure, unadulterated tripe.

At Job 40:15, the Conservative and Fundamental scholars (independent, premillennial Baptists included) take the word of the unsaved commentators to be superior to the word of God, and [without checking ANY Hebrew text ]{.class_3759}(which they talk about so much), they blandly tell us that the ["behemoth"]{.class_3760} of the passage is a "crocodile" or a "hippopotamus" (see the [NASV ]{.class_3759}or the [NSRB]{.class_3759}, and match them up with the worst liberal translation you can find: They are in agreement). Not even the Jehovah's Witnesses made that gross of an error (see [NWT]{.class_3759}).

Dummelow says that behemoth is an [elephant]{.class_3759}. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}says that it is a hippopotamus (along with the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}). Fausset says that behemoth is the "water ox." George Williams, speaking for Cornelius Stam and the [New English Bible]{.class_3759}, says that the behemoth is a crocodile and that the "obscurities" in the [AV ]{.class_3759}can be cleared up with the [RV ]{.class_3759}(1881--1884). (The [RV ]{.class_3759}of 1881-1884 was the most corrupt piece of apostate trash that ever cursed the Body of Christ; it made 36,000 changes in the correct text, and the text it finally used was an unpublished text smuggled into the revision committee by two men who never even professed to believe in the verbal inspiration of ANY text \[see [True or False?]{.class_3759} by David Otis Fuller\].)

The [Wycliffe Commentary ]{.class_3759}(as tactfully and as politely as an English dandy) quietly dismisses the correct identification of the animal as "this MYTHICAL interpretation" (Meredith Kline). Recognize the footprints? [Mythology?]{.class_3759} Legend? (Evidently an Orthodox Conservative can get his education off of the same assembly line that produced Astruc, Juene, DeWette, Graf, Wellhausen, and Strauss.)

But the ["behemoth"]{.class_3760} of our text is no more a mythical monster (or a crocodile or hippopotamus or elephant) than Lassie.

"[Behemoth"]{.class_3760} is God's own scientific description of the beast of Revelation chapter 13 and Daniel chapter 7---the Antichrist; and no amount of knowledge of "original languages" would enable any scholar (Liberal, Orthodox, or Fundamentalist) to find either reference, in nineteen centuries of study, from [any translation of any manuscript]{.class_3761}, if that scholar had set himself up as a competent ("qualified") judge of God's Book. Robertson could not find the cross references in [Sinaiticus]{.class_3761}; Gregory could not find them in the [Septuagint]{.class_3761}; the [ASV ]{.class_3761}(1901) committee could not find them in [Nestle's Text]{.class_3761} and Driver, Delitzsch, Gesenius, and Keil (Hebrew scholars) could not find them in any [Hebrew ]{.class_3761}text, although Job was written in [Hebrew]{.class_3761}. And if all the faculty members of every Christian college and university in America and Europe had the "God-breathed originals" in front of their faces, they could no more locate ["behemoth"]{.class_3762} than they could locate the source of their blindness and confusion.

[Linguistic ability ]{.class_3759}never enabled any sinner (saved or lost) to understand the Bible, where that sinner thought he was smart enough to correct God Almighty.

The Hebrew word here is the feminine plural of ["bahemah,"]{.class_3759} for "beasts" or "cattle." ["Behemoth"]{.class_3760} is a PLURAL animal: "animals." Three ANIMALS that make up this "animal" (sing.) are given in Daniel chapter 7 and Revelation chapter 13. And to the everlasting shame and confounding of the faculties at Bob Jones, Piedmont, Northwestern, Mid-South, Pillsbury, Northeastern, Columbia, Lynchburg, Wheaton, Hyles-Anderson, BIOLA, and San Francisco, let it be noted that not one Greek or Hebrew scholar (living or dead), in 100 years of attacking the [AV ]{.class_3759}text, was ever able to locate this simple, primary, elementary, prophetic truth. When we say that Origen could not read [third grade Greek ]{.class_3759}in his own [native tongue]{.class_3759}, we are not "overstating" anything. There are 500,000 Greek-speaking, Greek preachers on earth, [today]{.class_3759}, who can no more understand Revelation 11--13 than they can skin themselves alive with a marshmallow.

God likened His Son to a LAMB; He likens the counterfeit of His Son to a LEOPARD, but it is a LION---BEAR---LEOPARD with seven heads (Rev. 13:2). It is ["behemoth"]{.class_3760} in 1611 English, without the aid or assistance of any lexicon or grammarian and without the assistance of any depraved dingbat who thinks that thirty years of formal education equips him to pose as a [Biblical ]{.class_3759}scholar, when he is nothing but a dead orthodox [linguist]{.class_3759}. God likened His Son to a Worm (Psa. 22:6), a Serpent (John 3:14), a Lion (Rev. 5:5), and a Lamb (1 Pet. 1:19). Why shouldn't the Son of Perdition be likened to a Lion, a Bear, and a Leopard?

"Hippopotamus," your father's moustache.

"Crocodile," your foot!

"[He eateth grass as an OX"]{.class_3760} (vs. 15). When God cursed the serpent in Genesis 3, He classified him with [cattle ]{.class_3761}(Gen. 3:14), for he was a horned cherub originally (Ezek. 10:14) with a split hoof (Ezek. 1:7) before he fell (Ezek. 28:14). Observe how Daniel 4:33 puts the greatest type of Antichrist in the Old Testament into this same position: The position of an ox.

So here, as in Genesis 1:3--7 (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_3759}), the monstrous conceit and unbelief of the "recognized scholars" sweeps over their revisions and commentaries like a wave of hysteria. They cannot and will not take a chance on "scientists" making fun of them; so they must (like any dialectical materialist) convert ["behemoth"]{.class_3760} into something for the kiddies to feed at the zoo. In spite of the fact that "science" (per se) has proved to be nothing but a money-making clown for twenty-five centuries, the foolishness goes right on. Aside from furnishing jobs for people engaged in making a living, "science" (with the accumulated findings of twenty-five centuries from seven continents) has never accomplished anything for mankind but the following two minor items:

1\. It has enabled [some men ]{.class_3759}to live more comfortably, if they could afford certain luxuries.

2\. It has prolonged life a few more days, [if ]{.class_3759}certain materials were available.

And aside from that, there is no "scientific evidence" on earth, in hell, or in heaven that Ampere, Archimedes, Fahrenheit, Priestley, Ochoa, Berzelius, Thieme, Cavendish, Descarte, Urey, Riemann, Leeuwenhoek, Newton, Einstein, Kepler, Galileo, Winer, Landau, Compton, Davy, Dewar, Bibbs, Hubble, Janssen, Franklin, Lawrence Harriot, and Pascal (plus every major scientist alive today), with their combined talents, discoveries, inventions, and theories, accomplished anything more than the two items above. The 30,000 top brains of the philosophers and scientists combined (with the accumulated results of their experiments) have never solved [one major problem of mankind ]{.class_3759}in the history of the universe: The problems of disease, death, poverty, religious disunity, war, famine, and political strife are just as contemporary and perennial as they were when Noah got out of the ark. Correction: They are more so; Noah only had seven sinners to deal with.

And should we Bible-believing people bow down and kiss the toes of this insipid, silly, commercial "Baal?" To blazes with "science" unless it is the knowledge of God (see comments under Proverbs 1:1-15) or the knowledge of His Word.

You see, the defining of ["BEHEMOTH"]{.class_3760} as a [crocodile or hippo ]{.class_3759}was due to the fact that the modern scholars (any critic of the [AV]{.class_3759}) had already altered 1 Timothy 6:20 (see the [NIV ]{.class_3759}or the [NASV]{.class_3759}) so that the warning against ["SCIENCE"]{.class_3760}---["FALSELY so called"]{.class_3760} (see [AV ]{.class_3759}text)---went unheeded. ["Behemoth"]{.class_3760} is not a physical beast that you can see; God made him, and God sees him, exactly as the ["Leviathan"]{.class_3760} of the next chapter.

"[His force is in the navel of his belly"]{.class_3760} (vs. 16). Naturally, for his belly is WHITE; the leopard is an integrated animal (see comments on Revelation 13), so the "Japheth part" of him is his BELLY. ["His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron"]{.class_3762} (vs. 18). (See comments under Cain and Tubalcain, in Gen. 4:22.) Now, these are some bones that can only be pierced to the marrow (Heb. 4:12) by the sword (Heb. 4:12) of the One who made behemoth; so we are not surprised to find these words: ["He that made him can make HIS SWORD to approach unto him"]{.class_3762} (vs. 19). Quite naturally (and normally, if you are acquainted with the bungling stupidity of the modern translations) the [RV ]{.class_3761}committee tells us that it should have been "He (God) furnishes the rhinoceros with a sword." ("Rhinoceros," your Aunt Harriet!)

"[He is the chief of the ways of God"]{.class_3760} (vs. 19). Then he is kin to the Leviathan of Chapter 41 who is ["a king over all the children of pride"]{.class_3762} (41:34). These ["kings"]{.class_3762} and ["chiefs"]{.class_3762} control ["princes"]{.class_3762} (Dan. 10:20) and ["rulers"]{.class_3762} (Eph. 6:11--12) in the heavenly places (Isa. 24:21), and they are represented by ANIMALS (see Dan. 7:4--6; Ezek. 19:2, 28:2; Luke 13:32). ("Hippopotamus," your crab apples!) Behemoth is Beast Number 13 in the list God has just given: goats, hinds, wild asses, unicorns, peacocks, ostriches, horses, grasshoppers, hawks, eagles, lions, and ravens---Chapters 38--39). No one who "took his Bible studies seriously or conscientiously" (apostate cliche used by those who promote the [NASV ]{.class_3761}and the [Amplified Bible]{.class_3761}) could miss the import of Mr. 13: He is described in Revelation 13, with his number in 2 Chronicles 9:13 and Ezra 2:13.

Now since God has used [real animals ]{.class_3761}many times to picture spiritual things (see 1 Cor. 9 for ministers and Isa. 40:31 for prayer warriors), we may say that there are some animals on this earth who might TYPIFY behemoth. Verses 20--22 could refer to some animal that is [LIKE ]{.class_3761}the behemoth, but he certainly could be no matchmate since no animal ["drinketh up a river, and hasteth not"]{.class_3762} (vs. 23); nor has anyone ever caught any hippo or crocodile swimming up and down the Jordan River. The commentators, in their fanatical rage against the [AV]{.class_3761}, evidently forgot that the river Jordan at the time of Job (note present tense: ["he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan\..."]{.class_3762}) hadn't floated crocodiles (or hippos) since Noah led them into the ark.

Again, one must never forget that TREES in the Bible (Job 40:21--22 and Ezek. 31:18) are not always as [leafy ]{.class_3759}as they look (Ezek. 31:3), and the ["field"]{.class_3760} (Job 40:20) is not always in a stadium; ["the field is the world"]{.class_3760} (Matt. 13:38). Again, we must not forget that the dragon in Revelation 12:3 casts a flood out of his MOUTH (Rev. 12:15) which he got from somewhere, and when he got it, [the Jordan River was SWELLING ]{.class_3759}(Jer. 12:5). Furthermore, this flood (Dan. 9:26) is a literal flood of water (Rev. 12:15), and only a miracle prevents the escaping remnant of Israel (Matt. 24:22) from drowning (Cf. Exod. 10--15 and comments).

We are plainly on supernatural grounds when dealing with behemoth and Leviathan, and when we get on these grounds, the failure of Conservative and Fundamental scholarship is so glaring and so pitiful that we are not only led to question their "loyalty to the Bible," we are going to be led to disregard their profession of faith in it. They are not Bible believers: [They are destructive critics]{.class_3759}. Observe how every new translation on the market made a question out of verse 24 ([ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV, Phillips]{.class_3759}, etc.) because there is a perfect agreement between Fundamental scholarship and Liberal infidelity where the authority of the Bible is concerned. There is no interrogative ["he"]{.class_3759} (Hebrew) before verse 24 in any Hebrew manuscript known to man. The verse was made into a question (vs. 24) to match Job 41:2, 18, since no revisor or translator since 1800 had any more idea of what he was dealing with in Job 40:15 than a five-year-old Hottentot field-stripping a Swiss watch. Observe how C. H. Dodd (a rank Liberal) has made both behemoth and Leviathan refer to the same animal by changing the word ["Leviathan"]{.class_3760} to "whale!" (The [NEB ]{.class_3759}should be called "The Play Pen Bible.")

[]{#IMh58iSMTFKKU0J9qyaeHw3524.xhtml}

CHAPTER 41

41:1 Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?

2 Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?

3 Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?

4 Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?

5 Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?

6 Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?

7 Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?

8 Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.

9 Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?

10 None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?

11 Who hath prevented me, that I should repay him? whatsoever is under the whole heaven is mine.

Having "blown it" in the previous exposition, the majority of "recognized scholars" go right on "without the foggiest."

1\. [ASV ]{.class_3759}(1901): Leviathan is the crocodile.

2\. [NASV]{.class_3759}: Leviathan is a crocodile.

3\. The [New English Bible]{.class_3759}: Leviathan is a whale.

4\. [RSV]{.class_3759}: Leviathan is a crocodile.

5\. Adam Clarke (postmillennial): Leviathan is a crocodile.

6\. Williams (premillennial): Leviathan is a crocodile.

7\. Dummelow (amillennial): Leviathan is a crocodile.

8\. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}(postmillennial): Leviathan is a crocodile.

9\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown: Leviathan is a crocodile.

10\. The [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_3759}: Leviathan is a crocodile.

11\. The Lockman Foundation ([Amplified Bible]{.class_3759}): Leviathan is a crocodile.

12\. Mickey Mouse: Leviathan is Snoopy.

13\. Pogo: Leviathan is the Wizard of Id.

Having sampled the best brains of every branch of Conservative, Orthodox, and Fundamental scholarship (plus the Liberals), let us take the only proper course of action which one should take in view of the "scholarly agreement" of those listed above: Let's just [throw all of their scholarship out of the window ]{.class_3759}and pretend they never posited an opinion in the first place

After all, where 100 percent of the godly scholars agree in correcting God, the best policy to follow is just to ignore their godliness and their scholarship. So for awhile, let us put the kiddies on the shelf, and let us pretend that 300 years ago God told it "like it was" and gave it to us "as it is" and that we have the revealed truth of God in our hands; consequently, we have access to information denied to 100 percent of the scholars who think otherwise. Since this chapter is the longest chapter in the Bible on the person and work of Satan, we should have known from the start that the best brains of Christendom couldn't "hack it." They couldn't cut Genesis 1 rightly, so why should they cut the deck right here?

1\. The being described is ["king over all the children of PRIDE"]{.class_3760} (vs.34).

2\. Unlike the "crocodile (that's a "crock!"), he is ["made without FEAR,"]{.class_3760} and, unlike the crocodile, he beholds ["all high things"]{.class_3760} (vss. 33--34).

3\. Unlike the crocodile, [Leviathan has more than one HEAD]{.class_3759} (Rev. 12:1--6, Psa. 74:14); in this respect he matches the dragon of Revelation 12.

4\. But all doubt is dispelled about Leviathan's identification when Isaiah tells us (in good old 1611 Elizabethan English!) that "Leviathan" is a DRAGON and a SERPENT (Isa. 27:1).

("Crocodile," your bunsen burner!)

When the deluded scholars---any on the New Scofield Board of Editors or the Lockman Foundation or any other Fundamental or Orthodox group---try their hand at converting Satan into a "crocodile" or a "whale," they give out with the most pitiful expositions heard on this earth since Cain let out with his first yell. Recognizing that a white whale is a type of Satan (see Psa. 104:26, Moby Dick, Jonah 2:1, etc.), any fool could tell that the Leviathan of Job chapter 41 is moving out beyond the solar system. For the purpose of expounding the passage as a unit, we shall print the remaining verses.

41:12 I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.

13 Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?

14 Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.

15 His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.

16 One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

17 They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.

18 By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

19 Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.

20 Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.

21 His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.

22 In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.

23 The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.

24 His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone.

25 When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.

26 The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.

27 He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.

28 The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

29 Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.

30 Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.

31 He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.

32 He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.

33 Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.

34 He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.

Having read the passage, let us now ask the reader a question: "What would any 'godly' Christian think Leviathan was if he [read the passage ]{.class_3759}and compared it with [what God said it meant ]{.class_3759}in Revelation chapter 13; Psalm 74; and Isaiah 27?" That is: "What would any 'godly' scholar think if he had enough sense to read a third grade English grammar?" (And I meant "third grade" exactly as I said it.)

Second question: "Why is it that everyone of these 'godly' Bible wreckers missed the right interpretation when any fool could have spotted it [without any knowledge ]{.class_3759}of Greek or Hebrew?"

Third question: "How does a knowledge of the original languages and an orthodox view about 'verbal inspiration' help any student understand the Bible if the professionals who had this training cannot understand a passage as simple and as rudimentary as Job 41?"

The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}glories with "The last application (crocodile) is NOW almost [universally accepted by ALL scholars."]{.class_3759}

All right then, we can be perfectly safe in [discarding ]{.class_3759}it [immediately]{.class_3759}.

1\. Leviathan is a water animal of the serpentine, reptilian, or amphibious class (vss. 15, 31).

2\. Leviathan has [seven heads ]{.class_3759}(Rev. 12:1--16), and he is RED; not green, gray, or dark green (as a crocodile) or gray, brown, or white (as a whale).

3\. Leviathan can whip anyone, anytime, anywhere (vs. 8), although he has "no hope" (vs. 9).

4\. If you could see Leviathan (vs. 9), you would faint (2 Kings 6:17).

5\. Nobody can stand before Leviathan; so how will they stand before the Creator who made him (vs. 10)?

6\. Nothing but the word of God can sunder his scales (vs. 17) or reach his bones (Heb. 4:12, 13), and that is why Christ uses the Sword of the Spirit (Luke 4:1--13) in dealing with him.

7\. Leviathan is like a real Chinese dragon---firebreathing and all (vss. 18--21).

8\. Leviathan is worshipped, by those who raise him up (Job 3:8---ah, the unsearchable riches of the Elizabethan text!), with self-mutilation (vs. 25, cf. 1 Kings 18:28).

9\. You cannot fight Leviathan with carnal weapons (2 Cor. 10:4), for he is a spiritual being (vv. 26--29).

10\. His followers, who are like him (John 8:40--44), smoke cigarettes (vs. 19) and kindle the coal by inhaling (vs. 21) the material they will inhale in Hell (Rev. 14:10--11). \[Ah, the unsearchable riches of the archaic, outdated, "hard to understand," mid-Victorian Reformation text. How profound are its corrections on the "original manuscripts," and its judgments past finding out on the New Scofield Reference Bible!\]

11\. The ["deep"]{.class_3760} of verse 31 is not the Jordan River or the Mediterranean Sea, nor is it your kitchen sink. The ["deep"]{.class_3760} of verse 31 is the deep of Genesis 1:1--3 and the deep of Job 38:30; and if you are going to learn about the ["deep things of God"]{.class_3760} (1 Cor. 2:10), revealed only by the Holy Ghost (1 Cor. 1--2), you are going to have to dump Hackett, Keil, Delitzsch, Dummelow, Clarke, Kline, Budde, Peake, Robertson, Cheyne, Machen, Davis, Warfield, Alford, Wordsworth, Souter, Schaff, and Wuest like you would dump out a sack of rotten potatoes.

12\. The ["deep"]{.class_3760} (vs. 32) is not ["upon earth"]{.class_3760} (vs. 33), and where the credulous "scientists" of 1492 put their dragon in the Atlantic Ocean, the credulous dupes ("scientists") of 1980 LEFT HIM: wrong place. Leviathan is in the waters over your head, [beyond the solar system ]{.class_3759}(see extended criticism of C. I. Scofield and his followers in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_3759}, Gen. 1:1--6). When in doubt, always throw 100 percent of the scientists out so that they can fellowship with 100 percent of the modern Fundamentalists you just pitched out.

[Leviathan is a detailed description of Satan as God sees him]{.class_3759}. Leviathan is no more a "crocodile" or a "whale" than Adam's house cat.

And if thirty years of "Christian education" would make your teacher think that Leviathan was a "crocodile," you'd better leave that kind of "education" alone. Any education that would cover up for the Devil, in the greatest chapter in the Bible [about the Devil, is inspired by the Devil]{.class_3759}---no matter what scholar or school does the cover up work. ["Rebellion is\...WITCHCRAFT"]{.class_3760} (1 Sam. 15:23). Rebellion against the written authority of the Holy Bible, on the grounds that the "warlock" is smarter than the Author of the Bible, is ["witchcraft."]{.class_3760} "Fundamentalist witchcraft" and "Liberal witchcraft" and "Neo-evangelical witchcraft" are no different than Voodoo witchcraft or Black Magic. (See Riplinger, [New Age Bible Versions, ]{.class_3759}1993).

A number of questions are asked Job in regard to the one who brought the first accusation against him back in Job 1:9.

"[Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 1). No, you can't, although the animal described is plainly aquatic. ["Or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 1). No one can "go fishing" for the Devil with a hook on the end of a line, let down over the side of a ship. ["Canst thou put an hook into his nose?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2), like a ring is put through the nose of a bull? ["Or bore his jaw through with a thorn?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 2). Here, the thorn is used as an augur to drill a hole that a line or chain can be passed through; the most common modern form known by any farmer is the "fish stringer" that is run through the lower jaw (or the gills) of a fish that has been caught.

"[Will he make many supplications unto thee?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). No, the devil has never had to go in supplication to anyone---not even God. You will notice that in Satan's conversations with the Almighty, there is never any cringing or cowering or begging (vs. 24). (Like Lord Byron said on his death bed: "Shall I sue for mercy? No! Come, come, let us be a man to the end.") Leviathan is ["made without fear"]{.class_3762} (vs. 33), and those women who listen to him are made the same way (see Job 39:16 and comments).
