["Will he speak soft words unto thee?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). He didn't plead with Eve, and he didn't plead with Christ, and he won't plead with you. Any ["soft words"]{.class_3762} from Satan come through Liberal preachers, Catholic priests, and Fundamental scholars in the form of Romans 16:18 and 2 Peter 2:18, but he never "pleads" with anyone.

"[Will he make a covenant with thee?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 4). He will with Israel (Isa. 28:18 and Dan. 11:32), and that covenant will be worth about as much as the NATO pact or the SALT agreements or any treaty signed by Russia or the USA in the last seventy years. From 1400 to 1945 there were 8,000 treaties signed between nations, and not one of them held good for any longer than four years on the average. ["Wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 4). God can, and as surely as Nebuchadnezzar was the Lord's "servant," so Satan will only have the power of Romans 13:1--4 under the permissive will of his Master (Dan. 11:31--36). You might put some aquatic or marine animals in a bowl or an aquariuim and keep them, but not Leviathan.

"[Wilt thou play with him as with a bird?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5). You can with gold fish, minnows, canaries, parakeets, parrots, and doves, but you cannot do so with "Leviathan." Men can play with crocodiles; they have been known to jump into the water with them and wrestle them, but you won't jump into Satan's "pond" for a wrestling match unless you are as stupid as those who are trying it at Houston Space Center (Jude 9). ["Or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?"]{.class_3762} (vs. 5). No, but God will: Look at Revelation 20:1---which is done for the sake of the Virgin Bride, the restored wife (Hosea 2), the concubines (S. of Sol. 6), and the ["virgins without number"]{.class_3762} (Song of Sol. 6:8). Ah, what a light that 1611 sheds on the Greek and Hebrew lexicons!

"[Shall the companions make a banquet of him?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6). Not [Job's]{.class_3761} companions, but [Elijah's]{.class_3761} companions will! Leviathan is given for ["MEAT to the people inhabiting the wilderness"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 74:14) when the events of Exodus 15--20 will take place again (Micah 7:14) in the Tribulation (Rev. 12:13--17). The manna is connected with Leviathan's heads (see Exod. 16:14 and comments). (Ah, what light the 1611 [AV ]{.class_3761}sheds on the obscure mumblings of Jean Dixon and Edgar Cayce and the gurus of Rama Krishna.)

"[Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons\...or\...fish spears?" ]{.class_3760}(vs. 7). No, because of verses 26--29, he is marine, he is aquatic, he is reptilian, he is serpentine, and he is amphibious; but you cannot land him or gaff him with any method known by any fisherman, ancient or modern. Of the 3,000 Fundamental and Conservative infidels who wrested the text to their own destruction, Martin Luther is perhaps the only "faithful man" to be found, for the familiar hymn (written by Martin) states: "And armed with cruel hate, on EARTH IS NOT HIS EQUAL!" ("Ein Feste Burg"). This line is from Job 41:33, and Martin was close enough to the devil in his dealings with papists to understand the text; he never mistook Leviathan for a moment to be a "crocodile."

"Crocodile," your CB antenna!

The description given is that of a fire-and-smoke-breathing, seven-headed dragon, moving up and down through the universe and leaving a Milky Way (vs. 32) behind him. The ["sea"]{.class_3760} of verse 31 has no more reference to the Mediterranean, or the Sea of Galilee, than it does to Evil Knievel's Grand Canyon rocket (cf. Psa. 77:19; Ezek. 32:6; Rom. 10:7, etc.). The thirteen words in verse 34 point directly to the King of Terrors himself and tell Job that if he is trusting in his own righteousness, the "king of the children of PRIDE" (vs. 34) is his daddy (John 8:44).

What a compliment to a whale or a "crocodile!"

In closing our comments on the passage, we will point out one more red light to the Bible believer: That is, the radical fanaticism of the lunatic fringe (the new translations) in covering up the Roman Catholic act of "penance" (done for Satan) in the passage. Verse 25, in the God-given, God-preserved, God-honored text ([AV]{.class_3761}, 1611), says that ["By reasons of BREAKINGS they purify themselves,"]{.class_3762} indicating the masochism and self-mutilation of the black-robed Baalite priesthood of 1 Kings 18, who worshipped the Queen of Heaven (Jer. 44:19) with priests called ["father"]{.class_3762} (Judg. 17:10). The pro-Roman versions (anything published since 1800) have altered this verse (vs. 25) to the following:

1\. The Dodd Version ([NEB]{.class_3759}): "bewildered at the lashings of his tail."

2\. The Taylor Version ([Living Bible]{.class_3759}): "terror grips them."

3\. The Lockman Foundation ([Amplified]{.class_3759}): "because of terror and crashing they are beside themselves."

4\. The Apostate's Version ([NASV]{.class_3759}): "because of the crashing they are bewildered."

5\. The Dean Weigle Version ([RSV]{.class_3759}): "at the crashing they are besides themselves." (Observe the ecumenical agreement here between Bob Jones University and the National Council of Churches; [they both recommended the same corruption]{.class_3759}.)

6\. The Jehovah's Witness Version ([NWT]{.class_3759}): "due to consternation they get bewildered."

7\. Adam Clarke: "No version, either ancient or modern, appears to have understood this verse, nor is its true sense known" (Clarke's Commentary, p. 190). But, Adam. Since you [changed the order of the verses ]{.class_3759}(see 40:1 and comments) and got rid of the devil before you got into the chapter, why would anyone with any sense (who believed the Bible) think your opinion about the matter was worthy of consideration? After all, didn't the [ASV, NASV, RSV,]{.class_3759} and [NRSV ]{.class_3759}simply follow the corrupt Alexandrian [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}written 200 years after Paul died? Yes, they did. Origen's corrupt African manuscripts---which contain the Apocrypha as part of the Old Testament---read: "When he turns himself he terrifies all the quadrupeds of the earth." Umbreit says "they flee away bewildered." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown say "they wander from the way." Dummelow says that the "deer are afraid that slip among the broken places." The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}winds up our funny farm with "terror dances before him."

Well, how about this: "When a whale thrashes his tail swimming down to the Dead Sea, and blows frost out of his nose, all the antelopes dancing with the crocodiles slap the pedal to the floor board."

See how it's done? You just make a "do-it-yourself Bible."

You see how it's done? If you don't [understand ]{.class_3759}the passage you:

1\. Pretend the [AV ]{.class_3759}(1611) is "obscure."

2\. Pretend that some Hebrew or Greek text is "clearer."

3\. Then [when you can't expound the Hebrew or the Greek ]{.class_3759}as it stands (in ANY text), you invent a reading that is clear to YOU and then tell the stupid Christian that "no one can understand the passage." Did you get that "NO ONE?" That is, when a Greek or Hebrew scholar can't master what a Boy Scout could handle, then "[no one ]{.class_3759}can understand the passage!" How is [that ]{.class_3759}for "egotistical crackpot fanaticism?"

The reference plainly is to Communist Catholics in the United Nations (1980--1993) under the Antichrist (Rev. 13, 17) mutilating themselves to purify themselves from sin, as in the purifying rites of Bacchus and Pluto (see Hislop, [The Two Babylons]{.class_3759}); DRUNKEN DANCING accompanies the "worship service" (see Exod. 32 and comments).

To make sure that you miss the truth of the passage entirely, the Taylor paraphrase (["Living" Bible]{.class_3759}) mistranslates the last verse to read: "he is monarch of all that he sees," instead of ["he is a KING over all the children of PRIDE."]{.class_3760} This deft twist of the wrist, which Taylor in his naivete calls "a paraphrase" (and which buyers of the propaganda accept as a "paraphrase"), leaves the modern Christian facing a passage that deals with no creature known to God, man, or the devil. [Taylor's ]{.class_3759}Leviathan can stand up (vs. 25), but he is not in the deep (vs. 31), and he is not in the "sea" (vs. 31). But he moves like a steam roller (vs. 30), and he has flesh (vs. 23); yet no one can capture him (vs. 9) or get at him with a spear, harpoon, or iron javelins (vss. 7 and 29).

("Crocodile," your Aunt Harriet's can opener!)

Crocodiles [are ]{.class_3759}caught with spears; they can be captured and skinned (and sometimes eaten); they are not without "fear"; and plenty of natives "stir them up" (vs. 10) to get their hides, their eggs, or just run them off from the laundry at the river's edge. Taylor's "Leviathan" is a Disneyland joke.

And here we shall end comment on the greatest chapter in the Bible on Satan. There is not as much material in Ezekiel 28, Revelation 13, or Isaiah 14 as there is here; so we may expect the destructive critics of the Reformation text (Christian universities and colleges first) to either reject the text, alter the text, confuse the text, or misinterpret the text, and then cover up for the Devil so he can slip through undetected. God said that He would NOT ["conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12), but they are concealed by the deceitful, slovenly, infidelic works of Conservative and Orthodox scholars who are always mouthing off about "the fundamentals of the faith."

CHAPTER 42

42:1 Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

2 I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee.

3 Who is he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.

4 Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

5 I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.

6 Wherefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.

You've got to hand it to Job; he does "gird up his loins like a man," and he does answer (vs. 4). The only catch is that this time he lets his soliloquy of Chapter 31 drop like a hot rock. Not one word that breathes of "self" passes Job's lips in this final speech, nor does Job waste any breath pointing out the hypocrisy and inconsistencies of his "comforters." Face to face with the Supreme One, to whom "everyone of us will give account," Job accounts for himself (Rom. 14:12), exactly as you and I will. In spite of the efforts of Liberals (and Conservatives and Fundamentalists) to "cool" his confession, Job says the right thing (see vs. 6) in the right way, and God Himself bears witness to it in verse 7.

"[I know thou canst do every thing"]{.class_3760} (vs. 2, cf. Psa. 145 and 1 Chron. 28.)

"[Who is he that hideth counsel\...?"]{.class_3760} (vs. 3). Job repeats the Lord's question of 38:2 and explains, ["I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not"]{.class_3762} (vs. 3). And this is what Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar should have chimed in with on the spot; all three of them had said plenty ["without knowledge."]{.class_3762}

"[I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear; but now mine eye seeth thee"]{.class_3760} (vs. 5). Job is looking at the Theophany who appeared to Abraham (Acts 7:2), Manoah (Judg. 13:3), and Jacob (Gen. 32:24). This is no "astronut" (it's spelled right) speaking out of a flying saucer. The UFO "humanoids" (and the literature on ancient civilizations and mysteries) are as unlike the Angel of the Lord (or Christ) as Charles Adams' cartoons are unlike Thoreau's [Walden]{.class_3761}.

If Jesus Christ were a "humanoid" from outer space, He was the most critical, negative visitor this earth ever had (see Matt. 23 and John 9). He professed to have come this way for warfare and division (Luke 12:51) and would only save people who trusted Him as much as they trusted God. Furthermore, He stated flatly that there wasn't any point in wasting money on helping poor folks when you could give the money to Him (John 12:8). (My how "characteristic" of the "helpful humanoids" who keep popping up to show men how to "live together peaceably.") Imagine a UFO occupant telling you that bigger and better wars are on the way (Matt. 24), that you should get yourself a weapon for personal defense (Luke 22), and that the only hope for this planet lay in the finished blood atonement of a JEW. Ever read about any "Venusian," "Atlantean," or "Lemurian" who talked that way?!

Christ mentions His "Father" [over fifty times ]{.class_3759}in His discourses, and these discourses take up less than fifty pages; you never heard a UFO occupant spend five minutes talking about HIS ["father"]{.class_3759}! When one of God's real "men from outer space" shows up---see Genesis 16:9 for example---he doesn't ask where the power supply is; he just butchers 185,000 men (without a "ray gun" or chemicals) and goes on about his business (2 Kings 19:35).

"[Wherefore I abhor MYSELF, and repent in dust and ashes"]{.class_3760} (vs. 6). Atta boy, Job! Sock it to 'em! Sock it to these "positive thinking," "I'm okay, you're okay, we're all okay" girls. Lay it on 'em Job. Phase out these goody-goody "youth conflict, dynamic tension" kiddies who are always getting you to "find yourself" and "have faith in your fellow man." Put it on 'em Job---abhor YOURSELF.

This is the classic example of contrition: it is not sorrow for SIN; [it is sorrow for SELF]{.class_3759}. It is not sorrow for what we have [done]{.class_3759}, but for what we [ARE ]{.class_3759}(2 Cor. 7:10). When Balaam. Saul, Pharaoh, and Judas say, ["I have sinned,"]{.class_3760} it means no more than "Oops, I got caught." But when David and the prodigal son say it, the angels start a jubilee in Heaven (Luke 15; Psa. 51). ["Godly sorrow" ]{.class_3760}(2 Cor. 7:10) is sorrow for BEING WRONG, nor just DOING WRONG. No Catholic, Communist, Campbellite, or Charismatic will ever fully understand this great Biblical truth, for all of them leave a certain margin for SELF in their plans for salvation---both for the salvation of individuals and the salvation of "society." As proof of this, let us examine some of the last thirty-five trays full of garbage that have been served up to the Christian as "Bible versions."

1\. The Dodd Version ([NEB]{.class_3759}): "Therefore I melt away."

2\. Jehovah's Witness Version ([NWT]{.class_3759}): "I make a retraction."

3\. Dean Weigle Version ([RSV]{.class_3759}): "I despise myself."

4\. New Apostate Version ([NASV]{.class_3759}): "Therefore I retract." (Observe how this translation---recognized by John R. Rice and Bob Jones III---has adopted the Arian reading of the Jehovah's Witness Bible, The [New World Translation]{.class_3759}.)

5\. The Old Apostate Version ([ASV]{.class_3759}, 1901): Margin--- "loathe my words."

6\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown: "I abhor the rash speeches." (You see, the matter is as we have stated it. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown cannot imagine Job abhoring what he IS, only what he has SAID.)

7\. Little Red Riding Hood: "I am sorry I spoke so hastily."

8\. Charlie Brown: "Sorry 'bout that."

9\. The Great New, Brand Spanking New, NEW Authorized Revised, Common, Standard, Ecumenical, International, Federal New, NEW NEW AMERICAN STANDARD VERSION: "I realize that my isolated orientation developed a sectarian mentality with no adhesive factor to sensitize the expanded world view, with its renewed emphasis on the deversifiability of a cruciform ethic, where a scientific breakthrough could give a significant thrust to relationable structures in the blankety, blank, blank\...."

Anything but the truth.

The word ["abhor"]{.class_3760} ([AV]{.class_3759}, 1611) is too strong for the stomachs of the modern Fundamentalists and Conservatives, for the word means "something detested with extreme repugnance." And when did any modern revisor (saved or lost) feel that way about [himself ]{.class_3759}when confronted with the authority of the Holy Bible? Job looks at himself like a man would look at the rotting body of a dead frog, and that is the "look" which puts a man in line with Job 15:16---right on target.

It isn't "man is the measure of all things"; it is ["I am a man of UNCLEAN LIPS"]{.class_3760} (Isa. 6). It isn't "worthy of the noble name of MAN"; it is ["Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord"]{.class_3760} (Luke 5:8). It isn't "I think therefore I am"; rather it is ["Lord, what wilt thou have ME to do?"]{.class_3760} (Acts 9). Get it straight. Not one of those wilfully ignorant, self-deceived idiots from Socrates to Sartre had any more idea where he (or you) was headed (or how to get there) than a blind bollweevil in a blizzard. There is more real power in an ounce of [negative truth ]{.class_3759}than 15,000 tons of "positive thinking" where that positive thinking is the self-induced hallucination of a Bible- rejecting Pharisee. Now Job is in a position where God can bless him. And the Almighty never fails to meet His own obligations (Gen. 18:18).

42:7 And it was so, that after the Lord had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.

8 Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is right, like my servant Job.

9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job.

10 And the LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.

11 Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.

12 So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.

13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.

14 And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Kerenhappuch.

15 And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.

16 After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his son's sons, even for four generations.

17 So Job died, being old and full of days.

Verse 10 is the key to proper interpretation of the passage, and since not one Fundamental or Conservative scholar ("godly" or otherwise) ever paid any attention to what the Biblical text said where it crossed his "training," they quite naturally missed the greatest prophetic truth in the entire passage: [Job's children were resurrected.]{.class_3759}

Verse 10 placed the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}"smack dab" down on Psalm 126:4; Zephaniah 2:7, 3:20; Amos 5:27; and Jeremiah 33:7, 11, 26. That is, it does if you believe in the Scriptural method of comparing ["spiritual things with spiritual"]{.class_3760} (1 Cor. 2:13) instead of comparing Scripture with nineteen centuries of scholarly fungus. If you believe in ravaging the text and tearing it to pieces with the murderous scholarship of the "recognized authorities," then, of course, you can never find the cross- reference to truth. One must never forget that real Bible students who really DO "take their Bible studies seriously" never waste five minutes with the slime and algae of the revision committees (any and all since 1800) when trying to find Biblical truth. The revision committees of 1959--1970 ([NASV]{.class_3759}) knew nothing about Tribulation salvation, the Mark of the Beast, the chapter and verse system of the AV, the seven resurrections, the three raptures, or the doctrine of the Kingdom, although Bible believers had been preaching and teaching these truths ten to thirty years [before ]{.class_3759}the committee sat down at the table to undermine the truth of God.

They have no knowledge of anything that anyone needs, and[ if they needed it,]{.class_3759} anyone could find it without consulting the "pros" for anything. All the Bible they know, they got from Bible believers who preceded them anywhere from ten to 500 years. You will find nothing in any book John R. Rice ever wrote (aside from personal illustrations of practical experience in the ministry---which any minister has) that was not clearly presented by one to ten writers [fifty years before Rice was converted]{.class_3759}. You may read the entire set of books by Kenneth Wuest from cover to cover, and you will not find [ONE doctrinal truth stated from ANY Greek text ]{.class_3759}(or study) that is not clearly stated by Pieters and Scofield [fifty to sixty years before Wuest wrote his first book]{.class_3759}. You may read any set of books by any "recognized" Fundamental scholars since 1930, and you will only be reading the work of a man who God has showed nothing to since Larkin and Scofield got their material (1890--1930) from a [King James 1611 Authorized Version]{.class_3759}.

It is true that [occasionally ]{.class_3759}Larkin and Scofield (as Spurgeon and Torrey) yielded to the pressure of the "godly brethren," who always were around to tempt them to sin against the Bible. Once in awhile, you will find an isolated quotation from an [RV ]{.class_3759}(or a reference to the "Greek") in the writings of these men; this was done in a moment of weakness (["all have sinned!"]{.class_3760}) when they wanted to be "accepted" as Bible scholars. But these men got their revelations from the same place that Carey, Goforth, Livingstone, Brainerd, Edwards, Wesley, Tennant, Finney, Sam Jones, Bob Jones Sr., Frank Norris, Billy Sunday, and Peter Cartwright got theirs: [A King James 1611, Authorized Version.]{.class_3759}

And if you want to put one of these man-pleasing, man-following copycats on the spot, just ask him this: "Sir, would you please show me one thing---just ONE---you have learned from your studies of the 'original languages' that I can't find in a dime store [King James Bible]{.class_3759}?" And then watch him hit the skids. All you asked for was ONE; surely he can show you ONE. [Just ONE will do]{.class_3759}. (All a linguist does, if he is Orthodox, is use the "original language" to prove what any Bible believer [already knows]{.class_3759} or can find out by reading a man who [didn't]{.class_3759} refer to the "originals.")

"[My wrath is kindled against thee\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 7). The speech is aimed at Eliphaz, but it includes his two buddies: ["For ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant JOB hath"]{.class_3762} (vs. 7). In the first place, Job did say, ["I have sinned"]{.class_3762} (7:20), and none of his comforters said that about themselves one time in thirty-eight chapters. It is true that the [New English Bible]{.class_3761}, the ["Living" Bible,]{.class_3761} the [RSV]{.class_3761}, and the grossly corrupt [ASV ]{.class_3761}(old or new) have all altered Job 7:20 so that Job did NOT "speak that which was right," but changing Job 7:20 to read "if I have sinned," or "if I sinned," etc., doesn't relieve the evangelical apostates of their responsibility to God. Job DID say, ["I have sinned,"]{.class_3762} and that was the right thing to say. The irresponsible Fundamentalists who slapped the truth out of Job's mouth sinned without saying it themselves, but ["be sure your sin will find YOU out." ]{.class_3762}He did say, ["I abhor myself," ]{.class_3762}and that was the right thing to say. What the new versions---"in the modern, updated English"---say Job said is what he did NOT say, and till heaven and earth pass away God will not write His Book like Machen, Cheyne, Warfield, Keil, Robertson, Delitzsch, Olson, Weniger, Bauer, Hort, Aland, Metzger, Thieme, Origen, Gesenius, and Walvoord THINK it should be written.

We have never referred to Robertson, Warfield, Machen, and Hort as "unbelievers" or "liberals" in regard to John 3:16, but since the apostasy within the body of Christ has always been carried on by saved people who "love the Lord" (to quote one destructive critic of the AV), who are "loyal to verbal inspiration" (to quote another two-faced hypocrite), we are not really concerned with "liberals" or "Neo-evangelicals" at all. What we have to deal with in the last half of this century is puffed- up, bloated, egotistical, SAVED stuffed-shirts who think they are smart enough to correct God's Book simply because they have been exposed to nineteen centuries of scholarly trash.

What we have to deal with is men like A. T. Robertson, Warfield, Wuest, Hort, Machen, et al., who became deluded, self-deceived dupes in the hands of Satan, as he sought to over-throw Biblical authority---not the "fundamentals." These men trained the faculties who trained the faculties, who are now training young men for a full-time ministry of correcting and repudiating the words of God. They were tools for the greatest apostasy the world has ever seen, and nothing that the Catholics or Liberals pulled off between 1582 and 1980 could match it. We may say, without fear of anyone (or consideration of anyone's feelings), that these "Conservatives" did more to destroy the character and manhood of American Christianity than the National Council of Churches, the Communist Party, and the Roman Catholic Hierarchy. Their damage to the minds and ministries of 50,000 young preachers (1890--1980) is simply incalculable, and it will never be computated accurately until the Judgment Seat of Christ.

"[Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams\..." ]{.class_3760}(vs. 8). The reader will observe that the requirement places the time element (see comments on date of writing in the Preface) back to Gentile worship before the Law. Numbers 23:1 and 14 shows that Balak and Balaam recognized the sacrifice of Job 42:8 as the standard sacrifice. ["Lest I deal with you after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is right, like my servant Job"]{.class_3762} (vs. 8). Then Job was right all along, [even when he was wrong]{.class_3761}. He was wrong (vs. 3) in that he had tried to justify himself and had tried to put God "down" for dealing with him unfairly, but Job was right on three counts which his buddies had all missed:

1\. He was right in assuming that in this life the wicked do not always get paid back in full.

2\. He was right in assuming that the same things can befall a saint which befall a rascal.

3\. He was right in assuming that since man was a sinner, he needed a ["daysman"]{.class_3760} or "mediator" to stand for him in the presence of God.

"[So Eliphaz\...did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job"]{.class_3760} (vs. 9). That is, the Lord accepted Job's petition on their behalf. This time Job himself got to be the ["daysman," ]{.class_3762}or mediator (vs. 8). Since Abraham did this same thing in Genesis 20:17, we are again reminded of the patriarchal setting of the Book, and once again, we should make a mental note that if some brilliant grammarian (with access to "more ancient manuscripts," etc., etc.) pokes his head through the bushes and tells us that "light from the Dead Sea Scrolls shows, blah, blah, blah, that Job took place after the time of Moses," we should give him the raspberry and send his way a token greeting of No. 6 squirrel shot.

And now, again (for the "umpteenth time"), the fat hits the fire.

"[And the LORD turned the captivity of Job\..."]{.class_3760} (vs. 10).

With two dozen clear indications that the [Book of Job ]{.class_3759}is a future prophecy (see Preface), the ravening critics of the [AV]{.class_3759}---Fundamentalist foremost---draw out their bloody butcher knives, hatchets, frog stickers, shivs, daggers, and meat cleavers and begin to hack and mutilate the Holy Bible like killing was going out of style. 1\. "And the Lord restored the fortunes of Job" (Lockman Version, miscalled the "[NASV]{.class_3759}").

2\. "And the Lord turned Job to repentance" (Jerome's [Vulgate]{.class_3759}).

3\. "The literal use of this phrase is common, the metaphorical use of it uncommon. Still it is so simple a metaphor. It signifies, as Professor Lee remarks: 'a restoration to former happy circumstances"' (the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, p. 663).

4\. "Turned back the captive condition" (Jehovah's Witness Version: [NWT]{.class_3759}).

5\. "The Lord restoreth his wealth and happiness" (Taylor Version: [TLB]{.class_3759}).

6\. "The Lord restored the fortunes\..." (Dean Weigle Version: [RSV]{.class_3759}).

7\. "So the Lord restored Job's fortunes\..." (Dodd Version: [NEB]{.class_3759}).

Quite naturally, the compromising [Amplified Bible ]{.class_3759}(sometimes used by Oliver Greene) has the Reformation reading and the Communist reading together in the same verse; that is, the [AV ]{.class_3759}text has been adulterated with the [RSV ]{.class_3759}text [by putting them together in the same verse]{.class_3759}. Typical.

Now. the question we need to ask ourselves is this: "Since when should the opinions of Dr. Rawlinson (The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}) or Dr. Lee be taken seriously since neither man believed two-thirds of the Old Testament?" Do you think any of us who really "take our Bible studies seriously" would go to any man [who has been delivered to Satan ]{.class_3759}to get a "qualified opinion"? And do you think any of us who believe that Book are stupid enough to worry about "slandering the dead" or slandering "the sainted memory of these 'giants' of the faith"? Why worry about a sinner who slandered the [King James Bible ]{.class_3759}every day of his life? These two-faced punks, who worry so much about "the due reverence to the dead," have never failed to [slander and vilify the translators of the AV 1611 and the men who defend it ]{.class_3759}(Hills, Ray, Fuller, Burgon, etc.). A little "double standard" there, eh Doctor?

Verse 10 says ["turned the captivity," ]{.class_3760}and it says this after forty-two chapters, matching the forty-two months of the second half of Daniel's Seventieth Week (see Dan. 9 and Rev. 12--13), and after seven days and nights (Job 2:13), matching the seven year period (Dan. 9:25) where Satan (Job 1, 2) will be persecuting Israel in the land of Edom, in Uz (Lam. 4:21). The Book of Job was preceded by a Book describing the return of the Jews to Palestine and the rebuilding of the Temple (Ezra and Nehemiah); it is followed by the King Messiah of whom David (Psa. 1--2) was a type. With this much prophetic information available in an English text, which any sixth grade reader could read---[without referring once to any Hebrew or Greek text used by any scholar]{.class_3759}---how do we explain the irrational attack on the verse and then the blindness which follows it at verse 13?

("Crocodile," your aching back!)

Psalm 14:7 and 126:1 are the divinely appointed, infallible authorities for properly interpreting the verse, and what any Hebrew or Greek scholar may think the verse says can be classified under "N.T."---non-essential trivia.

Job is a type of Israel: Cast off, persecuted, rejected, restored, converted, and blessed.

Any system of Bible study, promoted by anyone, that denies [that ]{.class_3759}(or tends to obscure it) is SATANIC. And if you are gullible enough to think that Satan could not deceive Christian scholars (like the men who taught the men who wrote the textbooks used in Christian universities and colleges (any of them), you have been hoodwinked by the master deceiver (Rev. 12:9). No Roman Catholic or Communist ever got that far into Biblical theology to worry about altering Job 42:10. Only Fundamentalists and Conservatives were anxious to cover up the truth on that verse; the Catholics and Communists would change any verse on no other grounds than the desire to replace the Authorized Text.

Verses 11 and 12 point far beyond the oppressed saint on the ash heap in Uz; they point out the great prophecies of Isaiah 2, 11, and Amos 9, and scores of other prophetic passages which were discounted by Matthew Henry, Adam Clarke, Warfield, Machen, Calvin, and Moulton as "figurative metaphors."

"[They bemoaned him, and comforted him"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11, cf. Isa. 40:1, 49:13; Jer. 31:13; and Zech. 1:17.)

"[Every man also gave him a piece of money"]{.class_3760} (vs. 11). Compare this with the wealth the Jews will have in the Millennium following the Tribulation: Romans 11:15, 23; Isaiah 2:1--4, 66:10--14; 2 Chronicles 9:1--12, etc.

With passage after passage confirming the great prophetic typology found in Job, Biederwolf, Umbreit, Steiger, Roesch, Starke, Briggs, Welhausen, Riehm, Pusey, Tuch, Zahn, Vincent, Strong, Swete, Rosenmuller, Oosterzee, Duhm, Schulten, Kalisch, Keil, Kurtz, Hitzug, Weiss, Trench, Paulus, Plummer, Cheyne, Davidson, Oehler, Gunkel, Gratz, Dillman, Bleek, Bahr, Osiander, Schults, Semler, Riddle, Moulton, Reinke, Tischendorf, Westcott, Hort, Driver, Kennicott, Horton, Knobel, Lowth, and Bennett go sailing by the passage like a regatta in a 60-mile-an-hour wind---hot air and all. They, and their "kin," represent 5,000 books by 1,000 authors, through 1,000 years of church history, with nothing in them on the passage apart from what any Christian layman could find in his own language: Nothing else except pure unadulterated BALONEY.

"[So the LORD blessed the LATTER END of Job"]{.class_3760} (vs. 12). This is the fifteenth warning that the Book of Job is a future prophecy: Compare Numbers 24:14; Hosea 3:5; Daniel 10:14; Jeremiah 23:20; and James 5.

"[He had also seven sons and three daughters"]{.class_3760} (vs. 13). At verse 13(!) we see why there was such a concerted rush by the Catholics, Conservatives, Liberals, Fundamentalists, Neo- evangelicals, and Atheists to get rid of verse 10. For as sure as Job's ten children vanished in Chapter 1, they show up again in Chapter 42. To get rid of this Biblical truth as quickly (and as neatly) as possible, Taylor ([TLB]{.class_3761}) inserts "seven MORE sons" and "three MORE daughters" into the passage. In spite of God's warning to Kenneth Taylor (see Prov. 30:6), he has added in his notes that (p. 44) Job now has TWENTY children.

By [whom]{.class_3759}, Brother Taylor?

Where was Job's wife?

I said, "WHERE WAS JOB'S WIFE?"

Do you remember "Lot's wife"? Didn't Jesus Christ tell you to remember "Lot's wife"?

How could any man who "took his Bible studies seriously, etc." overlook the fact that Job and Lot are both types of Tribulation saints? And if they were, what would their WIVES be doing bearing children at the end of the Tribulation? When a commentator or revisor ignores the Bible's own dictionary for types, he develops a sort of "Disneyland exegesis" like Kenneth Taylor's.

Quickly, will someone come to Brother Taylor's aid and help him get rid of the truth?! Ah, yes. Dr. Rawlinson (the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_3759}, p. 664) is on hand. he is "a good man" who "loves the Lord, etc.," and surely he will not mislead us! "We need not suppose however that either the round numbers or the exact duplicity are historical." (Rawlinson) Ahh, so.

But then, on the other hand---are you listening? All of you stuffed shirts who think that the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}was the word of "scholars"---"we need not suppose that any writer in the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_3759}knows what he's talking about when he attacks the history of the inspired account on the grounds of his own stupidity."

(There are two ways to look at everything, aren't there? I thought you would agree. There is nothing like being [broadminded]{.class_3759}, is there?)

Job had ten more children, did he, and then lived 140 years after that, did he? [Look at verse 16]{.class_3759}. This would put Job at 190 years old if he was fifty when God restored him. That is, ten children before he was fifty and then ten more, [without a wife]{.class_3759}, after he was fifty. Now tell us something. In Job's time, Isaac lived to be 180, but he never married till he was forty years old, and then he had two children in the next 140 years. Are we to suppose that Job's wife---who told him to curse God and die and then never showed up again---had twenty children, ten of them after Job was fifty? One a year till he was sixty? One every two years till he was seventy? It took a miracle to give Abraham a child after he was ninety, and Isaac had no children after he was sixty. Aren't our commentators stretching things a little to make us think that the same woman who told Job to curse God and die came back to him and added ten more tax exemptions to his family before he got to be seventy? And if that doesn't stretch your credulity, aren't you just as capable of believing the following:

1\. There is a resurrection of dead saints at the end of the Tribulation.

2\. This resurrection [includes ]{.class_3759}the dead of Israel (Ezek. 37:1--12).

3\. Job believes in a resurrection in the "latter day" (Job 19:25).

4\. Christ's resurrection had an addenda on it which pictures this resurrection (Matt. 27:52, 53).

5\. [Israel gets her dead back later ]{.class_3759}(Isa. 26:17--19).

6\. Abraham, the father of the nation of Israel, believed that he would get his dead son back again (Gen. 22:5, John 8:56).

[Job got 'em back]{.class_3759} (vs. 13)!

The reason, then, for the exact number (seven and three) is "simplistic" (cliche by socialistic educators, 1970--1980) in the extreme: These are the children whom Job lost in Chapter One. The fact that the [names ]{.class_3759}of the daughters are given in verse 14 caused the expositors to reject the mass of evidence given above; for it is either assumed that Job would not have [renamed ]{.class_3759}the first three daughters, or it is assumed that the names are [different ]{.class_3759}names---not the original ones. But neither of these assumptions could counter-balance the testimony of Ezekiel, John, Genesis, Isaiah, and Matthew where they deal with a Post- tribulation resurrection (Matt. 24:31) and rapture of Tribulation saints (Ezek. 37). Job 42 is the exact duplicate of these matters, including the restoration of Israel and their subsequent Millennial blessings.

Plainly, and [Scripturally]{.class_3761}, Job got his children back from [the dead]{.class_3761}. And nothing more is ever said about Job's wonderful "help mate." She wound up with Lot's wife. Like Lot's wife (Gen. 19:26), she seems to have ["turned aside after Satan"]{.class_3762} (1 Tim. 5:15), and Lot and Job finish their trips alone. Lot finishes as a widower with daughters, while Job finishes as a widower with sons and daughters and then grandsons (vs. 16). Longevity is one of the characteristics of the Millennium (Isa. 65) since many of the Edenic conditions will be restored (Isa. 2; 11; 33; Amos 9:13; Rom. 8:18--22). ["The right of the firstborn"]{.class_3762} (Deut. 21:17) belongs to Israel as the "first born son" (Exod. 4:23) of Jehovah, and the right of the firstborn guarantees a ["double portion"]{.class_3762} (Deut. 21:17), so that is exactly what Job gets (vs. 10).

The commentators and revisors who made the Book ninety percent "figurative" or "metaphorical," must now suddenly take the phrase ["twice as much"]{.class_3760} (vs. 10) [literally]{.class_3759}, after altering the first half of the same verse. But Job did NOT get back [two wives]{.class_3759}; he didn't even get back the shrew he had. Therefore, to say that ["twice as much"]{.class_3760} refers to [people ]{.class_3759}is stretching the text to accommodate the unbelief of the scholar in the typology so clearly presented in the Book of Job.

"[So Job died"]{.class_3760} (as they will in the Millennium) ["being old and full of days"]{.class_3762} (vs. 17). As a little girl said in Sunday School: "All of God's stories have happy endings." The unhappy endings are found in the historical narratives of those who follow science, education, money, humanism, sex, religion, philosophy, or the devil to their own destruction. ["The end of the wicked"]{.class_3762} (Psa. 32:38) (see comments under Prov. 4:14, 10:6, 10:2--25, 15:9, and 11:10, 23) is easily predictable: It is outer darkness and a lake of fire. Where a man repents as Job did and accepts God's revelation as superior to his own wisdom---at the same time accepting God's judgment against himself as a sinner---the end is also predictable: A new heaven and a new earth and an eternity with a sinless Saviour in New Jerusalem (see Rev. 21--22, and comments).

And though the screams of wounded and dying Christians will yet echo up over the highways and skyscrapers of America and though the groans and cries of tortured saints may resound off the walls of jails and hospitals in the next few years, the end is predictable: New Jerusalem. The worst thing that can happen to a child of God is GLORY in the end, or the "final analysis." In the "end" Job comes out healthy, wealthy, successful, honored, blessed, and happy.

The man who cried out in his misery: "Oh my God, though you SLAY ME, yet will I trust in thee!" lived to see the day when he cried: "Oh my God, how good Thou hast been to me!" The man who spit out: "Leave me alone till I swallow down my own spit!" survived to proclaim: "Bless the Lord, oh my soul, and all that is within me bless His holy name!"

"[For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us\...for our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory\...we are more than conquerors through Him that loved us!"]{.class_3760} (2 Cor. 4:17 and Rom. 8:18, 37).

This great Book (which is quoted more than sixty times in the Bible) teaches us seven great lessons, and the plain, simple-minded, ordinary Christian can learn all of them without referring to any version, by anyone, other than the [King James 1611 AV.]{.class_3759}

1\. We learn that ["all those who live godly shall suffer persecution."]{.class_3760}

2\. We learn that God uses Satan to glorify himself.

3\. We learn that the best of men are sinners.

4\. We learn that some things cannot be explained apart from supernatural revelation.

5\. We learn that God's ways are not our ways, nor His thoughts our thoughts.

6\. We learn that ["all things work together for good to them that love God."]{.class_3760}

7\. We learn that God will be merciful and loving to any sinner who forsakes his own righteousness and accepts God's righteousness.

And thus we shall close the covers of the Book of Job and leave our nomadic friend from Selah, Petra, in Uz, strolling through his pastures, chatting with his friends, playing with his grandchildren, offering sacrifices for his sons ["lest they curse God in their hearts,"]{.class_3760} and maintaining his integrity---this time without [trusting ]{.class_3759}in it. As the ancient scroll rolls together, Job's ["hedge"]{.class_3760} (Job 1:10) has been rebuilt by the One who planted it, and in the portals of eternity when the Adversary approaches, he is warned: "No deal. Lay off him. He has been tried and tested. You'll have to pick on someone else."

"[Behold, we count them happy which endure. Ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very pitiful, and of tender mercy"]{.class_3760} (James 5:11).

And it would seem like a shame to add one more critical note to such a sublime ending, but lest we should "rest on our laurels" or be "at ease in Zion," may we not forget that the corrupt Alexandrian manuscripts promoted to the seat of authority by Stewart Custer ([Bob Jones University]{.class_3759}), Roberston ([Louisville, Southern Baptist Seminary]{.class_3759}), Machen ([Princeton and Westminster]{.class_3759}), Wuest ([Moody Bible Institute]{.class_3759}), Afman ([Tennessee Temple]{.class_3759}), and John R. Rice ([Sword of the Lord:]{.class_3759} Recommendation on [NASV]{.class_3759}) have deliberately violated Proverbs 30:6, exactly the way Kenneth Taylor violated it (see vs. 13), by adding fifteen words to the book of Job. These words are so obviously fraudulent that not even the idolators who worshipped [Sinaiticus ]{.class_3759}and [Vaticanus ]{.class_3759}dared to copy out their text at this point. The text of the [Septuagint ]{.class_3759}here reads as follows: "It is written that he will rise again with those whom the Lord raises up."

Nothing of the kind is "written" or "has been written" or ever will be "written" by the Author of the book of Job. This final scribal blooper is just as spurious as the depraved Greek text it came from---the Greek text used for the [NIV, NASV]{.class_3759}, and Taylor's paraphrase.
